*** IMPORTANT: This battery only properly charges from a laptop USB port.  DO NOT attempt to use with a car charger, wall charger, etc. unless you confirm that it puts out ONLY 500 mA, and no more.  See [UPDATE] section below, for more details. ***[Capacity summary: was able to (carefully!) charge my Droid X **which has an extended internal battery with 20% more capacity** 1.3 times.  If product worked as advertised, I should have been able to charge at least 2.3 times, assuming my Li-ion phone battery is at the poor end of the charging-efficiency spectrum.  Would expect at least 2.7 charges for Droid X with regular (non-extended) internal battery.]Overall design is very nice; it looks very nice, shape is good, very attractive.  But... several problems.First, this battery was supposed to be in 'new' condition, but was very clearly (and heavily) pre-used; the box had stains, sticky stuff, and extra circular stickers on it (from where the previous 'owner' broke the seal).Second, it didn't work.  My phone (Droid X with genuine Motorola extended battery BH6X, which holds 1800 mAh instead of the usual 1500) could only charge from it a total of about 1.3 times, before the external battery was totally drained.  I was surprised, so I repeated the test, and got the same results.  I was super careful: I let the external battery charge for 36 hours; I waited til my phone was down to 30%; then I charged it up to 100% (cell was on; gps/wireless off; all tasks killed; screen off/no activity).  I then disconnected the external battery right away, when the phone reached 100%.I then repeated the process; I plugged the phone into the external battery at 30%.  About two hours later, it was at 70%, but the external battery was 100% drained.Very disappointing performance.  It was only able to transfer 45% of its alleged  energy to the Droid X (1800 x 1.3 = 2340 mAh, out of 5200), but Wikipedia says that Li ion should be able to charge at 80-90% efficiency -- so something is clearly wrong here.I would stay away; I was worried when I bought it, noticing that many recent reviews (as of April 2011) had complained of the same.  I think they're selling a bunch of their returned units.  This really irks me, as I'm extremely busy, and really don't have time for this kind of stuff; I want to just buy a product that works and lasts, and I'm happy to pay whatever it takes.[UPDATE]Satechi contacted me.  They claim that smart phone chargers provide too many amps, and that this fries the external battery.  They recommend *only* charging from a laptop (where the USB is limited to 0.5A), whereas I used (in my two tests) chargers with 850 and 1000 amps.However, this is no excuse; this is a design defect.  You should be able to charge from any source, as long as it has at least 500 mA.  Every other electronics device on the planet has circuitry to limit the amperage it takes in to what it needs, and gracefully rejects excess current.  They tried very hard over the phone to convince me that this was not a design defect; I found this very disingenuous.  Review remains at 1 star... sorry, Satechi.[/END UPDATE]

In some ways this is just an unusual radio.  A glaring omission is a direct frequency entry keypad and the manual tuning is a bit slow and clunky.  Fortunately these setbacks are overcome by its truly superb ETM feature.  The digital signal processing (DSP) on this radio is much more effective at scanning and identifying frequencies than a typical frequency scan of a non DSP radio.  Just go to the band you want (AM FM or SW) and hit ETM and it will store every station available in the ETM memory.  Then you just rotate the dial one click at a time for each station much like one would with a TV remote.  Even weak stations are detected.  Not perfect at finding everything but probably 99% accurate.  Unfortunately with no keypad it will not function like the Tecsun PL-390 in that you can key in a station and hit the ETM button to add it to the ETM rotation.  This is handy in the rare occurrence when you know ETM missed a station.All of the Tecsun DSP radios I have tried have excellent FM reception and the 606 is no exception.  The 606 also has very good AM reception especially for it size due to an internal AM ferrite rod antenna that is nearly as wide as the radio.  It is my favorite radio to use at work because of how well it handles the interference you get in a steel structured office building.  The generous selection of 4 different bandwidths can also help block out some emf noise in addition to blocking interference from adjacent stations.  I consider the 606 a pocket radio.  I have yet to hear a pocket radio that plays as loud and clean as the 606.  And its sound is not tinny but rich and non fatiguing which is remarkable for its size.  My unit is only average when its comes to SW sensitivity but responds nicely to an external antenna which is supplied.  As other reviewers have reported my unit does have a keypad that can be difficult to press.  I have to press a little more deliberately and hold in the button in a split second longer than my other radios.  Not a deal breaker for me.Despite its quirks the Tecsun 606 is a fun high value high performance radio.  If you are looking for a pocket sized radio that has much better reception and sound quality than your average cheap radio then this is a good option.  Even if you are only interested in AM and FM I can think of no other pocket sized radio currently available with better reception and speaker sound.

My wife and I have back problems so we bought a high end mattress, ove $1000.  After sleeping on other beds on trips we decided it was really too firm so we got this mattress topper. It is incredible, sleep like a baby, I notice my wife does not move around as much, or make as much noise sleeping, and sleeps soundly.  It has just the right firmness for our backs and creates no pressure points so you sleep longer in one position getting more deep sleep.  We got a King Size, rolled it out and it did not look big enough, don't worry, it takes about an hour to expand so let it sit on the bed before putting the mattress cover and sheets back on it.  I actually "Crave" sleeping in the bed more.  When I rolled it out, and let it puff up, I then took a test ride, fell asleep immediately at 3PM it was sooo comfortable.  I did not notice any smell as reported by others.  I cannot recommend this product enough, and it is a great value.Update: 5/27/11  We bought my sister a similar topper at Kohl's and it is not the same as ours, is not as firm, we love this topper and cannot say enough about it.  When we sleep on other beds we really know the difference.  On this topper we sleep deeper and wake up refreshed!

Unfortunately this thing only lasted 6 weeks and I'm starting to have trouble with the left button on it. Sometimes it doesn't register a click at all and other times it registers multiple clicks, making it relatively useless (who wants unexpected double-clicks happening?). It's too bad, because I really wanted a decent upgrade to my previous logitech trackball which lasted several years. Previous ball has a nice solid click noise on the buttons, the new one has kind of a muffled click. I suspect cheaper microswitches were used.Also, the ergonomics of the new trackman are terrible. It feels like it was designed for the hand of a 7 year old rather than the hand of an adult. The primary buttons are too close together making it uncomfortable for extended use (especially gaming). There's two secondary buttons that are just not in a convenient place (unless your hand is the same size as a 7 year old). As others have commented, the ball itself doesn't glide as well as the previous model.In short, I'm quite disappointed both in the quality (failure in 6 weeks) and the ergonomics (doesn't fit my hand). I think Logitech needs to go back to the drawing board, redesign this thing, and spend an extra penny or two on decent switches.
The package says DC60. The mark on the PCB says UTV007. EasyCapViewer for mac does not detect it.This is my second easycap, the first was a genuine DC60 that actually had DC60 printed on the PCB.

I already have a lot of stuff sitting out on my counter, so I love that these stack. I have them filled with sea salt, kosher salt, and ground pepper. When I'm cooking I unstack them to have them ready, and then when I'm done they go back in their corner. I'm really glad I bought this.

I have a Winfast 2000XP Deluxe video capture card that I use to record my VHS tapes to my computer. The capture card has a 3.5mm port for the input audio, I don't know why it never came with a cable like this. Since my Sony TV does not have the ports for gaming systems such as PS2 I can now hook up my PS2 to the capture card and play on my monitor through my computer. This product works perfectly, the audio sounds as it should without any distortion.

BedBands are a great creation!  They actually do hold on sheets that no other type of band has held on.  I have a hospital style bed and everytime the back is up, the sheet pops off, but not any more!  BedBands holds my sheet in place like no other.  I love this product (have used for 1 day now).

This food processor (and others in this size range, I'm sure) just don't have enough room.  Anything that I wanted to chop / dice / mince had to be chopped small to fit into the hopper.  There wasn't much point in using it because I had to do so much prep work first.  I finally bought a full size model (Kitchenaid), and I suggest the same for anyone else looking for a food processor.I have found a use for this one, however.  I keep it in the laundry room.  In the summer the humidity makes my powdered laundry detergent clump up like a brick.  I put a chunk of the soap in here, and take it for a spin.  It works great!

I have a long skinny house and wanted to add another wifi access point at the other end.  I had a 50 Ft. cable and a 20 Ft. cable and joined them together with one of these and was able to reach, so it definatly does the job.

Gosh, I paid less than $3.00 and got 10 of these shields because they were so inexpensive.  Very happy thus far. Probably purchased too many but no big deal. I like them even more than the invisibleSHIELD, at a very small fraction of the cost.  Fits fine on my AT&T Samsung Galaxy SII Skyrocket.  5-Stars, no doubt about it.

It's o.k. for the price--but just barely.  A leisurely toaster, it also has the annoying habit of browning each side differently.  And NO, I do not mean when it's on the "bagel setting" either.  The slots are not particularly long, designed for a standard sandwich-sized slice; so if you bake your own bread or buy the artisanal variety, your choices are either cutting the slice in half, or doing a double-dip switching ends (and side-to-side, too, if you are in a mood for both sides to be toasted equally).  Nor are the slots especially generous in width.  Forget trying to jam a croissant down there, and if you set it on top, it will burn.Next time, I'll go for one of the better Cuisinarts or a Breville, or maybe get lucky and find one of those dandy all-stainless English Dualits on eBay for under a hundred or so--and I suggest you do the same.  I mean to say, this thing is likely to be sitting on your counter for years, so you might as well get something you actually enjoy, as opposed to a half-baked unit that is mildly annoying every time you use it.

I bought 2 different underbed storage items to try and I really liked the convenience of this.  We just got rid of our platform bed that had drawers so we definitely needed something more to put clothes in and hide away.  This works great!  It is the perfect height for sliding under our queen bed and I can easily fit 2 on one side.  I'm getting the second one today.I am currently using it for shirts.  I put a ton of tanks, tees, and camis in it and was pleasantly surprised at how much fit in there.  I would imagine you could use it to store just about anything you want.  Great buy!
very clear works fine

As an amateur photographer, this little guy is perfect. It can do so much for somebody who spends time trying to figure out how to frame a shot (love the fold-out screen!) OR how to bring out the aspects of a good photographer (I spend most of my time flipping between aperture control and some of the "creative" modes!).18MP is enough to edit with and still make a decent print, and this camera will help you learn all the basic tricks of the pros. I'd love to move up some day, but for right now I think spending some dollars on a few nice L-series lenses will get me further than a new body would.Enjoy!
They protect the screen great. They'res nothing else to say other than that but I have to keep writing until i get enough words hahahh :)

While it was mostly bubble free, it had a few and I do not like bubbles with a screen protector.  It seems to peel off too easily, in particular when it was rubbing against a case like the Incipio Active case system.

This one seems to be more solidly built than some of the others in the same price range.  Not sure what they use for plastics on some of this stuff but its like a small child could easily crumble them to powder in their bare hands.  Not so with this - it's a bit more solid, price considered.
I'm so happy with this purchase I love the look of my new case so bright I'm so glad I brought it.

I recently picked up the Note2 phone and needed a case so as to protect my new investment.  I can safely say this will protect your phone against most abuse although I did find the screen protector to not stick on all that well (although still not an issue) but what is an issue is the fact that it is cut in a way that does not fully protect most of the outer parts of the screen.  I would have liked it to cover more screen space for full protection for the price.Either way, I recommend this case, even if you swap out the screen protector front for another brand.

The product was as described but very hard to see through, which I have had other mirrored screen protectors which i was able to see through a ton better.If anyone ever needed a screen protector, go with the regular crystal clear screen protector or a privacy screen protector which doesn't allow the person sitting next to you to see what you are doing.Not worth it.
I love the color and it fixs perfectly. I love the way it looks on my phone, and it should also protect the phone from bumps and briuses.

These like most generic iphone/ipod products are a hit and miss. I'm batting .500 and because I'm a gambling man, I like my odds. MAy work perfect for you for a long time, may not work at all, or may work for one charge. Your taking a low risk and I think its worth the risk.

The only thing that could possibly make this item any better would be additional pockets for Secure Digital cards. I'm managing quite well regardless and find it to be of good quality and very compact.
Love the look of this key rack.  Perfect for a 1 bedroom apartment.  Solid hooks and easy mounting.  Looks very nice in entry-way.  Highly recommend.

My husband got me this beautiful tea kettle for my birthday last May. I have loved and used it daily since. I had the reviews so I really followed the directions for care and use. I will say I disagree with many reviews, you do fill only to below the spout but thats still plenty for 3-4 cups of tea, and as long as  you dont overfill it never dribbles all over it pours right into the cup, so great functionality. However, today, I have now had this tea kettle for about 8 months and I have discovered a huge chip in the enamel that somehow burned right off! I always use medium heat so this wouldnt happen and the chip is near the spout right under the fill line, about the size of my thumb, and not even touching the heating element. Im heartborken! I was hoping there was some sort of 1 year warranty but I cant find eveidence of 1...for $60 I would NOT purchase again!

Who needs a special tool to core and quarter an apple?Nobody really needs it unless they process a whole lotta apples. But this tool works so perfectly and so quickly that it is just plain elegant and fun to use it.  I see myself eating more apples...

this replaces the navy blue one I have had for 3 1/2 years that the back binding became frayed.  I use my ipad all,the time and this is a great cover.  Protects it well without being too bulky.  it easily sits horizontally for reading in bed.  fits well in a larger purse.

I gave these 4 stars because they are every bit as good as styluses costing 10x more. I would've rated them at 3-stars but that wouldn't really have been fair because it would be because I just don't care for using a stylus and not because these are lacking in any way. As it is, they work just fine. The plastic clip felt a little cheap to me, but I have seen this very stylus hanging in retail stores for everywhere from $5 to $15. So, if you like using a stylus these will probably work just fine for you... and you won't beat the price. Plus, at these prices, when you lose one (and you will) you won't feel as bad about it.

It does what it is supposed to do. It charges devices with micro USB. The cable is only 4 feet which isn't uncommon, but can be limiting in a lot of situations such as being able to quickly use the device without unplugging it. The charging block is twice the length of many other chargers, so if you are plugging it into a tight space or on a power strip, it can be a problem.

It's heavy, so I don't take it with me when I travel outside the country. I like it for the car trips and stopping at a hotel. I've secured it to hotel chairs and to bathroom toilets. Functions as expected.
I bought these as Christmas presents, so they haven't been used yet. They seem to be good quality. I'm happy with my purchase.
great knife.  did not expect it to be this sharp at such a cheap price.  cuts my homemade Crusty Italian bread with ease. Be careful or you will cut right into the cutting board.

It's a nice size. The magnet closure is a nice feature and the top is sturdy enough to pile towels or other essentials on without damaging it. I'd definitely order it again.  I have to say though, that I'm a little baffled by the reviews about how hard it is to move and needs handles.  This isn't the size or type of hamper I would move.  It stays in the bathroom and I unload the laundry into a laundry basket for transport.  This is more of a stationary hamper, if you ask me.  Even if it did have handles, I wouldn't be dragging it around.UPDATE: No smell.  I can't figure out why some would have a bad smell and some wouldn't, but I can assure you, I'm sensitive to odors, and ours doesn't smell.  It sounds like it could be a reoccurring, but not consistent, problem.  ?  I like the thing enough that I'd probably risk ordering it again and hope for the best, though.

Moved into a new house where there are numerous clothes rod at various vertical heights.  This allows me to put out of season clothes way up high, out of the way and not have to stand on a ladder.  Sturdy, light-weight, worth the money

I'm rapidly becoming a non-fan of Bluetooth travel mice. I'm a tech and I've seen multiple brands just stop working for a few minutes at a time. I can't narrow it down to one brand of mouse because both Logitech and Microsoft mice seem to just fall asleep mid-use even with fresh batteries. It doesn't seem to be one brand of notebook or adapter. I've seen it with Intel and Broadcom chipsets. I can't narrow it to one operating system because it happens under Windows XP and Windows 7. A search will turn up tons of threads on the subject but no answers.But getting beyond the general flakiness of BT I find this mouse particularly uncomfortable. It not much smaller than the Logitech Blue travel mouse but it feels much smaller and cheaper and with sharp lines where there should be smooth curves. Perhaps it feels cheap because it weighs a bit less but I find it far less comfortable to use than the rounder Logitech.Bottom line it's a decent travel mouse if you like the shape and feel but not my first choice in a BT travel mouse.

I wasn't real happy with his case. I've been riding my bike for a long time, and I have always put my phone in my fanny pack, but since I got this case I thought it would be great to have my phone attached to my handle bars on my bike. The instructions were not very clear, they seemed to be missing some key points, but it wasn't hard to figure it out. However, while riding my bike, I did notice that when I went over bumps, the phone wasn't very secure, and I knew it wouldn't be long before this case opened and my phone would fall out. So I decided to stop using this case and put my phone back in my fanny pack when I go for my bike rides.
This battery lasts all day. It's a huge improvement over the standard battery the phone came with. A must buy!!
This cable also just works. Good for in the car or by bedside. Like all though,if you don't hold on to the base before pulling away from the phone, thats it, its over!

On the positive side the battery life is good.The negatives I'll split into ergonomics, sound and RF performance.Ergonomics: It's secure on my big ears, but as others have mentioned that seal is nothing more than marketing and window dressing. There aren't many ear mounted devices that I can get on with at all, so if you have big ears this may be for you.Sound: Received sound is weak at best, if you don't have good hearing this isn't going to work for you. Transmitted sound is also weak, apparently, I have to accept what others tell me. If I want to talk quietly I have to use the phone, the headset forces me to talk loudly so that I can be heard. Noise cancelling is non-existant, if it was supposed to have any. The draught from the AC vents on the car is enough to cause callers to complain.RF: With the v505 it takes an unreasonable amount of time for the headset and the phone to get it together. You can't answer the phone by switching the headset on when you hear it ringing, by the time it's connected to the phone the call is in voicemail. Received audio is OK, but whenever I used the headset I am told that I sound very digitized. As a communications engineer I believe that some people need a serious talking to over the bluetooth performance.All in all I'd keep looking, which is why I'm browsing headsets now.In ten years I really haven't yet found a "good" headset, nothing approaching the standard of the military and aviation headsets I used to use, like comparing a ZX80 to a 3GHz Dell Pentium 4. Perhaps I just have unreasonable expectations, but if I could actually get a headset with good audio and good noise cancelling I'd pay a couple of hundred bucks. A decent noise cancelling headset will let you talk in a normal voice while standing between the exhausts of a 1500hp tank engine.
I'm replacing all my plastic cups with BPA free plastic.  These are the same size as my big red cups that I used to buy at Sam's for my morning smoothies.  And the price is right...

I absolutely love this infuser.  I've bought three different ones for my own personal use, the first one I purchased was the Ultimate Tea Infuser and I liked that one, however, after buying this one I gave the Ultimate Tea Infuser away.  What I like better about this one is that it sets well over every coffee mug (what I drink my tea out of) that I own.  The Ultimate Tea Infuser doesn't have a handle that spans the whole mouth of the mug so you have to balance it on the side of the mug, still that's not a deal breaker but after using this one I decided I like this one much better.  One person mentioned that the porcelain dish sets rather precariously on top of this infuser.  I've never used the porcelain dish that way, I only use it to set the infuser on once I'm done steeping the tea leaves in my mug.  Another person said it was hard to clean and that it rusted.  I haven't noticed either of those things so far.  This looks to be made out of stainless steel and I haven't had a bit of a problem cleaning it.  I've cleaned it both by hand and also ran it through the dishwasher.  Black tea leaves do stain it a bit but that can be cleaned up with baking soda and warm water easily enough.Another infuser that I absolutely love is the Finum Brewing Large Basket.I've bought a lot of loose leaf teas as Christmas presents this year and I've included both the Finum Brewing Large Basket, as well as, this infuser with each Christmas package of tea.

It took a good while to be delivered and the micro USB cable is a little short. Short and sweet. Great for a spare charger but not worthy of being your day to day charger. It works great with my Galaxy S4, my Kindle Fire and my Nexus 7 FHD(gen 2). I think the purchase is a good one just for the plug and the fact you can change out the cable for a longer one is a simple fix to the only complaint I have. It took nearly a month to arrive so the 3 stars are a rating based on the long wait and the shorter cable.

Pros:- Great sound- Easy setup- Bluetooth dongle range was amazing.  100 feet with no walls, 40 feet with walls.- Binds easy with other Bluetooth devices- Get a cell call in the middle of listening to music, it silences the music and beeps in this headset.Cons:- Too many buttons on headset, always hitting the wrong button.- No where can you buy a spare charger or dongle.- Dongle light quit working after 3 days and now it seems to always stay on.  Keeps a good charge, so charging it nightly seems to be ok.- For people with ears that sit back on their head, the bulk of the ear piece push the ears outward, causing a headache after a few hours.  Should of had more for the back strap instead of behind the ear.

I had the battery pack for one month and one half.  It has stopped charging my phone.  I contacted Knips and advise was to not charge the battery pack and the phone together.  That should have been directed bold in directions when opening the product.  I am sending it back.  Customer service responds quickly and advises on trouble shooting.  I have to buy another battery pack because my iPhone battery dies quickly.
I love the samsung phones, this time I am replacing the white S3 buy for me Facino vary and are very practical and works
This is my second LifeProof case.Love the cases, take underwater pictures and it is not too thick.The blue one looks kinda funny, the white looks much better.
Love this product, and it is so well made. I use it to protect my iphone. It is so light and can be carried around if you travel. This is a well made product.

Excellent crock pot! The lid seals well (unlike my last one) in the unclamped mode. I would have given 5 stars if there was a power light (sometimes when taking a crock pot to a pot luck or other similar function, you really don't know if the outlet is working or not until you find your dish cooling). The beautiful red finish ends the 'isn't that mine?' issue.

I bought this item to steam vegetables, mostly broccoli (and yes it does steam in the microwave with zero water added- although you could do that with just about any covered dish or container). But I have ended up using it to heat all kinds of things in the microwave as it is safer than plastic, doesn't melt at the highest temperatures, and won't burn your fingers, even after heating something like soup for several minutes. I like this so much I bought anOrka Personal 20-Ounce Oval Steamer, Bluefor heating up soups at work, as well as one for my Mom and Dad!

I own a good amount of high end cookware, and do a large amount of cooking.  I own several pans in the Calphalon Commercial line, including this pan.  This is the perfect size pan, and that is originally why I purchased it, but other than it's size, it's useless.  Everything sticks to this pan, even when the pan is greased.  It's been very frustrating to cook with the pan, and find out that your food is stuck to the pan.  Cleanup is diffcult.  I wash all my pans by hand, to preserve them, and this is the most difficult pan to clean.  It still has marks on the bottom pan where food stuck while cooking.  I read previous reviews and followed the advice about cleaning the pan, but it still looks the same.  I've decided to order a pan from a different line.

We have used this for eggs about 10 times now.  It is fun and easy to slice eggs.  We love how compact it is.  We have washed it in out dishwasher on top rack and it seems to be holding up fine so far.
The sound quality was not what I expected (not as smooth or bass), but I will keep as a gift.

Although I own a Breville juicer, I found that I rarely use it because of how difficult it is to clean after use.  I also wanted a juicer that was effective at juicing leafy green vegetables.  After considerable research, I decided on the Omega 8006 and am very happy with this machine!  It makes delicious green veggie juice or apple/carrot juice, the pulp is quite dry, and clean-up takes just a couple minutes of rinsing.  For me, the easy cleaning and ability to juice leafy greens is worth the extra time the Omega 8006 takes to make juice compared to my Breville.  Would a Norwalk juicer be better than this Omega?  Probably, but the price difference is too vast in my opinion; the Omega is a great value for a very good machine.

I purchased this to protect my investment (I bought a "non subsidized phone at $597, and hoping this adds SOME protection!)This fits the phone with the extended battery just like a glove:  ALL the cutouts are properly positioned and none are covered especially the IR blaster which enables you to used the phone as a remote for your TV/home theater.Bright blue, I'd probably get a black next time, but the blue sure does make it easy to spot in my luggage  ;))

Nothing too special about these styli; they're not the most stylish things, although the tip stays on well, its easy to pin point on the iPad just right and most importantly, a 3 pack at a good price, where we won't feel bad if one or two get lost.

For what it does, it's an amazing little product. I work in a mailroom with hundreds of different sized boxes. This little puppy attached to the right size box astounds my co-workers. I hook it up to a over sized shoe box and play tunes all day. Sometimes I have to turn it down because I'm disturbing people in another room through a solid door. I had to test it out myself and, sure enough, I could hear every lyric, guitar, drum and bass pattern in their room ... with the door closed.I have fun with the carriers when they bring boxes in to shipping & receiving. I say, "Do you hear that?" pointing to one of their boxes. Then I say, "Let me use this little amplifier to enhance the sound level of what I'm hearing inside your box." I place the speaker of my Rock-It 3.0 on the box and watch their eyes widen. Sometimes they jump back away from the parcel like it's alive. With a big smile, I explain what's up and they all get a kick out of it. We try a bunch of different sized containers on the trucks. The best we've found (beside my shoebox which I guard jealously) is an empty pizza box.I'm sure my demonstrations have sold a ton of these things. They recharge easily and quickly and are a great deal of fun.

We love this electric skillet.  We seasoned it the first day and have used it at least 10 times so far with no complaints.  It is very large (I can fry up at least 15 rounds of eggplant at once).  The temperature is even throughout and the cover is wonderful because you can see through it without lifting it off the food.  I love that I can cook up big amounts of food and freeze the rest for quick dinners.  Cleanup is very easy and it says it can be washed in the dishwasher (but I don't want to do that because the stainless steel is still so pretty!).  It's sturdy and the skillet sits high enough off the counter that the counter top does not get hot. It is a very large skillet though -so storing it may be an issue.  I keep mine in it's box because my home has narrow kitchen cabinets.  You can hang the pan itself on a hook, but the lid cannot be hung (that we have figured out yet).  Great buy for the price!

I am very happy with my skytex skypad alpha 7! This tablet has so much to offer. The arm cortex A8 cpu processor is so fast. searching the internet is a breeze. It is nice to have a expandable storage of up to 32 GB. This tablet also has bluetooth and a web cam! I was easily able to install all Google services such as Gmail, Google talk and more. I also easily installed android market. I enjoy watching my movies from  Netflix on this tablet. The movies are clean crisp clear and audio is also excellent. Skypad has many capabilities just waiting to be used.This little tablet packs a big punch! I highly recommend this tablet over the other tablets out there.I had one of the other tablets, and it rebooted all the time and froze the first week I had it.This tablet has never once reboted.The other tablet would reboot in the middle of applications and it was frusterating.I dont have to worry about that now with this tablet! The sxytex skypad is a steal for the price! This tablet has it all, quality,and proformance. This is the right choice for under $200.00. you can't beat this bargin! I'm very happy with my skytex skypad!

I almost bought one of these from another site but it was pricy: $10 for ONE, however it was flocked. I thought I'd check Amazon and it was $10 for TWO. Flocking isn't necessary at all. Tank tops won't slip off due to the design. Very pleased.
The item was as described and arrived in the time promised. Reasonably priced and served its purpose well.The colors were as described.

Where to start - it took 20 minutes to get into the item as it was triple boxed!  It did arrive undamaged though, so that was a good start.  I was, however, completely surprised at the size of this thing.  When you see the photo, it leads you to believe it's compact and somewhat "sleek"....ahhh NO.  It's BIG and bulky.We made sweet potato fries first time out.  It works ok but it's not the Tfal Actifry...not at all.#1 - it's a small basket on the bottom of the unit and the product just lays in there#2 - It really doesn't hold a lot.  If you were making fries for 3 or 4 people, you might as well eat in shifts.#3 - safety - the handle on the front does not "lock" into place on the unit.  Any child walking past can bump this or pull it and the basket comes out easily.The overall make lacks a "sturdy" quality in my opinion.  It's ok - but I have a Tfal Actifry and would choose that over this item every day of the week.  With the Actifry, people complain about the paddles sticking but at least it's moving the food around to brown all sides.  It has a clear lid so you can see what's happening and how the food is cooking and browning. Actifry slightly more room and seems better made.I am not one for brand names so tfal doesn't mean squat to me one way or another.  But I will say that the tfal item is better made with more room and easier to use as far as on/off - start/stop - open/close.  Phillips has the digital stuff and all that does is make simple functions more complicated.In the end - they are both bulky and a person can make low fat food without the addition of any appliance of this type.  If, however, you're dead set on getting one, compare the Actifry to this before purchasing.

It's a nice cover, but not really what I expected.  First, there are two notches in which the Note can sit for upright use, but the case into which the Note snaps has rounded edges.  That, plus the nice, velvety interior means that the Note tends to slip out of either notch often when touching the screen. Second, there is no place to put the stylus that comes with the case.  The Note comes with a stylus, and a place to put it, so the one for the case has no place to go but somewhere else.  I'll keep it in a drawer in case I lose the Note's stylus, and just hope I remember where I put it.  Third, the screen protector provided is almost useless.  I expected that, though, and have ordered a better one.  Overall, I expect to look for another case soon.

This battery was one of the best accessory purchases I made in recent years. It holds enough power to charge my S3 fully when in need, or to top it off a little bit when it's starting to get low and if I don't have access to power yet.The winning feature is the built-in microUSB cable, which makes this a one-item toss-it-in-a-bag situation, instead of having to worry about brining an additional cable with you. I usually use it 2-3 times per week for quick top-ups, and it lasts me about 2 weeks until I have to recharge it again. For the price and the convenience, this is almost a no-brainer recommendation to anyone.

As her first telescope, and she loves it. I've had some experience with Celestron (owning a 60GT), and have seen the moon up-close-and-personal plenty of times. No, this camera doesn't have all the bells and whistles of the more expensive cameras but it's not expected to since it is a basic entry level camera. For my needs, it is perfect. I don't really need super high def pics since mostly what I use a camera for is to take pics of items I plan on selling on ebay or maybe uploading some photos of family(pets included) and friends on one of the various and numerous social sites on the ol' interweb. There probably is better entry level cameras out there for the price, but there's so many it's hard to know which is which. What I can say for sure is that this camera gets the job done if your needs are basic and it's easy to use. It took me about 2-3 hours of "fiddling" around with it to get the hang of it.I've had it almost 2 days now and have had no issues with it holding a charge. I also like that this is a really thin and sleek looking camera. Stylish I guess you could say. And it seems pretty durable although I don't  plan on dropping it anytime soon ;) The bottom line is this: If your needs are basic, like mine, and you don't want to spend countless hours researching cameras on the net, then do youself a favor and buy this badboy. I really don't use this unless I want to make a double batch of ice cream or sorbet because it is noisy and my Kitchen Aid is always within reach, but it does as promised. (My first choice is the Kitchen Aide attachment I purchased AFTER buying this.)Still, it's a good product for the price. I can think of no reason not to buy it. Desktop speakers have almost always been an afterthought to computer systems themselves. To be fair, desktop speaker designers have tough parameters within which they have to work: space (desktop speakers need to be small), cost (no one wants to pay a lot for them, even serious gamers and audiophiles), and acoustics (most desktop environments aren't conducive to getting the most out of a sound system).Definitive Technologies (DT) has done an absolutely incredible job of meeting the first and third of these issues. The second, cost, is a tough one. At $400 these speakers are more expensive than many decent laptops and PC towers. But a true audiophile, especially one who has a decent computer, will probably not balk at the cost.Size-wise these DT Incline speakers have a relatively small footprint, with all the important real estate up above the solid aluminum bases. The heavy bases are important, as they really help dampen vibrations. As their name implies, these speakers incline slightly backward, aiming the sound up off the desktop toward the general direction of the listener's head/ears. This little design element makes all the difference.I connected my speakers to my iMac via USB, but one can also use the 3mm/AUX jack. Note that there is no automatic on/off or standby, so you will need to manually turn them off when you put your computer to sleep. I didn't do this at first and found that the speakers periodically woke my iMac.So, how do these speakers sound? They sound AWESOME. Wow. Holy whatever. A. Maze. Ing. After I connected them I started to work though a list of tracks in my iTunes library from Pink Floyd to Rush to Mozart. I could simply not believe how well these speakers sounded. After years in the desert of tinny, tiny desktop PC speakers, I didn't know how parched my ears actually had become, so these DT Inclines were like a half-gallon of clear, cold water. Etc. Etc. (C'mon, don't most speaker reviews have an obligatory descriptive metaphor to describe the otherwise indescribable sensation of sound? Cut me some slack here, people.)I am not an obsessed audiophile, but I get the general concept of the "soundstage": the 3D effect of sound in the space around you. On Rush's 2112 Overture I heard spacey noises dancing around me that I had never picked up on before, and when I closed my eyes I was sure I could reach out to my left and touch Lifeson's guitar. Don't even get me started on Peart's drumming in Tom Sawyer.Pink Floyd's Delicate Sound of Thunder was an aural feast, especially when Gilmour's voice sings the first lines of On the Turning Away. Mozart's 27th, a lesser known but still fun symphony, was a real treat.All the descriptive words in the world can not do the sound of these DT Incline speakers (or any good speaker, for that matter) justice. They simply need to be heard to be believed. But the bottom line is these speakers have made me more excited to dig deep into my iTunes library than I have been in years. They produce sound that is wonderful, rich, and probably without par in the desktop speaker market.A professional review can be found at: [...] (I have no affiliation whatsoever with that site.) If you are at all serious about these speakers, read that review, as it is written by someone who knows speakers far better than I and it also contains some important technical information. If you are an audiophile considering these speakers, then you have probably found that review already.So my 2 cents? These DT Incline speakers are worth it if you love music and if you spend a lot of time at your desktop PC/Mac. BUT, and this is my tongue-in-cheek final warning: don't buy these if you can't play them loud (i.e. if you have easily-annoyed coworkers or family near your desk). Once you experience these speakers at decent volume, you can't help but let them open up and do their thing all the time.

This item came in fast, but it was not the right one, I am so upset because after finally getting it in the mail and knowing that was the last thing you were waiting on to turn your phone back on. It takes 2 days (48 hours) to charge and only 3 hours to run it dead...

Lately, the wife and I have been on a kick to wall mount our televisions. The first one we started with was a SANUS for our 60" LED Samsung. I couldn't say enough good things about it. It was extremely straight forward and easy to install. Secondly, we decided to mount our bedroom TV. This time around I thought i'd be cheap and look for alternatives.I came across this Cheetah mount and the price (and reviews) seemed promising. My mount was received very fast. Upon opening the package I noticed there were a LOT of pieces to this mount. The mount itself came in SIX different pieces that you're required to assemble. OK - This is not a total bad thing. The way this mount is put together can leave room for a mount that isn't perfectly square. I was able to assemble the mount and the end product looked and felt rigid and sturdy. Lagging the mount to the wall was also straight forward as you'd expect. I'm not too crazy on the different sized lag bolts. It'll tell you to use a 3mm drill bit and a 5mm bit for pilot holes. Yeah, not the 7/32s like you'd expect. None the less I matched them up closely enough with the bits that I had.The next step was mounting the "hangers" to the TV. This was also easy. I'm not at ALL thrilled about the "tilting" function as it requires you to loosen two screws, get it where you "semi want it" and tighten them back down. At the full tilt (15*) the hangers would not set on the mount correctly. The top was in place but the bottom of the hangers were unable to reach underneath the wall mount. So, I had to rid the tilt function for a more "flat" look.( Also - unlike better mounts, the hangers do not automatically "click" in) Which is OK but I was hoping for a lot more.Once you're on correctly and you feel semi depressed as you could have spent forty bucks more for a much higher quality mount, you'll ask yourself "How do i get to the screws on the bottom of the TV hangers to tighten to the wall mount?" Well good question!!You need a (hyperbole) 5' screwdriver! We did what we could and I had my wife pull the TV ever so slightly forward and I tightened the screws as much as possible. They're not tight but they're in far enough to ensure me that the tV isn't going anywhere.The HDMI cable, plastic levels are just added insult to injury. They're junk and you wouldn't get quality parts at the bargain bin price you just paid for the mount. I tossed them all immediately. I'll be sure to stick with the mounts I know are completely straight forward and easy from here on out.terrible, terrible, terrible.

I bought this speaker to replace a Mirage Omni-C3 center channel that just didn't seem to be doing a good job of clearly and accurately reproducing the dialogue and other center channel sounds in most programming. The Mirages are nice speakers but the omnipolar design tries hard to fill the whole room, rather than directing sound to the listener, and my room is a large one to fill. The speakers also aim up at a 45 degree angle so the sweet spot seemed to be when I was standing up halfway between my couch and TV.After a search for the largest speakers I could find to fit in the slot under the TV in my entertainment center (about 7.5" high I think) I settled on the Klipcsch. Unfortunately I couldn't do the 62 or 64 but I knew the 52 would still be an upgrade over my old speaker. I also looked at other brands like Polk and Yamaha but settled on the Klipsch due to their horn speakers and higher level reference line. I have had Klipsch horns on computer speakers in the past and have liked them and a coworker also has Klipsch speakers that he raves about.This speaker is large and solid. Without the grille it looks pretty impressive but those copper cones can be distracting so I leave the grille on. After hooking it up and before re-running the dynamic EQ on my receiver I found that this thing is loud! Klipsch speakers are usually more efficient than others and my levels were clearly set for the weaker old Mirage I was replacing. After running dynamic EQ the level was reduced from 1-3dB up to 7.5db down!On first listen the first thing my wife and I noticed was how much more clear the audio sounded. The dialogue was now clearly locked on to the center of the sound stage. What had we been missing these last months with that old speaker?! Now we know.The only "downside" to this speaker was that buying it made me second-guess the rest of the speakers in my setup, my fronts were also Mirage towers. They used to hack it in my old, smaller room. In my new home with a very large room their audio also sounded muddy and diffuse. My old fronts now also weren't timbre matched with my new center. I always thought this was an audio snob thing to notice and say, but it really was obvious in watching content. So buying this speaker "forced" me to go out and pick up the matching RF-52 speakers so the whole front setup were the same basic type. Hardly a negative I know. :) And honestly the system sounds fantastic now. I love it!
I was shocked. I got the entire thing with no manual and nothing in my hand in just one plastic bag ? No company packaging, How come ????

I'm actually kidding about the dual-message of the headline - I know people in their 40s with hearing aids from too many concerts and they're pretty embarrassed about it, so I am an advocate of listening at lower levels, particularly when using earbuds. I got these because I was doing a long commute and couldn't hear some movies' dialogue on the loudish train, and sometimes noticed someone watching over my shoulder - great way to meet people, ask if they want to listen in to Mad Men (or whatever) on a long train ride.The amp made it easy to hear, and with enough power you can actually listen at lower levels and have it sound better - I know that may not make sense to those who don't know about audio, but with the weak amplification in most media devices, it strains to put out a louder signal than it easily can which distorts the signal and changes a smoother curve of the signal to a "clipped" one that sounds poor - even at the same amplitude or sound level.Use a little amp like this and you have very clear, rich sound coming through. I recommend it highly and love the built-in battery and sharing option.

They're a great idea, but poorly executed.  The idea is you put your tea and your hot water in the top, let sit a few minutes to steep, and then you place on the top of your mug.  The brewed tea pours out into the cup, leaving the leaves behind to reuse or compost.  If you buy loose tea, they give you the convenience of a teabag without the waste or stale tea.Unfortunately, they are far from perfected. I've gone through two of this model before giving up and going with another brand. All suffer from the same issue, which is that the delicate mesh on the bottom begins to accumulate gunk, eventually clogs up and then corrodes.This all happens much too quickly for this to be any value, even for the convenience, and is exacerbated if you try to clean them with anything other than water.

I juice almost daily, and have for years!!  So, you must understand I am a bit of a juicing snob. I have researched every juicer on the market, and am very particular about juice quality and nutrient value of my juice.I have owned four juicers in the past year, with this being one of them.Here's my take on the other two, and then more in depth on the Kuvings.Breville Juice Fountain: Nice easy juicer to use. Quick and easy, and allows for big pieces of fruit to be put down the chute, so less chopping. Clean-up is the easiest of the the three. Great intro juicer to get into the habit without a lot of work. Not so great on juicing veggies, and especially not great for greens like kale. Which is the main reason I decided to upgrade from the Breville because I juice greens daily. Nice choice if you'll just be doing oranges, apples, etc. Not very versatile, but great at the basic fruits. It's a great all-round centrifugal juicer, and you can purchase for around $150-200.  But in all honestly, a masticating juicer will give your more bang for your buck in the juice quality, juice quantity, and LESS pulp department.Angel 5500: Amazing juicer. But, it comes at a hefty price tag of $900-1100. It is ALL stainless steel and built to last. It's unsurpassed juice quality, with very little waste/pulp. You will really make your money back in the end with more juice output and less pulp. It's fantastic for fruits and veggies, and does a tremendous job on greens, which is why I wanted it. The Angel is a masticating, dual-ager machine, which means it mashes and then juices the food. It works at low-temperature and slower speed than Breville so juice quality is higher and juice lasts longer before it oxidizes. I really can't say enough about this machine. I will admit clean-up takes about 2 minutes longer than with the Breville, but for me that's a trade-off I'm willing to take for better quality, more nutrients, and less waste.Kruvings NJE-358OU: This is a great juicer for those who would like a masticating juicer with one auger. One thing I really liked about the juicer was the fact that it really "pulled" the food into the feed tube. Where as with my Angel I sometimes I have to push it in. The feed tube on the Kuvings is smaller than the three juicers I'm comparing, so you will have to spend some time cutting up the fruits and veggies in order for them to fit in the tube. The Kuvings is almost all made of plastic, which isn't a big deal, I just wonder about durability over years of use.The juice quality with the Kuvings is very high. Better than that of the Breville, but slightly less than the Angel. It's a great tasting "thick" juice, but no pulp in the juice.Here's the juicer review you've been waiting for!!Kruvings NS-950 Silent Upright Masticating Juicer: This juicer amazed me.  It gave almost identical juice quality as my $1000 Angel!!  And, was faster than my Angel and easier to clean! The other thing I loved about the Upright Kuvings was that it feeds itself.  With the Angel, I admit I have to actively "push" the produce in.  The Upright Kuvings did create about 5-10% more pulp than my Angel, but strangely enough seemed to produce about an equal amount of juice. The juice quality with the Kuvings is very high. Better than that of the Breville, but slightly less than the Angel. It's a great tasting "thick" juice, but no pulp in the juice.  One thing to be aware of, is that the Kuvings Upright has safety features that are great, but you have to line-up the spots accurately or the machine won't turn on.  No biggie, just have to get used to putting things together just right.  I love that the Upright Kuvings takes up less counter space, and the design is also less messy on your countertop.  The other two styles of masticating juicers tend to be a bit messier than an upright model.  For the price, juice quality, speed, and clean-up THE KUVINGS UPRIGHT is the clear winner!!!!!I juiced my daily recipe with all four juicers:2 apples5-6 stalks celeryfresh ginger (1-3 inches)1/2 peeled lemon5-8 kale leavesThe Breville produced the least amount of juice and most pulp. The Kuvings NJE produced the medium amount of juice and pulp, while the Angel and Upright Kuvings produced the most juice by about 1 1/2 cups.As far as pulp, the Angel produced only about 1 cup of the pulp. The Breville produced about 3 cups of pulp.  The Kuvings NJE produced about 2 cups of pulp, which the Upright Kuvings produced about 1 1/2 cups of pulp.Clean-up on the Kuvings NJE was about the same as the Angel. In fact, it might have taken a tiny bit longer.  The clean-up on the Kuvings Upright was a snap! It comes with two handy tools to assist with cleaning.I'd say Breville clean-up is 3 minutes, Angel is 5 minutes, and Kuvings NJE is 6 minutes, and Kuvings Upright is 4 minutes.I really don't know why anyone would by the Centrifugal Breville, as there are so many better options at that price point that give you MORE juice and LESS pulp!!  I highly recommend both Kuvings masticating juicers, depending on your budget.  I also love the Angel juicer, but taking all into consideration (including PRICE) the Kuvings Upright is the clear WINNER of these four models!!!HAPPY JUICING!!!

...there is a vent hole in the glass which I wish were not there. When I'm serving whatever I cooked, I prefer to retain as much of the heat as possible and not vent some out. I have been trying unsuccessfully to get information from the manufacturer about this juicer.  They are very secretive about the types of plastic used and the very real potential for plastic contamination and chemical leaching into food during use!It appears that they are using several plastics that come in contact with food.  These are polycarbonate, Utrem, silicone, and melamine.  They ALL are potentially toxic.  Of special concern is the fact that the plastics are in contact and rub against one another while in motion.  This can create a far greater risk for contamination.  Hurom and its distributor have apparently done NO testing for the amount and safety of plastic contaminants. It is sad that we purchase this item to improve our health but may actually be damaging our health without knowing it.  By the way, BPA free means very little since there are MANY other chemicals in plastics that are at least as bad as BPA.  In fact, bisphenol A (BPA) is often replaced with other equally bad bisphenols  (bisphenol B, C, D, etc.), and can still claim to be BPA free.  Also, there are pthalates, melamine, and many others that are worse than BPA. It seems that manufacturers, and our government, do not care about these important health issues until it's too late.

Sorry, but this just isn't a "smoothie straw".  It's not large diameter; it's a small-caliber straw, for liquids, not smoothies.  Smoothies and milkshakes require a large-caliber straw, unless you enjoy trying to suck golf balls through garden hoses...What this is, is a nice titanium straw.  What makes it worth more than twice as much as the almost identical ones that come in a four pack elsewhere on Amazon?  It has a nifty little Chinese character printed on it, it is infinitesimally larger diameter, and a half-shade darker.  Does this more than double its value?  You decide!Two stars for totally false advertising and high price, nice straw otherwise.

Got this to take along with me when traveling and camping. My iphone 4 tends to drain battery quickly when roaming and being used heavily. The unit came charged and ready to go. It included adapters for many difference devices including: iphone, blackberry, motorla, samsung, htc, LG and PSP. All the little bitty adapters were quite confusing at first, but the user guide explained it all. Works like a charm for charging. You can either run your device off of the battery pack or charge it up. It's a nice, substantial unit (not cheapy plastic) that does have a little bit of weight to it. The only con I see is that the unit "dangles" from the phone when connected by either a 18 cm line or a 70 cm line/cable. I really love the idea of making one cup of coffee at a time...I have waisted so much coffee since I can not drink a whole pot full and it seems that if you make one or two cups in an 8 to 10 cup coffee maker it just is not good.  I have tried the 2 to 4 cup models and did not care for those machines either.  I love my French press but it is just a real pain to clean out every single day!  So I got this Keurig Platinum for Christmas.  I really love it and the coffee is good...not fantastic like from Starbucks but it's very good...you have to work with it for a while to get the cup you like the best...like using two different size brewing methods, using the K-cups twice or 2 different blends to get just what you want.  It took me a few weeks of trial and error but I finally found my perfect cup, for this machine.My only complaint is this...for which I am going to have to call the Keurig company...I do not like to leave my Keurig machine on all the time and since I do not use the coffee maker at the same time each and every day I do not set the time ON feature.  I tried it one time and it just was not for me...so I turn the entire machine off with the switch on the back of the machine and I thought this was the answer.  All was fine for about a month when suddenly my husband...who gets up before me kept telling me..."Your coffee machine is on!"...and I never turned it on!  I switched OFF the automatic ON/OFF feature and for some strange reason my machine has decided to turn itself on every morning...just out of the blue!  I can not get the machine to stop turning itself on...no matter if the feature is OFF or not.  I personally feel this could possibly be a potential fire hazard... the only thing I can figure to do it set the ON/OFF feature to come on at 9:00 AM and to go off at 9:00 AM so far it has not come on...if it does I will just have to unplug it all together!  I am going to get in touch with the company to find out just what the problem is and I will up date later on this.

When I travel I tend to have a collection of cables with me. The Monster Connect2 cable lets me combine two cables into one - MicroUSB and Apple 30-pin.  The cable itself is about three feet long, is flat and fairly rugged in appearance. The regular USB connector has a partial metal shell and a strain relief - it is attractive, but I found it was a bit difficult to plug in to some of my USB jacks.  Perhaps this was deliberate on Monster's part to make the connection tight.The other end has a MicroUSB plug and, attached to a short tether, a MicroUSB to Apple 30-pin adapter.  While I liked the idea, and it worked well, there were some annoyances.  First, the tether is short and stiff - I found I had to slide it down the cable to get the 30-pin connector out of the way when using MicroUSB.  Second, neither the dock connector nor the 30-pin connector have obvious markings as to which way is "up".  USB connectors are supposed to have the USB "fork" logo (as is on the other end), and the dock connector should have a logo on the "up" end as well.  There is a rectangular depression in the sheathing for the dock connector so maybe that was intended to be the marking.Electrically, it worked fine. No problem charging an iPad (some thin USB cables can't handle the 2.1A current). An alternative you may want to consider if you also have devices that take the MiniUSB connector is theGriffin Technology GC17097 USB Mini-Cable Kitin conjunction with a USB extension cable you may already have (I have this and like it), or possiblySmart Phone Charge & Sync Kitwhich is a similar concept and includes an extender cable (I don't have this one and the reviews are mixed.)

Bundle packs are great I gave a few to friends and used one for myself I do wish that you got 6 long lanyards too I have to say I never used a stylus with a LONG lanyard before boy have I been missing out one of my stylus was dented but it didn't bug me too much I also liked the quality of the  micro cloth that comes in the pack cleaned up my iPad better then some others I have

I've always thought using a stylus was a good idea for our tablets (iPad 3rd Gen, iPad 4th gen, Acer android tablet), but every time I get a stylus, I use it for a few days to a week, then it finds itself in a cup or pen holder somewhere.  Between the styles, the tips, and just the overall feel, I could never find one that I kept using.  The New Trent Limir Dual Purpose Stylus has changed that for me though, as this stylus offers great functionality, style, and accuracy.  First off, it has a built-in pen.  You never realize how often you go to reach for a pen and it's not there - I do it all the time.  Now, each time I reach for a pen it's there, because it's built into this stylus, which is kept in my pocket everywhere I go.Next the micro-knit tip makes a huge different.  If you've never tried one before, then this will convert you.  Hands down the best type of stylus tip that I have ever used.  It writes smooth and is accurate, and I've never had any issues with using it.Finally the style and presentation of this stylus is very slick and professional looking.  Whenever I'm in a meeting and put my iPad down, the Limir goes right next to it and draws stares each time I take it out.Overall this is a great stylus.  If you have never used one before, make this your first one.  And if you've sworn off styli in the past, this will bring you back!

I bought this vacuum on Oct 29, 2009.Don't get fooled by the 6 year warranty.We've really had only one major problem with the vacuum cleaner since we got it. One day, the motor started to emit a strong odor like it was burning. So on Sept 3, 2011, I took it to the Hoover repair shop near my home that is listed on Hoover's website. The shop inspected it and I was told the motor needed to be replaced. I knew it was under warranty...so I thought no problem. Well, it's November 15, 2 1/2 months later, and the repair shop has still not received the parts from Hoover that it needs to repair the thing. The repairman told me that this is how Hoover operates. They are extremely slow to send parts for repair, he said.The vacuum cleaner actually is a good vacuum cleaner. Up until the motor died, it was performing beautifully. Even the repairman said that this model is one of Hoover's best models and they have very few problems.Unfortunately, Hoover has chosen to prove that its warranty is really of very little value to any household that uses a vacuum cleaner to clean because of the 2-4 months wait for repairs on one of their machines that is under warranty.BTW, we use the vacuum for regular household cleaning, AND the repairman did offer me a loaner.......ONLY AFTER I CALLED TO COMPLAIN ABOUT HOW SLOW THEY ARE FOR THE SECOND TIME!!!6 year warranty?......Yeah.....Sure. It's a 6 year warranty alright. It takes 6 years for these Hoover knuckleheads to repair it.You know what, Hoover? I gotta 6 year warranty for you......It's right here(as I grab myself)......

The Jabra Street 2 took less than two hours to charge, out of the box, and it paired instantly with my HTC Android phone. The headphones that come with it are really comfortable and have good sound quality. The device also looks cool and is really simple to use.My 30 something brother and teenage nephew just happened to be over when I was testing it out. My brother, who is an iPhone user, was in the camp that thinks headphones should be hooked directly to the phone and the Jabra Street 2 is an extra step. But my nephews reaction was wow cool. So the Street 2 has the street cred cool factor, and I think it's target audience will enjoy it.As for me personally, I thought it was easy to use and sounded great. The sound quality for music and calls are much clearer than my other bluetooth headset and when wearing it you can discretely use your expensive smart phone for music and audio, while still having the ability to take calls. I also didn't have any issues with sound quality, and for me using the headset makes listening to calls much more clear on my end. Currently, this is my favorite blue tooth device.

I had ordered the OtterBox Defender Series but it was too bulky, the sides (around the display) were way too high (made for difficulty accessing the pull down menu) and I did not really like the built-in clear screen protector that was over it.  That said, the holster was great, the protection was awesome and if I worked in Construction or some other rough-and-tumble trade, that would have been the perfect option.So I sent that back and ordered the Commuter Series.  I like it!  Only I am giving it 4 stars because I would have loved it if there were a holster or belt-clip with it.  For some reason, I hate putting my phone in my pocket or purse and prefer to have it somewhere that it is always accessible and this happens to be my belt or hooked on my pocket.  And, this way the phone does not collect dust and "pocket fuzz" (and the Commuter Series does tend to collect it quite well on the silicone cover). But, alas, the holster I purchased (also through Amazon) will not fit the phone with this case on it.That said, the case has a lot of plusses!!!  The bulk (from the Defender Series) is considerably less.  The sides around the display are not high and this makes everything accessible.  The protection is amazing.  I am not a phone dropper but I love to have a nice phone.  The OtterBox Commuter Series, in my opinion is classy and sleek and makes for a professional phone. I do kind of wish it came in other colors (such as royal blue) but I have not found that yet.The self-adhesive screen cover is great.  It comes with a OtterBox installation card (so you don't have to use your credit card - lol!) and when I put it on, following the installation instructions, it was simple and there are no bubbles.  It also comes with a little screen cleaning cloth which is cute.Yes, it does collect finger-prints but I think, in time, as my other self-adhesive screen covers did, this dies down after using it for a while, and believe-you-me, I'd rather have that than a scratched, messed-up screen. (My last screen protector saved me from a serious scratch when I was working on the construction of my house roof, the scratch only made it through the screen-protector and could have been BAD; all the way through the screen).  Also, I use my Friendly Swede styluses (also highly recommended) a lot so this cuts down on the finger prints.The price was great!  The price listed on the box was $34.00 and I believe I got this, with shipping, for around $10.00.  What a bargain!Anyhow, I am looking for a holster that works for this (and will fit my phone with this case) so if anyone knows of something like that, please let me know.Otherwise, I highly recommend this case!

The DLink wireless router is very easy to install for a simple home network.  It offers a setup Wizard which took only a few minutes and everything came up with no problems.  The wireless speeds and range are equivalent to similar competitive products such as the Linksys E1000 router.  For the money its a great unit. It offers a manual configuration capability which is extensive for expert users with more features then I have seen in Linksys routers.Negatives:Functional Omission:  It lacks a fairly important functional encryption feature.  It will not do simultaneous WEP (older more vulnerable encryption and WPA/WPA2 as for me an older laptop).  So if you have older devices in your network then you either have to select all on WEP or buy a device or an adapter like a USB wireless interface so everything can be WPA.  Simultaneous WEP/WPA is offered in the Linksys products.Setup instructions for Access point:  In my network I added a 2nd Dlink as an access point. The documentation provided is very minimal and does not provide any instructions on how to do this.  I found some web postings that explained how and the access point works well when its configured.  I think this lack of tutorial instruction material is similar to all manufactures but is still an area that should be improved for this product.  THey advertise the access point feature on the box and they should be more diligent about providing clear instructions to set it up.
It was a little tricky to hold the cables, then tighten the ties. Needed a third hand. The velcro is very strong.

For the price, this is a good camera. Not a "great" camera, but a fairly good one. I would consider it high end for the average consumer, with features superior to other point-and-shoots in its price range: Wi-fi capable (ability to connect to iOS and Android devices for viewing/sharing your photos and videos via the optional WU-1a adapter), built-in GPS (to geotag your photos), 18MP CMOS sensor, a zoom lens with incredible focal range (24mm-1,000mm), and 1080 HD video. For the price, that's a nice set-up, a camera with advanced features with a decent ease-of-use, requiring little knowledge of photographic techniques.Many of us, however, want to go beyond 'picture-taking' and seek an advanced camera that is not in an advanced price range. And this is where the P520 is attractive but, ultimately, falls a bit short of our desires. For example, there's no RAW: your images are captured in JPEG only. There's a decent pop-up flash but no hot shoe. Also, the impressive zoom is not so impressive with regard to its speed, either its lens speed (f/3.0-5.9) or autofocus speed (fairly slow but not quite horrible). However, it is a nice, glass, Nikkor lens; and, to be fair, it would be quite a feat to bring such a long zoom well under f/3, given the price range. Personally, I would have traded focal length for speed: yes, it's impressive to go out to 1000mm, but I would have taken 800mm with an f/2.8-5.0 lens; though Nikon has built-in stabilization features for both photos and video (called 'Active VR mode' for video), you're not going to do hand-held shots at that 1000mm focal length, especially video.Other aspects of the camera will please everyone: full manual exposure control along with multiple auto/semi-auto exposure modes, a nice and large tilting Vari-Angle LCD monitor, an excellent CMOS sensor (1/2.3 in.), and a nice weight and feel to the body. This last point is what drew me to the 520 in the first place: I just bought a P310 and although I love it, especially for its small size and portability (shirt pocket), I cannot get used to shooting outdoors with such a small camera. The 520 is just perfect; that solid DSLR feel but with a modest weight (20 ounces).Yes, I wish the P520 had a few more pro-like features and wasn't so extreme in its focal length, but this is a very good camera overall. I'm giving it 3 stars for the reasons stated above. However, if you're someone not interested in advanced photography and just want a very nice camera with higher-end consumer features, this is a borderline 4-star camera and not bad for the price.

This Panasonic can be set to turn off the annoying loud beeps, which can disturb one's sleep when someone is using the oven at odd hours.  Also, it can be set to operate on set time durations using one-button a operation or by manually entering the time duration for each use.  Most of our use is for reheating under 3 minutes - mostly between 30 seconds and 2:30 minutes.  One-button operation is quick versus entering the length of time for each use.  Some of the nice things are no clock to set, no turn-table to get off-track.  This thing just works without frills.  Programming this thing to cook/heat using multiple power levels can be confusing.  Luckily, we don't use it at all. This oven seems perfect for home use, although it is designed for commercial use.  Shop around for lowest prices. Amazon.com sometimes is not the best place to find the best prices.

Belkin has created a very basic armband case with its EaseFit. There are no openings for the volume controls. You'll need to have earbuds with volume control, because you can't get to the sides (where the buttons are located) once the iPod is in the case.The device fits snugly in the pouch and the screen works fine through the see-through plastic. There's no room in the case for anything other than the device--such as a slim-fitting case. (Am I the only one who likes to keep mine in one of those and never take it out?) The only cutout is for earbuds, oh, and to slip your iPod in the case.The pink/grey is attractive with lightweight material, which might be too light. When jogging, the armband did stay in place, but the iPod case moved around. Gave a flopping feeling.The fit size says 11.4" - 14.6". At 14-1/2 inches, you're on that last bit of Velcro.The Belkin EaseFit Armband Case for Apple iPod touch 4th Gen is void of frills. It's basic and will get the job done.

*Disclosure: I was contacted by 5 Star America and given a sample of this product specifically for the purpose of reviewing it.*Test devices:HTC OneHTC EVO LTE 4GSamsung Nexus 4G LTESamsung Galaxy S2Samsung Galaxy S3Google Nexus 7EVGA Tegra NoteNVIDIA ShieldSatechi Touch USB LED LampiPadiPad MiniiPhone 5Ok, let's get this out of the way right off.  Though this product is sold by 5 Star America, and is logoed with "Designed for America," don't think this was made or designed in the US.  Like those special commemorative coins one sees ads for on TV, it's a foreign made/designed product.  Looking at the 5 Star Amaerica website, there is a claim: "We are an external battery manufacturer based in United States of America."  The product is manufactured in China.  The website is registered to Ching Yat Chan, who lives in Hong Kong (China), and all relevant contacts are located there as well.With that out of the way, how about the battery pack itself?  I've reviewed quite a few of these, and this one is pretty decent, especially for the price. While it doesn't pack a ton of backup power, it's enough to refill most devices at least once.  Thin is right, and pretty light weight.  It reminds me a lot of an older iPod Touch in size and feel.  I've even has a couple people ask when I moved over the Apple camp, having only taken a sidelong look at it.  The construction is pretty solid, though the glossy front picks up a lot of fingerprints and scratches.  Unlike most of the units I've had a chance to play with, this one has auto detection.  Plug something in, and the unit immediately starts charging.  No on/off buttons to fool with. The unit included a 3 LED indicator let you know how much of a charge it has left, which is somewhat poorly located along the bottom edge. With all that real estate on the top surface, why it was decided to put the indicator along the edge is beyond me.  I can't agree with the claim that this is the fastest charging external battery pack out there, as there are other models with the same output available.  That output is what determines the charge rate, as well as the cable on some occasions.  While in use, the battery pack doesn't get hot, nor does it do this when charging up.  Checking the output with an oscilloscope and current clamp, I'm unhappy to say that the unit does not output the advertised 2A.  ~650mA was all I could get out of it on any device.  As a secondary test, I measured the charge rate of devices connected to the 5 Star America product vs a 2A AC adapter and one of the aforementioned competing battery packs.  Except for the devices that pulled under 650mA while charging, all devices connected to the 5 Star America battery charged at a substantially lower rate.  So, the claim to be the fastest charging pack was false, as was the 2A output rating.In conclusion, I can't suggest anyone buy this product.

I needed to replace my All Clad tea kettle I've had for 15 years so decided to try this one after so many glowing reviews.  I ordered the matte.
