Our 13" ancient GE tv in the basement started to get annoying trying to watch movies and sports.  So I took my 13 year old heavy 25" Samsung tv that was upstairs down to replace it.  Now I needed a tv for the upstairs.  I was looking for a used 32" $[...] tube tv to replace it.  My wife said why don't you get an updated tv to replace it, you don't have to tell me twice.  I jumped on her off and I was off to do my research.  I was deciding between a Sony Bravia or this SamsungA450 or the older SamsungA330.  I have basic cable(step below standard cable and far below digital cable) so I didn't needed a 1080, all I needed was a 720p.  I was to make sure to chose between 2 reliable name brand tvs.  When I finally started browsing the LG 32" also caught my eye.  For the price I took the SamsungA450.  After some tweaking and hooking up a s-video cable to my ancient dvd player to my new Samsung my dvd's look superb over my old setup.  I was amazed at the Hd channels I was picking up aside from my regular cable channels.  Easy hook up and so light weight, the tweaking took a little bit of time but the on screen menu is easy to navigate through.  I was amazed that HHGregg had this for under $[...] plus tax.  The LG and Sony were at least $[...] more than the Samsung.  The only reason I didn't order this online, because the hassle of sending back faulty electronics has caused me frustration and has been a very time consuming task in the past, besides I wanted to see and hear the comparisons after reading the small amount of negative reviews.  I truly like the hd picture quality of this tv, the cable channels are mediocre.  I really like the fact I didn't think I would be able to pickup any Hd channels by only having basic cable.  The sound quality is good for everyday cable, not for movies.  Besides from the people who have out of the box problems this is truly an awesome tv, even if I had an out of the box problem with Samsung, I think I would return it for another Samsung.  We now own 2 Samsung tvs and our family gives the new flat screen two thumbs up.  Every manufacturer will have some type of lemon now and then, but don't make that steer you away from this Samsung which is one of the major brands of tv.

I've been meaning to buy a pretty set of salad tongs forever, but it's one of those things that I never think about until I need them, which I did over the recent days of Christmas. It was too late to do anything about it when we wanted them, but after everyone went home, I came to Amazon to have a look around. I saw these and thought they were perfect. They arrived this morning, and I'm very happy with them.I love that they're made from a sustainable and renewable resource, the Acacia tree. Mine are a gorgeous dark color, which I just love. The spring mechanism is a mystery to me, I have no idea how it works, but it does! I think it's all in the brilliant cutting, shaping, and positioning of the two pieces because there's no sign of a metal spring that I can see. Of course that doesn't mean there isn't one, it could just mean that it's very well hidden.They're finished with a very smooth surface which is lovely to touch and hold in my hand. I can see where it would be nice if they folded flat, but I just put mine in a drawer that has extra space in it (yes, I have one of those, don't know how it happened) so it's not a problem for me.I would recommend these tongs to anyone who wants a pretty, well made, and functional set.

I've been a LONG TIME Shure fanatic. Been living with the E2Cs for well over 6 years. I was very excited to hear about the new "improved" se110s. Until that is, I put them on. I know all about the techniques of in the ear headphones, and how to adjust them for the maximal sound. The FIRST pair of these I returned the next day. Why, cuz to me it sounded like one of the speakers (the right speaker) was defective. Music was clearly distorted. So, Amazon very kindly send me an exchange and when I tried them, I thought, at first, the problem was solved. Not so. What I sadly finally realized was that these new headphones have no midrange capabilities above a certain volume level. I'm not talking about nosebleed levels, but I am talking about serious listening levels. I had my original E2Cs handy for a side by side comparison, and the difference was absolutely clear. Or, I should say muddy, even after a full day of "burn-in" where I left my ipod on all night with the phones in. These se110s do have a lot going for them in terms of "spaciousness" and "musicality", but once you pass a certain decibel level, it's all distorted misery from there. Very, very disappointed. Now I'm afraid to even try the higher-up model ... Shure, what's happened??????????

I bought my first T-fal Professional Total Nonstick pan on the basis of the stellar review it received on the PBS show America's Test Kitchen and its very reasonable price, and I wasn't disappointed.  The pan performed beautifully, and its size makes it convenient for sauteeing larger amounts of food.  (Remember, though, that it isn't deep and therefore wouldn't be good for frying chicken.)  I was so pleased with the pan that I bought three for my adult children last year for Christmas, and they were all as satisfied with it as I was.Unfortunately, my husband cooked breakfast in the pan one morning recently and left the burner on for FOUR hours, so that pretty well cooked the pan as well. :- (  My reaction was to turn right around and order a new one, so  now I'm smiling again.  : - )

I wish I could say I had some important business or even artistic reason for trying out the Adonit Jot Pro Dampening Stylus, but the truth is I wanted to have more fun with the Draw Something app on my iPhone, and this looked perfect for that task.I've demoed some of the other styluses (styli?) on the market, but their rounded ends didn't do much that my fingers couldn't already accomplish. This one has a fine point just like a ball point pen. Sure, there's a little round plastic disc on the end, but at least you can see where your lines are. Once you get used to that, you've got a very handy tool that lets you draw with much more precision than a standard stylus. It even looks and feels good too, which is an added bonus.I haven't tried the Jot Pro on a tablet, but if it's this effective with an iPhone, I bet it's even better on a larger screen. Just be sure to use a screen protector, as this could leave some scratches.

I purchased this machine specifically to do a FSND juice fast. This machine does an adequate job and can get you through the FSND juicing fast easily. It's a bare bones model that does what it advertises and will take you through a FSND juice easily. Unfortunately you will quickly outgrew this model if you decide to make juicing a permanent part of your diet.The machine out of the box appears brand new. I purchased this remanufactured model because I could add a Square Trade warranty for $5.91 which gave me a two year warranty for less money than the new machine with only a one year warranty. So I get double the warranty period this way. This machine will juice anything you can throw at it but be prepared to spend time in the kitchen cleaning up. When you are juicing you have to completely dismantle the machine, clean it, allow it to dry, then reassemble. Imagine doing that multiple times a day. Now you have a day on the FSND juice diet.PROS--It assembles and reassembles quickly and easily-Very simple to operate-Cost is relatively cheap compared to most juicers-Pitcher helps defoam your juices. Important with vegetables juices primarily.-Great starter juicer if you aren't sure you will continue to juice long termCONS--You must disassemble and reassemble every time you juice to clean. This is a pain for serious juicers-No ability to line the pulp receptacle bin to make clean up quicker-Mesh juicer bin is made of cheap metal. It crumpled like a piece of paper when dropped off a kitchen counter the first day. Fortunately we could bend it back into shape and it "almost" looked and worked good as new.-While it juices vegetables well it leaves many fruit pulps too wet. Pineapple becomes pudding consistency. Orange pulp is soaking wet. If you plan to do more fruit juicing consider another model. Or do what I do. After the FSND juicing fast was over I actually started to reclaim my fruit pulp and blend the pulp and juice together for more like a thicker fruit smoothie drink. If you just plan to juice fruit every morning I definitely recommend you keep looking or plan to reclaim your pulp for a smoothie drink.Overall my impression is it's a great starter model. If you already know you plan to juice as part of your diet permanently I would encourage you to upgrade to another model. My best recommendation for the FSND is try to find a friend, relative, coworker etc who is not using their juicer and borrow one. If you want to purchase one this is a great starter model but plan to spend more money in just a few months to upgrade to a better model. Now that we have made juicing a permanent part of our lives I'm having trouble deciding whether to invest in a top of the line juicer or go for a Vitamix/Blendtec blender type that does the same thing but makes smoothies instead. To summarize- will this blender work for the Fat, Sick, & Nearly Dead juice fast? You bet but it's a bare bones model. Let your budget and your plans for use help determine how sophisticated a juicer you want to purchase. As for me I'm taking my time deciding this time.As for those considering to do a FSND juice fast.  Don't hesitate to make an investment in your health. I have to say the juice fast was a great experience and I plan to repeat it again in just a couple of weeks. We now juice everyday. I drink two oranges and one-sixth of a pineapple every morning (pulp of both added back together and blended together) to make one luscious orange pineapple smoothie. I also drink two glass of Joe's Mean Green juice every day. Juicing is addicting and I know it's healthy.

I just got one of these and it's about as basic as such a product can be.  Safety was a significant factor for me - I don't want any product that gets glowing hot, so I looked into ceramic heaters with overheating protection.This ceramic heater works, and puts out a significant quantity of heat.  The low power setting uses 1000W to avoid tripping circuit breakers.  The high power setting uses 1500W.  The "thermostat" just controls the duty cycle (heater turns itself on/off frequently; the lower the setting, the more time the heater is "off")My only complaint is that the fan is a little loud.  Not terribly loud, but certainly audible.  Perhaps a larger unit with the same power rating might be quieter.
I don't know that I could rate this poorly.  Doesn't do much other than enable you to have multizone music throughout your house.  Good times.

After spending some quality time with the Anker iPhone C 2 piece cover, there are some great positives that stand out.First of all, it is two pieces, so I'll focus on the main cover.  I live that the back has a soft texture that is easy to grip and does not slip in my hand. The plastic that surrounds the rest of the phone frame has great surface area coverage while not being restrictive to button usage.  The cover also has enough of an overhang on the front to allow the phone to be set face down so the glass screen and the surface its set on to not touch. As a single piece, this phone case is superb.The second piece is a removable folio cover for the phone. The phone (in the Anker case) is meant to be placed into the folio and clasped shut.  The folio clasps using a strong magnet.  The phone is also held in the folio by its own set of magnets! When the phone in the case is in the folio, I'm confident it will not slip out at all.  It's a really ingenious way to personalize the phone based on mood or need.  I've used the folio cover when I have my phone in a bag or pouch, or I just use the phone case if I need to carry it primarily in my pocket.There are only 2 downsides I came across while testing this case, and both come from the folio part of the case, but neither are deal breakers for me. The first is the unsupported area around the camera hole opening on the folio case. It only has the 2 layers of material holding the top corner together, and in the course of normal usage it can get bent back, but It hasn't permanently creased (it has bounced back rather robustly). The other minor issue is the phone (when inside the folio) sliding down when pushing the power button, or sliding up when plugging in headphone or the power cord.  It is only a slight matter of sliding the phone back into position; again not anything close to a deal breaker.Anker provided me this case to try out in return for an honest review of the case, and by far, this is a neat and innovative product within a very overcrowded market.

I bought the pair of these (for red and white wines) because Amazon's price for a bundle was very good. So, when they arrived, I grabbed a bottle of red and a bottle of white wine and did some taste comparisons with the wife. We tasted a wine before and after aerating. I have to say, if nothing else, these things are very entertaining. When you pour wine into the top and it comes out the bottom into your wine glass, the wine bubbles loudly and it makes you laugh - very entertaining for a party. The difference is very noticeable. Wine run through the aerator is smoother and has more flavor. Nobody has ever guessed wrong about whether a wine was aerated or not. The quality of wine affects how the aerator works. A really bad wine may become drinkable, but don't expect it to become even good. On the other hand, a mediocre wine can become quite drinkable. So, your mileage will vary, but you should taste the difference clearly. I liked them so much, I bought another package and sent it to my duaghter who is also a wine lover. Great product.

My NEEWER intervalometer seemed OK out of the box.  All the buttons and the LCD seemed to work as described and as portrayed in YouTube reviews.  However, there must have been a connection problem inside the plug that prevented it from triggering my camera shutter.  The LCD counted down just fine but it only triggered the shutter every once in a while.  I tried wiggling the plug around a bit in the socket, and that caused the camera shutter to go rapid fire. I also rotated the plug around 360o and found a few positions that seemed to work OK, but I don't want to take the chance this thing craps out in the middle of a 4-hour time lapse.  I returned it to Amazon for a prompt refund (thank you Amazon).I'm giving it 3-stars because you're always going to have some defects -- trying to be fair.I replaced it with an Aputure intervalometer, which seems to work perfectly – see my review there. I'm just glad it was a problem with the NEEWER intervalometer and not the plug in my new camera!

We have often used regular pompous in our Vacation Bible School, and these add a whole new dimension to the crafts. With the yarn Pom in the middle, and the tinsel on the outside looking fuzzy, these work very well for anything you are making that needs a fuzzy look.Two ideas I discovered while testing these for my review:Take a yarn needle and thread it with yarn with a knot at the end. You can also use a small button to help the poms stay on. Have your children string several balls before knotting it off. Glue on eyes or small beads on the front for a VERY cute fuzzy caterpillar.Take one sparkle ball and two pipe cleaners. Cut the pipe cleaners in half to make four, then criss cross and twist them to make your legs. Use thread or yarn to sew the legs on. You can also use glue, but I found sewing them worked much better.  Instant spider. You cad add a string to hang it, or even glue on eyes.If you simply want to use these for decorating, you can run a string through them and tie them on anything.I received a complimentary sample of these to consider and check out for a review. I am happy to review them and pass them on to our Vacation Bible School who will be making spiders and caterpillars this year as they explore God's creation around them.

This router has solved all my home networking woes and I've had a lot of them.  Granted, it's fairly complicated for a home network in that there are many devices connected both wired and wirelessly from various macs and PCs, home theater receiver, music server, network printers, network storage drives, game consoles (ps3, xbox, wii), etc.  In the past I've used a combination of various routers to make things work properly.  From different versions of Apple airport (express, extreme, extreme "N") to various Belkin and Linkysys models, even the supposedly foolproof WRT54G model.The problem with the Apple airport models has been their inability to allow open NAT for Xbox Live gaming, not to mention the playstation network.  I love Airport networks and their integration with OS X and all its cool file/music/drive/printer sharing and networking features but I've always had to combine an extra router to handle the non-Apple side of things.  The D-Link DGL-4500 is the first router I've had that plays nice with everything on the network.  It just works.  It works with Apple-based networks, it provides Open NAT for Xbox Live, it works with PS3 (with some minor tweaks), and it works with just about any challenge I throw at it.  Music and video streaming, latency-sensitive online gaming, you name it.The Xtreme N gaming router is the jack of all trades.  It does it all and looks great to boot.  The OLED display  looks sharp, has access to basic functions and status on the unit itself (a nice touch), and setup was a snap.  It basically worked with everything right out of the box without much customization.  The only change I made was to set the PS3 in DMZ in order to get from NAT type 3 to type 2.  But Xbox 360 had open NAT out of the box.  My favorite part about the DGL-4500 is the web-based interface.  Although it's not exactly simplified, it is very flexible and well organized.  If you have a basic understanding of designing networks, this user interface is everything you could ever ask for.  The web-based interface on my previous Linksys and Belkin were all cryptic by comparison.  The only thing better is Apple's own Airport utility application, but you can't win 'em all.A small nitpick I have with the router is the bright blue power LED that shines like a beacon in a dark room.  While the OLED display is just as bright, at least it shuts off after a few seconds.  The power LED stays on all the time and is quite annoying if you have it setup in the bedroom, bookshelf, or on your home theater cabinet.  There is no way to turn it off or turn it down.  Cutting a circular piece of duct tape and taping it over solves the problem quite easily.Although I purchased this router at an attractive price of $175, it still seems pricey for a router.  I do think it's worth every penny considering that an Apple Airport Extreme "N" costs as much brand new but isn't nearly as capable or compatible.  Highly recommend if you want a comprehensive, do-it-all, compatible-with-all, high-speed 802.11n router.

I'd like to knock this thing down to four stars, explaining as I do that the Canon equivalent to this third party substitute is of better build quality and so on. Trouble is, I can't because the Canon equivalent to this remote shutter release is equally as shoddy. At under a fifth the cost and the same level of (admittedly poor) construction as the Canon shutter release, I'd say this bit is a no brainer.Specifics: By shoddy construction, I mean this shutter release is composed entirely of plastic, just like the Canon model. There's nothing wrong with that--it's just an itty bitty circuitboard that shouldn't be taking abuse anyway--and the build quality itself is fine. The cord seems securely anchored to the device, the button (it only has one) has a nice, tactile feel to it (but doesn't give a very good feedback click at mid press--see below), and the plastic part itself feels solid enough that I don't worry about breaking it from letting it drop and hang from the camera. (I wouldn't let it hit the floor, though.) I've done close to a thousand shots with one unit without having a single hiccup, which puts reliability at least at "good enough for me." Because they are third party, though, I heartily recommend not trusting QC. I keep three tested units in my bag for important shoots. Considering that buying three is still almost half the cost of one Canon unit and considering the Canon unit isn't any more reliable, however, I don't feel at a disadvantage doing this.My only gripe is a mild one. Because of the poor tactile response when the shutter release button is halfway pressed, it can sometimes be difficult to use whatever function you have tied to half-press shutter button. This is rarely a problem for me because I tend to use these releases for portraits and landscapes (where I use manual focus). YMMV. This problem isn't critical, though. I fully expect it's something you could adapt to with practice, so those who regularly use HPSB shouldn't consider it a dealbreaker.

The tripod is well-constructed, not very heavy, operates smoothly, and comes in a convenient carry bag. The onboard levels are fairly difficult to see (for someone without great eyesight), but they are a nice touch. I'm not a tripod expert, so I can't really give a detailed, comparative review, but I'm satisfied with this tripod so far.The bag, on the other hand, is disappointingly NOT the standard Tenba 15" messenger bag that is so well-rated. I was unable to find out what the "LE" stands for, but I'd guess it means "Lightweight Edition." According to someone at Tenba, the bag was custom-made for this package, and the material used is a much lighter grade (they don't state the specs). It doesn't appear as durable as the stock bag. Also, a warning about the carry handle: If you have substantial weight in the bag, plan on the stitching to give out where the handle meets the bag. I'd recommend always using the included (and nicely designed) shoulder strap. As another reviewer stated, it's iffy as to whether you would want to trust this bag with high-end camera equipment. That said, for an everyday messenger bag, with the option of using the camera equipment insert, it's okay.

There are so few "cons" to this pot, it's hard to write a review that doesn't sound sappy.I have a full set of Ultrex, a snappy modern line sold by a company that makes pans for many big names found in department stores. 55 year warranty, they work fine... But they're non-stick and I've started to worry about that.Not long ago, a study came out that suggested eating foods cooked in Teflon pans left molecules of something or other floating around in the person's blood for FOUR YEARS. I thought twice about this and decided it was time to look into other pans. It might turn out to be benign - but what if it isn't?I was addicted to good browning, was used to it from Cuisinart pans and Ultrex. I tried Calphalon hard-anodized aluminum. One burning incident and that pan was RUINED, totally ruined, nothing would clean it.I've cooked in this Lodge Logic pre-seasoned for a dozen meals now, meat and fish, vegetables too. I've used all types of fats - olive oil, butter, corn oil. Tonight it made a gravy to die for - using heavy cream and mushrooms.You do have to get used to its different heating cycle. It heats up slower than some other materials. It cools off slower too. But it is fuel efficient and you can do a lot of cooking on Low.My #1 demand was that it brown meat well. It does that SPLENDIDLY. Really beautiful. Heat the pan for 5 minutes on near High. Throw in two tablespoons of olive oil, throw in stew meat and it will brown in four minutes, magazine picture perfect. Stir with a wooden spoon. Throw in vegetables once the meat's browned. A few cups of water, put the lid on, turn it down to Medium for 15 minutes, Low for another 15 minutes -- it will look like a magazine cover stew. Browning a roast is even easier -- and you can put it into the oven after browning.If I had to complain of anything, it would be that cast iron is heavy. I'm not sure how much of a problem this will be. This is still a small enough pot to handle for most people. You can roast a whole chicken in it so if that's the quantity you cook, buy this right NOW, you can't go wrong.

This is a great drive!!! Had it for a week, works great and quiet!I burned ~22GB of data onto a BD-R disc at 1x and it took 30 minutes! Not bad for 22GB of data!!!So I guess that at 4x, because the discs I have can burn at 4x max, will probably take less than 10 minutes!After installation, some computers might need an update, so this is what you do:Go on google and type this: pioneer bd-rw bdr-206dbk firmwareOne of the first searches will say something like Firmware DOWNLOAD: PIONEER CORPORATION, you'll see the site starts with pioneer.jp.....  THIS IS THE ONE!There, you go to the BD/DVD/CD Drive Firmware section, and then find BDR-206DBK (because that is what this drive is) and select download.  It will download a file called BDR-206DBK_FW106EU.EXE  - Run it and its done. You might want to reboot your computer but I didn't.When I did this, it said the current firmware is up to date.Now if you want to play BD disks, get PowerDVD that is what I have.Enjoy!

With the pizza stone in the BBQ unit, I heat the BBQ to 700 degrees for about 15 minutes then slide the pizza onto the stone. Hot summer day in Southern California.  July 4th in fact.  Barbecue at our place between the parade in the morning and the fireworks show in the evening.  Too hot to sit outside.  Until I brought the mister out.Works great. It is strong and fairly loud even on the lowest setting of fan. The mister connects easily to the hose with a long connection from the fan itself to make this convenient (though usually a hose itself has a fair bit of length).  Three setting of mist, one, two or three nozzles.  Builds up pressure inside so even when you turn off the hose it will mist for a couple minutes. Blows out misty and nice.  Once working, I really liked it.  Not too much dripping and pretty clean overall.Some complaints now.  The power cord is pretty short, six feet seems long, until you realized it's coming from the motor body.  Surprisingly inconvenient.  The power plug itself is also pretty large, set up with a breaker at the plug itself, making it pretty big. I thought it was going to be too big for an outdoor covered outlet, but it did in fact fit fine.  Another issue with the plug is that whenever I move it, it trips the breaker. In fact, it was tripped when I first got it, making me think it arrived broken.  Hit test and reset, the fan turned on fine. But, it happened each time I unplugged and moved it.  Now that I know, it's fine, but I was sure I got a bad unit at first.As to the barbecue, this fan added a bit of drama as people tried to jockey for position in the mist.  I turned on the oscillation and that kept the crowd from getting too unruly.  Now when my propane ran out halfway through cooking the dogs and burgers, well, a minor riot almost started. But that's a story for a different review.

It was in pristine condition and had 50% less play than the original. Just finished putting it thru its paces and Im well satisfied. Thanks Amazon and Fuji.

Foremost, I love Verso. I have a stack of different cases from this company and love one more than the next! Now, this case was a bit of a disappointment (maybe because I am used to absolute excellence from this company).The outside fabric is a surprisingly rough texture ... so much so that I find little "fuzzies" from my blanket stuck to the covers when I read in bed. And, when I carry it in my messenger, I find little pieces of paper stuck on it (from the spiral-bound notebook-paper that my students often use for quizzes). It seems that everything is either attracted to/or gets stuck to this case.I do like the polished leather spine and think the choice of matching stitching works really nicely here. (The company's signature contract stitching would have probably looked "too busy"). Good choice!Weirdly, I was disappointed by the interior. The fabric was also rough. (I have already noticed dust sticking to it). I am used to either microfiber or a lush velvety fabric. So, I probably would not make this my day-to-day case but a "special occasions" case.As with all the Verso products, I like the little pocket that lets me hold a few notes.Bottomline: it will do for special events (or even to complement a black dress) but it may not be your go-to case.

Ever since I got my Nexus 4, with glass on the back it felt fragile compared to the aluminum bodied HTC phone I used to own. I purchased the official bumper at the same time as the phone. The bumper looks nice and allows showing off the phone but offers no real protection. What is worse, the bumper is surrounded by smooth plastic which doesn't help with the grip. In the month and a half I had the phone, it slipped out a couple of times though no damage due to either falling on soft grass or carpet from a short distance. Still, I was done waiting for the next accident to break my phone so I went on the search for some really good protection.I had in the past had phones with Otterbox Commuter cases and they were great. Otterbox decided not to make one for the LG Nexus 4 so I went looking for something just as good or better. I believe I found it with the Ballistic SG series case.I agree with most of the other reviewers with regards to the top and bottom could have been just a tad tighter fitting but it is a minor nitpick. The lint attracting is another nitpick but to be expected.The look is understated. The matte black back with dimples and the beefy silicone make it feel solid in the hand. I like the bare phone but it feels much more secure and better with this case on. The power and volume buttons have excellent tactile feedback. The ports are all accessible and aligned. Due to the deep recess to the camera, it has a larger opening to ensure the case does not obscure the picture.In summary, this is the case that I should have started out with that would have eased my concerns about it breaking and enjoyed it more. Now I can!

Although it took me several minutes to position these headphones correctly (more on that later), the quality of the sound coming from them impressed me at once.  The balance is nice, with both lower and upper ranges clear and without undue emphasis.  I can adjust the volume; skip or go back to a track; answer, reject, or make a voice recognition activated call -- all with a press of a button on the headset.  Best of all, these headphones sound much more expensive than their retail price would suggest.The only potential problem with these is the fit, and, as I mentioned above, this took some trial-and-error positioning for me to find a comfortable position.  This is the kind of headphone that is worn looped over the ears and across the back of the head or neck.  That took some getting used to.  I found that when I wear glasses, sunglasses or regular, there isn't room on top of my ears to keep both securely in place.  Because the bar can stick out behind my head/neck, I find that the best, most secure positioning is to rotate the earphones a little so that the bar rests against my lower neck.  The bad part about this positioning is that, if I look up, the headphones move.  Fortunately, that isn't something I do often while listening to music.  Still, I took off a half-star for the awkwardness of not being able to adjust the length of the bar for a better fit.These headphones fold up to roughly a 5" x 4" size.  They fit easily into the included rigid, zippered case.  A short charging cable is also included, and it fits into a side pocket inside the case.  The Bluetooth range is about average; I could walk about 24 feet from the source, with a wall in between, before the signal started dropping.These headphones are a great value, especially if you don't mind the over-the-ear, back-of-the-head style.  Note:  I received a sample for review.-- Debbie Lee Wesselmann

This is my 7th New Trent item. This New Trent 10W 5V-2A Dual USB Port high-speed AC Wall Charger and it's a useful convenient item to have given all the electronics I have in my home and the limited electrical outlets. It's not bulky and the prongs can fold in for travel. It's powerful and and can be used internationally as it can take AC 100-240V. The top usb port is labeled "Tablet/Smartphone" and bottom USB port is labeled "iPad/Smartphone"...The description on Amazon says "Split between two ports, alternate between charging 2 Smartphones at 1 amp each or a tablet device at 2.1 amps." However, it does appear the ports operate differently and perhaps at different outputs(?). I have a Samsung Galaxy Tab (1st generation) and it seems to be able to charge on both USB slots -- however, it gives me a normal "green indicator" on my status bar when charging on the top USB slot but when a "green with red X" indicator when I charge it on the bottom USB slot. I am able to charge my Samsung Galaxy S4 smartphone with both slots without any issues. I like it and would recommend to my friends and family.

My wife brought one of these home from a local store. And it's going back. The concept looks good but the performance is dismal. It takes quite a while to heat up, which is a minor annoyance. Once the green light finally turns on, it delivers nowhere near the stated temperature. I measured the plates with an infrared thermometer. If it's set to 400F, the average temperature is around 350F on the bottom and 325F on the lid side when opened flat in grilling mode.An even bigger problem is the wild temperature fluctuations due to the cheap design. The problem is the griddle plates are thin aluminum that have almost no mass to store heat. Even with nothing cooking, the surface temp swings over an 80F range in a matter of minutes. As soon as cold food touches the plate, it drops the temp very quickly and the machine struggles to heat up again. There is just no substitute for cast iron or at least very thick aluminum in an application like this. If that were an optional upgrade, this might rise up to 3 star status -- I doubt higher because it's underpowered to start with.

If I could give this zero stars, I would.  I have a white, wire lid holder that I have had for many, many years.  I stack up all my Farberware lids in it.  The one I have is not as sturdy as this one, however, a big plus to the one I have is that the lids actually fit well.  I recently purchased 6 new Farberware pots with strainer lids.  They are slightly wider than the older lids.  They fit in my lid holder fine.  I received this one in the mail and I cannot get the lids in the holder.  AT ALL.  And the space in the very front? Forget it.  You can't get any lid in there at all.  I ended up putting the new, larger lids in the old holder, and cramming the old lids in the new holder.  What is the point of having a holder if they don't give you enough room from the simplest lids?  Never again.  I'll look for something else because every time I look at this thing, it annoys me.
Love it Love it Love it! Great price I got some for nieces and myself and I get a lot of compliments I use mine on my iPad mini!! Can't wait to give them out for Christmas!!

Worked immediately with my Onkyo nr-609. Came brand new in the original box. Small in size and looks stylish with the receiver. I'm happy with the product.

Have used it chopping up jalapenos and other peppers, julienning carrots and dicing onions Slicing tomatoes seems to be a breeze. The edge may not last forever but that's to be expected. A good touch up with a good ceramic rod will keep it sharp.It is a double edge ground blade but suspect it is 14 degee angle ground in Japanese stle.
I love this bag it has places to hold everything for my DSLR camera back up camera and all my accessories.  I recommend this bag to anyone not looking to spend a arm and leg for a bag.

I bought this for my dad who is a photographer and a camper. I thought, wouldn't this be perfect for when he is out hiking, etc and wanting to take pictures - an adjustable tripod!Turns out he had done some research himself and thought about buying one. His comments were he was surprised at how stiff the joints were, but that should be good to grip onto things as well as bear the weight of the camera. He tried it out here and there to test it and seemed pleased with the result.It's been two years now and I haven't once seen him use it. When I asked him why, his response was that it is useful when you're in a pinch and have an uneven surface you want to shoot from. But it is pretty awkward to get your camera level and takes some time, especially if you're a perfectionist like he is. He uses his regluar small tripod more - is more familiar with it and gets consistant results, but he enjoys the flexibility he has this the Gorillapod.So apparently he HAS used it... just not on a regular basis.

This set of stoneware from Paula Deen is beautiful. The white color shows off food well and the simple raised design makes this an elegant set of dishes. The dinner plates are really large, the salad plate (or I guess you could use them as saucers) are fairly large as well. I love the bowls. These are huge with good depth and will hold quite a big serving of soup or cereal, or whatever else you use them for. The cups are sort of plain but go well with the rest of the set. The dishes are very heavy. I have had stoneware in the past but I tend to gravitate to the more festive designs of Corelle Wear. I like the feel of Corelle Wear and the lighter weight and the durability of it. I am a bit dismayed that these dishes were made in China (although some dishes in Corelle Wear are made in China also, much of it is USA made). I've had other dishes from China and they did not hold up. I am a bit concerned because the instruction sheet that came with the dishes had a lot of "don't" statements in it. The dishes really need to have protectors placed in-between the plates to protect from scratches and chips, the cups cannot be nested, and many other precautions too numerous to mention. Since I just received them and have just started using them, I'm not sure how they will stand up over the long haul. However, they are elegant and I think that they will make a lovely table. Sometimes simple and plain make the best table settings.
The clamp is totally adjustable and works well on my little steaming pitcher. This is probably not the highest quality temperature reader around, but it works fine for my home use.

These are great measuring spoons. The only negative is that the arms are a little thin, there seems to a lot of flex. I hope that they can stand up to day to day use. I'd certainly order them again even with the thinness.
This replacement battery lasted longer than any substitute I bought previously. I would recommend this battery over a generic battery.
I bought this as a birthday gift for my nephew. I charged it first before giving it and it seemed to work. Hopefully, it is charging his cell phone.

I have had this for over a year now and have used it quite a lot.  I bought this product because my vehicle does not have an input jack for the stereo or built in blue tooth.  If you plan on just using the built in speaker for hands free talking it works well.  The quality and clarity of the call are good on both ends.  However, if you plan to play music through the speaker it does not sound very good.  Also, if you plan on playing your music/phone call through your cars speakers with the FM transmitter, even on the best of frequencies there is some static.  And as you drive you will have to keep adjusting the station to stay on an open frequency.  Overall, its good but not great.
I love having portal power at easy access when I travel.  Extra bonus to be able to connect 2 devices and to have two different charge rates.

Everything about this backup battery is great. Build quality, weight, size and charge time.I would prefer that it came in a slimmer profile. Other than that there is nothing of better value than this product.

I used this on my vacation to the lake, I was afraid if I went out kayaking and wanted to take a picture I might drop my iphone in the water because I can be that klutzy.  Nothing happened, the case was easy to open and put my iphone in and I took a picture and it was clear as can be.The delivery was fast and the price was great! I would recommend this seller.

The thing I've been most happy with this screen protector is that I haven't noticed it since installing it a month ago. It hasn't pealed around the edges like screen protectors often do. Installation went smoothly; I've put on my share of screen protectors and if you are careful to not trap dust and you have nerves of steel then you can align the protector, sweep out the bubbles and have a long lasting protector.Finger feel is decent, not too sticky, and smooth with no underlying texture.I've been keeping my iPhone 5 in a case, so I haven't been testing how resistant this protector is coming lose from frequently being popped out of its case, so your mileage may vary.

This is a little loud (not silent like others) when on the higher setting but it seems to do a good job. I use this in my children's bedrooms and it is a good size for that. The permanent filter is easy to clean and convenient because it does not require replacement parts.

The pillow was purchased for my daughter.  Low loft.  Both pillows, solid and shredded, came with a wonderfully soft cover and were instantantly a favorite.  Less expensive than others of a plush nature, but led to difficulty getting my daughter up and moving in the morning--hardly a bad thing.
I used this for a chocolate tart, and it came out fantastic. Truly non- stick. Easy to clean, and once the tart cooled down, the sides came off neatly

It is easy to use and with 1/2 cup serving lines inside the rings it really helps me gauge my carbohydrates better. I don't have a dishwasher, so I can't tell you if it's safe to clean that way, but it cleans up easy in the sink. It's a nice alternative to weighing my food.

Products like this are really a compromise.  No one likes large, bulky cases, but these protect the best.  On the other hand, no one likes to have their phone damaged when they drop it.  Unfortunately the best looking cases offer practically no drop protection.This case walks the line between protection and looks very well, IMO.There is just barely enough cushion to protect from a 3-4 foot drop, while still being slim enough to be attractive.  The case provides a little "grip" while not being overly sticky as to attract dust and dirt.This case fits the HTC one exceptionally well.  It slides over the phone firmly and snaps into place.  There is a significant lip on the front of the phone case to protect from falls on a flat surface.  On the back there are circular holes to allow for all of the microphone and camera hardware to do its job.  On the top and bottom though, the cutouts for the headphone jack and USB cable are larger than they really need to be.  I'm not sure if there is a technical reason for this, but it's something that caught my eye.Three weeks into owning the case I have had no issues with dust or debris getting in between the case and the phone - despite spending a lot of time in my pockets.  I also have had no issues with my phone overheating despite the very powerful hardware that the HTC one has.Overall I'm very happy with this case, and can fully recommend it to others looking for a slim case for the One.

It holds lots of laundry. It comes with a separate bag with handles for soap bottles and a separate netted bag for socks and undies. My laundry room is in the basement of my building and I just pull it into the elevator to the machines.So far the wheels are holding up well and I have been filling this up.Best laundry bag ever.

I was excited to receive this tray.  I thought that it would help me to organize my "potions" drawer.  My tray came cracked and damaged.  Pieces of sharp plastic were everywhere and I will not be using it.  It was a mess and we had to grab our cats because they were near the tiny sharp pieces of plastic.  It did not seem to be pretty at all in its cracked condition.  I noticed lots of five star reviews for this but again...mine is not usable.

I love to bake, but in the past, I'd been hesitant to make apple-based baked goods due to the huge amount of time and effort necessary to core, peel, and slice the apples.  I'd always wanted to try one of this little peeler gizmos, so finally, I broke down and bought one.  I'm so glad I did--it works great!  This amazing little gadget is so simple to use in just three easy steps:1) Attach the peeler to your counter with the suction cup.2) Press the apple firmly into the holder.3) Turn the handle until the apple pushes all the way through for a perfectly cored, peeled, and sliced apple.  (You can also pull back the peeling blade for sliced apples with the peels kept on.)This has got to be one of my best speciality kitchen gadgets, and I use it much more often than you would think--it's great not only for apple pies, but also for apple muffins, apple pancakes, apple cobblers, and much more.Highly recommended for anyone who loves cooking and baking with apples.

Works with iPhone5s and iOS7 without the "not designed..." error.  Also, it puts out enough power that my iPhone charges while in use in the car (running Waze...backlight, GPS, and data all running).  Highly recommended.

I ordered this primarily for its length (6 feet), which would make plugging in my phone easier. However, the "mini-5Pin" is larger than my mini pin to my Motorola Droid phone. While it may have plenty of other uses and may fit someone else's cellphone, it doesn't fit mine and renders the product useless to me.
I use this always. I depend on it but didn't plan to when I bought it!!

It's a great utensil so far as I expected. However, unlike the picture, my cobalt blue french oven came with a large white rectangle on the side emblazoned with Le Creuset. Yuck. It's conspicuous and ugly.  I wouldn't have bought it had I known, but I've decided to keep it since the pot itself seems great.

The PocketEnergy2 is a very nice, single charge power bank. I like it because it offers both a Lightning charger for iPhone 5s and also a standard Micro USB charger all on one device. And even better, the cables are built in.The built in cables are a fantastic idea. They are short enough to fit into your pocket with your phone plugged in (unless you put a lot of stuff in your pocket). You can easily plug the phone in and put it into your hand bag as well.The power bank only holds about 2600 mAh. Because of the way the efficiency of charging works, this comes out to maybe 1.2 - 1.3 charges for an iPhone. On my Lumia 1020 I can get 1 charge max.But here's the thing. This isn't meant for charging a device many, many times. It's more of an emergency charger for your phone when you really need the extra capability. I use it while hiking and I would imagine walking around town with it, if you don't think you'll be close to somewhere you can charge, would work just as well.It is possible to plug 2 devices into the charger, however it will charge the iPhone first, then the second device. So if you have 2 phones you want to share power with, maybe plug in just the other phone for a little while first and then swap in the iPhone.And since it's only holding 2600 mAh, it is fast to charge and very light. Definitely allows for traveling with ease.This is a handy charger, well worth the asking price.I received a free sample for review purposes from Brightech. Given the price and the capability, I would definitely purchase one again when this one no longer works.

The Annex Quad Lock provides a great, secure mount for your iPhone.  The phone case is actually two parts with rubbery backing that works just like most other phone cases, and a "poncho" that wraps over the front of your phone to completely seal it.The case snaps onto your iPhone like a regular phone case, with holes along the sides for the buttons and the charging port.  The back slightly raised to give room for an interlocking hole for the mount to snap into.  While it definitely makes your phone thicker, it's actually a lot slimmer than I expected it to be.  The thickness of the case could cause issues with connecting to charging cradles, but my OEM Lightning cable works fine.The poncho is a rubbery, clear case that wraps over the front of your phone, completely sealing it for weather protection.  The sides of the poncho have raised bits that line up with the buttons so you'll still be able to use your phone through the protective casing.  Also, the touch screen will still work!The mount itself can be secured to your bike using the provided zip ties or rubber bands.  I went with the zip ties because they're easier to attach, but both seem like reliable options for keeping the mount attached to your bike.Other reviewers have noted that the phone case is prone to catching dust and smell debris in the back, which I've noticed too.  Attaching the poncho seems to offer some additional protection, but it also makes your phone considerably bulkier.  I usually switch to the Annex Quad Lock only when I use my bike though, and I switch back to my regular case when I'm done, so I haven't had to deal with this problem too much yet.Overall, this is a great system for securing and protecting your phone while on your bike.
The rack itself is fine but the size is a little small for the roaster even though this is the size recommended. Still, it works and the next size up wouldn't fit so I'm satisfied.

This is a beautiful smooth black leather holster with nice stitching.  I wish I could use it, but it seems to be a perfect fit for an iPhone 5 with NO case.  I have an Incipio 2-piece case, and there is just no way I can fit it into this very nice accessory.  I will sadly return it and search for a larger holster like this one.

This SIM card will work with any AT&T; Wireless phone. It will not work with iPhone 4s or 5 or any other phone that uses Micro-Sim Card.You do not need to use an AT&T; Go Phone (but you will need an AT&T; Phone) to use this SIM Card. I have used my old AT&T; Phone and I was able to activate this SIM card just fine.To activate, insert the SIM card, turn on the phone, and dial 611. Or you just go to AT&T; Go Phone website to activate the SIM Card. The calling rate is around $25/month for 250 minutes with unlimited texting. International calls, however, are severely restricted as they mostly allow only Go-Phone to international landline calls, and they charge extra for the international plan.Pros:1)Works on any AT&T; Wireless Phones.2)Easy to activate. Good way to limit cell phone usage.3)Reasonable monthly rates.Cons:1)Will not work with iPhone 4, 4s, or 52)International calling plans are both expensive and limited (can only call mostly landlines).3)Data plans are very expensive.4)Only works with AT&T; network, which has limitations of its own.Overall, this is a good SIM card if you want to use your phone for talking and texting within the US, Canada, or Mexico. If you want to use data or make international calls, you may find Go-Phones plans both expensive and limited.

This item was purchased as a Christmas gift for my granddaughter. She has enjoyed playing with it and so far it has held up in not only the 5 year old's hands, but also those of her 3 year old brother.

Great wall mount stand, comes with all the hardware and bolts, fit our 50 inch TV great. I built a wood stand of my own design to resemble a small movie theater, and used this mounting kit to attach the TV in side it. It worked great and looks great. The mount comes with a level and a heavy coated HDMI cable. Great Price !

This is a really generously-sized air-tight container. I love Lock & Lock for its ability to keep everything perfectly fresh and moisture proof. I use this particular container to store the salad vegetables that I use every day for much lunch salad. It will hold a head of romaine, a cucumber, a carrot, bit chunk of daikon radish, and a cleaned and cut bell pepper with no problem at all. I have a second one for celery and/or greens of other sorts. It has a perforated tray on the bottom to keeps the veggies perfectly. The food kept in Lock & Lock always tastes clean and fresh like it just came out of its sealed packaging container. It lasts forever. Gotta love it.

beautiful rolling pin,i wanted to buy  this rolling pin from long time finally i bought  when i recieved this i  thrilled to see how heavy and well made works perfect every time.i do not need new rolling pin for long time.very smooth rolling every time.made in usa .i would recomend  if you are looking quality rolling pin  this is it.
I absolutely love Otterbox. This case has already paid for itself with all the times it's been dropped and protected my Ipad. Would definitely recommend

Was looking for a deep fryer that would be good for frying single servings. Out of the many deep fryer's Amazon has for sell, this one caught my eye. It's very lite, easy to move around (even with grease in it). I also like the shape of this fryer better than those others too. Because the handle is in the middle of the basket, it's easier to control what your doing,,,,,, dropping, raising and pouring out whatever you may have deep fried. And plus, by not having too much grease in it, should be easy to dump out, clean and refill when time to do so. I have used this thing twice so far. Have deep fried french fries and breaded okra. Both came out perfect !!!! This fryer for a family,,,,, would NOT be a good buy. But for 1 or 2 people,,,,, a good choice it would be. :)

These 3 tools to sharpen - give you the ability to sharpen most of your household tools that might require honing. Many people have a problem using sharpening stones or steels, and this, even though it does not give the `professional' results like one of those other instruments at least achieves its purpose.The scissor sharpener is better than any other I have used. I had a pair of scissors which I really like, but was reaching the point where it was not cutting some paper, even not cutting stamps apart. This brought the scissors back to near their original sharpness. The sharpener that came with the scissor set had failed to achieve any satisfactory results.The knife sharpener has left some marks above the knife edge; but I had a knife which had pitted edges, from a family member (who will remain nameless) trying to hack apart a chicken. The deep pits were not removed completely; but at least the knife is back in useable condition and the edges shaper.The garden sharpener has not fared as well. Hoes and some of my other garden tools seem to be thicker and this cleans up the edge a bit, but I cannot say that it really sharpens the edge.Altogether it is a good set which will sharpen tools, knives and such that are hard to get sharpened. The guards for your fingers are a good idea and achieve their purpose of keeping your fingers and hand from running down the blade.

My Aunt walked around with a broken screen for a year and if I knew how easy it was to replace the screen I would have did it for her  a lot earlier.  The only thing you need is the sticky tape , and I bought a pre-cut piece from Amazon and it fit perfectly

RECENTLY PURCHASED THIS Panasonic NN-SD967S Luxury Full-Size 2.2 cu. ft. Microwave Oven with Inverter Technology, 1250 Watt High Power and Easy-to-Use Pop-Out Dial, Stainless - TO REPLACE MY HEAVILY USED 6 YEAR OLD PANASONIC (previous model with the same size and features - magnatron failed the 2nd time - now out of warranty).  Using this microwave constantly over the past two weeks, it actually performs better than our previous unit of the same power rating - cooks with 30% less time.THE INVERTER TECHNOLOGY feature continues to be a great cooking helper as well as the larger size.  Multiple containers can be re-heated simultaneously which demonstrates its added flexibility.As with our previous Panasonic Genius/Invertor Micro, this unit maintains a high level of build quality.  The front is finished in top end looking brushed Stainless Steel (finger-print resistant) and the wife loves the way it blends with our other appliances.We expect to again get many years of extremely heavy home use.  Would highly recommend this item for any busy kitchen.

These are the perfect whisks for mini-mixing jobs.  My husband likes a latte in the morning and cappuccino in the evening, and I've been through AT LEAST three mechanical, hand-held mixers.  Unfortunately, no matter how much I spend or what model I try, the little motors eventually give up the ghost.  One morning, staring at my latest dead carcass of an electronic device, I thought "gee, it would be great if this whisk attachment was longer--then I wouldn't even NEED the motor" when I had a V-8 moment & thought--hello, dum-dum--find a little hand-held WHISK that can fit in a coffee mug! Well, DUH!So I sifted through a few reviews at Amazon & settled on these.  They're just what I wanted; the handle is coiled metal and rigid, easy to hold, and the whisks work just as they should.  I actually can mix hubby's latte more quickly and thoroughly by hand with these than I could with the little battery-powered mixers I've used in the past. I can even create a little foam on top of the cappuccino, just for authenticity's sake : )  These are a winner--very versatile & easy to clean.  Can't beat the price; highly recommended for quick kitchen tasks; very happy with my SIMPLE solution.  I wish I'd been smarter, sooner!
These pillows are very nice. Perfect for the purpose. They made my bad look "finished" when it's made. I love them

This is the perfect quilt set to go in any bedroom in the house.  This is a QUILT..not a Bedspread set.  I do know there is a bedspread set available; so be sure you know that the specs would be different in the items.  I love this quilt set it is beautiful to look at, hangs perfectly, and is easy maintenance...no ironing.  Amazon delivered it on time, packaging was great, and the pricing perfect.I have a cotton dust ruffle with this in neutral...Looks wonderful.  Thanks Amazon.  This is a great purchase...look elsewhere and you will see, all the way around...you are better off buying at Amazon.  I just love this set.
Only tried it when it arrived and seems to work just fine.  One cannot really review the quality until it has been used, charged and discharged many times.

I bought a pair for around $18 at wal-mart, so even less is even a better deal. When I bought these, the package said studio headphones, or something like that. If you need headphones to do some mixing, I think you need to keep looking. I hear a smack around 1.6 K. It's fine if you just need them for listening to some music. Good for the price though.

This solved my problem when my phone was dying and I was talking to a person on the phone. It is so much longer that I can move around and not have my head stuck to the wall while talking! That was so irritating! You would think Apple would be aware of this problem and make the cords longer. I do find it takes longer to charge the phone though and I don't know why. Too long of a distance for electricity to travel? So, I keep the short one to charge.
So well made. Great fit and finish, and I found nothing comparable.  Has really helped declutter the bedroom.  Would definitely recommend.

beautiful minimal case, feels great in handling, looks amazing, perfect amount of tactile-ness on "rubber" back.my only complaint is that the charger port is too small...& it would be nice for more bumper colours

This has become my primary case for my phone.
This product works great. Love the color too. Friends ask about the quality and I have never had an.y issues so far
Fits good in my ears, which is why I ordered it, but the sound quality was no where near as good as an original iphone earbud headset.

I have only had the device for a short time. Therefore, the reader should understand that caveat. The device arrived and I have used it succesfully. It look of good quality and does the job for which I bought it. It charges the iPad and the iPhone just fine. There were no issues related to the safety of the device. The brief instruction clarifies that although it has two USB ports, one is for Apple devices (A) and the other is for non-Apple devices (NA). I have not tried to use one of each, but I suspect it will be ok if you respect the power limitation of 10 watts. That is you may charge two telephones, one Apple and one not at the same time, since each requires only 2 watts. However, I have not tried this configuration. I have Apple iPhones. All in all I am satisfied and the price is right.

Best cup of coffee 've ever had at home.  Consider a larger size if you want to make more than one cup at a time though.  I have no idea how they came up with 6 cups for this thing, as it barely fills my standard size coffee mug.  The coffee is delicious though.  This is a well-made product too.  I previously had a cheap Chinese made one tht lasted a week before the plastic handle broke off...this one is WAY better.
great price, great product, just wish i bought more colors so i can change and interchange colors to make my own. well got to go search other colors!

Like the other reviewer, I was a bit taken back by the $50 price of a filter that is supposed to be replaced every 6 months. But I bought the BlueAir 402 anyway.This is one of those products that truly works! No false hype, no bogus claims. Since I don't have pets, and nobody smokes in my home, I have found that I can get a year out of a filter without a problem. At the same time, I am impressed at how well this filter performs. The intake side of my 402 air purifier gets dusty - the filter does show a darkening over time - and the outlet screen of the purifier has *never* needed cleaned. That says a lot about the effectiveness of this filter.There may be cheaper, and there are systems that use multiple pre-filters to extend the filter life, but there are no better. If you are considering a BlueAir 402 or other model, don't let the price of this filter turn you away. You ARE getting what you pay for.
Someone wrote, the smell was awful. If you have anything close to an allergy, or sensitive to smells, save yourself the time to return. Plus the stand. Worthless

I am thrilled with the weight and warmth of these sheets.  They are in between a jersey and a flannel, but really a good quality fabric.  I know they will be warm in the winter and could be used year-round (if it weren't for the design I chose for the holidays).  They washed beautifully with no pilling.  These are going in our guest bedroom and I am very happy with them.  Now I have to find a queen set for our master bedroom.  Love them!  Oh, and the pack that they came in, like a square of plastic, had actual fabric on the sides of the pack.  Really nice.

The design is bizarre.  I sort of see the problem they had, but the result is not great.  The first part of the problem is the inner lid you don't see.  They threaded it, so opening, closing, and cleaning are a chore.  Then, you have to pour tiny cups into the lid.  Why not offer one big cup?  For the price, I'm not won.  As far as heat retention, after an hour in my backpack, the coffee is warm, not hot.

Purchased this particular model primarily because it only requires a single 6-pin power connection. Absolutely satisfied with the performance of the card for gaming.
The item makes my phone lock up and unresponsive.  I would not recommend it to anyone.  Please do not buy this product.

There's not a better sunglasses hard case around, as far as I know. Great, rigid case that fits easily into the pockets of my messenger bag or backpack. It also has a high quality, 360 degree swivelling ABS plastic clip for hanging off gear when there's no pocket available. Finally it has a belt loop, or shoulder strap loop (I use it to attach the case to the shoulder strap of my messenger bag) that snaps with a button clasp. The case seals with a nice heavy zipper and as zippers go this one seems to be high quality. Never jams on me, ever. The case is very spacious inside too. Every single one of my glasses fits into it. Aviators, reading glasses, big-frame Oakley glasses, etc.  I'm a big fan of this case. I wish it was a tiny bit less expensive but at the same time, I do not at all regret my purchase. I don't think you would either.

got for my husband, I have the white one, this one had glitch sound.. I've always used professional quality sounding system, (alltime sony professional user) but this one had the lowest quality that I have used so far - ordered again for boss headset

Just got this today and all I can say is WOW this thing ROCKS!! It has a very good sound -- this is not just the cheapest driver they could throw in it. It has a pleasing feel to it. It has a nice Heft, it is much more solid and weighty than I expected. It is all metal and SOLID. It is smaller in diameter than a pop can and less than half as tall. It pairs very quickly and easily as soon as you turn it on. It has buttons to skip to the next track and an aux in jack. It is plenty loud. This single speaker is louder than both my laptop speakers which when I bought it was the highest rated speakers in a laptop for what that is worth. It will fill up a 15 X 15 room no problem and has way more bass than I was expecting for such a small driver. It will put out at full volume with little to no distortion. I can not speak to how long the battery last but I had it on for the whole Chiefs Chargers game and it is still going strong. One thing to think about if you plan on having it unplugged all the time is the built in battery will go bad eventually. I think it should be a good quality battery judging on the rest of the unit but if you have it unplugged all the time you might think about a unit that takes AAA's.

The stylus looks nice and has worked and held up well, but I do have a small complaint/wish: I wish that I could somehow cover both ends to protect the rubber stylus tip. While it has showed no signs of wear, my pens get thrown in my backpack pocket together and often have keys or dirty/dusty things in with them. It took me a while to figure it out, but apparently depending on your particular TV model, the connection sequence really matters.  If you happen to have Vizio and you keep getting "No signal" on your TV, try the following connection sequence:1. Turn on TV and set Input to whatever HDMI port you will be connecting your phone to.2. Plug in Powered micro-USB cable into the female micro-USB socket on the side of the adapter.3. Plug in the other end of the HDMI cable from your TV to the female HDMI socket on the adapter.4. The very last step, after everything is connected, plug in the adapter into micro-USB socket on your phone.The phone should instantly recognize the connection and display "HDMI Video Out Connected" on the notification bar.  Once your phone is connected - you will see your phone's display being duplicated on the TV screen.  You will be able to play games or watch movies streamed to your phone directly from Amazon or other services, if your TV does not have its own internet connection.

If you're debating between the Photon 4G, GS2, EVO 3D, or this Nexus S 4G, there are some important things to consider about the other handsets in that list.  I've lived with all of them, and I would rank them in the following order-1- Samsung Nexus S 4GPROS -Stock 2.3, getting 4.0 any day nowWonderful screenResponsive, always responsiveCONS -Lacks notification lightSignal sometimes finicky (but I've never dropped a call)Battery life is so-so2 - Samsung Galaxy S2PROS -Even more amazing screenLightning fast (most of the time)Best phone camera I've ever seenCONS -TouchWiz UI - almost the worstAwkward shape in hand - all hands are different, but phone is too big/rectangularSometimes buggy with accelorometer and other build features3 - Motorola Photon 4GPROS -Excellent radiosVery good batterySolid buildCons -Mediocre screenOdd shape, objectively uglyLousy cameraMotoBlur - mellower than in the past, but still awful4 - HTC EVO 3DPROS -Solid build qualityPerforms adequatelyCONS -Useless 3D feature, mediocre camera in 2DSense is non-defeatable and pointlessScreen lacks in contrast and brightnessBattery life is awful - I got 4-5 hours with 4G onThat's a rough review, but I would select the Nexus S 4G if I were you - at least until the Galaxy Nexus hits Sprint.

I finally have the startrek PADD, which I've wanted since I was a kid. The design is very slick, and the device is made out of metal and is very well put togeather, I'm not expecting this to break for a long time. The screen is amazing, I didnt see one in real life before I bought it, I love the look of this screen.I started reading the user manual right out of the box and thought I had crashed it, but it turned out that the power ran out on the device since it wasnt charged when it came from the factory. When the power runs out the screen doesent have power to re-ionize the screen, and the page is "stuck". I plugged it back into my computer for about 30 minutes and used the reader for 4 hours with no power issues, then let it charge overnight.Linux compatability: I downloaded an app called libprs500 (google it for instructions on how to download for your build of Linux), it works great. I am able to add/remove books from the device with ease. I found that I just need to navigate to the Audio folder to add mp3's, the reader will build the library/metadata for you when you unplug the device.I've been grabbing out of copyright books from manybooks.net, which allows download of Sony Reader format so I dont need to convert anything. These books look great and are 100% free.The only issue I've seen with this so far was the audio quality of the mp3 playback. I hooked up a set of noise cancelling headphones to it and had some slight feedback when listening. I plugged in my Apple headphones which came with my ipod, and do not have static problems. My noice cancelling headphones do not have static on other devices.

Imagine you and your friends going to a drive in movie. You can enjoy time with your friends knowing there are no lines and no wait to leave, especially once the movie has ended and everyone is trying to leave. A Video Theater Projector doesn't seem to be too common to have around the house, but then you would be unique in having one. Say your friends want to come over again and hang out watch a movie, but it's the same thing every time: get a few snacks, gather around the sofa, and sits. There can also be some minor details such as, it's hot inside the house or it's a boring atmosphere. This doesn't always happen but once you start having movie nights at the same place more than once it may get a little boring.A theater projector can make things new and different, a new experience. For example, if you have a big enough back yard everyone can go outside to watch a movie where it would be nice and fresh. It would also feel like a drive in movie without the wait and with all the type of snacks you want. Owning a theater projector can also come in handy when having certain parties such as weddings, sweet sixteen/quince, and the family gatherings. You can have a slide show going while guests are walking around or waiting for something to happen. It's always nice to have something keeping the guests entertained instead of waiting around criticizing or complaining. It is always nice to look through old family pictures and hear different stories from everyone.Having a theater projector isn't used very much, but if you are someone who is into technology and likes to keep things interesting, it's a great item to buy. The set up in fairly simple, nothing to complicated. The buyer can come up with fun projects to do such as a public event or something personal and as simple as a movie night with friends and family. Remember, not everyone has one, so it would be a great topic to talk about and usually the outcome will be making plans to use it.
