12/18/2012 Update:  About a month ago, I could no longer just plug this into the wall and go away while a battery charged.  There is a short of some kind and I must now fiddle with the unit to make sure it stays connected.  That means I must check it occasionally just in case it stops charging.  Kind of a pain.Original Review: This item arrived a day earlier than predicted by Amazon, so kudos to the seller for prompt shipping.  So far, my only comment is that the charger seems a bit fragile.  The charging pins slide, and the carriage rotates, to accommodate different battery configurations, which is very clever.  However, the charging pins are thin, so you must be careful not to bend them.  It takes a bit of practice and effort to properly connect a battery because you must align the pins to touch + and - poles of the battery. Now, I'll see how the charging goes...7/10 (a few days later): The batteries are great, and the charger charges them in a few hours.  If you have more than one phone that uses this type of flat battery, as I do, this charger is ideal.  Just take care to properly align the pins with the battery's poles.  The USB connector is also very handy for charging another device while the unit charges a battery.  But what is really nifty is the USB input connector that allows plugging a car charger into the unit so that you can charge batteries in the car.  So, whether you are in a room or a car, you can charge a battery and an additional device at the same time.  My previous charger worked only on household current, unless I used an inverter in the car.8/23: I now appreciate the ability of this unit to adapt to other batteries.  It takes a bit of time to set a battery properly, but it's great having only one charger that will charge the batteries of other phones in our family - 5 in all.  Also, it is proving not to be as delicate as I once feared.  All in all, a good buy.

When i first opened the NT120C's package i was impressed with how nice it was packed. This battery has a lot going for it. And recharging the NT120C is a snap, it charges overnight! No more fearing the battery meter on my iPhone as long as I have the NT120C along, I'm always set! The only negative thing I could possibly mention is the user manual. As is the trend with so many products, the user manual for the NT120C is simplistic & mostly graphic images with very few words. All in all though my experience has been positive & I'd definitely recommend this to anyone who needs power on the go!

I just received these matte screen protectors yesterday and applied one to my iPhone 4S.  After seeing a friend of mine who had a Zagg branded matte protector and bragged about not having to wipe off fingerprints, I decided to try one out for myself.  I'm not a fan of Zagg products (had one on a Palm Pre years ago and it turned yellow on me) so I ordered the PhoneDevil version instead since it's the highest rated on Amazon.  After many years of owning smartphones I've become a pro at installing screen protectors, getting this one on with no bubbles was a cakewalk (clear scotch tape is your best friend).  All of the holes lined up well, the cuts seem very clean, no weird edges.As far as the look the matte protector goes there is definitely some "pixellation-like" screen distortion as documented in the product description.  Not that it makes the screen unreadable, but it is more than noticeable if you are used to having a clear protector or none at all.  For me, this is not a bad thing as it is a tradeoff.  Fingerprints do not smudge up my screen anymore and glare from indoor lighting is no longer an issue.  After a few hours of using my iPhone with this protector on, I've gone from a middle-of-the-road reaction to loving it, and here's why : it makes the screen look and feel like electronic paper.  It has a very neat texture and the "graininess" of the matte finish gives the screen a paper-like look.  This probably won't work for everybody, but I find it very enjoyable to use.Overall very happy and pleasantly surprised by this product.

This is my review of the FRIEQ Waterproof Case, at first I didn't want to trust this case with the life of my device as anyone wouldn't; however, for reviewing purposes I gave it a shot. I placed my HTC EVO 3D inside of the case and it fit well, the first thing I noticed is that the device is still fully functional in the case. The screen works as if the phone were outside even when under water. I obviously can't test this 100 feet underwater, so I can't guarantee that claim to anyone; however, I ran it about 4 feet deep under water and it worked perfectly, this is quite impressive. I left it there for about two minutes, I tried the phone again and it still worked flawlessly, screen responsive and everything. The case it's self is nice in design, it comes with a lanyard that you attach to the case. I can highly recommend this product to anyone that needs a water proof case! I rate this product 5 stars out of 5 stars!I hope this review has helped you out!I was provided a sample for testing and evaluation, my review of this product is honest and the words stated above are my own.

There are a lot of wireless bluetooth speakers out there, and I figured they would all be pretty much the same.  But I was very wrong.I already had Urge Basics Soundbrick when I bought this Photive CYREN speaker.  To compare those two:Call quality (microphone) : Urge Soundbrick > Photive.  People complained they couldn't hear me well when I was using Photive speaker's mic.  However no complaints with Urge Soundbrick.Bluetooth connectivity : Urge Soundbrick > Photive.  Urge Soundbrick never had trouble playing music via bluetooth.  However Photive would lose bluetooth connection frequently.Packaging: Photive > Urge Soundbrick.  Photive speaker only wins on packaging.  But what's the use of good packaging when the product sucks?

We have had this a few days now and it is doing exactly what we wanted it to.  It's a place to park the iPad 2 and charge it.  Notable positives are that it is rather stable when pushed from the front (much more prone to tipping forward) and the bottom of the base has a nice rubber "grip" that also helps the stability.  The one downside is that it is very difficult to get into the dock.  It takes some fan-angling to line everything up just right so the connection meets properly.  Now I will say that it is difficult to get the original plug to connect to the iPad to begin with.  It's just really tight and has to be lined up perfectly.  As I recall, we had the same issue with our iPhones initially but they must be warn in now, as they connect right up.  So hopefully after time the same will be the case with the iPad.  Even with the uncooperative connection, we still rate this 4 stars.  It does what we want and is a good value compared with the almost identical Apple brand version for $29.99.

The Mophie Juice Pack Air works well with my iPhone 5.  I bought this and the Maxboost Fusion Detachable External iPhone 5 Battery Case at the same time.  The Maxboost seems to provide slightly more charge to the iPhone and I like its design a bit better than the Mophie - the ease of removing the battery when I don't want the extra weight and thickness is a little easier with the Maxboost.  But both have served me well.

I use these filters all the time.  $9.24 is outrageous.  I just purchased a new box for $1.59 last night from a Grocery Outlet.  My major store sells them for $3.99.  But the filters are wonderful.  I love them.  Been using them for years.  This price is waaaay out of linewith normal store prices.  If you look where you normally shop for groceries you may find a better price than this.  The quality of the product is how I rated this item, the price is the sellers problem.

i'm re-writing this review at the 6 month mark.after 3 months, days before my warranty expired, i i lost volume in one side. returned okay, and got a new pair. now it's 3 months after that, and i have the exact same problem. no or very low volume in one ear. when i talked to sony for the return, they asked me if i wanted to pay $30 to extend the warranty. my answer was no, because if they keep dying, i'm better off getting something else than wasting my time packaging and mailing and being without for weeks.well, they did die 2x, and of course the warranty is expired.these have great sound and ergonomics, and reasonable battery ... but i don't feel so hot about spending $100+ for essentially disposable headphones. 2x w/ the exact same problem, occurring right at the warranty period is more than a coincidence.
A subdued vs gaudy gold glitter Iphone cover; used it a few times and for the price it does the trickand is fashionable.  Have not had problems with glitter flaking off either.

I am VERY pleased with how quickly this battery recharges my iPhone. It's really surprising. It can bring my iPhone from 33% charge to 100% charge in one and a half hours (and only drops about one fourth of it's total charge). I think that's exceptional. It's really light and weighs about as much as my phone (inside it's case). It looks and feels very sturdy and I haven't thought twice about it as I just keep it in my bag all the time.The one thing is that you have to make sure to press it on when you want to charge something - you'll see the little LED come on, which also indicate what charge level is.It's easy to charge and I like that you can plug any USB item into it for charging. A really good product that I recommend.
Purchased as part of a gift, and was very pleasantly surprised at what great quality it turned out to be. I wish they had my kitchen colors, I would purchase them in a heartbeat if they matched!

I received theSony SAL70400G 70-400mm f/4-5.6 G SSM Lens (Black)on the scheduled day, Its quality is undeniable, I own an Alpha 350 which is an APSC camera, this makes this lens a 105-600, And this is where I was surprised, 105 is very narrow. It is also heavy. In low light, I have had some focusing issues, it tends to get lost before finding it. I like shooting at the end of the day when the light is best, this lens requires a tripod or monopod in those circumstances. Using it for what it is intended, it produces very sharp pictures. I recommend it to bird lovers, it is also wonderfull for day events where you are distant to the subject, overall it is a great lens, now I cant wait to get me the 900 body to really show off its capabilities.
Very well made. Colors look great.  Feels comfortable in the hand. Completely covers the phone. The 4 star rating is because removing the stylus requires a bit of maneuvering.

I bought this key rack since I thought it would go well with the color of the floorboards in my apartment and it is a near-perfect match. The construction of the rack is good enough. Although it seems weak when taken out of the packaging, it holds up quite well handling a few keys and couple of envelopes and coupons. I didn't use the grey anchors it came with and just used the screws to mount it on the wall. As long as I'm not stuffing the mail holder with heavy items or use heavy keys, I think I have a product that is nice to look at and performs its job well.A note regarding the key hooks - the rack itself is smallish and hooks don't curve out much - so you wouldn't be able to suspend a large set of keys without them touching the walls.

This is a lovely pillow. The colors are soft and muted, exactly what I wanted. I've used this outside on porch furniture, on my brown couch, and now on my bed. It is easy on the eyes and well made.  I needed a little something to soften the strong colors I normally use. This is a great compromise. A touch of femininity without going over the top.

I got this for my mom and she really liked the only problem was that its so light it gets dirty really easily and she has been using it for a couple days  and there are wrinkles where it bends already. It does grab the phone pretty well for a wallet case and it holds a good amount without the magnet falling off. overall its a good case, not the best but definitely one of the nicer ones.

Had this a few weeks now and it is still working fine. It replaced a pansonic which only lasted for about a dozen recordings, unfortunately it died after the warrenty. Seldom used the panasonic and never put "library" dish in it. The Toshiba makes it so much easier to label segments that I am glad that I had to replace the panasonic. I only miss being able to change the clip index image (and the wasted money for the panasonic).I thought that I would replace the panasonic with JVC or Sony but was surprised with all their negative reviews and the positive reviews for this Toshiba. It is important to read the manual carefully because it is poorly written and those tiny DVD icons are worthless and need to be annotated with a magic marker.Also wonder why equipment designers use no common sense and insist on making equipment with tiny buttons and then making them black on a black panel but then having chrome trim where it is least needed.

They do what they cliam to do, which is being cheap disposable screen protectors that last a few months and need to be replaced.I'm happy with how they worked out. This pack of 5 lasted my sister the life of her phone. And she's rough with her phones.

Isn't it great that you can still buy a case for a phone that's four generations old?  I kept my trusty old iPhone 3GS as an emergency back-up.  Its Otterbox case had stress cracked and so I was thrilled to see that for only a couple bucks you could still get an iPhone 3 case.  I was even more thrilled when I got it.  Absolutely nothing fancy, but reasonable protection, tight fitting and perfect alignment for all the buttons, camera and connector. Things mechanical are not my forte so when I write the Fagor Multi-Cooker is easy to set up and use it must be :) This is a preliminary review as I received the Fagor cooker early this afternoon and I read the book. I then proceeded to clean all the parts and then prepared to cook some cube steaks.The unit is capable of browning, pressure cooking, slow cooking and has a warming cycle. You can brown food and then move onto the selected cycle you want to use. As I was looking for a slow cooker with the ability to brown, and I looked at some very pricey units, I found this cooker at Amazon. The price was right and I thought why not give it a try.This is what I did to prepare my first dish in the Fagor Muli-Cooker:I dusted the cube steaks in some seasoned flour while the oil was heating in the unit. I then added the steaks to the hot oil and browned them using the browning cycle. The steaks turned out nice and brown.I then poured in a cup or so of reduced sodium beef broth (the directions say to use a minimum of 1 cup liquid if I remember right) sprinkled some dried mushrooms over the steaks and placed the cover over the cooker.I then selected the pressure cooker cycle, used high pressure as I live well above sea level, and then set the time. Approximately 30 minutes later the timer beeped and I turned the unit off to allow the steaks a few more minutes cooking time under pressure. Then I turned the knob to steam and the pressure was released.I found some very tender steaks with mushrooms inside the pot. In fact, I ate one of the small steaks for a late lunch. I can personally attest the meat was tender and well cooked.Overall, based on this first test run of the multi cooker I am very impressed with the ease of use. It's almost like magic!Highly recommend.

I simply love my snapwares.. Recently I decided to give my pantry a makeover and wanted everything in airtight containers.. I really love glass but then the idea of one falling down and breaking and cleaning the glass steered me towards plastics.. I was looking for a cheaper solution and visited the dollar stores around but soon I got the idea that the pantry will never look like what I wanted with stuff picked from the dollar stores. I decided on the snapware and ordered 3 of the 23 cup size, 3 of the 17 cup size, 12 of the 10.8 cup size and 12 of the 4.5 cup size.. The footprint of all of them is the same, so they stack up nicely. To open and close the lids is so easy and they are certainly airtight.. In the 23 cup ones I store my flour, bulk rice and pasta.. This holds 5 lbs of flour easily.. So far I really love them and they look great in my small pantry..

After sleeping on the Ultimate Dreams mattress for 5 nights, I can only say WOW...it is a fantastic mattress and both my husband and I are sleeping so much better than we did on our interspring mattress.  We tried the Temperpedic memory foam beds and I liked the Luxe while my husband preferred the Supreme.  I think the mattress is very much like the Supreme...it is much firmer than the Luxe in my opinion, but it's not too firm for me.  I haven't had any problems with rolling off the side, or trouble turning over.  It takes a few minutes before the mattress actually conforms to your body, but even that is not bothersome.  We ordered on a Thurs and received it on Tues the following week.  I was a bit leary buying a mattress without trying it first, especially since it can't be returned, but after doing a lot of reading on Mattress Underground and all the positive reviews on Amazon, I was pretty sure it was the right one for us, and it is.I wouldn't hesitate to buy another product from Dreamfoam.  You'll never match the quality and price from a store.

Very cool looking design I always love fosmon covers so its a no brainer for me.

This appears to be a quality product, but has a couple flaws that may not make it a good choice for you.  First, the packaging is very nice, I find it hard to believe they can afford to provide this package for the price.  Upon putting the screen protector on I had multiple "microbubbles" and streaks.  It looked horrible, but all the reviews, and even Skinomi, indicated that this would improve over the next couple weeks until the screen protector was clear.  Over the next three days the screen did clear up a bit, but before all the microbubbles disappeared my case (a Belkin Grip Candy) managed to push up the edge of the screen protector, ultimately ruining the screen protector.  If I had not had a case, or had a different case that didn't contact the edge of the screen protector, I suspect the screen protector would be clear and functioning great at this point.With my original order, Skinomi provided me with a certificate to get a second screen protector for free.  I submitted the certificate and in about a week I recieved my free screen protector kit.  That night I installed it.  I used much more of their spray than I did the first time, and the screen protector installed much easier and looked much better right from the beginning.  I let my phone sit for about 12 hours over night.  In the morning it looked pretty good, although I still had a few of those microbubbles.  I popped my phone back into it's case, and within a hour my case was once again pushing up the corner of the screen protector.  I thought this might happen, but with the aluminum exterior on the iphone 5 I'm certainly not going to carry my phone without a case.  And since the screen is pretty scratch resistent (and aluminum is not), I figure it's better to have a good case than a good screen protector, at least for the short term.  Thus, the Skinomi screen protector didn't work out for me.I did notice that the screen protector left no residue behind when I removed it.  Also, the screen protector had a softer texture to it than the dry install screen protectors I have used in the past.The screen protector appears like it would work great if you don't use a case, or you have a case that doesn't contact the edge of the screen protector.  If you have a case that will contact the edges of the screen protector (such as the Belkin Grip Candy), this screen protector probably won't last long.  Second, this is the first screen protector I've used that had these microbubbles.  They apparently go away, but that may take a couple days to a couple weeks.  Many other screen protectors are flawless right from the beginning. Still, I don't fault the screen protector, it looks like it'll work great under the right circumstances.

These Gel-filled Pillows are very large and feel very much like traditional pillows. So, if you want a nice large pillow that feels identical to a traditional pillow but stays cooler... I'd say that this pillow was made for you. My girlfriend seems to really like it and it's large enough to double as a body pillow.  However, I've been using memory foam pillows for quite a while now and have gotten used to a slightly more firm pillow. With that said, I feel that these pillows are a bit too soft for my needs. I also would've liked them to include a pillowcase... Even a super cheap one would've been fine. So please keep in mind that you'll have to buy king sized pillow cases as a separate purchase because they are not included. Other than that, these pillows are pretty nice. I would not recommend them for those who prefer medium-firm and firm pillows.

all of that for that price! A bargain! But honestly i have not used them yet, so i dont even know if they work.

This shelving unit is great. I originally bought the Cheetah Mounts, but was disappointed by a large chip in the front finish of the shelf. Also, the Cheetah mount stuck out further from the wall and I was looking for something lower profile. This shelving system is great.

I must say that I have purchased a couple different cases and none of them have impressed me as much as this one.  The actual case was even prettier in person, the picture did it justice but didn't prepare me for how well the case would fit.  It is is snug as a rug and what I love even more is that the volume and power buttons are actually covered, they aren't a cut out.  Since you touch both of those areas so much it is easy for debris to get into those cracks and damage/shorten the life of your phone.  This cover helps you not to do that.  The cut outs are extremely precise and I am just tickled "purple" with my purchase!

This Kindle cover is perfect for travel, protecting my Kindle really well.  It is a quality product.  My husband got a different color cover so he no longer taking by Kindle by mistake.  Highly recommended.
I have not used the scarf yet, but I will. I love these scarves on my dresser. My grandmother always had the prettiest scarves on her dresser, guess I inherited her likeness.

I picked one of these up when my son got his first iPhone. His phone has been dropped, kicked, sat on, you name it, and there's not a mark on the phone anywhere. I recently gave a Galaxy Tab as a gift and given his two little girls who love gadgets, I knew I HAD to get another Otterbox for it to protect it. I now have an Otterbox on all my devices, including two iPhones, an iPad Air, and an iPad mini. I won't use anything else until my kiddos are older and can handle these things more carefully. I still cringe a bit when I see my very expensive and very shiny gadget falling to the floor, but it has yet to fail me. It's been dropped from as high as 4 feet, and though I don't dare do it myself, I've seen video reviews of it being dropped from 3 stories and then being run over by a car with no damage to the phone.Just want to point out, this case is NOT waterproof, but it will protect it from minor spills. My mini has been drenched in apple juice more than once and come out completely dry. The case seems complicated when you first look at it, but it's actually really simple to put on and also to take off. Love the built in screen protector also. Keeps moisture out and no annoying bubbles to deal with! Can't recommend enough for all your gadgets.

Excellent SD card.NO problems using it in the Canon SX50HS (I'm using it just for my camera).This is very fast and it has enough space for my needs.Update:1 year using this SD card in my camera without any issues losing my pictures or videos.I recommend it.
i am obsessed with phone cases and i love this one. it is very durable. fits the iphone4 PERFECT i couldnt ask for a better case!
actually works as advertised with many bluetooth devices. Plug and play with mouse and keyboards and speakers as well. Short range broadcast.  Only for Windows however. not useful for Macs

Good dvd's but not the best... Had a few that have blemishes like they have been in the sun but they where like that right out of the package.. Some have dimples and some discolored on the side where the writing happens. So there where a few coasters.. TDK isn't the quality as it used to be.

I'm not sure what I expected for the cost other than the platform to be bigger.  This is my third attempt at a food scale.  The first was too wobbly. Ok this is the cutest heater ever! next time I need another, I will def get it again. Everyone always says "what is that" they would never believe it's a heater. I like that you can adjust the temperature 65-80, max and low. I would HIGHLY recommend this to anyone. It works great!!!

I used to have literally hundreds of plastic grocery bags in my kitchen, stuffed into an even bigger grocery bag. (I use about a bag a day to collect and dispose of the waste in my cats' litter box.) I have been upgrading my kitchen and apartment, and decided I needed a more streamlined, attractive way to deal with the bags.I was optimistic about this device, but it's not a perfect solution. I didn't count the bags I put in, but it does seem to hold a lot. However, I haven't yet determined the best way to insert the bags so that when I pull one out of the dispenser, I don't end up with several at a time. Also, taking out a bag is not always simple -- sometimes a bag comes out easily, other times I really have to wrestle with it.In addition, my bag holder arrived partially disassembled. There are three main parts to the holder -- the black plastic bottom, the black plastic top piece, and the rounded stainless steel sides. One of the tabs that ensured that the black top was securely installed was not popped in -- consequently, the steel sides were not held in place by the top, so sharp edges were exposed. And they are sharp!Fortunately, I was able to unscrew the black top, reinstall it so all the tabs were in place, and squeeze the metal sides back into place so the edges were covered. However, remedying this was a rather unpleasant experience, although I am pleased that it was remedy-able at all!Overall, a good solution, but not a great one.

this charger is terrific.  it works on my blackberry and my wife's galaxy S4.  the red mood lighting is just plain pretty.  the cool and useful innovation is that when you go to plug it in to your phone, an LED at the mini-USB end lights up your path, making it easy to find the usb port and line up the plug with the socket.  this is a very smart charger....

First of all, I am very careful with my phone.  I always have it in my pocket with the screen facing my leg.  Also, I don't put change or anything of the sort into my pocket.  So I don't really feel that I need the armor that I had with my Otter Box.I like my Otter Box and for ultimate protection it is great.  However, it is rather bulky and you have to move some protectors to charge the phone or to silence it.  I like the screen protector that comes with the Otter Box (one of the best in my opinion) but Gorilla glass is hard so I don't use a screen protector.This case is barely lager than the iPhone itself.  It has a nice cushioned and grippy feel.  I have no problems pushing or using any of the buttons on the phone.  To me, Apple spent a ton of money sweating the small details of how the iPhone feels so I enjoy that with this case it feels like an iPhone.Overall, if you bang the crap out of your phone, this is not really the case you want.  However, if you take care of your phone but want a modicum of protection against accidents then you will be very happy with this case.  I love the feel of the iPhone and in this case it feels like that is all that I am holding.I was provided a sample product for a fair and honest review.

I have only owned one juicer in the past before I ordered this one. I must say, I am pretty impressed. The price is good. It preforms well. It takes up very little space. And clean up is easy. I have only used it a few times. It has breezed through everything IO've tried so far: Whole carrots, whole apples (one at a time), whole stalks of celery, pineapple, and broccoli stalk.  I love it! It makes juicing so easy. I'd absolutely purchase it as a gift, and recommend it to others.I looked around for a while to find what would be best for me. I am pleased with my choice. I only gave it 4 stars because I haven't tried things like ginger,  or beets yet. I'm shire it will juice them easily though.

The lemongrass/sherbert is such a FUN color. I was worried it would be too bright but it is accurately represented on the website. It is really a beautiful and fun combo. It fits snugly and the side buttons are a breeze to use (not hard to push like some covers can be)!The back may scratch a little... but it is a shiny surface that is constantly being slid over tables etc... what do people expect? The point of a cover is for it to get scratched... not your phone!! And honestly, it is not horrible... at least I don't really notice it on the lemongrass color. Maybe black would show scratches more?I do think it is a bit spendy at $30... but I like it better than the Otterbox Commuter I tried. It is slimmer and lighter and I don't feel like I am losing any protection... just weight/bulk. I returned my Otterbox. I plan on buying a second one of these in White/Grey.

I purchased this to go with the Kindle I purchased my husband and we are both thoroughly happy with the item.  It uses the battery from the Kindle for power and surprisingly it doesn't drain the unit as much as I thought it would.  He reads every night for approx 30-45 minutes using the light, but having the wifi off, and he charges the unit about every 9-10 days.  Actually he still has about 25% of the battery still available but he doesn't drain it all the way before charging.  He only turns the wifi on for a short time each day just to update the Kindle then he turns off the wifi, doesn't make sense to keep on 24/7 since that also uses battery life.Being a former Nook user, I am very impressed with the Kindle e-ink readers and plan on purchasing another one for a family member that is also a Nook user.  This person saw our Kindles and was overjoyed with the battery life as well as the convenient size.  Our family reads a lot and prefers e-ink readers, we just wish we really checked out the Kindle before purchasing the other brand.

This phone case is actually pretty neat. I didnt want big and bulky but I didnt want all rubber to where it'd be hard to get in and outta my pocket. The white is a hard plastic and the blue is a very soft rubber that doesnt strech like the otter box rubbers do.

I love this little camera! I shoot a Canon 60D with an assortment of lenses and have also been using my iPhone 5 camera (8MP) a lot in last year, but I have been thinking of purchasing a high end compact (P&S;) camera that had a hot shoe and shot RAW. However, the $500+ price tag on those I was considering was a turn off. Having heard so much about the micro 4/3 mirrorless cameras, I decided to investigate them. When I saw I could get this Olympus PEN E-PL3 (silver) for only $259 my decision was made! I got what I had been looking plus more: interchangeable lens camera with incredible customization possibilities along with larger sensor (=better resolution) than a P&S;, flash hot shoe, RAW format capability, small and light camera yet easy to handle.I was a bit concerned that I was buying a camera model that was already two years old with two newer models of this line already out, but I need not have worried. Though - with experience so far - images at ISO 1600 and above are not great (but I'm still learning how to get the best out of this camera!), I am otherwise very happy with image quality and sharpness. And, unfortunately, I am already - after owning the camera only one week! - lusting after other lenses for it! I am, however, happy with the range of views the "kit" 14-42mm lens offers me. I am also very pleased to see that I can use my old Canon lenses with my new PL3 as well. Along with the camera I purchased an inexpensive adapter that allows me to mount the 50mm, f/1.8 lens (FD) from my old (1972) Canon FTb camera, as well as any other FD lenses I can find to purchase cheaply. With that success, I decided to go ahead and order a more expensive ($115) adapter so I could also use the small collection of Canon EF lenses that I currently use with my Canon 60D. This Roku is a great streaming player.  The UI is responsive, the picture is beautiful, and if you're just streaming, it is almost perfect.  But there are two things that are big drawbacks:- It does not do YouTube.  At all.  You can download other channels that let you get to YouTube videos, but it is very slow to the point of being unusable.  So if you want YouTube streaming, this is not the box for you.- It does not stream content from your home computer.  To do that, you must download and install a third party application on your computer and on the Roku.  I decided to buy a USB external disk (no USB 3.0 support, FYI), and use that instead, and it works fine, but I have to disconnect it periodically so I can add content.

This basically worked as advertised for me right out of the box. I have a Toshiba R705 laptop that has the Intel Wireless Display capability built in.I connected the adapter to TV and power using provided connectors. Then I fired up the Intel Wireless Display software, typed in the 4 digit code displayed on the TV and then the connection was rock solid.Pros -Connection never failedAudio and Video were extremely good.Streaming movies from netflix was excellent, no different than streaming from my LG blu-ray playerYoutube etc etc etc, everything was excellent.Google Web albums looks greatConsIf you fail to break the wireless connection between the adapter and laptop before closing the laptop, the laptop basically has to be "hard" rebooted. (i.e.disconnect from power and remove battery) since the LCD screen refuses to function after waking up.The screen has a little bit of space (half an inch all around) around the edges where the wireless display doesn't put anything (even when you set the laptop resolution to match the TV resolution). My TV has a adjustment using which I can fill it up,  but other TV's may not have such adjustments and it is a little bit of a pain.UPDATE: Found a adjustment in the Intel software that lets me increase the size to fill the screen. Extremely happy with the product now!Overall, I am happy I bought this product. I'm able to do a lot of stuff like stream foriegn TV channels etc etc that I could not do before. It's a bit pricey for 99 bucks, but doubtless there will be price drops.

I did not purchase this item from the amazon website; I bought it at a local shopping center. They are by far the worst sheets I've ever bought. I was very excited because I had just got a new bed a couple days ago and bought these for it. I was immediately disappointed when I opened them up. They are very stiff and rough, almost like laying on a low grade sandpaper. I expected them to get softer when I put them through that initial wash but apparently it didn't help. I'm now looking into new sheets after only a few days of irritated skin. Also, the fitted sheet seems to be coming apart at the seams a little and it's brand new, only went through one wash so far. There are definitely better quality sheets out there worth paying this price for and I'm looking for them now.

For a true 1080p LCD monitor, by Sony, I was terribly disappointed in this television, so much so that I returned it within 24 hours.  I upgraded from a Sony Grand Wega 720p LCD projection set to this, and out of the box the first thing I noticed was how red everything looked.  I played with the tint, adjusted the individual color levels and enabled and tweaked every conceivable "gimmick" feature on the set, but just could not arrive at a setting that would show accurate flesh tones without distorting some other aspect of the display.  There was nothing defective with the set-- this is a well-known weakness with Sony televisions and in this particular model it was just too much for me to bear.I was also disappointed that the set yielded such poor contrast.  I'm not talking about the difference between black levels and white levels, but rather the more subtle variations on people's faces, clothing and other textures.  Again, I adjusted the sharpness, picture and brightness, and all the other "gimmick" features of the set (detail and edge enhancement) and was only able to make minimal improvement in this area.After having this set in my home for barely 24 hours, I packed it up, took it back to the retailer and exchanged for a 50" 1080p Panasonic plasma set.  I could not be happier now.  Sony lost a customer here.

This case feels a little plastic but probably does the job for someone who has their iPhone working in areas that need more protection, wet hands, wet areas, sand, dirt, etc. The protective area film is well fit and does not impair sensitivity of touch. I would recommend it for anyone who really has to have a waterproof case. However, I was not willing to try the waterproof feature...You need to carefully fit the iPhone in the case and snap down the cover well as designed to ensure that the waterproof works. It has a screw in headphone plug which is tight and there was an extra plug included in my case as well as an extension cord for the headphone slot. It's hard to remember to have the extension for the headphone unless you leave it on the headphones you use (plug it into the headphones) but that has a small plastic piece that has a screw plug for the headphone so you don't have to remember to bring that along when you need to plug the phone to make it waterproof.I had the Griffin Survivor for my iPhone 4s and it was easier to put on but much more bulky and not waterproof (but water resistant). The LifeProof case is slim and makes the iPhone a little wider and longer, not much. The Survivor came with a holster clip which was handy as it is unlikely with the bulk, that you were going to put this phone in a pocket.For a comparative case, try the Incipio Atlas. It just came out and is rated by reviewers on the Web as better. A little pricer, $100, but it has a guarantee for one year (non-transferable) that you can fill out via an app. The Atlas gives LifeProof a serious run for its money and Incipio makes other types of well-made and thin but protective cases too for those times you do not need this type of protection.

I use a kitchen scale extensively because I bake bread by weight. The scale I've been using works well but it's larger and requires an electrical outlet and takes up space on the shelf I store it on when not in use. This scale is light years better for my purposes!It's a flat scale that measures 9 1/2" x 5 3/4". It has a large, lighted, digital read out and smooth, touch pad type buttons that won't catch food or gunk under them. It weighs in ounces, grams, milliliters and fluid ounces with an 11.4 lb. maximum weight capacity. It has a tare feature and even a timer! It takes 3 AAA batteries (included) and features auto-off after 60 seconds to conserve battery life.The fact that it weighs in ounces and grams is so helpful for people who bake. Different recipes are listed in different weight measurement conventions so this scale saves me from having to pull out a calculator to convert from one to the other. Yeah! One less thing I need in my kitchen.It even features a hole for hanging this scale out of the way when not in use. I love this! I have several rails fitted with S-hooks in my kitchen and this scale hangs out of the way but readily accessible so I can still use the timer when it's hanging. Fabulous for me because my range is a commercial style and doesn't have a timer.The top of the scale is stainless steel that has been treated to reduce fingerprints. The bottom of the scale is black plastic and you can access the battery chamber through the bottom. It's fairly lightweight and probably wouldn't withstand a lot of dropping but really, any sensitive scale shouldn't be dropped and then expected to be accurate.It comes very well packaged and protected with plenty of packing cardboard. The small included instruction manual is well written and concise and describes all operations, trouble shooting and error codes.The scale seems to be extremely accurate and as a result, quite sensitive. The manual suggests several times to be sure and use it on a stable, vibration-free surface and even suggests placing it out of drafts in the corner of the room! That's probably overkill for my bread making needs but it's nice to know the weights will be accurate.If you need a kitchen scale, check this one out because the flexibility of being able to hang it out of the way makes it an excellent choice for a lot of kitchens that are short on space.NOTE: I was provided a sample for reviewing purposes but a positive review was neither promised or implied.

This motherboard really is quiet impressive considering how much it cost, it has a turn of features and can even support the new fx am3+ cpu's but the only con to that is that u need to use a am3 cpu to update the bios before you can put and am3+ cpu in it. The reasons I used to choose the iPad mini. I put my specific needs above buying the biggest and the brightest. The following are what I thought was important for me, it may not be for you.As much as I wanted the retina display, the larger sized iPad was simply too big for me to regularly take outside the house - I made that mistake with buying a too big, too heavy laptop once. But the mini, at 7inches, is big enough to watch movies, read books and magazines on my couch and bed with ease. Those who think a smartphone or ipod does the same thing, are mistaken, even at 7inch, a tablet makes the world of difference - I typed this review on my iPad, I would still be typing if I had used my phone.  If you have never experienced the quality of the retina display, you probably will not miss it here. The thing is solidly build as you would expect from an apple product; the speakers do pack a punch, you can literally feel the air coming out.As for choosing apple over android or windows 8. The need for the use of flash (a deal-breaker for me when the first iPad came out) has lessened substantially, as HTML 5 is now the go to standard for web pages.  Even though I'm a PC user,  I am familiar with apps via my iPod touch. The amount and more importantly, the quality of apps in the apple eco system are far superior to the ones on android. although the novelty has somewhat worn off on the appeal of my impulse buying of apps, it's still a major reason for buying a tablet. Take for example - my optimum cable iOS app, it is so far superior to its android cousin, it's hard to believe that it was written for the same product. Based on my experiences with dubious apps in the (for better or worse) largely unsupervised google play store, via my Samsung Galaxy, there is an advantage to being part of the paternal Apple market. Although, I expect this to change dramatically, the next couple of years, Windows isn't even on the same planet, in terms of app selection.  If you need to multitask, are price conscious, or simply despise Apple, the Android based kindle fire HD or nexus, with their better screen quality, may be for you.I bought the 16 gig version, using the extra $$ to buy 4G capability instead, to take advantage of the growing options of cloud computing when I travel. If you have kids and travel a lot with and without them, or for business, you should seriously consider the 4G option, as ubiquitous, free, reliable Wifi, like flying cars, has yet to be a reality. If you get the wifi only version, don't have an iPod, but still can't live without your stored media, you probably should consider 32 gigs minimum, especially if you don't get the 4G. The other issue of Verizon CDMA vs AT&T;'s GSM network, is a whole other story. One slight peeve. The "lighting fast" connector looks nicer than the older one but it's clearly a money making device (apple could have finally accepted the virtually USB standard but why give your loyal customers the option of using a ubiquitous $10.00 cable, when you can charge triple times that, if you need a replacement) Mine still does not charge my iPad, when connected to my "older" (30 month old computer,) I  have to do so from a wall outlet.Long story short, I've had mine for a few days, with no regrets. Yes, the retina display will be tempting but is not worth waiting months for on the mini. Life is short. You can always trade up next year.

For years, I have used Krups 203-70 Fast Touch coffee grinder, white. coffee grinder to grind Indian spices. How finely ground spices are depended on how long you grind. So you never really get perfect grind despite all the experimentation. Black pepper and Cardamoms  needs a coarse grind, Coriander semi-coarse and Cumin really fine etc. etc.With its 14 grind settings, Bodum Grinder is perfect for grinding Indian (and perhaps others) spices. Another great features is much less spill and mess with its Borosilicate glass catcher. Noise is not bad, definitely a lot less than my earlier Krups grinder. Having two buttons to turn in on and off is also a nice feature.So why am holding one star back? Waiting 5 minutes after 20 second grind is annoying - really annoying when you have lot of spices to grind. And I have not used it long enough to assess longevity. in the past I have had a bad experience with Black & Decker grinder that that died after 6 sessions of grinding.With my short experience with this Bodum grinder, I think it is perfect for grinding spices.

I used to have my computer hooked up to a really nice sound system.  It required me to power on a separate amp and included these super nice "studio" monitors.  You know what?....these work BETTER (for the computer and my requirements)!  I watch a LOT of movies on my computer and these speakers never fail to get the sound quality that I'm looking for.  The sub that is included powers the system and sounds just as good as any expensive one you would think to hook up.  Standard 1/8 inch connection to your "speaker out" or "headphones" jacks.  Includes a separate volume knob with an additional "headphones" jack in case you need it.  Would these replace my living room sound?  No, but they do the job they are built for an I can't imagine a product that could do it better for the price.

Looked at many scales and at Food Network's Alton Brown's recommendation tried to find a scale to weigh up to 11 pounds.  Did not like those that did for various reasons, mostly large size.  The Polder weighs up to 6.5 lb. and has proven to be just fine.  My main purposes are to weigh flour and other dry ingredients for baking, and for smoothies.  My glass blender jar is 3.6 lb and the 6 smoothie ingredients, at 4 oz. each, are another 1.5 lb so well under the max device capacity.  Scale is easy and quick to tare as each ingredient is added.  Only concern is that when adding liquid ingredients, there is sometimes a delay for the added weight to actually change the display, so add slowly or stop for a second or two until display catches up, otherwise you could easily add too much.  As other reviewers mentioned this scale is compact, absurdly simple to use, very easy to clean, works well.

I wasn't specifically looking for a 'solar' keyboard, but this works great.The design/feel is about the same as typing on recent Apple keyboards where the keys are like 'chiclet' candies.It's very comfortable and easy to use. I was looking for an ergonomic replacement for my aging/sticky Microsoft wireless ergonomic keyboard... I was sick of changing out rechargeable batteries all the time.This uses a VERY tiny USB adapter which allows other wireless logitech devices to connect to 1 adapter.PROS:-very comfortable / fast typing / very responsive-not overly 'fancy' with all kinds of volume/media controls... although it does have the capability for some functionality such as music, shortcuts, media controls, calculator, etc...-VERY thin (feels like typing on a thin laptop)-you don't "need" to install any software... so it should work on Linux for basic functionalityCONS:-not 'ergonomic' / wave-shaped-shiny black surface might attrack some fingerprints, but it's not too bad as it seems-incline 'feet' are a little thin, not very stable (but I don't use them)SOLAR:-I was worried the solar panels might not get enough light but I've never had the built-in batteries drop below 100% (I'm within a few feet of a window-overhead room lights are more than sufficient to charge solar panels-cool little 'Solar App' shows if you're getting enough light at that particular time
These screen protectors (regardless of who makes them) are a drag to put on without any problems.  Good for the price :)

Recently picked this up for $11.45 (a year after it had come out), and for the price it's a great way to spend $11. This strong, durable (I think!) thermos bottle comes in shiny black...very sharp looking. The bottle is 10 inches long and 4 inches wide. The  opening at the top is 2 inches wide, allowing room for ice cubes and making cleaning relatively easy.  The attached instructions say that although hand washing is preferable, the thermos is dishwasher safe (top shelf recommended.) The  bottle is mainly stainless steel, and though it looks and feels very substantial, it is relatively light. I tried to get it to leak, but was not successful. As the company claims, the outside does not get hot or moist when the thermos is being used.One drinks through a hole in the plastic cover. This is a little bit awkward while driving, but doable. The bottle just fits in the wide cup holder of my car. The top of the bottle unscrews with a little bit of effort, but that probably makes the whole thing more leakproof. The only downside to this kind of top is that it makes sharing whatever is in the bottle unlikely.To investigate the claim that it keeps things cold for 24 hours, I filled the bottle with ice and water, and 27 hours later the ice was almost gone, but the water was definitely still  cold. The bottle also kept liquid very hot for 18 full hours, even though I did not prep it as recommended.This company makes really good products and has for many, many years. I have an old (smaller) thermos I bought years ago that is still in excellent condition, but the opening is very narrow,  which makes it hard to clean the inside. This new thermos is just great in every way, and will most likely last for a long time. Highly recommended.
Much less expensive than the next model up, yet provides all of the uses.  A handsome appliance that looks good sitting on the counter

This review is written from the perspective of someone who has owned a Harmony remote for years. If you read other reviews and think to yourself, "Yeah, but that guy probably didn't read the instructions or didn't understand them," then read this review. I assure you, I know EXACTLY what I'm doing with Harmony remotes. The Link is just a (currently) substandard product.I loved my old Harmony remote and over the years I'd learned to configure just about every nit-picky thing that could be configured. Last month it died and I was forced back into the market.  I saw the Harmony Link and was intrigued.  The four of us in the family have a total of 6 iOS devices, so I figured what the heck?I read the other reviews of the Link before I bought it and was skeptical given the low score. Still, Harmony remotes do take a bit of tinkering to get them to work spot-on, so I figured it was possible that the low scores were from people who had unrealistic expectations or who, for whatever reason, weren't able to correctly perform the configuration. I was wrong...  Again, as an experienced Harmony remote user, I know that I'm looking at maybe 1 to 2 hours of configuration to get everything working PERFECTLY ... latency times, order of IR transmission, configuration of custom buttons, learning IR commands, etc.Well, after horsing around with the Harmony Link for about three nights now, it's going back.  Here's the thing:  Many of the features that make Harmony remotes absolutely awesome are simply missing from the Link.Want to add custom buttons to the remote app?  Not an option. Want to fiddle with wake-up times and inter-signal times?  Nope, can't do that either. In essence, if the Harmony Link doesn't set itself up properly once you've told it what devices you have, you're out of luck.  And that's just a few of the missing features that Harmony remotes have had for years. That's not even getting into the features that ARE there, but just don't work. For example, the playback buttons for my Blu-ray and DVD players are grayed out. Can't play, Fast Forward, Rewind, Pause, anything. That's just a sample.Here's my advice to you if you want to try the Harmony Link. Buy it from a place where you know you can return it if you have to. Take it home, set it up and configure it. If it works out-of-the-box, great. (Of course, if your system is simple enough that it works out of the box, then chances are you don't need the Link in the first place. Maybe a cheap $15 universal remote is all you need.) If the Link doesn't work right away, don't spend more than 2 hours horsing around with it. If you haven't gotten it by then, chances are what you need is simply a missing feature. Return it and buy a (normal) Harmony remote. It's a couple of years old, but theLogitech Harmony 670 Universal Remoteis a rock-solid product and you can get it new for the same price as the Harmony Link and refurbished for less.The Link really has the feel of a beta release. I can see the potential here. If only they'd make it work at least as well as what their 5-year old remotes did, it'd be a good investment. The hardware is solid and the IR blaster can control things just about anywhere in the room. The software is just half-there. For now, it's going back.I've since purchased aLogitech Harmony 670 Universal Remote.  Two hours later, ... everything works to perfection.
The quality just isn't here. These looks and feel cheap! I kept them but wouldn't recommend or buy them again!

After receiving the KT-HDP1 for Christmas and realizing it didn't come with a car installation kit, I immediately found this product.  It does exactly as advertised, and it's really easy to install.  The black box works as an antenna pass-through, where you plug your car's antenna to the black box and then the additional antenna wire that comes out of the black box gets plugged in to your existing head unit.  The other cables are for hooking directly up to the KT-HDP1, and then of course there's the mounting piece for the dash and cable sleeve to keep things looking clean and organized.  The only problem I had at all was with the mounting piece; it doesn't swivel a whole lot (maybe 60 degrees) and as such it doesn't fit quite right on my dash.  Of course that's a per-car thing, but it still works fine.  I would wholly recommend this over the other installation kit that's available, as this one uses your car's antenna rather than antenna strips, and those usually don't have that good of reception.

This thing really works.  I was amazed actually at how many channels it picked up and how easy it was.  It comes flat in the box and you assemble the 2 pieces together along with a few screws.  Took like 2 minutes.  Then you connect the coaxial cable to the back of your tv, and depending on your tv, scan for channels, sit and wait a few minutes and VOILA!!!, you got HD local channels!I bought this for my parents who live in New Jersey, and they picked up most of the local channels with no problems.  They didn't even have to mount it to the wall or place it outside.  It just lays flat underneath the TV on the tv stand.  The only channel they didn't get was ABC, but I am sure with a little adjustment, they will be able to.  All other channels that we expected came in fine.I highly recommend this product.  If you have somewhere to mount this to outside or in the attic, you should.  It works great but it's not pretty to look at.  You don't want this hanging on your wall in your living room.

After ordering this player, we had great doubts that this unit was going to satisfy us.  We developed second thoughts.  This Sony didn't provide streamed video.  We became so convinced that video from the internet was so important, we contacted Amazon to get an RMA number to return it so we could buy the Panasonic BD60.  Luckily, we decided to watch both a Blu-ray disc and a 480P DVD to see video quality differences.  We were simply amazed and not just the video but with the audio as well.  We then started to consider the worth of video streaming.  The cost to pay a monthly fee for membership.  Having  a monthly fee regardless of the fact that there were no decent movies during those thirty days.  Then, we found that the number of Blu-ray DVD's for rent was limited.  Heck,  it is just as cost effective to occasionally rent that good movie for Blu-ray viewing.  An exceptional movie (such as The Lord of the Rings and/or Planet Earth) would be purchased.  No, we weren't going to let features determine our Blu-ray purchase.  We choose quality.  And, quality is something that we feel this inexpensive player has.  We've destroyed the RMA return number and have decided to keep the Sony.  We feel very comfortable in recommending it.  If you want a feature laden unit, look elsewhere but if quality is your thing, consider the Sony.

I own a bunch of the Lock & Lock products, and they are the best containers made on planet Earth! I'm sorry the other reviewer got one with a broken clasp/latch, but mistakes happen. (He should just return it and get another one.) My wife & I use these all the time. They are the only containers you can rely on to let you get salad with dressing on it to work without spilling the dressing out of a small leak onto your clothes or books or whatever else you may carry to work with you in a bag.  (We have tried a large number of other brands.)We once took our special raspberry dressing to Colorado with us and when we opened it, it sprayed us with dressing! That was good! Why? Because it really proved how well they work. We had placed the dressing in it at around 800 feet above sea level, when we opened it we were about 4,000 feet above sea level, so the pressure difference (less at high altitude) made the contents try to equalize pressure and expand rapidly when we opened it! It gave us quite a surprise, but really proved how great these containers are. Buy them. I'm actually on Amazon today to buy a bunch more of them as they are so good we are using them for everything now.

This is my first GPS.  I decided I wanted a good one and not the cheapest model out there.  I chose the 1370 because it had a lot of cool features.  However in everyday use I'm not very impressed with it.1. I live in Texas and we have frontage roads.  These are roads that run parallel to Interstates and other highways.  The GPS can't seem to detect if I'm on the frontage road or the highway proper, so more times than not as I'm driving along the Interstate and the GPS tells me repeatedly to enter the highway at the next ramp.  In other words it seems to think I'm on the frontage road even though I'm in the left lane of the highway at least 150 yards from the frontage road.  This gets rather annoying to hear mile after mile.2. The battery life is HORRIBLE.  In the car this doesn't matter since I'm plugged into the 12 VDC.  But I bought this GPS to take advantage of the CityXplorer maps.  As a pedestrian I don't have a power source and I only get about 30 minutes of life out of a fully charged battery.  My cell phone lasts all day.3. The traffic updates are pointless.  It does detect traffic tie-ups, but the suggested new route always involves exiting the highway only to get right back on the highway.  This doesn't get me past the jam up but rather delays me even more.  I was hoping it would go find an alternate route on back roads, but it doesn't.4. Some of the roads around Austin are misidentified.  For instance it refers to RM-620 as FM-620.  Okay, that's not a big deal, but what is a big deal is it calls this road "Research Blvd".  That's completely and totally wrong.  US-183 is Research Blvd and it crosses 620.  As a local I know better, but this would really confuse someone not familiar with Austin.I am going to keep with GPS, but only because I don't want to hassle with returning it.  In hindsight I wish I had bought a less expensive GPS.

This case appeared to be the case of my dreams. Polycarbonate full body with a Smart Cover for 13? SoldIt's not that this case is bad, I like all the openings, there was just some stuff that could've made it perfect. First of all, the screen smart cover doesn't have a microfiber back to it. I'd imagine its fairly cheap, albeit not as trivial to implement. Edit: Actually, after having it for a while, I believe microfiber is ESSENTIAL. Which is weird, other Khomo items have the microfiber back, how come this is advertised the same as "apple's smartcover" but this doesn't? If you read other reviews, you would realize Khomo's "covers" have a problem where the middle sticks up, that's because the entire cover really wants to stick up but the edges are magnetized so it stays down, leaving a bump in the middle. Now with a microfiber back, the cover will essentially become lighter and I would assume the fiber will create more flexibility and friction with the screen. All conjecture, but the lack of microfiber is definitely disappointing.Second disappointment was That I assumed the polycarbonate back covers the ipad mini all the way around. Instead it doesn't cover the left side fully, pretty much where the cover folds over. To me this is annoying because when I hold the case, my left hand feel the sharp chamfered metal while my right hand feels smooth polycarbonate.Thirdly, the smartcover itself is flimsy and, I can't prove it yet, I doubt it'll survive the test of time. I wondered why apple chose just three halves, well having four makes a smaller triangle that's much less stable.All in all, fast ship yay. Good protection when the case is closed. Just ok product all around.

A well-constructed, reasonably priced light modifier.  Being able to use it in either bounce or shoot-through configuration makes it very versatile.  I am a little worried about taking it outside, for fear of strong winds, but it should be fine if you use sandbags on your light stand.  The build quality is quite good and should hold up to reasonable use.The light quality produced by this umbrella is soft, due to it's large size, and looks quite nice.  At full spread in bounce mode, it spreads the light out quite a bit.  For more controlled and directional lighting, you can partially collapse the umbrella over the flash, producing a light similar to a small softbox.  In shoot through mode, the light even softer and it can vbe moved much closer to the subject.I highly recommend it!

Initially was in doubt about the mixed comments in the reviews. When received package was happy, got it shipped in 3 days, came in a blister pack (See photo), the quality of the back cover is nice, not flimsy as mentioned by some reviewers.Grood:1)Nice sturdy back cover does not feel cheap2) NFC works3) Cheaper compared to Gorilla Gadget and HyperionBad:1)Battery performance degraded drastically within a week, initially with a full charge I could get more than a day, with 4 hrs talktime and 5 hours screen time(unbelievable see photos),but now it is reduced to just about a day (7:30 a.m to 5 p.m with 1 hr talk and 3 hrs of display time.2) None of the extended cases would work but the back is groved to make the flash work but the TPU cases have not adapted to the new design yet.3) Had to let go of the otterbox case and other cases and with this battery the phone gets heavy and the screen will crack if you drop it.Conclusion: Better than the stock battery, as of the 2nd week of usage, can't say where it will stand with few more days of usage. I would not recommend as the battery performance is dropping. I have recycled the battery 3 times before taking the screenshots and I charge it properly with the original 1.5 A Samsung charger that came with the phone and when fully charged take it out from charger.Well I got the white one for $16.99 but they have increased the price now.Updated review:Anker Customer Service contacted me and send me a new battery with 1 day shipping for free. The new battery is still holding up quite well. I never expected such a good customer service. They do stand behind the warranty and are very prompt. As a high school band director I spend much of my time in the fall outside in the football stadium, running back and forth between the stands and the field. My iPad has become my primary teaching device.  It carries everything I need.  Music, drill, metronome, video camera, gradebook, etc.  But even though I am a type A person who takes very good care of my devices, I still worry about accidents happening in this very mobile teaching lifestyle. So I am always on the look out for a good case that will help protect my most important of teaching tools, but still allow me flexibility for other uses. I've tried the Otterbox case but it is very bulky. I have used snap on back covers but they don't play well with my Logitech Ultrathin Keyboard. Skins provide scratch protection but nothing substantive. What to do?!The Grabbit case by New Trent was an intriguing choice so I decided to give it a try. It is a very affordable option, about the same price of many of the snap on rear covers. When it arrived it comes out of the box looking pretty attractive (the leather strap especially). The case comes with the back cover, the leather strap, the front cover and a white snap on stand that has the ability to also act as wall hanger. So how does all this work on the field?The Grabbit fits my iPad 2 great.  It snaps in very easily by attaching to the four corners of the iPad, leaving the sides of the iPad open. What I like about this set up is that it still allows for use of the Apple Smart Cover, though I have found that I don't use the combination as much as I thought I would. It also lets me take the case out very easily to use with other peripherals such as my Logitech keyboard. The case has the appropriate cut outs for the speaker, camera, volume buttons, etc and are easy to access.The leather handle feels good and really does class up the unit, which is hard to do with any plastic cover. I am curious if the leather will end up stretching over time. The strap is attached to the back cover by a very simple process of threading through the back, and it also adjustable. The center of the back cover rotates 360 degrees so that while holding the iPad you can create the most comfortable viewing angle. I have read other reviews that said the back of the cover creates circular scratches but what I found was that those "scratches" clean off and aren't actually scratches at all. I suspect that it is dirt that gets in from the sides being open as well as the opening for the attachable stand.The front cover snaps on very securely, almost too much. Many times when I am trying to take the front cover off, I end up taking it out of the back cover by mistake. But I do like how the unit feels with both covers attached. A real sense of a secure and protective cover combination. I know some have complained that there isn't a place to store the front cover. And while that is true, I am not sure that there is a solution. It can't fold back because of the leather strap. I just don't use the front cover when I am actively using the iPad. I think of the front cover being available for protection when I am throwing it in my backpack or walking from the school to the stadium. And again, the back cover works with the Apple Smart Cover, which is a bit easier to fold up and store. Overall, I think the front cover does what it is suppose to and does it well.The stand/wall hanging mount snaps into the back center and allows you to set the iPad up for viewing movies or for use with a external keyboard, but only in portrait. The stand snaps in very securely and then rotates in the same process that the strap does.  And yes, you can leave the strap on when using the stand. The wall mount feature is a neat idea and I want to like it, but I just can't figure out when I would realistically utilize it in that fashion. I will keep trying though! The stand pops off but it does take a bit of effort, which probably isn't a bad thing. At first it did feel that I was going to break the stand when trying to remove it but over time I got use to it.Overall I would give this product a rating of 4+ out of 5.  It is well thought out and constructed.  The thing that I love the most, the way the iPad attaches to the back cover, also gives me hesitation that it could pop out a bit too easily. I have been thinking about what would make me feel more secure, perhaps a thicker plastic. But that would add weight. Securing beyond the four corners would do the job but then the iPad could very well be too hard to remove. Ultimately I think I should just realize that while the case is great for walking around during a presentation, it isn't to be hurled around like Captain America's shield.  It just feels like it.

These are really good vacuums for a good price.  They have great suction.  Being a bagged type, they DO lose suction as the bag fills up but it has more suction to start with than many of the newer "no suction loss" models so it doesn't matter unless you let the bag get completely stuffed.  Change the bag before it gets packed and it's no problem.Bagless sounds cool but they are a dusty mess to empty.  Changing bags is less of a mess, especially if you change it when it's not stuffed to the gills.  Bags are not expensive.Negative reviews cite the heavy weight of this unit.  It is heavy at nearly 30 pounds, mostly because of the self-propelled mechanism and a huge motor that provides that great suction.  You have to consider your situation.  Don't buy this if you have to lug it up stairs.  However, if you have a thick nap carpet, and will be using this on a single story, self-propelled makes vacuuming literally effortless.  If you have a low nap carpet or berber, tile or hardwood, you don't need self-propelled and can get a much lighter model. It's not fair to downrate the product because it's heavy - you have to buy the right vacuum for your home.The embedded dirt finder sounds like a gimmick but it works great.  You will be surprised at how much extra dirt accumulates in the traffic lanes of your carpet.  With this red-light/green-light system you'll do a better job.I had one of these for 13 years, and really beat it up using it for everything including remodeling debris.  No problems, still runs, and still has the original belt.  I got another simply because the old one looked like it had been through a war.  I imagine like any other product there are some defective ones.  Probably also true that, like many internet reviews,  some of the negative reviews are by folks that did some really stupid things with their vacuum.  These look a but old-school but if you need a self-propelled with great performance, good price, like bagged, and don't have to carry it up stairs then this is a good choice.Get the extra length hose.  It's really necessary to give the accessory tools reasonable reach.

I'm a fan of small applicances and kitchen gadgets - mostly for fun.  I've been seeing tons of these in thrift stores for a few years now so took that as an indicator that there might be a reason people weren't keeping them.  Now I know why.Good: it does exactly what it claims to do just fine.  It heats cheese between two tortillas - aka "quesadilla" and it seals the edges very well.  It heats pretty quick and holds sufficient heat to do the job in 3 minutes with no turning.  Much easier and faster than the stove-top.  It's pretty easy to clean by wiping down if you happen to overfill it.Bad: it only holds 1/4 cup of cheese.  That's not a lot on a 10" tortilla.  And it needs a 10" tortilla which is larger than the more common sizes in most grocery stores.  If you overfill it, it WILL run out of the machine and make a mess.  It seals the edge very well but the resulting seal is a 1" wide crispy flour tortilla cracker.  For me that portion is waste - some people like it.  Making one quesadilla every 3-4 minutes could take some serious time if you're preparing for a family meal.If you're hoping for more than a simple quesadilla, such as one stuffed with meat or veggies, then this probably isn't going to make you too happy.  It just doesn't hold much filling before becoming a mess-maker.  If what you want is a simple cheese and salsa quesadilla though - it works fine for that.  If you're only making one then it's easier than the stovetop too but for many it's going to eat up a lot of time.If you're the sort that would really prefer a thick pie that happens to use a tortilla as the crust, this baby won't do it at all.  It only holds 1/4 cup of cheese.Note:  some people don't like that it gets hot or that fillings ooze out.  Yeah, go figure.  A cast iron comal on the stovetop gets hot too and fillings ooze out.  It's part of making quesadillas and some of us really like to snack on the part that oozed out.  :-)

I bought the Bonavita with thermal carafe four months ago.  I had been using a Chemex for about a year prior to getting this coffee maker and I still have my Chemex and thermal pitcher because I do love it.  I thought making my morning coffee would be easier with the Bonavita but it is not.  The coffee does taste fresh and clean with no bitterness.  That's about it for pros.  Saying it makes 8 cups of coffee is a joke.  I have a vintage diner coffee mug that I love.  It holds 7 ounces of coffee.  I fill the water reservoir on the BV to the 6-cup line and I get a little over 3 cups of coffee.  This is OK for me because three cups is all I drink each morning.  I do not drink coffee throughout the day so I don't have to contend with making it several times a day.  I pre-heat the carafe and I also pre-heat my coffee mug.  My first cup of coffee is nice and hot.  The second cup, not so hot, and when I drink the final cup after having my breakfast, not hot at all.  Lukewarm at best on that third cup.  The coffee dribbles out so slowly when pouring that I justloosen the lid enough to get a decent pour and then tighten it again.  The lid is quite warm which means heat is escaping from the carafe.  I too, as another reviewer noted, have to use one scoop more of coffee to get the desired strength.  Would I pay $150 for this coffee maker again?  No.  Will I continue using it for awhile to feel that I got my money's worth?  Yes.  Do I recommend it?  Not really.

Bigger lemons don't fit...you'd have to quarter them...so I just use the other item. Very happy with this purchase. It is very sad that companies that used to make quality products are outsourcing from China now. I put this product on my wedding registry as well as theOster BVCB07-Z 6-Cup Glass Jar 7-Speed Blender, Stainless Steeland I got both. At first I was much more partial to the Oster because my mom owns a 30+ year old Osterizer (older than me!) that still works to this day... but as soon as I took it out of the box my heart sank. The plastic base was like a toy! My mom's was entirely stainless steel... only the buttons were plastic. Of course, my basis of comparison will always be my mom's Osterizer so there's no way I could be happy with the one I got. Anyway, I tested both the Oster and Cuisinart and the Oster has more features (but who needs lots of blending features anyway)?  So I returned the Oster and kept the Cuisinart.  The Cuisinart has bad reviews but at least it looks and feels much sturdier than the Oster. My husband and I aren't smoothie people so we use the blender only 2-3 times a month.  I've used it to puree soup and make mango juice and it worked ok.  I don't have a problem "helping" it by pushing things down... even with my mom's antique Oster, when the mixture is really thick you'll need to help it.  Otherwise you get that burnt plastic smell.  The only thing that annoys me right now is that the removable bottom ring attached to the jar feels a little flimsy in my hand. It's no biggie but the picture led me to believe that that part is solid metal. Well, as long as it doesn't die out on me real soon I guess I'm ok with this blender.

I was a little hesitant to pick up this Urban Armor Gear case, one of the things I really like about my iPhone 5 is how sleek and thin it is. The last thing I really wanted to do was to buy a case that turned this perfect little phone into a brick. The Urban Armor Gear case does a really good job of adding hefty protection to the phone without adding a lot of bulk. The case is surprisingly light with a harder composite on the sides and back and more rubbery material on the corners.One of the things I really like about the case is how well the buttons on the case work. They look and feel like actual buttons and they work perfectly. I've gotten a number of compliments on the case which is nice and it has managed to stay white even after a couple weeks of use.THe Urban Armor is a surprisingly good case that is rugged without being bulky.

My 1st one still working great - that is how much I love this one!  I saw it at a decreased price and bought one to have in the supply closet!  If you are a tea drinker, you shouldn't be without this.  Or a cup-a-soup eater.  Or a hot chocolate lover.  It's great for making instant coffee.  It came fast, great packaging.
