Works fine and turns on my phone. the charges last very long like a brand new battery everyday. I love how long it lasts and turns to be one of the best investments i had for this phone. Do not buy those extended batteries for phones with the built in case just buy this and it will save you room!

For the price, these are excellent headphones.  I bought them primarily to use on my iPod, but they sound great on my laptop and home stereo.  The earbuds I own are Senheiser CX-300's.  While noise blocking earbuds like my CX-300's are great for airplanes and other noisy environments, they just don't produce the quality of sound of "cans" or outside the canal headphones.  Even with earbuds costing $100-plus, I would still argue there is a sacrifice.  Buds are still a great choice if you want something small, mobile and discrete; however, if you don't mind lugging around something a little larger, the V150's are great.With an impedance rating of 24 ohms, an iPod, laptop or other mobile device won't have any problem lighting these up; and even at 24 ohms the V150's sound pretty darn good - especially when you consider their price. I also have a pair of 64-ohm Senheiser "cans" (HD-250) along with a portable amp.  This set-up is ideal for my iPod as far as sound goes, but a pain to lug around.  The MDR-V150 won't fit in your pocket, and are a bit bulky, but are still portable and for less than half the price of a decent set of buds, sound twice as good.As far as comfort, they are snug but comfortable, and the cables and jack are heavy duty and well insulated.One note, if you use these in an office or tight space and like to blast, take in mind your neighbor is going to hear you.

I've ordered three of these fans from two web resellers.  Only one functions as advertised.  Other two won't spin beyond ~600rpm regardless of switch position.I advised Silverstone's email support department and they assumed I didn't know how to hook up or test a fan.  A second email convinced them their products were faulty, yet I was offered no RMA to factory.  They suggested I return them to the resellers.  Guess they aren't very interested in examining/diagnosing/fixing their quality control or design failures.The one good fan does work well and can either be either quiet or push a lot of air through a filter.
dont look any ,more you want find a better comforter. i looked and i read all the reviews stop here there is no better comforter then this one for the money.

This cable was purchased to allow a Samsung Galaxy Tab 3 to access files on USB thumb drives, and it works perfectly.  My Galaxy Tab is running Android Jelly Bean 4.2, and it connected immediately to the 4 gb Kingston thumb drive that I plugged into it. I was able to see the drive and all folder/files content in the built-in "My Files" App on the tablet, with no special tweaks or drivers at all.  I have not yet tried it with an external HDD, as I don't have one formatted as FAT-32 (Android does not support NTFS drives), but I would assume it would work. The cable end connectors fit just right: not too loose or too tight, so the quality appears to be at least average. RECOMMENDED for anyone owning an Android tablet who wants to be able to use it with USB storage devices.
Its expensive but this is the best thing I found to clean my cast iron skillets.  I would recommend this product

MAKE SURE YOU REALLY WANT THESE CABLES. Got the cables sooner than later, originally it was going to take over a month to get them delivered. Cables work fine when used with the Apple charging unit, however when you try to use a computer USB port, the cables are not recognized so this eliminates this mode of charging which forces me to carry my charger with me everywhere. I don't think these are still manufactured, possibly because the transmitters seem to burn out after a period of time. I give it one star only because it is better than nothing. If you want carpal tunnel in one week this is the way to get it (I never had pain in my hands and wrists until I got this thing). You have to hold the button down while you use it, painful and uncomfortable. The shape is awkward and twists your wrist funny too. Suction not that strong. I am constantly doing the thing where I go over the same dirt over and over trying to get  it up - because I'm trying to justify the purchase. But it's just wasting time, energy, etc. In the end I often have to get down on the floor and get the dirt with my hands. If there is dirt stuck on a rug or fur on furniture, it won't suck it up. The stick is stronger than the suction. Also, I spent 20 mins with their online staff discussing my needs before I bought it. Then when I called back to say I was in pain from a few days of using it due to the on button and how did I keep it on while I was using it, they pretty much told me to shut up and go away, tough luck lady. I don't think they ever did any pre marketing studies - just thought they were so smart to make something that looked different that we'd follow the shiny new concept like sheep to the slaughter. Their Mercedes payments are more important that satisfied customers. Goes to character.Don't be hoodwinked. Don't bring that energy into your home!

The Simple Audio Go Compact Rechargeable Bluetooth Speaker is exactly what I've been needing (okay, wanting).  I was tempted to purchase a new Bose system or speaker.  I have an old Bose unit, but it does not have bluetooth.  I want the capability of using my purchased music on my cell phone or the XM app on my phone (HTC One).  The phone itself sounds really great for a phone, because it does have beats audio with dual front speakers, but it's still a cell phone and can only go so loud.  I could get a cable and physically connect it to my current Bose, but it's not always in the location I want to use it and I want to move around with my phone.  This speaker is half the price of the Bose speaker I was looking into.  Sure, there's a pretty good chance the Bose speaker sounds better, but I am 100% satisfied with my Simple Audio speaker.

If you follow my reviews, here is what you already know:I tried the $99 Breville & couldn't send it back fast enough. Very wet pulp, very loud, not easy to clean.I upgraded to the Omega J8006. It produced much drier pulp but produce got backed up inside. I couldn't even juice one whole apple without having to clean it out, even alternating with hard produce like beets or carrots. It was quiet and easy to clean though.I sent it back and upgraded even further to this one.What have I found?PROS:-Fits better on counter space-Quiet-DRY DRY pulp. Very dry. Even more than the J8006. squeeze it in your hand, eat some, you won't find hardly a drop of extra juice.I can juice about 8 ozs before I notice it gets a bit clogged. Not bad, mind you, but somewhat clogged.CONS --Clean up not quite as easy as the J8006 but not too bad. I am a stay at home mom so clean up is nothing new to me. What I want is a product that works well. So, not too big of a deal-I noticed that it is leaking a bit at the base but only a few drops of juice. Will keep my eye on it & report back here after some use-MUCH more (fine) pulp in juice than J8006. This is why I sent the J8006 back. That pulp would back up in the J8006 around the auger and prevent it from juicing correctly. Here, the pulp does not back up because it comes out in the juice. Now I do not mind some pulp but my kids do! I put my own strained over the juice catcher, strains it fine. But WHY didn't Omega have the sense to provide a fitted strainer for their juice catcher like they do for the J8006? Doesn't make sense!-Bit frothy. Not bad, I do not mind some froth, but some people do. A good fitted strainer would help with that, OMEGA! I hope they read these reviews-When you hit REVERSE it kinds of jumps a bit and makes a startling noise if a hard veggie is caught up in there. It's a bit worrisome. Again, will keep an eye on it & report back. Though if I hold on to the juicing barrel when I reverse, it won't do it.My husband and I agree this is a better product than the J80006. More juice. No problem so far with greens. With all the research I did on juicers (on YouTube, on here, everywhere online - it's a lot of $$ to spend on JUICE!) here is what I know...to save you some footwork yourself:There is almost no such thing as the perfect juicer. (Not having gone up to the $500 - $1000 lines, maybe there is). You have to know what you want from your juicer - fast? More enzymes? Easy clean up? Bargain? There is something to fit every need. Be prepared to try things out & not be afraid to return them.We did not find that it splatter out juice from the ejection spout. No issues there - for us.I compost so there is no waste with the pulp. I have seen some on YouTube that save the pulp, make them into veggie patties & fry them up in olive oil & salt. Not a bad idea.My needs were - the most juice/enzymes from my produce. Not sounding like a train in my kitchen. Juicing more than 4 ozs without clogging. Juicing greens.My husband and I were shocked at how delicious fresh juice can be. Any green juice can be flavored in the best way by throwing in a crisp granny smith apple. And make sure to try some ginger or fresh mint! SO yummy!! We go through a few heads of kale a week now. We never would have eaten that much before. Instead of an afternoon coffee break we have a juice break.I will update this review after a few weeks use. Happy juicing!*UPDATE* Sept 27, 2012I am still loving this juicer. It works quite well. All juicers are going to produce some pulp, just put a strainer on top of the juice container under the juice dispenser and you will be fine.(If you have a baby, listen up! The resulting pulp caught in the strainer after juicing fruits & veggies produces the finest, smoothest baby-food you will ever encounter. For example, when I juice apples, I get an 'applesauce' in the strainer that is so smooth I wish I knew someone with a baby I could give it to. Same when I juice carrots. Just so you know!)It is apple season here in New England and I am just loving the fresh crisp juice I am creating these days.

This review is more against Amazon than Samsung.  This product is okay.  For $250 minus discounts, the price is a good value.  Don't expect Mac Air quality.  This chromebook is plastic and keyboard is not ideal for typing.  However, I'll give this device 4 star since I still like it.Pro: battery life, light, I love the ChromeOS approach, comes with a rudimentary ssh terminal to access my ssh servers.Con:  Google Print, not many offline apps, and keyboard is quality is questionable.  I'm also concerned about the paint/finish of the computer.  Likely will wear out and scratch easy.As for Amazon, I wish I could give them a rating.  I requested a second replacement computer since the first one delivered had a nick in the case.  The second one was delivered quickly and in good faith.  Unfortunately,  the second one had a bad keyboard.  I noticed that it was difficult to type since the keys were not uniform.  Some required light touch and others required harder press.  This made it difficult to type quickly.I decided to keep the computer with the cosmetic defect (and better keyboard).  I wish Amazon sold "new" items.  I may call their customer support tomorrow.  This isn't the first time I got a "used" item sold as "new".If you have a difficult keyboard to use, consider a replacement from Amazon and cross your fingers.

Unlike the other reviewer I was able to get my iPhone 4s into this case, but it was certainly a tight squeeze, and not easy in, easy out.Sweat proof, low profile, fits your upper arm snug, and is adjustable.However, I don't find anything remarkable about it, and frankly am getting tired of overpriced iPhone and iPod accessories, sure I'm not a fan of made in China knock offs, but we need to start voting with our wallets with this stuff.OK, won't win any awards. 2 stars

I already owned a pair of Sennheiser CX300-B In-Ear Stereo Headphone (Old Version)  earbuds that I'd been using for about 3 years but the sound was starting to go and I wanted to kill two birds and get a pair of earbuds with a mic attached.Pros:- The mic is placed conveniently near your mouth so there's no need to lift the wire to your mouth like some mic'd earphones requre.- The clip is a nice feature for holding it in place-It comes with two different style of silicone tips and different sizes for each style.- Mic works fineCons-Unfortunately, the sound quality is vastly inferior to my old Sennheiser buds. There is virtually no bass and the sound is flat and tinny all around. There was a suggestion of a burn in period, but after roughly 6 hours of use the quality has only improved marginally. While I paid roughly the same for my Sennheiser CX300-B In-Ear Stereo Headphone, these are not nearly as good. It might be good if you only use headphones for calls, but I want to listen to music as well. I'll be looking for new pair soon.

Got these towels for my son and daughter-in-laws new home and when they came they immediately called to say how lush, thick and beautiful they were -- and just the right color.  Could not be more pleased.

I so wanted to give the Magicbox a full 5 stars, and only one minor annoyance kept me from doing so. First of all, the audio quality is first rate to my ears, especially for such a small speaker. Sound clarity was excellent, bass notes were thumping without getting muddy, and the maximum volume was almost at the hurt-my-ears level. Pairing was quick, with the Magicbox being recognized as "BIGBox" by my Nexus 7 tablet. The controls on top of the speaker worked well to adjust the volume, pause the music, and to advance/go back in my playlist. As for the Bluetooth range, I moved the speaker around, and it played everywhere in my house, even upstairs, without any dropouts. I also hard-wired my Nexus 7 to the speakers with the included 18" 3.5mm audio cable. It worked very well, but in this mode, the volume, pause, and forward/back must be controlled from the tablet or phone.There was just one small annoyance. Whereas most Bluetooth speakers use a simple tone and colored lights to signal that the speakers are ready to pair and when the Bluetooth connection is broken, the Magicbox uses a man's voice (English, with an Asian accent). Moreover, the voice was very loud. The manufacturer emailed me with instructions on how to lower the volume, and when I did so, the volume was lower, but it was still a little weird to use a voiceover instead of a beep to do this. The manufacturer says that they will correct this in new shippings.But that was the only negative issue I had with the Magicbox, although I should mention that the blue color looked a bit garish to me, so I recommend the black color.A USB charging cable is included, but I didn't see any charging information in the user manual. The 800mAh internal battery could be recharged from a USB port, but I also used a 5V~0.85A charger for an hour or so, and there were no problems.A product sample was provided by the manufacturer for review purposes.

These gaming headphones are a good, solid, workhorse-like option -- functional and durable, with good sound response, particularly in the lower register. It might not work equally well with all kinds of sound systems or computer sound cards... I've heard mixed things from other users. The ear pads can be uncomfortable -- again, it may be an issue with what kind of "equipment" you've got; different heads may fit differently.  I have a large skull and found them a little too clamp-like. Overall, though, these seem pretty good, and sure to improve with future generations of design.

I love this tool.  I use it for two things.  The first is to froth milk for my latte each morning.  The attachment on the right works perfectly.  If you hold it just under the top of the milk, it makes really creamy foam.  My espresso maker doesn't foam milk well so I heat it in the microwave and the froth it with this tool.  The other thing I use it for is mixing eggs.  The whisk type attachment works perfectly for this.  And it cleans up easily.  The next thing I plan on trying is whipping ice cold non-fat milk and sugar into a "cool whip" topping.  The attachment I use for frothing milk will work well for that task.For the low price, you can't beat this tool.

இ Fuzzy Wuzzy's Summary:ѾѾѾѾ Recommended with warm fuzzies.► Packaging, Style, Appearance: ѾѾѾѾѾ Excellent 5-fuzzies rating► Construction & Build Quality: ѾѾѾѾѾ Excellent 5-fuzzies rating► Design & Features: ѾѾѾ Average not-good not-bad 3-fuzzies rating► Competitive Pricing & Value: ѾѾѾѾ Good 4-fuzzies ratingփ Positives:փ This tea ball is larger than most mesh tea balls or perforated metal tea balls, making it suitable for use in a teapot while still fitting inside a large mug or cup (but it may be too large for small teacups).փ This tea ball is far more sturdy than mesh tea balls that are made of two mesh hemispheres joined by a fragile hinge and latching clasp.ჯ Negatives:ჯ The sieve that is used for the "laser-cut" stainless steel main body of this tea ball has ultra-fine perforations that are too small, resulting in much of the tea flavor remaining inside the ball and not enough tea flavor spreading throughout your teapot or mug.ჯ The 34 holes on the lid cap are too large, being more than four times larger in diameter than each of the sieve's ultra-fine perforations, and smaller tea leaves can escape through these lid holes.Over the years, I have used a wide variety of tea balls and mesh-based tea infusers.  I personally prefer tea balls over tea filters, strainers, or brewing baskets where you place the device over the mouth of a teapot or mug, let the tea steep, and then have to remove the infuser before you can drink the tea.  I usually drink high-quality teas that can be re-steeped several times and I often start drinking the tea while the tea ball is still left inside the teapot or mug, which cannot be done with most tea filters, strainers, or brewing baskets.  Also, some tea infusers, filters, strainers, and brewing baskets use plastic parts, and I never want plastic to come in contact with hot or boiling water in any of my tea infusers, cooking utensils, or kitchen appliances.Even though I also have three 2.0"-diameter mesh tea balls, I purchased four of these Teavana tea balls because they are larger in size and hold more tea leaves than my 2.0"-diameter mesh tea balls and are thus better for teapots and larger mugs.Since there are no measurements listed for this tea ball, here are my exact measurements of this tea ball's dimensions:Height of lid cap: 0.7"Height of sieve container: 2.5"Total height of tea ball with lid cap attached to sieve container: 3.05"Diameter of sieve container at the bottom: 1.5"Maximum diameter of sieve container at the top opening: 2.0"Length of hanging chain: 6.1"The bottom base is slightly curved, but it has three little nubby feet shaped into its metal base that let this tea ball stand upright by itself.  This tea ball can fit inside any cup or mug as long as it has an opening measuring at least 2.0 inches in diameter.  You can actually use this tea ball in a cup that is shorter than the tea ball's 3.05" height if you gently lift and dunk the tea ball several times while the tea is steeping to facilitate the dispersal of the brewed tea that is inside the tea ball.This tea ball is made of 18/8 stainless steel, which means that it has 18% chromium and 8% nickel.  18/8 stainless steel is used for most tea balls and tea infusers.  It is larger than most tea balls and its interior volume is roughly comparable to a 2.5"-diameter round mesh tea ball.  You should always use a large roomy tea ball to steep the tea leaves in.  Do not fill more than half of a tea ball's interior volume with tea leaves; filling it only one-third or one-fourth full is even better.  I have seen people tightly pack a small 1-inch diameter tea infuser with loose leaves, which is a bad way to steep the tea.  The dried tea needs both time and space to unfurl, expand, and spread its flavor, aroma, flavonoids, and antioxidants out into the surrounding water.  Like a fine wine, tea needs room and time to breathe and spread its essence out into the water.Even though this tea ball is listed as "Perfect Tea Ball", it is shipped in a box that is labeled as "Classic Teaball".  And it is NOT a "perfect" tea ball due to two issues described further below.You would think that making a tea infuser would not be rocket science, but various tea infusers/strainers just do not function very well, particularly when it comes to leaking out small tea leaves.  A well-designed tea ball should satisfy four main requirements: (1) it should prevent the loose tea leaves from escaping out, (2) it should not significantly impede the flow of water passing through it so all of the tea's flavor can be evenly dispersed throughout the teapot or mug, (3) it should be roomy enough so that adding your desired amount of tea only fills up the interior by no more than one half or one third, and (4) it should be easy to clean without needing a dishwasher or scrubbing.  This Teavana tea ball satisfies requirements #3 and #4, but it has issues with requirements #1 and #2.The 34 holes on the tea ball's lid cap are each 1 millimeter in diameter.  In contrast, each of the ultra-fine perforations that cover the main sieve container is at least three or four times smaller in diameter (only 0.3 or 0.25 millimeters in diameter!).The box of this tea ball says it has "ultra-fine perforations which allow perfect straining for even the smallest leaves".  The main sieve container is covered with holes that are really tiny, even smaller than the openings of some mesh tea balls that use a very fine mesh.  However, because the tea ball's lid cap is covered with 34 larger holes, each 1 millimeter in diameter, that provides an opportunity for a massive jailbreak by the tea leaves to escape through all of the lid's openings!  I have previously used tea balls that consist of a 1.5"-diameter stainless steel ball having several dozen 1-millimeter-wide perforations in the metal.  With these enclosed tea balls, too much of the tea flavor remains trapped inside the ball, never getting distributed into the rest of the water, while the holes are large enough to let tea leaves escape.While the ultra-fine mesh size of the main sieve container is great for preventing the tea leaves from leaking out, it also impedes the free passage of water into and out of the tea ball, and the concentrated tea inside the tea ball is more likely to remain there while the water outside the tea ball does not have enough tea flavor.The main reason why I rate this Teavana tea ball 4 stars instead of 5 stars, and why I consider this to be a "Good Tea Ball" instead of a "Perfect Tea Ball", is that I really wish the 34 holes on the tea ball's lid cap were smaller.  Perhaps the same metal sieve that was used to construct the main body could have also been used to form the top of the lid.But, as I further describe below, both the issue of tea leaves leaking out from the lid's larger holes and the issue of the tea flavor not freely dispersing through the ultra-fine sieve body can be fixed by pre-soaking the tea leaves first and either slowly stirring the water around the tea ball or slowly swirling the tea ball in the water while it is steeping.To minimize the tea leaves floating out through the lid cap's larger holes, pre-soak the tea leaves first.  Prior to adding your boiling water, place the opened tea ball on a saucer, load the tea ball with the desired amount of tea leaves, and drizzle two to five teaspoons of water over the tea leaves.  Add two teaspoons of water for one or two teaspoons of tea leaves, and four or five teaspoons of water for four or five teaspoons of tea leaves.  The water can be cold or room-temperature, and does not have to be warm.  Screw the lid onto the tea ball, and let the tea leaves soak in the water for a full five minutes while the tea ball is still standing on the saucer.  After five minutes of pre-soak time, place or hang the tea ball in the teapot or mug with the lid holes facing upward, and add the hot water.  When you add the hot water into the teapot or mug, slowly pour the water in until the tea ball's top lid holes are fully submerged.  Once the tea ball's top lid holes are fully submerged, you can add more hot water at a quicker pace.  When the tea leaves are pre-soaked this way prior to pouring the hot water into the mug or teapot, I consistently minimize the amount of tea leaves that leak out through the larger openings in the lid, and only a small amount of tea dust specks float out, which is comparable to my favorite tea mesh balls that use a very fine mesh.  If you pre-soak the tea leaves using room-temperature water, you may want to slightly lengthen the steeping time.  Even when using tea leaves that are finely milled into small leaf chips, such as rooibos or mate tea, the combination of pre-soaking the tea leaves with a little bit of water and slowly pouring boiling water into the teapot or mug to gently submerge the tea ball minimizes tea leaf escapees in your tea.  I also drink a lot of high-quality Chinese teas that are formed using larger tea leaf sections that are rolled or twisted.  These teas have the dual advantage that the larger leaves do not escape through the lid holes and the rolled/twisted leaves allow different tastes to be experienced as they unroll with each infusion and re-steeping.If you want to maximize the amount of flavor, aroma, flavonoids, and antioxidants that get spread out into your tea, you should also periodically either swirl and stir the water or gently move this tea ball around in the cup/mug/teapot while the tea is steeping.  Do not just let this tea ball sit motionless in the water while it is steeping.  Because the laser-cut sieve on this tea ball has such small openings, the surface tension of the water forms a "membrane" across all of the sieve's openings.  The water molecules want to join together to form a thin membrane between the small openings of the mesh, thus preventing water from freely passing through the tea ball and keeping more of the concentrated tea flavor inside the tea ball.  This surface tension tendency is less likely to happen in a mesh with large openings.  As an example of the water's surface tension on this tea ball's fine mesh, I can add three or four teaspoons of water into an empty Teavana tea ball, filling one-fifth of the tea ball with water, and the water will not pass through the mesh openings.  If I add two more teaspoons of water, water starts to slowly leak out through the mesh and drip.  If I then quickly add four tablespoons of water, water steadily leaks out through the tea ball's mesh until it is one-fifth filled with water again.  At that point, there is not enough force to break the surface tension of the water and cause it to flow out of the tea ball.  So by periodically swirling or stirring the water or gently moving the tea ball around a little, this breaks the surface tension and improves the circulation of water through the inside of the tea ball.  If you grab the tea ball's hanging chain to gently swirl the tea ball inside the teapot or mug, make sure that you only move the tea ball laterally, instead of up and down, or else you encourage tea leaves to flow out through the lid using up-and-down movements.  I use a wooden chopstick to stir the water around the tea ball while it is steeping; you can also use a long-handled stirring spoon.  You should also never pack a tea ball full of leaves because that prevents the leaves from fully expanding and blocks the passage of water through the tea ball.To REALLY thoroughly steep the loose tea leaves, you can drink tea the way that they drink it in China - by not using any infuser and just adding loose tea leaves to your cup or teapot and pouring hot water into it.  The loose tea leaves sink to the bottom of the cup or teapot after awhile, and unless your cup is nearly empty, you usually do not need to strain tea leaves by closing your teeth together :-)  By adding loose tea leaves directly to a cup or teapot, the leaves are not impeded from fully expanding, and the flavor and aroma are thoroughly mixed throughout the water, both of which can be issues when using an infuser.  Some people are averse to leaving loose tea steeping in their cup or teapot because they associate bitter flavor with oversteeped tea.  This may be true for some particularly bitter teas like yerba mate.  Black teas and green teas naturally have tannins that taste slightly bitter, but you can adjust a tea's bitterness by either adding more leaves and infusing for less time or adding less leaves and steeping longer.  Modern taste buds prefer stronger flavors (and more salt and sugar), while previous generations ate simpler diets and drank weaker coffees and teas.  Even though some people do not like the slightly bitter tannic aftertaste from teas, I do not mind it; I just consider that to be part of the character of the tea leaves, just as I accept the slight bitterness of Brussels sprouts.Previous reviews of this Teavana tea ball say that the lid cap is hard to open and close.  Perhaps this tea ball's design and dimensions have been adjusted since then, but the four Teavana tea balls that I purchased were easy to open and close.  The lid and main sieve container are not threaded; this is not like screwing a lid onto a jar.  The lid has two indented bumps that fit into two grooves molded into top of the sieve container.  You align the lid's two inward-facing bumps with the grooves on the sieve container and give the lid a slight clockwise twist to lock it.  To open the tea ball, you give the lid a slight counterclockwise twist.  This twist-locking mechanism is similar to how you attach a smoke-fire alarm to its base mounted on the ceiling.  Like a jar lid, this tea ball's lid cap is slightly larger in diameter than the rim of the sieve container, and it easily twist-locks on and off.  So perhaps Teavana realized that previous versions of this tea ball were too hard to open and close and adjusted the diameter of the lid to be slightly larger now?I can put at least four teaspoons of tea leaves inside this tea ball before it is filled halfway.  As mentioned, you should never fill a tea ball more than halfway full because the tea leaves need room to expand once the water is added.  So this tea ball is roomy enough for people like me who love their teas to be strongly flavored.௫ Fuzzy Wuzzy's Conclusion:If you want less tea leaf leakage and more even distribution of the tea flavor throughout your teapot or mug, a well-designed mesh tea ball can perform better than this Teavana design.  But all mesh tea ball designs are more fragile than this Teavana tea ball, both because the mesh can be easily damaged and because the small hinge and latching clasp are not sturdy and durable on mesh tea balls.  And because you would need a 2.5"-diameter or 3.0"-diameter mesh tea ball in order to hold as much tea leaves as this Teavana tea ball can hold, a larger-sized mesh tea ball is even more fragile than a smaller-diameter mesh tea ball.This tea ball should last many years if you just rinse it with water after use,  and do not bend the metal when handling it.  Even if you add milk or honey to your tea, you should not need to use vigorous scrubbing to clean this, and you should not put this in the dishwasher.  I keep an old toothbrush at the kitchen sink to lightly clean any specks of debris that do not rinse off by themselves.  Sometimes, I add a tiny dab of dish detergent on the toothbrush, gently brush the sieve mesh and lid holes with it, and rinse with water.  Over time, the stainless steel may discolor and stain due to the tannins present in the tea.  When this happens, rest assured that this discoloration is NOT rust on the metal.  If you really want to remove the tea stains from the metal, you can try boiling the tea ball in white vinegar and vigorously scrub the metal using a scrubbing pad or stiff toothbrush.  But you also risk damaging the metal if you scrub too hard.  Therefore, it is best to just accept the discolored metal as a sign of having enjoyed many teas, just as wrinkles that come with age tell of a life well-lived :-)

It's a simple elastic Velcro strap with a buckle.  I cut a couple slits in the silicone cover of my mp3 player and slipped the strap through them.  After sizing it for my upper arm, I don't have to ever again open up the Velcro.  The elastic allows me to easily slip my arm into and out of the strap.

I've used many screen protectors before .. for Gameboy, PSP, cell phones, etc... and I think I've gotten pretty good at putting them on, without getting fingerprints underneath them, or air bubbles, etc.  But for whatever reason - and I can't figure out why - these ones were impossible.  I wasted the first two in the 3-pack, just struggling to figure out which side was the 'sticky' side, because neither side would stick.  Meanwhile the more I struggled, the more dust and fingerprints got all over them.  The 3rd one, fortunately, stuck.  But with lots of air bubbles.  This was a big waste of money.

Gorilla Gadgets sent me a 'for review' unit (didn't ask for a 'good review' in exchange) and, so far, it appears to hold anything from an extra 2/3 to double juice you get off the 'regular' S4 battery which is not bad. Not much else to say. It's just a battery.Included is a an S4 backplate. It fits but... if you like the cheap look and feel of shiny, fingerprint magnet plastic, this is what you are looking for. In other words, if when you assess the value you are getting, I would not count the cover as an 'extra'. It's there but I'm not going to use it and I wouldn't miss it if it wasn't. My five stars are for the battery and the battery only.

My family spends a tremendous amount of time outdoors, fishing, hiking, and working on our ranch.  I am a distance walker.  So I have been looking for a GPS that would help me keep up with miles walked and let me know where I am in the state park where I often walk.  So when Amazon Vine offered to let me try out the Magellan eXplorist 310 I thought it might answer my needs.The eXplorist 310 comes in a box with a Quick Start Guide, and a USB cord for the computer.  There is a full manual online.  The on/off button is on the top right side and must be held down for 2 seconds.  To find this out, you either read the full manual or learn by trial & error.  It certainly doesn't tell you in the Quick Start Guide how to turn it on.  In fact the Quick Start Guide doesn't tell you much that is helpful.Once you have it on press the menu key and you will see the different screens that come up.  I suggest going to tools first to look at settings.  You can put in ownership information and I would also suggest that you go to Brightness & Volume and change the backlight timer to longer than the 15 seconds that it is set with originally to keep the screen from turning off every fifteen seconds.I found my home on the map, saved it, and then looked at points of interest.  It  found all the cities, bodies of water, public airstrips, and parks and public country clubs in a twenty-five mile radius.  Since I live in a sparely populated area and my address is not on mapquest I was surprised by this.The dashboard tells me how far I have traveled, how long I have been traveling, my average speed, if I stop how long I stopped.  I love this because people will often stop me to talk and I could never figure it into my walking time, but now I can. The unit fits nicely in my hand and will fit into a pocket or clip on a belt while walking.  It is easy to read, zoom in or out, go back, or use the toggle switches.  We used it recently to map roads and trails on a piece of property that was heavily wooded.I am not a geocache person, but I know that there is one hidden in the small rural cemetery near my parents.  It is interesting to see the people who drive way out of the way to hunt for it.  This unit has information that one could use to find and track geocaches, if that is something that interests you.This is not a GPS that I would use to travel in the car but for the outdoors it has been lots of fun.  This is not something that I have ever used before and it took me one set of batteries to learn how, but it is simple and easy to use.  I am looking forward to discovering more about it as I use itmore.  Be aware that the Magellan software does not support Apple computers so anything you do with the GPS on the computer must be done on a PC.

These are great headphones for the price. I stash them in my backpack and use them whenever I need to make a phone call as it isolates out the sound well and the other side can get good pickup. They make the ears a tad warm, but I'm not using them for long periods. They fold down quite compactly and I like the fact that there is little branding on them. To me, they are just nice matte black headphones, classy looking. The cable is good, doesn't tangle and everyone likes the fabric cover.

For what you pay for this the performance is right on target and it dose a lot for a system with less the super audio performance. I have 2 of these and use them on my gaming systems. They do improve the audio performance much more than you think until you hear it and the headphone jack is right where it should be for easy connection when needed.The sound card is one of the best I have used in this price range and some I had that were much higher in price but had less performance and I have not had any issues so far. I will update with use but if you need a sound card for USB this is the one to get. I have had others but this one is so far ahead they will never even come close so don't waste your money and order this one first.

cook, drain, steam, all in one very attractive pot!  and with a locking lid, safe too!  highly recommended to the pasta/steam-cooking lovers!

Update 03/27/2013:  I sent the case to Speck for warranty, and they informed me that this is a counterfeit product.  They turned the case over to their legal team, and are evaluating taking action against the seller.  I do not recommend buying this product, as the case only held up for about a month, and since it's fake, there is no warranty.  I'm going to contact the seller to see how they want to proceed.There has been a lot of buzz on here about counterfeit Speck cases, and I think this could be one of them.  Many of my friends use Speck cases and highly recommend them, so I figured I would give this one a shot.  Immediately, I felt the quality was questionable, and after a month of use, the rubber has started to rub off and peel away from the plastic.  Not impressed... I'm not even rough on my electronics, and this case already looks like it has been through a war.  I'm wondering if there is any recourse from amazon or the seller on this, because this case is awful.
Love that this has so much GB on it.  The Grand-kids and fill it up and it takes months for them to need another one and the price is great!

I am so glad that I did my homework and bought this instead of some $400 monstrosity. It was easy to assemble, works great (tho not so great that it sucks up things a foot away that you would rather it not inhale), is relatively light, and best of all the hose attachment is very conveniently located and can be used concurrent to the floor vac.A fantastic value. With 10 bags and replacement bands it still came to less than $85 total for the order. Highly recommend!

I listen to alot of audio books on my way to and from work so I have been on the lookout for a good Bluetooth headset for a while.  I had one that my husband gave me after he got a new one but it wasn't very flexible and didn't let me have much movement of my head.  I saw this headset and I thought that maybe it would work better.When I first took the headset out of the box I was surprised by the short cord that was on the charger.  Most of our outlets are the typical ones that are near the floor so I have to literally place the head set on the floor when I charge it in my bedroom.  The only place that I could charge it that I wouldn't have to do this is in the kitchen which at times is very inconveniant.I have to do quite a bit of adjusting to get the earpieces to fit my ears correctly so that they do not fall out if I move my head.  I really don't know how people could use these successfully if they were running.  I have used them while I was walking and I had to limit my head movements to very little movement or the earpieces would dislodge (still better than the other headset thoughThe distance that you can be away from your device is very limited.  With the other headset I could walk almost anywhere in my house.  Not so with this headset.  I am lucky if I can walk across the room and the headset doesn't cut out on me.Once I do get the headset adjusted it is very comfortable on my ears.  The quality of the sound is quite good.Since I do most of my listening in my car this headset works well (after the Initial adjusting).  I would not recommend for any activity that has alot of movement.

I purchased a few cases for the new Samsung Galaxy SIII, this Incipio Hard Shell Case with Silicone Core is really nice.First, this is a two layer case with a thin silicone layer first and then a hard shell outer layer. The combination of the two creates a safe way to protect the Galaxy SIII.  Alignment of the holes for the Audio Jack, background microphone, USB port, main microphone and camera are perfect.Secondly the thinness of this case really does keep the overall thin feel of the phone.  Removal of the case is easy for battery changes as well.The shape of the case enhances holding the phone.  The main screen area is not limited in any way functionally with this case.  With the price factored in, this is probably the best case for your Samsung SIII!
Large toaster, possibly alittle faster than most, Im happy with it, but it is pretty big for a counter. Its also very easy to clean the crumbs out.
Works perfectly! I use it to read my many mirco SDs. Transfers fast, no errors, plug & play is 100% functional.
First it is surrounded by a rubber case and then you attach the colored solid portion which locks it in place. Fitment was great and it's very durable.

My wife has back problems, and a "large vacuum" really gives her pain.  This little giant is perfect.  We have a litter-tracking cat that requires daily vacuuming ... this vacuum is perfect for the job, both wood floors and rugs ... wonderful !!!
i bought this keyboard combo just for an emergency use,i useing it for a spare computer and works really good,not like the $100 and up sets buti am satisfied.

I purchased this camera at a convention.  I have the Amscope  40x - 2000x tri ocular dark microscope I purchased on Amazon..  As other reviewer stated, I wasted two days trying to get my win 7 computer to "see" the camera.  Got the same error about not being hooked up to the computer or being used by another device.  Like the other reviewer, I too loaded the s/w, deleted it, reloaded it, rebooted my PC, etc.  I was getting really mad.  I finally got it to work, but it's horrible!  The s/w says it's win 7 compatible, but you have to run it in xp sp 3 to make it appear on screen.  Then, I can only get an image when I use the lowest pwr on the scope.  Anything higher and the picture is too large to fit on the screen.  My microscope even has a device specifically on the tri ocular lens which can be rotated up or down to get the image in focus. The camera still doesn't focus.  I've even hand held the camera higher and lower to the eye lens to try to get it focused and it still doesn't work.  The image is great in the microscope eye pieces, but the camera is worthless.Also, the software loads the drivers in windows as the camera is a video device only.  I tried everything I could think of to make my PC "see" the camera and not a Video device, but windows just kept telling me it already had the best drivers.  So, because it does that, the s/w locks out any options to change the camera settings, so you can't even try to adjust the pics that way.  I also get the black screen on my computer when trying to increase the magnification.  I spent a lot more money on this because I bought it at at the event.  They made it look so great and wonderful.  To me, when a product says it is compatible in win 7, that means it should RUN in the win 7 format and not have to be dummied down to make it work.  I did contact the company and told them all the things I tried to get it to work.  They did respond, but it was a joke.  They referred me to the s/w download I already had, and even told me it sounded like I've already tried what they were suggesting to me to try.  What the heck kind of help is that?  Telling me to try something they already knew I tried and didn't work.  It didn't make any sense.The comment about the help key is correct too.  When you go to help key, you get this slow scrolling msg about the dumb manufacturer.  NOTHING about really getting help.  Now I'm looking at having to purchase another camera that will work w/my new scope.  I'm in vet tech school and need this for microbiology.  My advice, save your money.  It's not worth it because it doesn't work.

This little handheld is not so little. It is great at picking up pet hair on the sofa and chairs and has a powerful suction and rotating brush. It is a bit heavy and bulky, but overall I'm very happy with it.

The Dakota 10 is a good product.  I've quite a bit of experience with numerous different types of GPS, handheld and otherwise, and I am very happy with this unit.  There are marine chartplotters that are clearly far superior to these, but as far as handheld units, this one is a great value and has good features.  Its waterproof, easy to use USB transfer for aftermarket topo maps and GPX files, touch screen is a huge improvement over the joystick of the stone age models.  This one has got everything you need.Long story short, I'd buy this unit again when it comes time to get a new one.

Great product, fits my 7 year old daughter's wrist fine and most importantly keeps it attached to her.  It's a little challenging to insert the iPod but with a little practice it get's easier.  The key is to open the band all the way which moves the band back in the opening creating a little more space.  For five stars they need to add a removable insert on the bottom to allow for charging without removing/reinserting the iPod.

Basically this is a nice little MP3 player.  Also works great for storage of large items I want to move between computers since it functions like an additional hard drive when connected to the computer.Pros:*Large storage (20 gigs)*Functions like a drive when attached to a computer*Does what it is supposed to do*USB 2.0*Button arrangement easy to use*Rechargeable internal batteryCons:*Plays only MP3 and not other formats like WMA*Record feature takes a little work to understandFor the price (especially with a rebate) it is the best value out there.

Have used for a couple of months now.  Vent mount in my 2010 Accord works well.  Swivel is one of the first of these I have had that actually holds its position.  I now use it with the non-flip Galaxy S5 cover and can just pop it in the dock and it starts charging.  I tried the flip cover from Samsung but it would only charge with the cover closed.  If you flipped the cover around back it would only charge if you pushed the phone up ~5mm or so in the mount.Combined with http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B009OBCAW2 for a BT audio connection makes the in/out of the car pretty convenient.  Of course (if you are reading this CHOE) it would be a BIG win if the dock included bluetooth and an AUX connection to the car stereo.)

These are my second Freshware silicone baking product, after theFreshware 12-Cavity Silicone Muffin Pan. I found that both have the same benefits and limitations.One of the interesting things about these baking cups is that they are free standing--I thought they were going to be used  to replace traditional muffin pan liners, but they work perfectly well on their own.  You can stick them inside muffin pans, or you can just line them up on a cookie sheet and use them as is. They have the same limitations as other silicone bakeware; you can't cook them in too hot of an oven, you will need to adjust your baking times, and popping them out takes some getting used to.I've found its best to let them cool, and you might have to gently pry them with a butter knife to break the seal. Once you get used to it, cleaning up is a breeze. They pop out cleanly, and with very little stick or break.One issue I have--which I also had with the "Silicone Muffin Pan"--is that they are tiny. They are smaller than standard paper liners, not quite mini-muffin size but closer to that than a regular muffin.  That's not a huge problem, but it does mean that most recipes won't conform to this size.  That small size actually works better with these than with the muffin pan.  According to the directions, you have to fill every slot in the muffin pan in order for them to bake right. But because of the small size you have leftover batter if you follow a standard recipe.I don't know why Freshware makes their silicone muffin supplies in such a small, non-standard size. Maybe it has to do with the ability of the silicone to retain heat--my guess is if they were larger they wouldn't cook through properly. Or maybe Freshware is committed to America's battle with obesity by forcing you to eat smaller muffins.

I adore the Acer Aspire One.  While it doesn't substitute for a desktop or larger laptop, it works perfectly as a lightweight, on-the-go computer to retrieve email, browse the internet, or perform non-intensive tasks while away from home.  It comes with a built-in webcam and microphone for chat/Skype, and  with pre-installed Windows XP Home.The new mini-laptops, or netbooks, are designed for portability, not heavy use, so the Aspire One does not have a CD/DVD drive, although owners can purchase an external drive, such asTargus PADVD010U USB 2.0 DVD/CD-ROM Slim External Drive - Blackto use for downloading software, listening to music, and viewing DVDs.  The keyboard, while tightly spaced, has nearly full-size keys (no numerical keypad), and a series of hotkeys to allow shortcuts from everything from volume control to displaying system properties.  Having the memory to download software is not a problem, as it comes with a 160 GB internal hard drive.  As for ports, it has three USB port, one LAN port, one SD card slot, and a Multi-in-One card reader.The display on the 8.9 inch screen is incredibly sharp and vivid, one of the best I've seen.  The best part of this tiny powerhouse is the battery: a six-cell, snap-on bar that connects to the back of the Aspire One and that does indeed last longer than 5 hours.  You could stick this computer in a medium-to-large handbag/briefcase and use it during all the allowable electronic device hours in a cross-country flight.The only true downside to this netbook is the touchpad, with its click buttons on either side. As a lefty, I've had to adjust to using my right finger to move the cursor so my left is free to click.  Most laptop users, no matter which hand they use, are used to clicking below a touch pad.  I highly recommend purchasing a wireless mouse such asLogitech VX Nano Cordless Laser Mouse for Notebooksto bypass this one awkward part of the design.  A much more minor issue is the rate at which the sapphire case picks up fingerprints.  Neither of these drawbacks warrant the removal of a star since this netbook sacrifices very little for its size.I highly recommend this mini-laptop to anyone who wants to cut down on weight and space while away from home.  Do not expect the Aspire One to be comfortable to work on for long hours, but it's powerful enough to drive PowerPoint presentations, do light editing/writing, answer email, and surf the web.-- Debbie Lee Wesselmann

There's tons of dried dirt in the dirty water tank, but it doesn't make a bad smell or anything. This is a great product, if mine were to break I would replace it in a heartbeat.

I'm a huge fan of the Vornado product line. I've gone through numerous floor fans over the years - most of them running 24x7 without fail. In terms of quality, this one is right up there and is well constructed and gives excellent air flow.The product is easy to remove from the packaging, and is well secured in foam and multiple layers of plastic. You'll also remove plastic film from both sides - which is to prevent incidental scratching of the surface in transport. On the filter side you will see some blue tape holding the filter in place - I left the tape there - it's not noticeable, and I won't plan to remove it until I change the filter.It is a bit confusing which direction you point the product, but you want the shiny side toward the room and the exhaust fan toward the wall or corner. Air is pulled in from the front and through the filter, then pushed out of the vents on the top back. By default it's on medium speed when you turn it on, and I wouldn't describe the product as "quiet running" at that point. Ticking the fan down to level 2 or level 1 is more of what I expect from an air filter fan...but for power cleaning, medium (level 3), (high) level 4 will do - just be prepared for it to be loud. It's advertised as "quiet operation" but...as I mentioned it can get noisy.There are a number of neat features here with the ability to use an automatic mode that senses how dirty the air is and adjusts the fan speed accordingly. They call this the "dust sensing technology" because it needs a catchy name. Sleep mode will sense when the lights go down and turn the fan speed down accordingly - only really useful if you leave it on continuously in a bedroom or such.The filter puts out fresh smelling air - it does its job to clean the air and give excellent airflow, which is what you should expect from a Vornado product. The fan blades move a lot of air no matter which setting it's on.But here's where I have concerns - and my recommendation for using it in enclosed spaces without open windows. The whole purpose of the purifier is to clean the air of dust and allergens, but if you live in a place where you regularly open windows then it sort of defeats the purpose. In my home office where I almost never open the windows - it's perfect. It catches floating cat hair and other particles and freshens the room. In the bedroom and living room, however, the windows are open on all but the hottest of days, so there's no reason to purify air that is constantly being dirtied from the outside.So a solid 4 stars for being a well constructed Vornado product that moves air quickly and efficiently and works great in rooms without open windows. 1 point away for the noise factor - but only because they specifically advertise quiet operator, which I have not found to be the case.
For the price-can't beat it. For most of us on a budget- it's worth the few dollars-a nd a good buy.

I've had this body for about a month now and sometimes I just pull it out just to remind myself that I actually have this camera!  I did not know how much of an upgrade I was actually making with this purchase!I came from shooting a T1i to the 7D, and right from the build, I was hooked.  I have a 70-200mm lens and when it is mounted on the 7D, it just works.  I love the grooves on the grip of this camera and because I have somewhat large hands, I can definitely handle heavy lenses without attaching a battery grip, unlike the T1i.Even though I have not put the auto-focus system completely to the test, so far, I really appreciate how you can customize the auto-focus to change depending on whether you are shooting horizontally or vertically.  That feature right there has already saved me precious time in recent shoots.  I can't wait to test out the 8fps!  Let alone, with the shooting speed of this camera, I can handhold a lot more HDR images.  I also appreciate the 19 points that I have to focus.  It's been a little hard shooting with the T1i now as it only has 9 points.The greatest benefit to me has been the Speedlite Transmitter that comes with the built-in flash.  I have a 580 and a 430, and thanks to theSpeedliter's Handbook: Learning to Craft Light with Canon Speedlitesby Syl Arena, my flash game has accelerated to new levels because of this feature.  I still have somewhat of a hard time with the flashes having to be within the line of sight of the camera, but I'll manage fine for now.  It works flawlessly in-doors though.Overall, this camera is great.  Sorry if I got too technical with the benefits of this camera, but this camera just works!  If you are just starting as a photographer, or if you are an enthusiast like myself, you won't go wrong with this purchase.  Either way, I think that we as Canoners, or something, should really take advantage of the wireless flash abilities that this body, and others after this one, has built in, even though Canon seems to be the last to integrate this ability.  Get the camera!

This is a bit of an unusual touchscreen stylus. If you are looking for a pen-style stylus this is not it.It is thicker than most and shorter than many. It is around 4 1/4" inches (11cm) long, weighs around 0.3 ounces. The stylus is thick, it ranges from 2cm at the tip up to 4cm around the clip area. Because of its thickness, it may not fit in many stylus loop holders. It has a built-in recessed clip that is not designed to work in the conventional fashion of a pen clip. I haven't found a practical use for the clip's recessed design.However, I was surprised that this stylus had its practical uses. I found it useful for page turns on e-ink e-book readers with touchscreens, especially since most of them (sadly) don't have physical page turn buttons. Because it's shorter and thicker, it's easier to hold than a skinnier pen-style stylus. It is also good for "stabbing" around the edges of touchscreens perhaps from five to eight inches long. On larger tablets, for example on 10-inch tablets, many will find it too short if you are reaching beyond the edges.The packaging is easy to open with your fingers. Inside you will find the stylus sitting on a near-disposable pen-holder but if you like, you can save and reuse/repurpose the packaging.

Upon first purchase, I almost sat down to share my initial enthusiasm for these seemingly superbly made bags with the Amazon community.  Now that 4 months have passed of one shopping trip per week with ten bags my praise has become nothing less than utter outrage.  They are ripping along the seams and holes have grown larger with each use!  The cardboard insert in the bottom seems to do more damage than good as too often these themselves have been the cause of the holes!  Once I dispensed with the inserts, the bags have continued to split along the stitching and can no longer be trusted to hold groceries.  They have also become impossible to fold and end up in a balled-up pile of worthless trash in the corner of my vehicle's trunk!  Yes, the reinforced handle is excellent and they do not break.  Yet, the bags are shredding and are basically a tremendous, total waste of money!  To say I am disappointed with this product is a huge understatement!  More to the point are feelings of complete disgust at the fraud perpetrated upon a well-meaning public by the con artists who take advantage of those of us who are making an attempt to protect our environment.  Instead, we will be adding to the landfills with these useless bags and watching our much needed funds disappear like the dust rising behind the bulldozers burying the ever growing mountain of waste to which the tattered material of these tissue-like bags are making a substantial contribution!

No, I was expecting problems based on the reviews. I had Lasership deliver my console which in my area handle their stuff better than UPS. I checked the HDMI port and it looked fine. I purposely booted the PS4 without the HDTV turned on and it still booted fine. I received notification of the system update but PSN was too unreliable due to everyone trying to do updates. So I updated the PS4 via USB.* I would like to pass something along. I read that the PS4 runs hotter if you stand the console. The reason being you are blocking one of the vent slots where cool air enters. The vent slots are on both sides of the machine. On idle with the console just idling I noticed a lot of heat on top of the PS4. I then placed the PS4 flat and there was a noticeable difference regarding heat. It runs cooler idling and playing games. *Onto the UI. This is why I gave it 4 stars instead of 5. The UI is slightly more confusing than the UI in the PS3. Sony tried to streamline the UI but now you have to figure out where they put stuff and when you find it you at times ask WHY did you put it there? To redeem codes you go to PSN which is fine but to download a demo you have to search for the game then once you find it, it will give you the option to buy or download the demo.Themes: There are none nor can you change the color of the themes.Auto Updates and Uploads: I read complaints how individuals have been kicked out of SP games because the system has tried to do an update to the game. Sony now treats games as "Apps" just like Android and iOS. I decided to turn off 'Auto Updates and Uploads". I'll monitor it and see it I have any issues while playing games.Mandatory Installs: I was dissapointed to learn that just like the Xbox One you have to install the entire game to the hard drive. I thought this was just a Microsoft thing but alas Sony requires the same thing so i'll need to look at expansion in the future. To Sony's credit you will be able to plug in a USB external drive and install and play your games from that.Overall I really like the PS4, I can't say it's going to be durable based on all the problems certain gamers have had but at least for me Sony has always built reliable gaming devices. Are they perfect? No but I tend to get 3-4 years of service which is fine by me. I still have a working 60GB Phat Launch PS3 and a Launch PS3 Slim. I'll also state that I like the Xbox brand as well so i'm trying to be fair and objective.So overall my PS4 experience has gone well. I really feel bad for those that got a bad console and hopefully it will be happy gaming for everyone. :)

The Bamboo is a great entry level pad that will control all of the functions in photo editing programs, really in all of the computer's functions.  It literally replaces a mouse.  And it is way more comfortable after a time than a mouse, more convienient and more functional, plus you get the "tablet".  Sign your name to documents on the web,just like writing with a pen, draw pictures, create your own desighns for cards and so on.  The pen functions like a mouse so, you can select for ex: paint brush in blue, and start painting on a blank page. But the stylus, like a pencil, is way easier to control and be precise with than a mouse.  You start with a blank page and create whatever you want, literally drawing and painting your vision.  Then send it to Grandma for Christmas after you create a card!  I will admit, it takes a little getting used to, there are more buttons (cuz it will do more functions than a mouse) and you have to get used to holding the stylus the correct distance from the pad, so the pad will correctly detect the stylus.  That distance is adjustable for your comfort and yes, you can put your hand right on the pad, or at least I can mine, without anything showing up where my hand is . . . ie, the inputs are truly triggered by the stylus, not pressure.  In fact I have mine set so the stylus doesn't even touch the pad to start making controls or marks, then the closer I get to the pad up to and including touching the pad (the harder you press the thicker the line or brush stroke gets)the line gets thicker.  But if I'm surfing the internet I can do all the mouse functions without ever touching the pad, making it last longer. The pad also has touch controls, sort of similar to some of the smart phones? But THIS pad responds with different fingers, up to four touches at one time.  (one finger touch selects, two fingers zoom if you pinch and open a new window if you swipe and so on.  I will admit I'm not a fan of the touch on this table, it only seems to be really accurate with what I'm trying to do and what I get about half the time.  But this is a super fun, creative way to kill your mouse.  believe me, once you try the pad, I mean try the dang thing and get used to it, you'll never go back to a mouse.  One last thing, in photo editing the stylus allows you to get very close, umm intimate control over your post functions.  The stylus is so much more accurate than the mouse, working on a photo is just like working on a hand drawn picture it's that accurate.  And less than a hundred bucks,  rediculous!  I also recommend the wireless so you're not tied to the computer.  I like to curl on the couch with the pad, the monitor fifteen feet away at the desk, and I'm just drawing or working. Freedom!

I first used these on a 20-hour flight, and they are comfortable enough that I left them in even when I wasn't listening to music, they minimized the drone of the engines and passengers enough that I could snooze very well. The only "discomfort" when wearing them for these extended periods is that empty-ears feeling when you remove them. They are light and with the included 6 different shaped rubber pads, you can find one that fits you best. If you have trouble getting them in properly, it helps if you pull the top of your ear upwards to help insert them fully.Since that flight, I've been using them near-daily for a couple weeks now, I've decided there's two ways to listen with these.Method #1 is to turn the volume down as low as you can and you'll have good, clear background music. With the noise elimination, you can still hear your music clearly and have reduced noise from everything around you. Mids and highs are good and clear at this level, but there is no kick to the bass at all. This is how I usually listen, since I have to be able to hear when people call for me in the office. Whenever I take them off, I am assaulted by how much louder the ambient noise is than the music I've been listening to. When putting them back in after being away, the music sounds very quiet, and takes a minute for my ears to return to that level sensitivity.A warning with this method: your ears will be so trained at the lower level that if you eat tortilla chips in this method, you will be deafened.Method #2 is to turn it up to a medium level so that they're "level" with noise around you. They'll sound like normal conversation volume, but with the isolation, it'll sound louder than it really is, and the bass will kick very, very well. I do this so rarely that whenever I do, it surprises me how fantastic they reproduce music. I have worn them this way when jogging and they work quite well.To take these up to full volume is to simulate a live rock concert, and I do not recommend it. They get LOUD.A final note - the earbud holder that comes with them is almost as good an engineering feat as the headphones themselves. It is made of a very soft plastic/rubber with rubber cups to hold the buds. This keeps them clean while in a pocket or your bag, since even the cleanest ears is going to make them slightly sticky and pick up lint, crumbs or dust from the environment.
It looks fantastic on the outside, but the most important feature is poorly constructed. The CD/DVD holder is sewn so close that after putting half of the CDS is hard to keeping adding more,

W A R N I N G:  A N O T H E R....TYPE OF ICE-CUBE TRAY IS JUST AS BAD AS THIS ONE...BUT IN A DIFFERENT WAY.  PLEASE...D O ..N O T..BUY ANY ICE-CUBE TRAYS MADE OF SILICONE!  I HAVE REVIEWED THE ONE I BOUGHT...SOLD BY 'GET ORGANIZED'.....BUT I FEEL NO-ONE SHOULD GO THROUGH THE AGONY I WENT THROUGH, WITH A N Y SILICONE ICE-CUBE TRAY.  AS FOR T H I S ONE...........................M A I N....R E V I E W..........................I, too, have had nothing but bad results from these sad-little-pretend-to-be-ice-cube trays!  Now, thanks to my fellow-reviewers-in-agony, I at least know WHY I had so much trouble!  (Thanks, guys!)  STIFF trays, which just WON'T twist enough, easily enough, to get the little, (TINY, TEENSY-WEENSIE LITTLE), cubettes out of the tray, to get them where you want them!I mean, it SEEMED like a good idea...  Lacking an ice-crusher, teeny-tiny ice cubes seemed THE way to make a drink cold, fast.  Only, the tray here is made of such STIFF plastic, the cubes just WON'T come out.  Unless you run hot water on the back of the ice-cube trays, and get them all in the sink, (which has to be pretty clean, beforehand, for one to do this!  But the cubes are SO tiny, that the warm water makes them almost invisibly small....and difficult as h__l to use!!!!DO NOT use these trays to make ice cubes!  Do NOT waste your money on them!  If you have already bought them, used them, and cannot return them....well, there are more expensive things that waste one's money, and you can comfort yourself that these weren't TOO expensive.  Comfort yourself by reminding yourself that great men, too, had their mistakes. (Abraham Lincoln failed at EVERYTHING before his first success, Thomas Edison failed about 1500 times, before he created the first light bulb, Harry Truman was cheated on by his parter in haberdashery -- and Millicent Fenwick's mother tragically died on the Titanic, when Millicent was just a little girl....)  Thinking of these and other tragedies will, I hope, put your purchase of these @!!"*!?! ice-cube trays in perspective!My advice is to drink some nice warm coffee, or tea, (which don't require ice-cubes), get a good night's sleep, and -- as soon as you're able to -- make a list of everything, (and everyone), in your life which makes you  happy.  Your one-time unfortunate purchase, of these admittedly dreadful ice-cube trays, will pale into insignificance, as you concentrate on what makes your life good, and how to make it even better : )

I recently tested this lens out during some light sports activities event in the park. I wanted something small and light but fast and tele. On my 7D it gave a good focal length, equivalent of about 135mm on a full frame. That was actually a bit too long and I had to back up a bit but no big issue. I wanted to freeze the action and so was using f/2 - 2.8 mostly. I already know f/1.8 isn't great so I avoided it. Here's how I got on -The lens focused really fast, no issues there. In fact its fast enough that I could use one shot AF and shoot before the subject moved a significant distance. Truly impressive.Bokeh was lovely too. The distracting background just melted away. Loved it.At f/2 the dof is super shallow, just a few cm in most cases. I had a lot of blurry shots and out of focus shots. Chalk that down to user error. I shoulda used f/3.5 or more for the close ups.But I wanted to really push this lens at the wide end so I just kept it there. I had enough decent shots thanks to the 7Ds AF so I wasn't too bothered.Now, it wasn't until I looked at the images on my laptop that I noticed a lot of purple fringing. Visible even without zooming 100%! There was also some green and red CA too. Now, I know I can fix this BUT I don't want to HAVE TO fix it. even 20 pics that need correcting is time consumig let alone 200! Not ideal if you're a heavy shooter.Skin on bright background was the worst culprit next to metal and reflective sharp lines. No other lens I have used gives this much purple fringing. It's ridiculous!That said, as a f/2.8 prime lens for portraits it is a phenomenal lens for the price.  But as a f1.8 its not that great. Seems like a waste. Though it is very sharp and the color reproduction is good too. Maybe not as good as the 50mm f/1.8 II which is freakishly good for a cheap lens!There aren't many other options for an alternative. I think there is a Zeiss but it's MF only. So that leaves the very expensive L option.If I was a pro and did a lot of stuff at wide apertures maybe I'd get the L instead. As it is I don't make money from photography so a bit of purple fringing for cheapness sake is acceptable, for now. I will use this lens to practice my technique more. When I feel it is no longer meeting my needs I'll think about the L!

Cant say I was thrilled with this product.  its very inefficient, and wastes huge amounts of juice.its quite large, and other centrifugal juicers have become very compact and small now - so one might trade off size for efficiency and it would be a fair trade. but in this case you dont even get compactness . you just get inefficiency.the engineering was not well though through, and therefore cleaning it is a chore, especially if you are like me and juice 2+ times a day.other major drawbacks are :1) juice comes out foaming (because of the speed of the motor).  this destroys nutrients by causing micro-oxidation - true its not going totally destroy the nutritional value of the juice or anything, but if you have other juicing systems which DONT do that, then why not opt for the superior system?2) the motor runs hot, and that also has the potential to destroy nutrients.3) if you make juice for more than 2-3 people at once, you'll need to stop and clean this half way through because the waste collection area fills up fast. - and as i mentioned above, cleaning this is a choreoverall - its FINE , but definitely not a smart or well thought through system. there are better systems out there.I have switched to an Omega juicer and it doesnt have any of the drawbacks, and it has significant advantages over this.

I’ve gone through numerous bath scales over the years. Some vary wildly on weight readings, others are hard to read, and some just don’t hold up past a few months.Previously, I’ve reviewed the Ozeri Precision Digital Bath Scale and it’s still a great scale.The Ozeri WeightMaster II has some additional useful features:• Body Mass Index Calculation• Weight Differential Detection• Color Alert• Auto Recognition• 440 pound Weight Capacity• Storage of 30 Weights per userI really love the color alert feature. It’s easy to see if you have a weight loss or gain with a display that changes colors (green if you have lost and red if you have gained). Unlike many scales, you don’t need to scroll through to find your user profile. Once you have programmed it, just stepping on it recognizes you based on your previous weight. It has a large capacity and is slip-resistant for added safety.The only real downside is that it takes a tad longer than my other Ozeri scale to register my weight. But if you’re looking for the added features, it’s a good scale overall.I received the product mentioned above for free from the manufacturer. Regardless, I only recommend products or services I use personally and believe will be good for my readers.

As more and more of my computers start supporting USB 3.0 I felt it was time to replace my smaller USB 2.0 Flash drives with a single larger, faster one. I put of getting one to see what Sandisk would release but I balked and the prices Sandisk wanted for their drives.  Instead, based on its reviews I chose this one.  After receiving it I ran a few performance tests on both USB 2.0 ports and USB 3.0 ports. All testing was done using Crystal Disk Mark with 5 runs of 1000MB eachUSB 2.0 PortsSandisk Cruzer 8GB (My older usb 2.0 drive)Read 19.45 MB/sWrite 4.75 MB/sLexar JumbDrive TritonRead 34.81 MB/sWrite 30.50 MB/sAs you can tell this drive offers a significant improvement even on computers without USB 3.0 ports.USB 3.0 PortsLexar JumbDrive TritonRead 179.3 MB/sWrite 151.2 MB/sIt was a bit shocking how fast files transferred over USB 3.0 after being so used to slow drives and slow ports.Pros:+ Excellent speeds+ Attractive appearance+ Good solid feelCons:- Price?Yes, this drive is more expensive per GB that others you can find but in this case I think that the adage "You get what you pay for" really does hold true.

This case is a good case for the HTC Incredible.  It fits well, is easy to put on and take off, and protects the phone well. My only complaint is that it is a little bulky, which is a problem if you have the car mount.  An Incredible using this case will no longer fit on the car mount-you have to remove the case.  Not too big a deal though-the mount is only worth using if you are driving a long distance or don't know where you are going, so you take it off then, and in short or routine drives you just put it on the console or the passenger seat.  The buttons and the headphone and charger ports are well exposed so that isn't a problem.  All in all I recommend this case if you want to have a case for your Incredible.  With that said, if I find a case that does the job as well but allows me to still fit it on the car mount, I'm going to switch to that.

This 64 GB mini TravelDrive failed to install properly.  Unfortunately I had not attempted to use it within several months of purchase, therefore I needed to rely upon the 2 year warranty.There is no simple warranty service or request.  Instead, you must communicate via email with a customer firm that supports Memorex and products from other companies.  They typically take 2-3 days to respond to an email.  Not surprisingly, the initial and continued advice shows that it is canned and generic.  For instance, 'make sure the lock switch is in the on position' -- there is NO lock switch on this USB drive.  Or, 'contact Microsoft to help you install USB drivers'!I am on my third detailed email with them and they are still trying to determine "what can be done."If you buy a Memorex storage device, then make sure a reputable seller stands behind it because the support service seems more interested in avoiding warranty on a device that should either run or not run.
