Reasonably priced coffee maker that works wonderfully and keeps the coffee at the perfect temperature.  I have no complaints whatsoever.  There were some negative remarks about the black coffee maker and that they couldn't tell where the water level was (because the reservoir is black).  It's not a problem with the white coffee maker.  I'm very pleased with my purchase.

We have only had this machine for 48 hours and EVERYONE in our house is breathing better. We let in run in the lower part of our home for 24 hours and noticed a fresher, easier to breathe air over the weekend. The next 24 hours we ran it upstairs (through the bedrooms) and both my husband and I woke up and immediately noticed that it was easier to breathe! I really wasn't expecting it to make such a big difference so quickly. It runs very quietly and is responsive to odors, adjusting itself since we've let it run on auto so far. We haven't checked the filters yet but will update when we do.

A great Camara and very good price too. Definitely if you want to take good pictures like a professional camara. And comfortable to traveling, not to big, fit in your pocket, a little heavy and the battery in video mode get outs to fast, you definitely had to buy another extra battery for recordings. And memory cards of course.But is really nice camara I Buy the pink color, is NOT RED, is Fucsia/Pink.
This is okay. Not a bad product. Kinda weird looking. I'd just get a normal cord next time. Make sure it suits you first.

My wife and I bought this unit along with a spare battery and CaseLogic case for our honeymoon in Europe. It went with us everywhere for 11 days in pockets and strapped to wrists. In the process, it got banged around a lot and rained on, and never had any issues. It was always there for us and started right up in 2 seconds or less; we never missed anything. We read reviews and accordingly set it to 720p, 60 frames per second. We bought 2 16GB class 6 SDHC cards for it and each one lasted for about 3 hours 50 minutes at these resolution settings. The video looks amazing! So much so that my home PC doesn't have anough power to play it smoothly without caching. The only very minor gripe I would share about this unit is that the battery only lasts about 50 minutes if you leave it on and frequently start and stop recording, which you probably will at an event, etc. Buy a spare battery! We got a wide range of stuff, from the Lippizaner Stallions in Vienna to the beerhalls at Oktoberfest, and everything looks like it was shot on a pro movie camera. Obviously, we avoided a lot of low-light situations since this beast has no flash, but it did well on lit objects at night like cathedrals, clock towers, etc. Oh, and I also pre-emptively put a small piece of GPS protective screen film on the screen before the trip, so I can't speak to the other reviews of the screen scratching easily. HIGHLY recommend this unit. At this price it can't be touched, and I'm glad we used it so our memories are so perfect of our trip.

The ZN9 is a great addition to my Zune arsenal.  It packs easily and it and sounds great in my hotel room, office and bedroom.  Using the aux input, it can be used with any MP3 player.  On the road I sometimes plug my laptop into it to use as a powerful eternal speaker.Gripes:The Alarm - It doesn't seem loud enough.The Remote - It's optional.  You'll have to order it from the site.  It's the card style, doesn't control all functions and scrolling through music can be painfully slow compared to using the Zune's touchpad.  Also, it can be tricky to learn.The Controls - To change most settings, you have to turn the right wheel ipod style.  The wheel feels like it would break if you push too hard.That being said.  I am very happy with it and I highly recommend it to anyone who wants to expand the usefullness of their Zune.

Right now I'm typing this out with a tablet stylus that cost me $1 and it works so much better than this Wacom bamboo. This bamboo simply had poor registration. I had to pass down hard. Not use a screen protector... And it was still inconsistent.

This is just like the OEM item I got with my Samsung phone. Works perfectly, in fact I ordered 3. Works even with an extension cable.  The only thing I could possibly wish for is a longer cable, but a standard 1M cable is decent. Good quality, good deal.
Product holds my iPhone much more securely on motorcycle than I could have ever imagined.  The rubber isolates the vibration form the cycle incredibly well.I wouldn't hesitate to purchase another.
This product is useless... if fell apart.  It's hard to ues and it went straight into our trash - hopefully it can be recycled!

I have purchased and used several of the AmazonBasics cables with the original iphone cable and now with the lightning cable.  I found these cables to be of great quality and I like them better than the Apple cables that came with the phone and ipad.  I've never had one of these cables go bad.
Fast data speeds, large capacity and worked right off the bat in my Samsung SIII. Love the fact it holds my large music and audio books library on the device.

The cameras arrived promptly and were will packaged. The installation was not simple as the setup software has errors and their technical support thought the cameras were defective. After some work arounds, and some trial and errors working with their software applications, I did get both cameras working both wired and wireless. I have a number of earlier DLink IP cameras that were easier to install. Once I got the 5000 series working the are great and are accessible along with my 932L DLink cameras. I wanted a camera that could scan, with IR and these work. Much more expensive that other competing cameras but they work. Technical support is noit very good for the inexperienced user but netowrking is not for the feint of heart.

The cable has a good "quality" feel to it and looks very nice. However, the connection with my brand new Surface RT does leave a little to be desired. You have to keep still or else the connection with the TV will be broken . . . even the relatively slightest move. But for $7.99 what could one really expect?
Amazing. Faster than my previous Keurig. I've got this one at home! Would  be great for a small office but it's even better for me and my friends!
I really liked the phone case I received.  It was in great shape, very affordable and was very easy to attach.

I ordered the UPC RBC109-UBC based on other reviews here to replace an APCRBC109 replacement for my venerable BR1500LCD that had a life of just over 1 year.  I can't remember if I ordered that one from APC or another source, so I can't place blame.  The battery that came with the UPS had over a 3-year life.  Hoping this one well do as well.  I ordered it from Amazon because I needed it quickly and could take advantage of the Prime 2-day shipping.  It was available from other sources, but once you add in shipping, it was a wash, and since I'm paying for Prime anyway.....  It arrived as promised, came well packed and fully charged, so I was able Install it and use it immediately.  Overall no issues so far.Only possible negative, depending on where you live, is that the battery comes with a recycling return label, but you have to pay shipping.  My county has a recycling center for batteries, so I'm good.  If yours doesn't, please be responsible and use the label.

Big and clunky. Short cable makes attachment difficult. No volume control, which is absurd. Off switch does not just turn off the amp- it disconnects you entirely. Fixed 2X amplification. On light difficult to see.Other than that, it does work.This is a market begging for a superior product.
as described and so cute, my niece loved it

Do not buy the generic rolls. These are more expensive, but you get what you pay for.1. These are thicker and more durable. I have vacuum sealed meat with pointy bones, and other objects that have broken cheaper bags. These hold up under all but the most severe abuse.2. Notice that one side is smooth and the other is rough? Those little channels help squeeze out the last bits of air because they keep the bag ever so slightly away from the food on one side. You will get a tighter seal this way.

This is the best Humidifier out there.  I have used wick versions and spent a fortune and decided I had enough in spending anymore money.  I researched out the Air-O-Swiss and found it to be the best out there.  The upkeep can be a bit annoying; however, I still believe in this unit and have bought several.  The things that I have found to be the most important is to remember to clean it when it tells you, if dust starts to get bad in your house -- replace the Demineral Granules, if you have algae growing in the base -- get a new silver stick, if the tank is leaking at the bottom due to a crack -- replace it, the base is fine.I hope this helps!
As soon as people saw these on my phone they all wanted one. They fit on the home button perfectly, and are not cheaply made so they stick on without falling off.

Keep in mind when purchasing ANYTHING brand new, it's going to have a funny smell... This definately did. BUT, air it out properly, and it's just fine. This matress is SOOOO comfortable! It's like sleeping on a cloud!I have spine issues, and I can't sleep on springs, because my back kills me when I wake up. But this... I have NO pain when I wake up in the morning! This film fits very well and provides a good anti-glare coat that makes it easier to use in bright light. It does not impair sensitivity on the touch screen, and sticks well with static. It's also quite easy to remove and comes with multiple pieces.The individual films are as good as some single films I've seen for KF/KFHD that cost similar amounts, so 3 is a good deal. There's a cutout for the camera and application is simple via the standard film application. I didn't use a card or smoother, it was pretty easy to press out the few bubbles that formed.The pack includes a microfiber cloth.

I am so happy I ordered this to replace my Shark steam mop. No more pumping the handle or awkwardly pouring water into the mop. This comes with a removable water tank, 3 settings for steam and works so much better than my Shark did. Highly recommend!

I ordered this mouse to replace one that quit working based mainly on the price. It worked right out of the box on my mac book with no problems. I've only been using it for a few days and am already looking for a new one with smoother sides.

I am using this sleeve to protect a new 15.6" HP laptop I got a couple of weeks ago.  The laptop is mostly going to be used at home, not moved around much for work or traveling... that will be exceedingly rare, so I think the facts that it is "just" a thin neoprene sleeve and it is too big height wise for the computer is OK.  The intent for me was to have something simple, pretty, and effective to keep it together with another item, a Logitech lapdeskLogitech Portable Lapdesk N315, and, protected from pets, spills, etc. when not in use.  It actually is pretty snug width wise and now the height issue doesn't matter because the Logitech lapdesk is very thin and fits in the sleeve with the laptop no problem.  It just makes the sleeve fit more snugly, which is an improvement over just having the laptop in the sleeve.

My old unpowered antenna was able to pick up all my local channels, but I was tired of walking across the room to reposition it when I changed the channels.  I bought the RCA antenna, but it picks up fewer channels than my old one.  I'm going back to the unpowered antenna.

I bought a tea sampler to go with it and it is well worth the extra money.  She is keeping track of which tea flavor she prefers.Others have noted it stopped working for them after a few months. But doesn't work to significantly help with sound. Doesn't work with my phone if in a case. Should have known.

This moderately priced vacuum does what it's supposed to do. It's likeable because it does a decent job as do the attachments. The easy assembly, easy-to-use factor and cord rewind are the pluses.It's noisy, doesn't have enough extensions for high ceilings or ledges and I'm not impressed with the Fabreeze bag. I wouldn't pay extra for that in replacement bags which are small, hard to find, and only one scent is available. The replacements scented and unscented are primarily sourced at Walmart or the Bissell website, which had some quirks at the time I was researching the vacuum and supplies. Bissell Customer Support is OK.Seeing the dirt path wasn't impressive, NOR something that piqued my interest. Replacing the bag in the small space wasn't fun either. Overall, this vacuum is just OK.
I ordered this for my nightstand and it works well with my Droid Bionic with the case on it. I like using my phone for my alarm both at home and while I travel.

This is a great phone! I had the Galaxy Note 3 but live in a rural area and it got practically zero reception. It also dropped calls and wifi a lot so apparently the antenna's in the Note 3 aren't that great. So I got this phone and LOVE IT. The screen is bigger than the Note 3 and is crystal clear. The front facing speakers are phenomenal and my friends and I can't believe how nice watching Utube videos is. It's really great to be able to finally have a phone with amazing speakers. The camera seems OK so far, very very good low lighting pictures. Phone seems fast and snappy. The build of the phone is a little heavy but I like the solid feeling in hand as opposed to the plastic of the Note 3. Best thing? Sitting on my couch where the Note 3 would have ZERO reception, the One Max has 3g and 3 bars. So, my problem is solved. Thanks HTC!

Certainly one of the best lunch containers I've had.The larger half of the container easily accommodates a sandwich or hotdogs, or a serving of pasta, salad, etc.The two smaller compartments are good for chips, vegetables, rice, and general side dishes.Pros: Perfect size, love the design, single piece construction with three compartments. Easy to clean.Cons: Not spill proof. On more than one occasion I've got to work and noticed my food had leaked. Also, you have to be sure you open it the right way, the divider only covers the larger section.

Bought it for the wife, replacing a similar case like this one. I doubt this one will last any longer then the previous case. Does exactly what aftermarket batteries and a matching charger should, namely they work right and saved money over OEM.Pros* Quick charge (under a couple hours, typically)* Fit is exact (no shifting due to too small, or can't fit due to too large)* No quality issues (rippling, leakage, damaged contacts)* Feels high-quality and well-built* Charger works fine* Charger uses 120V mains current plus has a 12V DC adapterCons* None observed.Other thoughtsUseful to have extras for this camera, so I was happy to order and receive these.

Got this for my mom for Christmas and she LOVES it. We were all amazed at how quickly it slices up stuff! In no time at all, a raw potato was cut up. Speaking of potatoes, she learned this machine's power by accidentally pureeing instead of mashing potatoes. Live and learn! The pieces are easy to clean and are dishwasher friendly. Parts are easy to put together and operate as well.****WARNING**** The blades are EXTREMELY sharp!!! My mom barely touched them and it sliced into her. Be careful when handling the blades!!!

This compact little microwave provides all the basic features needed for everyday cooking.  It is reasonably quiet and seems to cook evenly.  Its power allows me to cook in the middle of the time recommended on frozen vegetables. It wins no beauty prizes, but I am pleased with the price.
Excellent product quality and looks just as in the photo ! It is comfortable! Recommended ! Thanks very much bye

Seems everything requires a USB Charger now. I need them at work, in my home office, in my bedroom and don't forget the wife and kids. I have bought 6 of these chargers. I have them every where. With the correct USB cables, I can charge 2 things at the time from 1 outlet. I have never had one to fail. If I need more, this is what I will buy. This works with anything that will charge from an USB port, Not Just iPhone.

This device is not perfect but it does a good job.  The software needs improvment; however, it is much better than noting.  I live at 3209.  It never gets the number exactly right. Sometimes 3150 sometines 3287.  It displays an old message about deleting log information which it later tells you NOT to do.  Considering it has save me about $1,000 on my taxes since I got it I can tolerate the glitches.  I hope GiSteq will update the software soon.

I almost feel bad about not giving this thing 5 stars like almost everyone else. I feel 2 stars is being charitable. My initial impression after opening the package was that this thing is just a cheap piece of plastic. It really can't cost the manufacturer more that fifty-cents to make it and should sell for about 6-8 dollars. At 24 it is extremely overpriced. It is not even great plastic, the part that you slide the food on wobbles and bends as you move the food.The "dial" on the bottom doesn't look like it will last long and I have to agree with another reviewer that anything less than the thickest setting isn't very useful. The first thing I sliced was a cucumber. The little white plastic food guard was useless, I first had to slice the cucumber into several pieces. I used the second thinnest setting and most slices came out tapered instead of round. The same with an onion I sliced at the same setting.I'll probably end up using it from time to time, but won't be able to help thinking I paid way too much.

This is an entry price point that gives you many accessories to learn as you use it.  The unit is quiet, light weight and ergonomic to hold.  Being corded means you'll never run out of battery.  If you're looking for a trimmer this may fill the needs of a more expensive alternative.
It is neither big or small so I can't recommend the product.  I understand the size is provided and I should have measured.  I am also not a fan of the memory foam.

Great case I love it haven't had any problems it protects my phone wonderfully. I love the color as well and the feel of the case. It has these grippers on the side so you can hold onto the phone and want drop it so easily.

I love these things!  They are easy to fill and read, easy to pinch and pour, handle hot or cold and clean easily by hand or in the dish washer.  They nest so you can have three measuring cups and they don't take up a tremendous amount of room in a tiny kitchen.  I purchased them with the lids which seal really well - saving even more space because I don't have to save butter tubs for leftovers!  I love them enough they were my default - or even first choice - gift for Christmas last year!  Everyone I gave them to thanked me and had great things to say about them - including the "college kids" who usually don't say much about anything!

The price appeared to be reasonable for a couple of pedestrian chairs so I took a chance and was well rewarded.  They arrived fully assembled (joy,oh joy) well packed and in perfect condition and, by golly, they are actually attractive!  Add to that the fact that my wife and I find them surprisingly comfortable.  And finally, there also is a pleasurable sturdiness about them that we truly appreciate.  Huzzah, huzzah--we picked a winner!
Best case ever. I will not ever buy a phone without having an OtterBox case on it. My wife has this case so does my Ipad 2

I've got a drawer full of various Bluetooth headsets, for me it's the equivalent of a closet of shoes.  I found this one to be one of the better ones and a definite improvement over their predecessor.  Once the fit is solid, they sound very good although not as good as the jaybirds bluebuds x or the freedom sprints.  as with any of these though, it's a matter of can you get that good fit.  the freedom sprints are the model that most consistently give me the solid fit while working out and while the bludbud x's have better sound, the design change impacted my fit adversely.  the Plantronics fall in between on the fit scale, I have one side of the head that I just cant get them to stay in once I begin to sweat but I love the weight so I go to them quite often. Because their is no clip for the excess band like on the bluebud x, that becomes a issue as well as that can get annoying while working out as well.  The bluebud x are such a great set in the end but for me, the fit was horrendous when working out so at half the price, the Plantronics were a better buy for me.  the battery life improvement was great and was a consideration in not going back to the freedom sprints too.I wish the charge case was sold separately on amazon, I bought mine before the combo was offered.  But that said, overall the fit for me was fair, the lightness was great and the sound was impressive for a workout set.  The fits on these are so subjective person to person though, it's impossible to recommend any of these.  What fits me well will fit you horrendously.  End of the day, find a good return policy and start experimenting and spend some good time with each set's various methods of attaching.I never rank any of these on call quality, call quality on all of these sets suck and they just aren't meant for that.

I bought this cake pop maker last December for our niece Clementine for her seventh birthday.  Her mom has told me repeatedly that Clementine loves it!It does require adult supervision if you're using it with the kidlets, but it's easy to use and clean up.The recipes it comes with are also tasty (I've tried a few), and the cake pops themselves cook up in less than eight minutes.Great purchase for both kids and adults!

Samsung Galaxy S4I decided to give a matte screen protector a try, since a clear one holds a ton of finger print oils that's readily visible when the screen is off and sometimes visible with the screen on.Application wasn't difficult.  I can pretty much apply any screen protector without any air or dust bubbles on my first try...lots of practice.BUT I will say that although the anti-glare properties are quite good and there's no annoying "squeaks" when I swipe my finger on the screen, it does distort the screen quite a bit.  White becomes speckles of red green and blue.  It isn't horrendously bad, but it's quite a significant trade off.
I accidentally ordered for the 4 instead of the 3. Just a little snipping and all good! Arrived quickly and had no issues with the company and such.
My searches for a portable Wireless Speaker showed only very expensive items until I came across this Spracht.  This item is great!  I love using it. The sound is pretty decent, too.

This Circulon Genesis  is nice looking and is a decent offering of a basic cookware set at a good value.  I love the lightweight aluminum.  It is so much nicer to deal with than some of my heavier pieces I own--since my cookware is kept below the counter and as I get older it seems to get heavier.  So, in my mind, this would make the perfect gift for not only a young person out on their own for the first time, but also, for seniors with restricted strength.I've only had a chance to use the cookware for a short while now and will update later if anything noteworthy happens, but so far, I've enjoyed using this set.  The largest pot is perfect for my huge batches of chicken and dumplins or for boiling pasta!  That is definitely my favorite piece from this set.  The skillets have been great for frying and best of all, for me, scrambled eggs that don't stick to the sides and bottom too much and don't need any oil or butter added.
Purchased to go with my I pad. I use it sometimes when I have a lot of writing to do.
Can't get rid of all bubbles when applying it to the screen. The paper sticker is hard to remove; it leaves some glue on the surface of protector.

it's cute, substantial, nice quality, and the design is elegant. It won't disappoint you. It could just have easily been cheap or clunky, very  mass produced looking and it is not at all. we are pleased.  especially for the price.

The quality is great for ink'd headphones(Skullcandy focuses on looks, not quality). They broke within 10 months, which is decent considering it went through 2 wash cycles without being ruined. My ears are a little tiny for these headphones though, so i bought Sony Stereo headphones instead

I like this case overall.  It offers great protection for normal bumps and general use.What I don't like is that the case is slippery and it's difficult to press the side button under the rubber button.  Takes a big of effort to do so.  If there was more rubber on the sides of the case and along the back it would be a great case.Amazon did a great job shipping out the product. Just sent this one back. DOA!They are convenient but work for about 2 years.Why do we keep replacing?Well Built in insinkerators only last 3years but they cost $160 + $100 Install.=$260 vs $40 fordispenser boiler.In order to make these last even 2+ years you need to have a heavy duty timer to boil and shut down.The price we pay for convenience.. This case is it really thin I really like that it was then if only nothing was there and the mat feeling was awesome then again it's so thin that it was really fragile and I dropped it and the case broke

Sadly this lost 2 of the 3 fans after only 2 days. I really liked it when it worked.When it was working, it significantly cooled my ASUS EEE 1015. My notebook runs burning hot and the fans cool it if I reverse the stand to put one fan under the hottest spot.  I have 2 more complaints:1). The led light is nifty looking, but an off switch or a cooling stand without light would be better.2). The legs are about an inch too short. On any less-than-perfect surface (do you compute on a couch or from bed?) the fans bottom-out, hitting the surface and stopping. I have fixed this by adding cardboard under the legs, but it would be better to have longer legs. Longer legs also give better underside clearance for airflow cooling , allowing the fans to cool more than the current design. I had to turn the stand 180 to position one fan directly under the hottest spot of my PC and that also messes with the legs because the top 2 are higher than the bottom, for a slanting desk effect. The leg hight difference is unnecessary and detracts from users positioning the fans to best spot. Making all 4  legs even and about an inch higher would be the best solution.Still. for under $6, if it lasts, this would be a great tool! If you have a laptop that feels as hot as an iron, this truly helps. Id they all break so fast, however, it's a waste of money and resources. I might buy another to test again.
This was the most economical purchase for the capacity of the drive which produced very fast recording speeds both in shooting RAW photos and video. In my opinion it's the best bang for the buck.

The screen protector was fairly easy to put on my iPhone. The two covers protecting the screen protector were kind of difficult to peel away but other than that the rest of the process was easy. The holes for the home button, camera and sensors are large enough that you do not need to be 100% precise to get it to fit with the screen protector.The screen protector is a fingerprint magnet as expected, but a wipe with a microfiber cloth (one is included in the packaging) fixes the issue. Also, for the price these work great and now I have four more to use when this screen protector gets worn out or scratched.
Worked well with my Nokia phone. Would definitely buy another for another device. Very good price compared to other sizes.

The car chargers that they sell at the cell phone companies are so expensive! I think they wanted me to pay $30 for my Samsung Galaxy car charger.  The fact that this has two USB ports is really useful because sometimes I want to charge my iPod and phone at the same time but don't have enough outlets. Last time I went on a road trip with a friend, she also wanted to charger her phone but I needed my phone for the GPS so we chose to just charge one phone. This pretty much eliminates the problem (so I wish I got this sooner!) and now it's pretty spiffy having this. The fact that a tablet can also be charged is a win because it's hard to sometimes find a place to charge your tablet when on the road. I pretty much found that it also charged my phone really efficiently and I liked the LED light around it (it looked elegant) and it worked very well!
I only have the unit to play movies off of one ofmy computer. I had no need for burning with it.Owned this product since around March 2011 andhas not yet failed.

I purchased an official samsung wireless charging back cover for my note3, only to find that there was absolutely NO protective case that would fit it, at least not that I could find.  So although I could charge my device wirelessly, I couldn't use a case at the same time.  This bumper solves that problem!  I do wish there was a full protective case, that  I could use, but in the meantime, this works fine.The bumper fits nicely, and it appears to extend a bit on both the front and back, to provide some protection.  Not as much as a full case, but enough to cover the basics.  A couple of the reviewers mentioned the power button being more difficult to push, and they are absolutely correct.  I have to use a LOT of force to depress it to turn my phone on or to put it to sleep.  Not a fan of that, definitely.  The volume buttons work fine though, so I am wondering if making the power button so difficult is purposeful?  Either way, it takes away from the usability of the bumper....I would have also liked to have seen a few more color choices, I chose the black since my phone has a black face, and I was worried about the white looking odd, and collecting dirt.Overall, this bumper is a decent product, although it could be great with a few changes. And why hasn't anyone come up with a full case to fit the official charging back plate yet???
Looks good and works great. All that's needed is a case for the phone. I hope someone will read this and advise where to get a case.

I purchased my lifeproof case from best buy (for $80 ... yet its $100 here? ...). I was quite skeptical as to whether this case met up to all the hype. So I decided I'd give it a try.The first case I bought failed the water test (very concerning). So I went back to best buy and swapped for another one. The second passed and so I proceeded to install it. After installed it's quite a solid lock on the phone.Now for the sound quality...Audio being played (ringtones, music, game sounds, etc) is muffled a little bit. Not so drastic where you have to turn the volume all the way up. I usually have my iPhone at 5 squares (for the ringer)...I had to put it up to 8 squares and its about the same sound. Not a big deal for me. It can be considerably loud if you want it to be. For the earphone...sound quality is maybe a 1 square difference. Almost no difference. I can hear callers perfectly. As for others that call me, I have had no complaints and I did a test call to my house line and sound came through loud and clear.The sound was the biggest concern for me, but it works almost perfectly.The only complaint I have is the screen protector on the front. There is a slight gap from the iPhone screen and the screen protector. So it feels like almost a TFT pressure screen when using the iPhone.Other than my one complaint, the phone is still fully functional with the full protection. I am an active person so now I won't have to worry about water, sand or dust damage.If you want FULL protection from the elements give it a try. You might like, you might not. If you don't, just return it.

This is the first steam cleaner I've ever owned.  I am going to write one paragraph about my experience with this product, followed by one about the old way of cleaning for comparison.It only takes a minute to assemble the steam cleaner, and a few minutes to heat the water before it's ready for use.  In a matter of seconds it powered through all dirt and gunk stuck on my floors.  Pouring some water into the top and plugging it in is all the prep work it needs.  It even removed dirt stuck in tight corners.  Its height is perfect for getting the area beneath my kitchen cabinets.  The actual work of cleaning two fairly small floors (kitchen and bathroom) took about five minutes, followed by a minute to sweep up what had been pulled up from the floor but not stuck to the cleaning pad.Previously, it took heavy scrubbing to remove stubborn dirt and the various kitchen spills with the sponge mop I used. I had to fill a bucket with water, add chemicals, and move it around as I cleaned.  I could never get the floors as clean as they should be, so they ended up merely acceptable.  My sponge mop couldn't remove dirt built up in tight corners, which meant I had to get down on the bathroom floor and use a brush.  Because the floors never came out truly clean, I was discouraged from even bothering to mop them frequently, instead just sweeping up.If you have never used a steam cleaner before, I urge you to buy this one.  My home is the biggest asset I will ever own, and for a fair price I have a product that makes it much easier to maintain and take pride in.  As someone who loves to cook but hates to clean, I couldn't be happier with this product.
This card is really wonderful especially with the dual monitor support.  I am going to buy another card to use the SLI function.

I had an older model that finally gave out, and we were so used to the touchless soap dispenser that we had to replace it.  I ended up going with another simplehuman pump - the compact one.  I'm very pleased with the choice.Size:  compact and efficient, sleek looking, and unobtrusive.  Doesn't take up much more space that a container of dial soap.  We have the black one.Noise:  VERY quiet- my old one made a loud noise with every soap dispense but this one is noise-free.Functionality:  Excellent.  There is a +/- button on top to control the amount of soap per dispense.  Much better than the old dial style on prior models.  It dispenses the soap with no dripping.  There's a small rubber spout where the soap comes out that prevents it from  dripping after dispensing.  It automatically controls the amount of soap people are using, eliminating waste and excessive product use (kids will sometimes take several pumps of soap from a hand dispenser, which is totally unnecessary!)Cons:  None yet.  I suspect that eventually this unit will stop working like the old one did, but that unit did last for 5+ years.  I have only been putting the kind of soap that is not extra thick or ones that have moisture beads, etc.  This should help keep the pump functioning well and keep it from gumming up.  So far, I haven't noticed any issues with suction preventing it from dispensing soap.Bottom line- highly recommend if you want to try a touchless dispenser.  I like that if I'm handling raw meat or something messy, I don't have to touch anything to clean up.  It's definitely a good value and will last for several years.

I got this to replace an old Roku N1000 that I was happy with, but had some limitations on new channels. So I was looking forward to using this newer generation model that had the same specs as my older one except more built-in memory and of course the newer version of firmware and features. But when I first hooked it up, I found that when I started Netflix the picture appeared to be the lowest quality so I aborted right away not being willing to watch such a low-quality video. I spent much time troubleshooting and going back and forth between the old and new one. I finally figured out that the new Roku LT ALWAYS starts at the low-quality video on a Netflix program and then over several seconds figures out it can handle the HD quality and gradually increases to that level. So while the old N1000 would pause and give 4 dots plus the HD and then have the red buffering bar gradually go to the right until it reaches the end and the program starts in HD on the very first frame, this newer one just does a couple of ticks on the red progress bar (with no dots or HD shown) and then immediately goes to the right and the program starts in crappy mode, but several seconds later cleans up. It seems the emphasis is to get you watching your program as quickly as possible and then clean up the video later. Personally, I think I like the old way better, but this new way is acceptable and works as long as I know what to expect. I have confirmed that this is normal behavior for this player with Roku tech support. And note that this behavior just seems to be on the Netflix channel and not on the Amazon Prime videos which continue to start right up with good 4-dot video quality (which was another reason why I thought the Netflix channel had a problem). Hopefully this will help others upgrading from older boxes. Just be patient on Netflix--it will clear up in less than a minute into the program.

I am using this keyboard as a remote/keyboard for my HTPC.  For that purpose, it does a decent job.  There is nothing about this keyboard that excels and no aspect of it falls flat either.  Here are my thoughts after using it for about 1 month.The connectivity is okay.  If you stay within 6-7 feet using the mini adapter, you'll be fine.  Stray any further than that and you'll want to use the attached extender which boosts the range.  But then who wants to have a long stick sticking out of the front of their HTPC?  You could stick the extension and all in the back of the HTPC, but you lose range by doing that too so why bother?The key layout uses smaller keys than usual and takes a while to get used to.  But the keys just don't have a nice response and feel very flimsy.  The left click button on the top left of the keyboard is a nice touch and works well.  But it begs the question, if they had bothered to think of adding an additional left click button, why didn't they go ahead and add buttons for "play,"  "pause," "stop," etc....  You could program them in as a function key, but then you're left with the hassle of pushing two keys simultaneously for each of those functions.The build quality, although it doesn't feel like it will break straight away, it doesn't exactly inspire much confidence either.  Every plastic bit just feels cheaply made.The touchpad has a decent size and responds well, but it barely qualifies as multi touch.  Don't fall for the misleading advertising.  The only multi touch this keyboard does is vertical scroll. Nothing else. No forward or back. No horizontal scroll, at least not in any of Microsoft's products.  Horizontal scroll does work in Google Chrome which suggests Logitech has been lazy and didn't bother with writing better drivers/software for this keyboard, which leads me to their tech support.Tech support is horrible.  Using their email support, I reached out to Logitech about the lack of horizontal scroll in Microsoft products.  It took them three responses (taking at least 2 weeks to respond each time) before they could understand the issue and told me that this keyboard doesn't support horizontal scroll.  When I wrote back again for the 4th time reiterating that the horizontal scroll works in Google Chrome and suggested that this was a software issue on their end and something they could easily enable to help boost the functionality of their product, they sent back a response (again taking over two weeks) that my issue was resolved and closed.  Nothing else was said.  Way to shut the door on a customer who is actually giving you constructive feedback and helping you improve your product.All in all, it's an okay product.  Nothing to get excited about.  Think of this as a basic wireless keyboard with a trackpad built in.  Expect anything more and you will be disappointed.  And good luck to you if you ever need to seek out tech support.

I really liked the idea of a media server.  I thought of building my own but the positive reviews for this product in a couple places convinced me to give it a try.What a mistake.First of all, this unit is NOT possible to get to work with Linux.  Don't bother trying.  I really hate when companies invent yet another proprietary protocol for something that could have been just as easily (or even more easily) using STANDARD protocols that already exist.  Bad, bad, bad...So, then I got it working with an XP box and even then it was very SLOW.  I know some people complained that it was slow with large amounts of files.  My media library isn't that large yet.Even beyond that I found it cumbersome to use as I ALWAYS had to have the TV on.  A product like this should be useable on it's own with the TV.  It should have a small display on it and it should take less than 5 button presses (each with a pause between them) just to play music.  I should be able to hit one or two buttons such as PLAY then SHUFFLE and it should just play all my music shuffled.  Even though the remote is pretty good there should also be buttons on the box.  Even the most basic DVD players have buttons to at least play, stop, pause and skip to next on the panel.  I have kids who stash my remotes in places that take me a while to find.  In that case this unit is IMPOSSIBLE to use.After a weekend of messing with it I found it extrememly frustrating and it's going back.

The latest cookware is colored exteriors and  stainless steel with Teflon cooking surfaces. Other than "bragging rights" about the appearance, the real value is in the cooking surface. All Teflon pans and their cousins begin to flake off or lose their non-stick ability over time. Its just a question of how long until this happens to you.These start out as shiny nice thick pans. They do what they were designed and advertised to do. No complaints. They are affordable and will be well liked by almost all cooks. I really like the handles. The two sizes are the most commonly used frypans. Most people find that the 12" and above are just not practical unless you need to cook for a large family.I have to withhold 5 stars only because I prefer the more expensive cookware in which I can use metal utensils. I've read many 1 and 2 star reviews by people who bought 18/10 stainless steel expecting them to be non-stick. That has never been true. However, these are the longest lasting skillets and a little oil and preheating make them non-stick. They also don't warp (this does not apply to thin straight stainless steel pans). If you are willing to spend the big bucks for the top of the quality line and very close to non-stick metal surfaces, then Scanpan or Swiss Diamond are the way to go.

When the cast iron pan arrived I was surprised by the size ... looking more like the smaller sizes that were listed.  I took the pan to the sink and filled a one quart measuring cup,five times ... adding the water to the pan.  Yes ... it filled right to the top with five quarts but there is no way anyone will use five quarts.  All of us will use three or three and a half quarts or the food will spill all over the stove.It turns out the measurement of 5 or 5 and a half inches is measuring from the top of the black handle to the base of the pan.  The pan barely measures 3 inches in depth.  The pan is sized more like a fry pan.Color:The color is true to the photo at Amazon.Cooking:The first thing I cooked on low heat was noodles and they slightly burned when the water cooked out before I could pour more water into the pan. I haven't had that happen with any other cookware I purchased at Amazon. After taking out the food I let the pan cool on the stove and placed it in the sink and filled it with warm soapy water.  After and hour or so the food lifted off and the pan looked like new.  The next morning I scrambled ham and eggs in a little butter on low heat and I did see a little browning left in the bottom of the pan. The food turned out great.I like the look and shape of the pan and yes it is pricey. I'm sure glad I did not purchase a smaller size.I hope this info is helpful to others.Up-Date:Today I cooked rice in this pan and all was well until I tried to brown it with a little oil.  The rice kept sticking to the pan and I was cooking on the lowest heat, on the large burner.  I finally had to transfer the rice to the,"Wear Ever", pan to brown it.  After the rice finished browning it slid out of the,"Wear Ever", pan on to the platter.  I think this pan is highly over-rated for the price.  It's probably alright for soup or stews ... but not frying.  I put it on a lower shelf and pulled out the other  cookware I purchased from Amazon. I'll save this green pan for soups and stews ... something with lots of liquid.

First, my scenario: I want a photo frame that shuffles randomly through pictures in my living room.  When my family is sitting there, we talk about things we remember about each picture, etc.  Pretty standard.Out of the box, this picture frame did not support this, so I would have given it one star (only because there's no "zero stars").  There's a yes/no question here: does this frame support the basic task of shuffling pictures?  Instead, this frame's "random shuffle" feature was actually "walk from beginning to end, skipping a few images after each one".  So if you're sitting in the living room by the photo frame, you're just watching a chronological sequence.  200 pictures of wedding, then 200 pictures of baby 1, then 200 pictures of baby 2, etc.  I'm not crazy; this is actually what the manual says the "random" feature does.  Odd, but there's a happy ending...I bought this from Amazon in March 2013; turns out in January 2013 Nix released a firmware upgrade to fix this.  It was very straightforward to apply (though I'm a tech-type; it's not clear how straightforward it is for a non-tech-type to flash the firmware on *anything*), though I'm a little disappointed that March stock at Amazon still had the old firmware.Now this is a 4-star product.  The frame hardware looks great, the menu is straightforward and responsive (once it opens up, will get to that in a second), and the documentation is far and away the best I've seen for a photo frame (which are usually documented in severely broken English).  Online support (when I was tracking down this firmware fix) was *outstanding*; a human being replied to my email a few hours after I sent it.Only things that prevented me from going with 5 stars:1) In terms of image quality when I have to turn up the brightness, this is *slightly* worse than the previous frame I had (an older Pandigital frame), which was the same resolution, and showing exactly the same images, in exactly the same spot on the same wall.  But overall, very good image quality, and excellent quality when not in so much light that I have to turn up the brightness beyond the default.  I think this is just a subjective issue with the default contrast/saturation settings that makes jpeg artifact a little more visible.2) I had hoped than in the 2+ years since I bought my previous frame, the CPU clunkiness when dealing with a large directory of photos would have gone away, but not so much.  With about 2000 photos in a directory, all named image_000, etc., and resized to 800x600 (all as per Nix's recommendations), the "5-second shuffle" becomes "somewhere between 5 and 45 seconds", and the menu is very slow to come up.  2000 photos isn't even that much by today's standards; clearly if they recommend 800x600 jpeg's and they give you a 4GB USB key, they're imagining much more than this (I'm using <25% of the USB key).But overall, this is now doing what I hoped it would do, and I'd buy it again if I needed another.--Update: dropping to two stars after experiencing a number of problems also reported by other reviewers (in addition to the problems I reported above):(1) It went through a brief period of completely locking up and requiring a reboot after showing just a few pictures, which ended (after about 5 reboots) in the USB key (the one provided by Nix with the frame!) becoming completely corrupt, neither readable nor writable by Windows when I tried to fix it.  I'm using a different USB key now, and things seem to be OK.  The cute little USB key they provided is now in the trash. :((2) As reported by other reviewers, auto on/off has stopped working for no apparent reason.  Unclear whether a reboot or two will fix this; I don't want to "rock the boat" until I have some other reason to reboot.  The clock is set correctly, and the on/off are both enabled in the setup menu.(3) Most frustrating of all, pictures take anywhere from 15 seconds (which is where I have the slide show timing set) to 5 minutes (!) to change.  Usually close to 2 minutes.  This despite the fact that I have followed Nix's guidelines to the letter: all photos in one directory, all resized to 800x600, all written with identical jpeg settings by the same encoder, all named "0000.jpg", "0001.jpg", etc.  And I'm only using 1GB on the drive, so I can't possibly be pushing the limits of the frame in terms of capacity, since Nix provides a 4GB card.  SUPER frustrating.  Not surprisingly, this also means that the unit is nearly unresponsive to the remote, which is the saddest part; I'd like to be able to sit on the couch and flip through pictures, but this is not even remotely possible.  I am using the *same exact* directory of photos, also on a USB key, on an Insignia frame, and it's *completely* responsive.  I can flip through pictures interactively.I'm hoping these are firmware issues that will get fixed, rather than Nix just using a processor that's way behind what Insignia is using.  If this is fixed in firmware, I'll up my review in the future, since the frame still looks fine.--And yet one more update, taking this up to 3 stars.  Previously I had jpeg quality at 100% (though still these were all "small" in the grand scheme of things, and 3000 photos used only 25% of the key they shipped with the device...).  But FWIW when I *also* dropped the quality to 95%, the slide show got much smoother, basically 15s intervals work now.  Still not very responsive to the remote, but I'm satisfied.  Also the several reboots that happened as I was updating the photos also seemed to fix the auto on/off that had inexplicably stopped working.So currently I'm having about a 4.5 star experience, though 3 stars feels about right given how much hassle went in to getting it this far.

Since you're looking at this, I assume you are looking for a non-leaking travel thermos to keep your beverage chilled. Well, the Tritan Hydration Bottle by Thermos does this moderately well, but might not be your best solution.Under normal use, I've not experienced any leakage. But I did fill it and shake it around a bit, and some water did start seeping out. With the "loose" design of the spout, this didn't surprise me. I've not experienced any condensation issues on the outside of the bottle yet. Also, while it may keep a beverage cold longer than your standard water bottle, ice seemed to melt fairly quickly in the local S. FL temperatures and by the end of my daughter's soccer game the ice had all melted and the water was just cool, not cold. I also have some issues with some areas being hard to clean, but overall think this is an elegant thermos for a non-demanding user. People who need a bit more performance out of their containers may need to look elsewhere. I have an LL Bean travel mug, and though it is meant for coffee, I though I'd test it out with ice. I left ice in it over night and it was still floating in the water the next morning. The same amount of ice in the the Tritan was gone and the water was tepid.~ Kort
Perfect for keeping whole bean or ground coffee once the packaging stops sealing properly. Nice, heavy gauge plastic that should last through many reuses.

Its hard to believe that this cheap power backup can hang with the seventy dollar units but it does. What you are dealing with is basically, no exactly, a laptop battery pack. The cells themselves are Chinese instead of Japanese so you won't get the life or the power outputWhen I received this charger the display showed a charge of 57%. I plugged it into a wall USB charger that came with the iPad (10watt), and found that it took 7 hours before the display showed 100%.Charged the 25% iPhone to 100% in two hours.There are two USB outputs, rated at 2.5 amps and 1.5 ampiPad Air charged at 2.03 amps at 56%-iPhone 5 charged at 0.95 amps at 45%Took about three hours to get iPad air up to 80%In my experience you should not expect Chinese cells to last all that long in comparison to Japanese manufactured ones. Chinese tend to recycle old cells into new ones and japan does not. That being said this unit has an 18 month warranty which is longer than any battery pack I have seen.Looks good and works well.Recommended

I bought a fitbit and promptly lost it. The company only asked for a receipt and promptly sent me a new one.  So I bought this scale in white.  I love it!  It syncs seamlessly with my fitbit and only occasionally fails to record my body fat percentage.  I don't mind because I really don't think a scale can provide an accurate body fat reading anyway.  But the weight is very accurate.  I have a doctors type scale that I totally trust and I often weigh myself on it at the same time as my Aria scale and they are always the same.The only problem I have encountered is about a year after I bought this scale we got a new internet provider and router.  My scale would not recognize it at all.  Fitbit customer service worked with me and eventually just sent me a brand new scale that was set up and working in no time.  I never did figure out what happened but the company is so fantastic and I have a working scale now so I am happy and recommend fitbit products to everyone.

I won't make this a long review, but I will say that I own an LX3 and recently purchased a Sony NEX7. I did not like the NEX7 and sold it here on Amazon. I have been using this for months. Each time I used it my floors become cleaner and cleaner.  I have given up on my Swiffer Wet Jet because it smells awful and really used alot of pads to get my floor half way clean and leaving a residue.  This version comes with enough pads to do all of my tile (three baths, kitchen & foyer).  It may not remove everything on your first use, but it takes it off in layers. The Swiffer residue built up over the years. When I use the steam mop the floors feel very clean now. There is no odor. I throw the pads in the wash with a little bleach and the come out clean. The first time I used them they were a dark grey, now they don't pull off as much scum because they did their job. I had read about previous versions that had wimpy handles, but this one is very sturdy. The pads go on very easily, but you do have to wait for a cool down. I have even used it on my shower floor to remove scum that that gotten stuck in the grout. It is a work in progress getting better with each steaming. I do love steaming my tile floors, but don't understand the carpet cleaner, unless it is to sanitize only.

This small, lightweight heater puts out a lot of heat and is very quiet. The auto shutoff is effective, turning off at the slightest imbalance. We also have a tall Pelonis tower ceramic heater (HC-0130) that puts out less heat (though adequate for our purposes), takes up a lot more vertical space and is much noisier.  The Pelonis has better controls: a digital display shows actual room temperature in either C or F, has Low or High output options, oscillates (which we don't use), and you can preset a shutoff temperature in specific degrees. The heat controls on this Holmes is more basic, with Low or High output settings and only five temperature selections in 5-degree increments. You can set it to stay on continuously or set it to shut off when it reaches one of the five temperature options. Getting it to just the right combination of settings you desire can be inconvenient, as you must press the one control button as many times as it takes to get to that setting. Although the Holmes doesn't give you precision settings, it works so well and is so quiet, we wish we had bought another instead of the Pelonis.

I have a sectional couch with pieces. I always lose my remotes inbewtween the pieces and it drives me nuts looking for them. This D-Link KVM switch is one of the most frustrating pieces of computer equipment I've bought in a LONG time.About 80 percent of the time, it will work as advertised. I can switch quickly between my computers, using the same keyboard, mouse, speakers and monitor. I actually like the little button to switch between computers better than hotkeys.But my mouse frequently stops working. Just freezes. I have to switch over to the other computer and then back, or just wait anywhere from 5-20 seconds until the bleepin' mouse works again. The keyboard will often freeze as well.Worse yet, my mouse cursor will frequently and unpredictably jump across the screen. This is particularly disastrous when I click to save an important file, and find the cursor has jumped and I've instead closed the program without saving the file. Maddening. The cursor also zips around the screen suddenly and uncontrollably for a few seconds at a time, also with sometimes disastrous results.I have tried different keyboards and mice to no avail. This KVM switch is serviceable for now, but I'll replace it as soon as I find a good deal.

So far I've been fairly pleased with the Black and Decker 4-in-1 Waffle/Sandwich/Grill/Griddle/. We have made ham & cheese paninis, waffles, pancakes, & grilled cheese sandwiches. The appliance heats up properly, seems to keep an even heat, and all the plates are easy to wash. I like how the plates easily switch out, the mechanism to hold them in place seems strong. Since there is nothing to hold grease, I have not tried cooking hamburgers or grilling chicken. However, I don't believe this particular model is designed for grilling greasy or fatty items. I think it's just meant for waffles, pancakes, & paninis. It will be replacing my old belgian waffle maker, since the plates on that one were not removable. It only makes one panini or two waffles at a time, but that works well for us. If you would like one that grills meat items, I would recommend spending more for the G4TD 3-in-1. Otherwise, this is a good little appliance for a starter kitchen, or for a family that likes waffles or paninis on occasion.

The signal reception is so strong it is not unusual to pick up 12 satellites!I previously owned the Garmin eTrex for about 2 weeks and took it back. I got the Microsoft Wireless Laser Mouse 5000 and followed the 3 required instruction steps (install software, put batteries into mouse, plug receiver into USB; and the optional set up step of pressing a button on the receiver and then the mouse) and the cursor on the screen didn't respond.  Tried again, no response, switched to another USB, no response.  Re-booted computer, no response.  I was about to send back and write a nasty review, but tried one more time with a third USB port and pressing buttons on receiver, shaking the mouse and then pressing button on mouse and it finally sprang to life.  Working fine and very happy ever since.You should note that the receiver sits on your desk and is connected to the USB plug by a wire and NOT integrated into the USB plug itself, but since I use this with my desktop, I have no problem putting very small receiver on my desktop, especially for better reception.  It's not for notebooks, though.I LOVE the feel of it, very smooth, and I like the scroll wheel without the clicks, very slick.  It's a full size mouse, which is what I wanted.  Love being not attached by a cord especially at this price.

I love this mouse. I prefer its ergonomic design over the Apple Magic Mouse. Once you get used to the smooth, high-speed scrolling, you will miss it whenever you use a different mouse. However, for times when you don't need it you can click the button and switch it off! I use this mouse on a Mac in conjunction with the "Smooth Mouse" app from the apple store. It makes my mouse feel much more responsive and smooth like it does in Windows. Another plus is it doesn't tend to hold what I like to call "finger grease." Sometimes, after prolonged use on a mouse you will notice a build up from the natural oils on your hands. I wash my hands a lot so that probably helps but I haven't noticed it after using this mouse for months.I loved it so much I bought a second one. One for work and one for home.

I have used the same player (Creative Zen Nano Plus) for several years and have been very happy with it.  Still, this player offers SO many advantages at a low price that getting one was a no-brainer.  This is not an exhaustive list of what it can do, but it is the features that I wanted that got me to buy a new Clip+.  On top of all of these features, it SOUNDS GREAT!Aside from a review, you'll also find ideas for getting more out of your player, Clip+ or other kinds.UPDATE 3/19/2012Lately the sound of one channel has been intermittent, and this happens with most earphones I have tried.  It is better with some than others.  The earphones work properly with other devices, so I am certain it is not a problem with the plugs.  I have found a way of turning the plug and applying pressure in a certain way that makes it work right, but it looks like I will have to replace it soon.  I took a star off, and may take another.  This is a critical failure on an otherwise 100% wonderful unit.BUILT-IN RECHARGEABLE BATTERYThis is the real reason I bought the new unit.  My old player is great, but needs a new AAA battery on a regular basis.  The cost of these batteries quickly adds up to the cost of a new Clip+, and dead batteries add to landfill.  The Clip+ can be recharged with a computer via USB cable, or you can buy a very inexpensive USB power adapter (often under $2 with shipping).I have seen some complaints that in practice, the player does not achieve the full 15 hours of playback said to be available on one charge.  There's a reason for this.  The 15 hours is the maximum, based on mp3 files created with 128k bps, probably with the display as dim as it will go, and with no keys pressed to light it up again.  Many of us use higher quality bit rates, up to 320k bps.  Higher quality bit rates require more processing, which in turn consumes more electricity.  Since 320k is 2.5 times the rated 128k rate, it would not surprise me if the battery life with those tracks was more like 15 hours / 2.5 = 6 hours.Even rechargeable batteries do not last forever, and I think it would be very hard to replace the battery in this device, so it won't last forever either, but it should last some years.GAPLESS PLAYBACKSome albums have tracks that move from one to the other without silence between - Beatles Abbey Road, Radiohead OK Computer, many others.  Others are designed to have a brief silence at the end of the track (which can be removed while ripping).  The Clip+ plays either one correctly: it plays music uninterrupted from one track to the next if they are set up that way.  My old player had a brief silence while it loaded the next track.  My mobile phone makes a loud click then fades into the next track, missing the first half second or so.  I no longer have to rip contiuous albums as a single mp3 to be played correctly on my player.AUTO VOLUME ADJUSTMENT / REPLAYGAIN SUPPORTMusic is recorded at many volume levels.  You might turn up the player to listen to a quiet track, then get blasted by a loud track.  A technology called Replaygain is available to help normalize track volume to avoid this problem, and this player supports it.EXPANDABLE MEMORYThere's a slot on the side into which you can plug a MicroSD or MicroSDHC chip, which can bring between 1 GB to 32 GB of additional storage to your Clip+.  As I write, the price difference between a 2GB Clip+ and a 8GB Clip+ is more than $26 for 6 GB additional built-in memory.  For that much money, you can buy almost 24 GB memory via 4GB or 8GB chips, and the price of the chips is dropping.  For that reason, I suggest buying a 2GB or 4GB player and spending the savings on expansion chips.These expansion chips weigh very little and you can potentially carry a lot of music around with you without noticing the extra weight.  The chips can have customized music programs to make portable playback very flexible.With an SD adapter sleeve (that usually comes with the micro chips), the same chip can be played on my nüvi GPS, with the music and driving directions alternating as needed.  Your memory chip will probably work with other music devices that have a socket, too.  With the adapter you can also use these chips in your camera or other SD devices, handy if you run out of space for pictures.PLAYLISTSStandard M3U playlists can be used to set up lists of tracks you want to hear, in the order you want to hear them.  You can set the play mode to shuffle so the order is not always the same, if you want.  My old player played tracks only in alphanumeric sort order, so I had to insert numbers on tracks to get them to come out in a particular order.  M3U is perhaps the most common type of playlist, and is widely supported.UPDATEABLE, REPLACEABLE FIRMWAREI am reasonably satisified with the Sansa firmware (see Cons below for a minor issue), and they have updated it to add new features (like the ability to play files by browsing directories, without a playlist).The Clip+ is also supported by the open-source Rockbox firmware, which improves on the Sansa firmware in many useful ways.  It is fairly easy to install, and once it's there, you can switch between the original and the Rockbox firmware as you like.  Because Rockbox is open source, you could customize the way your player works.NO REMOVABLE / BREAKABLE PARTSMy old player has a plastic battery cover that broke, so it does not stay in place very well.  Removable or moving parts are the first thing to break on these devices.  Aside from the buttons and connectors, this player has none.  And it is so light that even dropping it is unlikely to break it.TRACK RATINGI love this feature!  It means that while I do my exercise walking, I can rate a track on the player, and when I get home, transfer the ratings to my music database, so that in the future I can select tracks by my own rating.USES STANDARD CONNECTORSThe Sansa Fuze, any Apple player, and many others tend to have proprietary connectors, especially for the computer connection, which require expensive adapters and limit your flexibility to connect to whatever you want.  The Clip+ accepts a standard mini-B USB plug, which is commonly used for many small USB devices from many vendors.  The headphone jack is the same as all players: the 1/8" (3.5mm) stereo plug is accepted.WORKS WITH STANDARD SOFTWAREThe Clip+, like many other players, allows you to use iTunes or any other software you might prefer - MediaMonkey, WinAmp, Windows Media Player, drag-and-drop from your file list, or your own custom-written software.  Apple requires iTunes for loading and playing tracks on its players, because there is a proprietary database on the player that is used for playing (I have heard that some people have figured out how the database works and written alternatives; also, some mp3 management software does support the Apple proprietary database).COMPACT, AND NOT TOO SMALLThe problem with really small players is they are easy to lose.  To me, this one is as small as I would want.COLORSI prefer brightly colored players, because they are easier to find in my bag (which is black inside), and because I like bright colors.  The colors for Clip+ are a bit dark, but are otherwise pleasing, and there are a few choices.NO VIDEO PLAYBACK / PICTURESPlayers that show video and pictures require larger, more expensive displays which also shorten the battery life, are bigger and heavier, and of course are more expensive.  Obviously the need to show video and pictures is a personal thing, and if you need it, don't choose the Clip+, which only plays music.  With Rockbox, there are actually a few small apps and games that work on Clip+.BOOKMARK / RESUMEIf you are listening to an mp3 book, it is very convenient to have the player remember where you were when you stopped, and resume there.  This player supports that.RADIOThis player includes an FM radio, as many do.  The autoscan missed some of the weaker stations, such as the college stations, when I tried it indoors, but it receives the other local stations very well.  You can record from radio, too.CONS(Warning, geek talk follows.)  The player requires that playlists use relative pathnames only, not absolute pathnames.  When the mp3 file location starts with a slash, it is absolute, and the player won't find it.  This defect limits the flexibility of using playlists, and causes problems for some mp3 organizers (such as MediaMonkey, though there could be a solution).  But if you install Rockbox, this problem goes away.TIPS FOR USING ANY PORTABLE AUDIO PLAYERTo keep costs down, player manufacturers provide a very cheap headphone.  Don't judge the player by the quality of this headphone.  You can get headphones that sound great for cheap money these days; for example, I like Koss "The Plug," which seals out noise around me; these days, there are many other good choices at all price ranges and qualities.I recommend one accessory: a cable that has a 1/8" plug on one end (plugs into the player) and a pair of RCA plugs on the other (plugs into your home stereo or other audio players around the home).  I have a few of these cables, and connected them to every device with speakers in the house.  So when I'm in the kitchen, I can easily listen to the Clip+ through the radio, when the radio isn't what I want to hear.Don't limit yourself to music.  Podcasts are a great way to learn.  Books on tape can be very entertaining while walking or driving.  One of my favorite: learning foreign languages during my daily exercise walk.  I sometimes even listen to recorded presentations for my job to expand my expertise.

The fragrance of this citrus scented demineralized water is lemon-lime with a hint of orange. It doesn't evoke a citrus grove on a warm summer evening. It evokes sticking your nose into a bottle of powdered orange drink. At least it does to me. Once in the steamer and turned to vapor, the scent does not linger longer than a few seconds. Probably just as well. I've tried the eucalyptus version of this and prefer it. The bar is probably lower on my standards for eucalyptus, but it smells more natural, lasts a bit longer and doesn't cloy.

This simple inexpensive screen works well with my HD projector. It has no special accessories to hang it or stand included. You need to get your own hooks or screen hanging device to hang it up. I'm hanging it in a basement with unfinished ceilings, so it was easy to put some hooks in and go. The screen quality is good. I will admit that if you have a high lumen projector, you may not need a screen at all if you have a light colored wall. This screen is quite large for a home. To use the entire screen, you'll need to put your projector rather far away. Measure up your space, I was impressed at how large this screen is.Retraction is quick and painless. Reminds me of grade school pull down screens. Next I need one of those 1 foot diameter clocks they had and a desk with hinges. For the money, this is a very good deal. I would recommend anyone considering a large HDTV to switch to a projector and screen. Just make sure you buy a quality projector. It's probably way more important than the screen. And expensive.The only downside was that one end of the screen and box had a bunch of brown dust on it from sitting on the floor of a dirty warehouse, or perhaps from being left outside during the shipping process. It wasn't tough to dust off, but certainly an unusual issue to have.

I am very pleased with this purchase.  If you are like me (work phone, personal phone, work ipad, personal tablet, and ipad mini for my 13 yr old, my wife's phone,my sons phone, and our home phone) ,  then you are probably constantly looking for chargers around the house. My first attempt at solving this problem was with the Best tek wall charger.. The Best Tek was way too big and bulky. while it did get the job done, it was a complete eye sore. After that, I bought the Anker 2- port charger.While this was effective, I could only attach two chargers to it. Finally, I got the Anker 5-Port charger. This is not too big, and will charge five devices simultaneously, with a total maximum output of 5Amps. This means that you can charge 5 Android phones at full speed, or two ipads and an android phone at full speed,etc... I am sure you get the picture. The only problem i see is that they make it sound like you can charge any five devices at full speed.  that would not be true if you were trying to charge 2 ipads, 1 tablet, and 2 android phones.  There are two ports that will charge at 2.1 amps, two ports at 1 Amp, and one port at 1.3 Amps, which is common for many Android Tablets. I am so far very pleased with this charger and it is pretty inexpensive.  Most of all, i am glad that i can reduce clutter on our desk by removing the power strip that i was using and i can now replace it with one really good charger.  I hope this is helpful, Chris.

When the distributor sent me this product in return for a review it was my first experience with an external battery pack.  It is lightweight, small and modern looking. CamelBak makes a lot of excellent gear, and this Relay Pitcher carries forward their tradition of having a clean, handy source of water readily available. The CamelBak Relay pitcher easily fits in the door of the fridge, helping free up the main shelf area. The pitcher is quick to fill, and also easy to pour without making a mess. My tap water has a bad habit of being very turbid out of the faucet, but the CamelBak filter removes the turbidity as the pitcher fills. The filter lasts up to four months, and doesn't add any kind of odd odor or flavor to the water. Bear in mind that this filter only removes the majority of chlorine taste and odor from city water sources. It will do nothing for cysts, microbes, pesticides, pharmaceuticals, minerals, rust, lead, or other impurities. However, this pitcher does do what it is advertised to do, so there is no real issue with that. It does what it says it will do. If your only concern is removal of chlorine taste and odor, this is the pitcher for you.

The "MyBat" fishbone cover is available in numerous colors and styles.  I have both the black on black, which has a conservative "adult" appearance, and the titanium red, which is draws attention...teenagers should like this color.  The Prevail and Precedent are identical phones, so this cover works equally well for both.Why is the fishbone the best cover for the Prevail and Precedent?  Because it has a double layer of protection...an inner silicone/rubber cover to protect against shocks and scratches, and the outer "hard shell" to help protect against impacts.Putting the cover on the first time requires a few minutes of effort.  Put on the inner liner, then press the outer shell over it.  Then pull each corner back and fit the hard "fishbone" flush into its slot in the rubber liner and fit it down flush to the corner of the phone.  Repeat this process with all six fishbones so they are snug in their slots and tightly fitted to the phone.I rotate my batteries about once a month, and after taking off and replacing the cover a few times, the job has gone from a ten minute job the first time, to about a two minute job.  It is worth this bit of effort to have a case that REALLY protects your phone.Combine the fishbone cover with a Zagg screen protector, and your phone will look like new as long as you own it.

This switch does not allow you to share internet access, it's written clearly on the troubleshooting section.  For me this defeats the true expansion potential.I did access the net once, when only one computer was connected, but it was lost almost immediately and I had not been able to get it back.----------Update: Mar-30-2011The original devices i wanted to extend it ports was Linksys WRT610N (http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B001AZ01EO/ref=wms_ohs_product_T2) i had seen many comments about me not being an expert on networking which is true, i am an average user, but the switch should had done the trick.Anyway i had reconfigured the devices by salvaging a dual wan Netgear (http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B0009RH7E6/ref=wms_ohs_product_T2) that was defective (wan capability not working, but the switching was okay)Since I got the dual wan switch in the middle Trident's switch actually got to pass internet connection, it helped me since now i have (7+3) ports on top of my routers original 3 ports (deduct one port from each device to pass signal).  i like weird connection due to its reliability and performance over wireless (even N) and with everything becoming networked these days too many available ports do not hurt much if you can withstand the cables. Use CAT-6 they are almost equal in price to CAT5/CAT5E and over better performance.

I wear these headphones for an hour in the morning and 1.5 hours at the gym late afternoon or early evening while streaming Pandora radio on my Samsung Galaxy Note 2. I do this 5 days a week and the battery easily lasts the entire week. I tried killing the battery once and used them for 10 days without charging them but the battery still wouldn't die so I gave up and charged them.  They pair easily, rarely skip even for a second and have a very decent range (25-30 feet). Some people complain about "skips". I'd say listening to 2 hours of music I might have 2 times where the music cuts out (for about 1 second each time). Expect more than this with other brands. I don't remember having Bluetooth headphones that ever skipped so few times!If I wear them longer than 4 hours they start to get uncomfortable because the plastic band is a bit thicker than other models so they start to irritate the back of my ear but in a way that is a good thing because they seem pretty darn sturdy.  I don't normally need to wear them this long so I'm just nit picking.Overall these are the best pair of Stereo Bluetooth headphones I've ever used and I've tried Plantronics, Motorola 305's and Sony.  The sound is crip and clear. Plenty loud enough for most users and insanely long battery life.  My Sony's would die after 3 days of use but I honestly think you could use these BTH240's for 2 hours a day for 3 weeks straight before you needed to charge them again.  Absolutely the best bang for your buck on Stereo Bluetooth Headphones!Audiophiles, please keep in mind this is Bluetooth technology so its not fair to compare these to your favorite $400 pair of wired Studio headphones. These are made for rocking out at the gym or taking phone calls while cooking dinner.  You can't beat them for the price!
