This GPS is loaded with features!  I love that I can uses the AAA ratings system when looking for places to eat or stay.  And the multi-destination map feature is great.  It makes planing my trips to the city (Phoenix, AZ) much easier.  I can input all of my destinations before I leave and have my entire trip set.  This keeps me much more relaxed when my time is tight.  These are two factors that set this GPS apart from the others for me.The best part about this GPS is the large size screen and clear maps.  I've tested some other GPS brands and models in the store and sometimes the maps are too busy and colors too loud.  This map looks great.  It's easy on the eyes yet also high enough in contrast to easily read the maps while driving.I haven't tried the WiFi yet.  But it is a nice feature.  If you do have data on your cell phone then using the GPS for WiFi won't be a big plus.  But it's nice to use a larger screen when you need to surf the web or check emails on the road.The dual orientation mode (landscape or portrait) is another neat feature.  I'll likely always use this GPS in landscape mode, but it's nice to know that I can change the layout if I need to.I'm very happy with this GPS and recommend it to others.

After a week of testing, the Duracell myGrid proved to be functional and easy to use but practical considerations keep it from receiving a stronger recommendation.The product arrives with a "myGrid Charging Pad," one "Power Sleeve" and an AC adapter. The charging pad is a simple, flat square (an 8" x 8" area of dead space that can't be used for anything else) with silver contacts arranged in rows along its top, which resembles a solar panel. The bottom is a no-skid material and worked well on all flat surfaces tested (wood, linoleum, metal, plastic, etc.).The Power Sleeve is made of no-skid flat, black rubber, which was easy to put on and remove from the iPhone and worked well on all surfaces as well. It has a built-in plug that connects directly to the iPhone's power connector and has openings for most components (e.g. speakers and vibrate toggle). The iPhone charges by placing it in the Power Sleeve and resting the Sleeve anywhere on the charging pad.Two problems:While the charging pad seemed to charge as quickly as the power cable that came with the iPhone, the Power Sleeve plugs directly into the iPhone's power connector, which meant I had to remove it in order to plug my iPhone into the wall to recharge (when I was away from the myGrid). The constant putting on and taking off was annoying (though not difficult).Solutions?Ignore the myGrid altogether and simply buy another charging cable.Wait for Duracell to redesign the Sleeve so that it allows the user to plug in a power cable.Buy a separate MyGrid for each place I plan to go or carry the MyGrid everywhere I go.Do I recommend this product? Not really; although it is easy to use, it manages to be convenient and maddeningly frustrating at the same time. A better Power Sleeve design would alleviate some of the problems but most techies will not like dead space on their desk.Rating: 3 stars.

This is a nicely built alternative to the little short adapters (likethisone that I have). It allows you to hook the older Firewire 800 (9 pin) equipment and drives to a computer with the newish Firewire 400 (6 pin) inputs. I need one because I have a few older Firewire drives and a few older Firewire camcorders that I can only extract video from using Firewire. This one is 6 feet long, giving you room to arrange things.It is worth knowing, though, that in spite of its name this won't work with all MacBook Pros. I have a 2011 model, that it works fine with, since that has a Firewire 800 output. On my early 2013 model, though, and on the just released 2013 models, it won't work since they have dropped Firewire support for Thunderbolt. So, to make this work on a newer Mac, you'd need one of these plus a Thunderbolt to Firewire adapter. Right now, as far as I can tell, no third party vendors sell those, so you need to buy from Apple. That adapter, at the time of this writing, costs a bit more than this one. I don't see any serious performance difference between this longer cable and the short one I already had, so unless you need the longer cable in order to fit things on your desk, you might save a bit by going with one of the shorter 9 to 6 pin adapters.

Finding these was a godsend because every other pair of small tongs I've considered were for appetizers.These are less than four inches long with a fairly small grabbing area that is perfect for the small cubes that come in a box ofDomino Sugar Cubes Dots. I like the fact that they are stainless steel because they are easy to clean and care for, and have a nice springy touch.If you are looking for appetizer or vegetable serving tongs these will not work. If you still serve sugar in cubes these are perfect.

This is wonderful for cheese, but don't stop there. I wish I'd had it the night I spent cutting cheesecakes to serve at my nephew's wedding reception. It slices through cheesecake and comes out clean--unfortunately I hadn't yet discovered this knife then and it was a messy evening. Also, I make pie crust with cold butter. Sometimes I cut it into the flour using the Norpro Deluxe pastry blender and other times I use my food processor, but either way you have to cut the butter into small chunks first. That was always a frustrating job because the butter always stuck to the knife as I tried to cut it. That doesn't happen with this knife. It makes the cuts easily and comes away without butter clinging to it. My mac and cheese recipe calls for cubing a brick of cream cheese and melting into the heated, thickened milk. Usually the cream cheese sticks to a regular knife and ends up more as a glob than it is cubes. This knife has made that a slick job. I hesitated to buy this knife because I thought the price was steep for a plastic utensil, but it is very sturdy and works beautifully--well worth the price!

Excellent balance between protection and looks. This case looks slightly bulkier than the cheap TPU cases but that comes with great benefits.  To start off the case has two layers of protection. On the inside it has a rubber layer that covers the entire phone including the phones. This is great because it absorb most of the shock from drops and bumps. Then on top of that it has a hard shell cover on the outside. The added bulk is beneficial because the face of the phone is raised throughout the borders. This means that you can lay your phone on a flat surface and your screen is not going to get scratched or anything. This also goes for the camera. It is going to be very hard to damage the lens simply because lens is not going to be in direct contact with the surface of a table or other objects.As far as the feel of the phone with the case i would say it is about an extra ounce of weight. The phone feels kind of like an Iphone 4S. Not super heavy but not exactly the lightest phone on the market right now. It is not that bad and not deal breaker in my opinion. I don't think people will notice much difference between this and one of those "slim" cases. The case provides a very good feel in the hand and it has makes the phone a lot less slippery than without it. The plastic body of the LG G2 is very slippery without any cover on. I am honestly scare to handle the phone without it.Now here comes the bad thing about this case. The texture on the back of the device is very similar to the one the Nexus 5 and Nexus 7 (2013) have. It is very prone to fingerprints and smudges. The finger prints are not as noticeable as without the case. The LG G2 without a case is a finger print magnet. They smudges are very noticeable specially under the lights though.. Also dust seems to stick a little bit to it too. I noticed it when i put the phone on the side compartment of my car. It is not a huge deal but it is a problem. If don't like finger prints on the back of the phone. It just makes the phone look dirty after a few days. I have the black on black version. I don't know if the other colors are more noticeable or not. This is the only reason why i am not giving this case 5 solid stars.To summarize my review, this case is very nice. The protection is excellent for normal every day use (don't go running over the phone with a car or anything). It should easily handle scratches and the occasional accidental drop. The feel and look of the phone is very good except for the smudges on the back. Considering this phone retails for around $600 dollars i would not hesitate to put a good $20-30 case as opposed to the cheap $10 dollar TPU case.
It's a ok case but a pain when you're trying to get the phone out of it, plus once that you separate the black part from the pink it doesn't snapped the same!

If you want a blanket that is light in weight, plush and cuddly, warms you much more than a sheet, but not as much as a comforter or a heavy blanket, then look no further.  The Aeolus Down All Seasons Fleece Blanket will end your quest.SIZEAt first we thought the wrong size had been sent, as the king size is very ample and generously sized.  There is plenty of extra blanket to fold over on the top, deeply tuck in at the foot, and hang at the sides.  There was no skimping here.  And that means no tug of war with your bedmate for the cover.DETAILSThe finishing on this blanket is professional and not the raggedy type we've encountered in some budget brands.  It washes like a dream, too.  We wash ours on a gentle cycle and tumble dry on low.  It emerges fresh and new without that worn, pilled look.Our blanket's color is sage.  It's far deeper than a celadon, but in a close enough range that, it complements the decor.  If you want an olive or more khaki color, or something more of a mint, this won't do.This blanket is so light that, at first glance, you may think it needs to be returned.  Don't do that.  This cuddly, comfy blanket is one you'll be happy to sleep under--not too hot, not too cold--all night long.  The fact that it's light in weight is great for those of us who don't like to have heavy covers weighing down our feet.  If the Three Little Bears were searching for a blanket, Baby Bear would say, "And this one's JUST RIGHT".

If you have a lot of USB devices and find yourself inundated by cables or constantly unplugging devices so you can plug in another, this is a handy solution.  It is much better than a USB hub. It was obviously designed for gamers, but will be useful to anyone who wants to connect lots of USB devices such as wireless remotes, mouses, keyboards, etc. that use a USB receiver, or anything that will connected long enough that an internal port will be convenient.Don't be scared of the installation process. It's very simple and the instructions are clear, if you need them. For the price, this is a great value and much cleaner than a hub.

Pros:-Fast-Sharp-Closer than normal focusing / macro-77mm filter size-GREAT image quality-GREAT creamy bokeh, with round out of focus highlights-Good control over chromatic abberation-Works well with full frame cameras-Best combination of wide angle AND macro on the market.Cons:-Front focusing on my XTi. (not the case on my 50D; it's right on target)-Two steps to switch between MF and AF.I won this lens on ebay for only $183. It's just as sharp as anything I own. It is unique being so fast, wide and macro.What I like the most about this lens is being able to compose macro shots and still have a lot of real estate left over in the frame for background elements. This is where you can really get some dramatic background blur like a row of objects with the first one being sharp and the rest moving off to an eventual complete blur. With a full frame camera, this lens offers more background blurring ability than any other lens on the market unless you are willing to pay an f/1.2 lens. This is due to the closer focusing capability. With a crop sensor camera, it also has the most background blurring ability of any lens, just not as great as on a full frame camera.The 77mm filter size is a great bonus for any lens. Most pro lenses, like a 70-200 f/2.8, or other f/2.8 zooms and most of the fast, professional prime lenses usually use the 77mm standard. This avoids buying duplicate filters in different sizes. Stepping rings are not practical IMHO since you can't use lens hoods with them, etc.This lens does seam to front focus a bit on my XTi, but it's right on target on my 50D and 5D, with no micro focus adjustment. You would think my XTi is suspect here, but it focuses right on target with all my other lenses. Especially at f/1.8, getting the focus right is SO important.I know Sigma does have some QC issues to watch out for. For this reason, I usually buy Sigma lenses from Amazon (if buying new) to get the 30 day return policy. My suggestion is to definitely buy this lens. If you think you may have gotten a dud, then you most certainly did. I feel this lens is as sharp as any lens I own, which includes two Canon L lenses. If you did get a dud, exchange it. Keep on exchanging it until you get one that is ALSO right up there as one of the sharpest lenses you own. I've read one reviewer say his focus ring feels sloppy in MF and he suspects that has to do with why it won't AF correctly. I think he is right since my copy does not feel like this. So watch out for sloppy feeling MF.

It fails miserably here, let me explain.  Even at the finest tip setting, this Boxwave Sylus will input the same amount of width as my finger would.  This causes words to have that bold look and I have to be extra careful when writing the letter "e" because it looks like a c".  It works better with Adobe's Idea app because I can get a finer tip when writing but this program was not meant for writing.  Therefore I can't find any real use for this as I really wanted to write notes with the kind of accuracy you get with pen and paper.I can't tell if the iPad itself does not have the ability to accept the proper input or the pen is at fault.  Since other people here claim that a stylus can work wonders, it leads me to believe that this stylus isn't what I'm looking for.

This cutter set is easy to use, safe with no sharp metal edges to catch little fingers, and includes all the shapes we will need to make and decorate cookies, and is perfect for young children.The designs, honestly, are pretty generic and the craftsmanship and quality of the cutters is nothing to write home about. I've separated them out by holiday to fit in storage bags, but this makes for bulky and awkward storage. Recently I received an email from a representative of Smart Weigh offering a complimentary Smart Weigh DVS250 Digital Vanity Scale in exchange for my honest review on amazon.com without any pressure or influence by others.  I review books and products as a hobby.  While I have never been enamored with seeing my weight displayed on scales of any kind, I reluctantly agreed.Folks, this is a quality product at an affordable price.  I still do not like the numbers; however, the scale is affordable, accurate, sleek and attractive product that tells it like it is!This scale is 13 inches wide, 12.5 inches long and .75 inches high.  It is powered by 4 AAA batteries that are included with the scale.It has the capacity to weigh up to 330 pounds and display the accurate results immediately in 1.2 inch tall digits in high grade digital backlighting while standing on a slim glass platform of high tempered glass with black accents.Like nearly everything now days, this product is made China, but it has a 2 year warranty.  It weighs in three different adjustable modes--and pounds, kilograms, pounds/stones.This durable, slim, glass platform provides clear instant readings that automatically turn off 10 seconds after one steps off the scale.This product comes with easy to understand tips and instructions to make sure that this product operates efficiently and what actions to take if errors occur.While the accurate readings are often irritating, this is a quality product that is easy to use and provides accurate reading at an affordable price.Enjoy!

Prior to purchasing these I had one large flat cookie rack, the problem being that I didn't have enough room to cool down more than 10-12 cookies at once.  These racks come apart completely so you can use one, two or all of them at one time.  There are also two ways you can position the legs - so the rack is tall like in the picture, or so that it is closer to the counter which is useful if you're not stacking them or trying to cool something heavier than cookies.That being said, these racks really are meant for cookies.  They're not as heavy duty as a single tier cooling rack so I wouldn't use it to stack pies or something.  The racks are easy to clean and are dishwasher safe.  I would highly recommend these if you're a bit constrained for space or need an affordable set of racks.  I don't think the set is a replacement for a heavy duty rack if you like to bake heavier items such as bread/loaves or pies.

I dehydrate sliced sweet potatoes for my Cane Corso Mastiff. Buying them from a pet store costs $15.00 per pound but you can get whole sweet potatoes from the grocery store for around 89 cents per pound. I bought a Nesco square dehydrator with eight trays for about $54.00 and started making my own dog treats. Making them myself saves about $1,000.00 per year.It's not hard to make your own, but the biggest problem encountered is cutting the sweet potatoes to the desired width. Previously I used a short and very sharp carving knife. Great concentration is required because sweet potatoes are very hard to slice and making 12 pounds at a time can really take its toll on your hands. Put those two things together and you have a potential recipe for disaster because of the force required to make the cuts. It's also hard to safely hold the potato and slice it uniformly lengthwise.I solved the problem by cooking the sweet potatoes in a microwave for three minutes on 'high'. This softens the sweet potato just enough to make it easy to score the top of one end of the potato to the desired width. Then all you have to do is position the Winco Serrator in each cut and push it down from one end to the other. Voila! Just like cutting cheese. What was hard before is easy now. No blisters, no sore fingers, and greatly reduced potential from not having to slice through a very hard potato with an extremely sharp knife.

This speaker has excellent sound qualities and has a slim design. I have the AMP SP1a.m.p SP1 Portable Bluetooth Speaker (Black)portable speaker which doesn't have quite as good sound quality as the SBX8, but the AMP SP1 has bluetooth. They are both about the same price, but will cost you $50 more to get the bluetooth version of this speaker (the SBX10Creative Sound BlasterAxx SBX10 Wireless Bluetooth Speaker with Microphone). Also, both units are limited in the volume level that can be achieved (meaning, if you like really loud music, this speaker isn't for you).  I like to be able to turn the volume really low at work so my co-workers don't hear it and this speaker is perfect for that along with regular-volume listening.The Size is: 2.5" wide, 6.5" tall, 2.2" deepCable included: USB power cable 39.5 inches, round cloth-covered (very durable and excellent quality)Other Cable: none (see narrative below)Bluetooth: NoDurability: Average, Foam-like fabric surroundIf you are looking for absolute excellent sound quality in a compact package, this is the unit for you. I have a couple gripes, listed below, but giving it a 5-star just for the prefect quality of sound and ease of use.The sound is crisp and clean and super easy to hook up to a PC or Mac. Just plug in the unit, connect, and play your music. Set your PC or Mac to the highest volume level, then adjust the volume on the speaker unit for the best quality. The volume control is on the top of the speaker along with DCL control which turns the base (little round opening on the back) on or off ( i leave it on for the best sound). One caveat, if you like REALLY LOUD music, you'll be disappointed because the output volume is limited, but if you like normal adult music volume (LOL) suitable for normal room listening enjoyment, it won't be a problem.However, I do have a couple gripes about this product, but not enough to make me lower my rating. Gripe ONE: it won't work with my iPad. I am running 6.0.1 on my iPad (the most recent system at the time of this review), and using a 3.5 mm stereo input cable- not included), it connects, but it will not play any sound. I had no problem connecting and playing using my iPhone (also with 6.0.1). To connect you use the AUX port on the back just like using headphones. Gripe TWO: No 3.5 mm stereo input cable is NOT included. For the price of the product, they could throw this cable in the box, even if it made it a couple bucks more.If you want to use this speaker on your PC or Mac, this is definitely the best product going for sound quality, compact size, and ease of use.

Since the Vine program now requires a review within 30 days, I assume they want a "first impressions" review rather than a long-term impression, so here goes:Delivery: CEVA Logistics handled the delivery, and it's easy to track your package on their site - they even have an iOS tracking app. When the TV arrived in our area (BWI Airport) a very nice lady called to set up an appointment for delivery, either 10-3 or 1-5. I chose the 10-3 window, and at about 2:30, the truck driver, George, called to say he was on his way. George carried the TV box around the back of our house and into the door of our walk-out basement. He offered to unbox and set it up, but I said no thanks, my husband and I will take care of it. Thanks CEVA and George for a smooth, easy delivery experience.Unboxing: Very easy, as long as you have a helper to help lift out the TV. Unboxing instructions are printed right on the box. You'll need a phillips screwdriver to bolt the included stand together and mount the TV; all bolts are included. The TV weighs about 90 pounds, in case you have to buy furniture or a wall mount for it.Setup: Still smooth and simple! Once your TV is plugged in and whatever boxes you have are plugged into the TV (we have an HD cable box, Xbox 360 and Wii), insert included batteries into the remote, switch it on and the TV walks you right through the setup. After connecting to your Wifi, a brief tutorial shows off the different "home screens" and how you can customize them.If you have an iOS device (I have an ipod touch) or Android device, go download the Viera remote app that lets you use your device as a keyboard (really beats pointing and clicking!), remote, or best of all, Swipe and Share. And if you have kids, go to Parental Controls in the settings menu and set up a PIN before they do it for you :)What I like and LOVE:- Picture looks terrific, either with our lights on or off. My husband watches a lot of sports and the graphics and scores look very sharp.- Streaming video streams very well. I've watched YouTube and Dailymotion videos with no problems.- Remote is easy to use with big buttons and well-marked numbers.- The Viera apps are fun to use. You can preview them online (do a search for Viera apps) although you have to download them through the TV. They have YouTube, Hulu Plus, Dailymotion, Ustream, various weather and news apps, and add new ones all the time. With the YouTube app, you can even search for videos on your handheld device and then "send" them to the TV.- LOVE Swipe and Share! Now I can take family videos and photos with my ipod and easily show them on the TV with just a flick. The same can be done with web pages, using a browser within the Viera app.- The TV itself looks very classy, with a narrow black bezel and clear lower bezel with "Panasonic" in small type (not an enormous logo).What I don't like so much (all very minor):- There's only one r/g/b input, so we had to choose between the Wii or the DVD player (Wii won out) and only 3 HDMI inputs. There are a couple of USB inputs on the side, but harder to access and not as well marked as on our LG TV.- You can only register a Viera account and buy/download apps through the TV itself, not through their web page which would be much more convenient.- Password or PIN protection for customized home screens would be nice; kids like to play around with that sort of thing!- The browser is pretty useless; it doesn't support Flash and keeps crashing.I'll probably update this review, but in our first month, we love this TV and it bridges TV and internet very well.

I have 34 of these WD RE4 2TB drives in production at work, in Promise Vtrak J610 shelves connected via LSI SAS cards, and have another 26 about to go into production in new systems I'm building using SuperMicro cases and motherboards and LSI cards.  In big RAID systems you must use hard drives manufactured to support RAID operation, particularly in regard to error recovery timing.  Desktop hard drives take a long time to perform error recovery - which causes problems in RAID systems leading to data corruption and premature flagging of a drive as bad.  These WD RE4 drives use a feature called "Time Limited Error Recovery" to prevent a drive in error recovery from being dropped by the array.  Supposedly enterprise drives are built more vibration tolerant to handle the stresses of being operated in a drive shelf.In any case, over the past year one of the Vtrak shelves loaded with 16 RE4 drives behaved flawlessly and the other one lost 4 drives in a single episode over a 48 hour period about 6 months into deployment.  My experience is, thus, mixed.  However the price per spindle is dramatically better than using branded drives from HP, Dell, or the other major storage vendors.  My advice is to use RAID 6 and designate 2 hot spares per shelf.Other than this single episode with 4 drives dying in short temporal proximity, these drives have been fine.  Their performance is good.  In the large array they provide large storage at a decent price with acceptable performance.  Prices have risen dramatically because WD's Thailand operation was damaged by the Fall 2011 flooding in Thailand.  Prices will probably go up further until production levels are restored sometime in mid 2012 or so.UPDATE - after the subsequent review warned about getting a pulled drive I looked at my last one (purchased 12/11/11).  It is a mint sealed WD2003FYYS-02WB0 RE4 drive.  My recent purchase was legit.

I have tried to like CC Products for years now, having owned a CC Radio, a few early LED flashlights, and 2-3 CC SWPocket AM/FM Shortwave, and a C Crane CC Pocket AM FM and NOAA Weather Radio. In almost all cases I have been disappointed with the products not holding up to the statements made about their performance.The CCRadio had poor reception, in conflict with the inflated claims, so it was returned to the company for a refund. Didn't notice any better sound quality as any other similar radio...The CC SW Pocket radio, as is frequently mentioned, has problems with the volume control- either too soft at low volume or then blasting loud. I have spoken with the Company and they acknowledge this shortcoming, yet have obviously done nothing to address it over the years.Also, the reception is less than adequate, and likewise in conflict with company claims made.I think the radio is handsome, and that's why I originally purchased the first one, which was exchanged for another, and finally for a third - all of which have the same problems.Lastly, the C Crane CC Pocket AM FM and NOAA Weather Radio is also inferior in reception, and returned.I will stick to my cheap AM-FM pocket radios which can be purchased at many outlets for half (or less) the price of the C. CRane products, and actually work better.The company needs to focus on actually producing a radio that performs to the hyped up statements they make.  Their marketing is excellent, customer service is good, but the products are just mediocre.

There's nothing better than bread fresh out of the oven. Thin slices of soft warm wonderfulness smothered in butter!My wife is the kitchen master in our house. While she does make fresh bread, she doesn't make it every day. So she experimented with the knife and used it in a variety of situations from finely slicing raw flank steak to chopping cabbage. While it is a great bread knife, it's also a very a great knife in general. The non-stick coating makes it a breeze to clean. The anti-bacterial coating gives me peace of mind. It is thin, has the right weight, and the blade is curved to make bread cutting effortless. It cuts through soft bread without smushing and easily cuts the toughest crust. It's well made and well designed.In fact, the knife only lost a star because of the packaging. I'm going to attach a picture so you can better see what I'm talking about. The knife comes in a clear soft plastic sleeve. That knife and sleeve is then put in a metal box filled carboard confette. The box and confette are both nice touches that I'm sure came from some marketting conversation on how to make this a better wedding gift. Unfortunately, it they measured the knife and not the knife in it's sleeve when ordering boxes. I assume they realized their mistake and decided it was just good enough!Because, you see, the solution to the long sleeve issue is to bend the sleeve. But the box isn't much longer than the knife. In my box the sleeve was bent right at the point of the knife. Then in shipping, my box hit a bumpy road or some turbulence. Because of the bend, the sharp point of the knife rubbed against the sleeve and cut it. The knife also slid forward and back in the box which caused the handle to ding one side of the box and the point to poke the other!Now, the damage isn't atrocious. It didn't seem to hurt the kife in any way. But when you're giving a special gift, you want the first impression to be a good one! The box was a good idea, but it was poorly executed. If you're getting it for yourself--who cares. If you're getting it for a gift, at least be aware that the packaging is less than perfect.But again, I can't state enough how much my wife loves this knife. She's making bread right now and can't wait to cut it up. Me, I just can't wait to eat it!

UPDATE:  This pan is wonderful - very attractive and cooks well and evenly. HOWEVER I finally blew it and forgot to use parchment paper one time. I did roasted potatoes with olive oil. The food didn't burn but the olive oil got sticky and is absolutely impossible to clean. Aluminum pans scratch VERY easily so you are really limited on cleaning products and methods. I contacted NordicWare so I am hoping they have a suggestion!  I will update my review again if/when I hear from them. So, a word of caution: ALWAYS use parchment, and if you do get food on the pan, clean it IMMEDIATELY before it cools.  I hope I haven't ruined this pan, I love it.======================================================================================================I ordered this to replace another baking sheet that was scratched, discolored and warped. I received my Sigma 17-50mm lens the other day and put it through my usual test before it goes into my camera bag. The normal zoom I have used up to this point was the Nikon 18-105mm VR. Well I taped my test chart on the wall, setup the strobe units, and tripod. I took sets at different settings and this is my results. The Nikon is no match for the Sigma. At F2.8 the Sigma is sharper than the Nikon at F8 both center and corner, this includes 18mm, 28, and 50mm. I had my doubts at the beginning but not now. In my camera bag is the Sigma 100-300mm F4, Sigma 70-200mm F2.8 OS version, Sigma 150mm F2.8 macro, and now my Sigma 17-50mm F2.8 OS. I never thought I would switch from Nikon to Sigma but here I am, a Nikon D300 with all Sigma lenses. Believe me, I went through a lot of lens before I ended up here. I wish I could go back to the beginning and start over again, I would have saved money in a big way.

This set will get you through in a bind, but it was missing 2 of the 'flat head' screws that go to the logic board. The longer of the 3 (top of logic board) was included.  The iPhone 4 contains 3 flat head that act as a 'screw within a screw' making it possible to fasten both the logic board and emf shields in the same location.The quality of the overall set is VERY POOR. Basically, these are meant to be inserted ONCE and never touched.  Be careful NOT to over tighten as the heads will strip without any effort at all.Does NOT contain the pentalobe screws (screws on outside of case near charge port) needed to hold the back glass in place.  Have to buy them separately.I bought a package of 10 from another auction site for less than $2.00. Below is a link for 2 for nearly $5.00.Full Set iPhone 4/4S/5 Pentalobe ScrewsOverall:  these will come in handy for under $2. However, don't expect them to be on par with original Apple fasteners.

I won't tout the merits of French Presses, other than to say that I'm a convert that finds it more difficult to enjoy drip now.  This is just a quick write-up about this particular press.I'd already had a larger 34oz French Press for my morning joe made by Thermos Nissan, but wanted something smaller for the occasional time of day or evening when I just wanted a single serving.  Advertised as having the same volume as a regular soda can, this seemed like the logical choice.  And for the most part it fits the bill.Even though I knew it was only a 12oz press, the unit seemed surprisingly small when I opened it. It's 12 oz, but probably closer to 10-11 usable oz, depending on how strong you like your coffee, as the grounds and lid take up more space than the manufacturer apparently accounts for.  I probably get 11 oz out of a batch, but I only add about 1 tablespoon of grounds per 6oz, rather than 2 tablespoons as is recommended.  Then I let it brew a little longer than the 4 min recommendation.  And the result is just how I like it, smooth, and not so strong you'd think it was brewed to be mixed with frothed milk or cream and sugar.The main reason I chose this model press was because, outside of the metal rod and press-part attached to the lid, the carafe and handle are all glass and plastic, so I can nuke the water right in the carafe.  So far that's worked out well enough.  Popping it in the microwave for a couple minutes, the plastic never feels more than mildly warm when I take it out.  Only the glass contacts the water anyway, so there's no risk of contaminents affecting the taste of the coffee - that is, unless they come from the cheap plastic parts of the plunger itself.  But, no warmer than it gets, I expect no problems to arrise from nuking the caraffe and handle, even though the manufacturer's literature doesn't specificy whether or not it was intended for microwave use.The coffee is just as good as my Nissan and the filter traps grounds even better than it does and disassembles for cleaning the same way as the Nissan:  you simply unscrew the pieces at the bottom and rinse.  That said, considering it's price, I was disappointed that the press part wasn't all metal.  The very bottom piece is plastic, which is bound to wear with repeated cleaning, if fully disassembled each time.  So, I try to get by with cleaning it intact most of the time.The downside of the carafe being glass is that it doesn't keep the coffee as warm as the metal insulated Nissan, and it sweats.  There's always a wet spot on my desk where it was brewing while I work.  And the condensation will drip as you poor your cup too, so you've got to keep a paper towel or a couple tissues handy.The glass is fairly thin, but doesn't feel too fragile.  And it's nice seeing the coffee inside brewing.  But, I'd never buy a larger size with a glass carafe.This last bit is just a rant about the measuring system used for this item, so skip it, if you don't want to read my hastily thought out two cents on the subject:On principle, seeing so many people have purchased this expecting something bigger, while I'm not one of them, I can sympathize, especially if amazon's listing didn't include the actual liquid volume in oz's at the time of purchase.  I feel it somewhat inexcusable that a product being sold in the US market would not have it's advertising modified to suit the expectations of the region where it's being marketed.  The packaging was clearly made for an english speaking populace.  I don't know about the UK, but we do not use 4 oz cups in America.  Why 6oz is even considered acceptable advertising is debatable.  6oz might have been the typical cup size 30 years ago, but it's not anymore, and 4oz isn't much bigger than a kids tea set.Eight oz is the measuring standard we use in the West.  The manufacturer not adjusting their measuring system to better suit any market it's being sold in can only be either an unintentional failure of marketing to suitably evolve with the time or adapt to the region, or a deliberate ploy to increase sells.  It's kind of hard to believe that after years of consumer complaints a company would be so resistant to change, unless they actively intended to misrepresent their product for whatever reason.

While starting life as a graduate student, I made the decision to get more serious about cooking to eat better and save money.  A functional kitchen begins with quality cookware and a good set of knives; toward that end, I considered purchasing several of Victorinox's knife sets:Victorinox 46892 Fibrox 3-Piece Chef's Knife Set,Victorinox 8-Piece Knife Block Set,Victorinox 7-Piece Knife Block Set,Victorinox Swiss Classic 15-Piece Cutlery Block Set.  In the end I found that, at Amazon's heavily-discounted prices, it's just as economical to construct a 'custom set' by purchasing the knives individually.  This also ensures that you get exactly what you want, and that you don't waste money on knives that you don't want or need.In the end, I purchased:Victorinox 40521 10-Inch Chef's Knife, Black Fibrox HandleVictorinox Fibrox 7-Inch Granton Edge Santoku KnifeVictorinox 5-Inch Mini-Chef's Knife with Fibrox HandleVictorinox Honing Steel 12-Inch Round Regular Cut, Black Plastic HandleHenckels 20-Slot Super Knife Storage BlockAll of these products are very highly reviewed, and together they form an excellent introductory knife collection at a reasonable price.As a vegetarian with a penchant for root vegetables, I am using this knife almost constantly.  The granton edge enables me to dice through fields of potatoes, rutabagas, celeriac, and parsnip with ease.  The construction is top-notch and the Fibrox handle ensures that it is easy to clean.  As is the case with other Victorinox knives, the cutting edge is amazingly sharp and, when cared for properly with handwashing and regular honing on a quality steel, it maintains this edge extraordinarily well.  At this price, the casual at-home cooking hobbyist is unlikely to find a better deal.

First off, this product is wonderful. I chose this backpack over a couple of other bags because it was big enough to grow with me. Since this purchase, I've acquired an external flash and one more lens to add to my other 4. All this, plus my Canon Rebel fit perfectly in here, and there is more than enough room for my close-up set, tons of sd cards, manuals for camera and flash, and batteries.I usually end up putting the contents of my purse in here when I go to, say, the zoo where I wouldn't want a purse in the way. It ALL fits, with PLENTY of room to spare! I'm completely happy with this bag, as far as space goes.Now for the not-that-bad news...I dropped my camera because of the way this bag was somewhat poorly designed. The zippers don't have any way of keeping themselves closed, so I was walking around with a semi-open bag and when I turned around to look, there went my camera! Everything survived and there was no hardcore damage (the plastic on my lens cracked just a teeny bit, but it works just fine).Now, the sad part is that I could have prevented this. The minute it arrived, I took it out to play with it and noticed this was a potential problem. So I took the straps that were already attached to the bag (they have little plastic clips), and I removed and re-attached them so that the straps clasped in the front of the bag, keeping that compartment from opening up no matter what the zippers decided to do.I hate having to modify my brand new products like that. I really wish Canon had thought of this, because it is a MAJOR drawback. However, if you're willing to spend the 5 minutes puzzling around with the straps, I say its worth it. This becomes a 5 star product that way. Otherwise it's a death-trap for your equipment!Out of all the bags and backpacks I looked at (and boy did I do my homework!), this was the clear winner in terms of size, price, and convenience (it has these amazing little side pockets that can snugly carry a lens so you don't have to open the whole bag if you know that lens is gonna be used, and all the other pockets are just so convenient). I highly recommend it! Just please buckle up your camera!Hopefully soon Canon realizes how silly it is to not have more security, and makes a better version...

I received three 3M screen protectors to review: this one, the anti-glare one, and the "privacy" one. I started with this one, and really didn't care for it much. First, I guess I should say that I generally don't care for screen protectors at all, as 1. they rarely go on without getting tiny pieces of lint/dust under them, no matter how careful you are, and 2. they don't feel right. Some of them make the phone's screen seem to stop responding correctly, or they feel filmy or streaky.This one didn't seem to affect the function of my phone, though it did feel a little streaky (not a lot; just a bit). The big issue I had with this, though, is that the center of it seemed to "rub off" a little bit...kind of like it was coated with some protective stuff on the front that rubbed off after light usage. To be fair, it didn't feel like it had anything on it, and when the phone was turned on, you couldn't see a problem at all. But when the phone was off, you could see a bit spot in the center of it that looked like it had rubbed off. Weird.Aside from that, it just didn't feel right to me. Like most screen protectors, I just didn't like the way it felt, although less so than with other similar items. Still, it certainly wasn't enough to keep it on there. Your mileage may vary, but it just wasn't for me. I've now moved on to the "anti-glare" one, and I like that much better. I'll do a separate review for that, but that one actually has a chance of staying on my phone for a while...the first screen protector I've ever said that about.

Like many other shoppers, I lost count of how many sets of non-stick cookware I have purchased over the years and was shopping for a new set.  I read the reviews of other sets and then liked the description and looks of the Cirulon 3-piece Open Skillet Set and after reading scads of reviews-good and bad-I decided to give them a try.What a good choice!  Unlike some other reviewers, I have had no problems with foods sticking or clean-up.  The main issue is cooking with too high cooking temperatures.  If your burners seem to get too hot, use heat defusers.  Most of the modern cookware today regardless of brand or price warns against using high heat to minimize wear and tear.  I use these pans several times a day and washing them by hand is a piece of cake.  They look great and I believe will last much longer than the run-of-the-mill non-stick skillets.

I bought this DVP5140 from Amazon for $47.99 to replace my damaged DVP 642. The biggest problem with the DVP 642 has actually been "sort of" fixed in DVP 5140; Certain Region 4 discs I bought ( legally)  would go from widescreen to Full Screen  for no Reason, well, with the DVP 5140 if I change the display setting for the problem discs, it "forces" the playing of the disc in widescreen. So, the setting is now there, hopefully a firmware upgrade will follow.The crazy thing about this, is that the DVP642 just needs a slight repair which will make it workable if I can find the time to do the repair ( which I have to, since I have a favorite disc jammed in the machine).The First DVP642 I bought lasted, and that was bought for 62-63 dollars. The DVP 5140 ( which is Multiformat, Multiregion, etc) was 47.99 and it is great. Complain all you want about what it has NOT got, but this has features. Yes I will admit that the remote takes a little getting used to, but for a multizone multi-everything gem, this little beast compares with items up in the 1,000 to 1,500 mark; and favorably. Anyways, the cost is low, just look at what you get for the cost of an Optical drive on a PC... its great.The problem I had with the DVP 642 was the flashing red Power light problem; supposedly this refers to a burned out resistor, and can be fixed "easily" ( I don't know about that, but the instructions on the web are fairly straightforward, you just need the time). So I upgraded with the hope that there was a fix or some change related to the region 4 issue as mentioned above, and I also have not had the time to devote to repairing the 642 ( yet), so I may wind up with two multi-zone players.Anyways, the 5140 is a keeper, and its great.

While file-based acquisition will be the way to go in the future, for now, this little baby gives me just what I'm looking for - a fantastic HDV acquisition with tape-based backup. The color fidelity is fantastic, the image stabilization works very well. If you do your own editing, the bandwidth of the HDV video is well within the capabilities of most PC's and Macs. Get yourself a decent editing system and you will be surprised at what you can get out of this camera.Some have complained about the joystick location. Is it perfect? No. But it is very functional and you should have no problem living with it. Like any camera, you have to learn its functions and capabilities and then make adjustments to those configurations. The accessory shoe is a nice touch, allowing anyone to add a higher quality shotgun mic to the unit for better sound. While the manual focus could be a little quicker (I would love it if the camera had the ability to pull focus more quickly) the automatic mode works very well. It finds focus incredibly fast.I am very impressed with the macro focusing capabilities of this camera. With proper stabilization and a little imagination you can create your own Discovery insects show! (if you are so inclined) Seriously, you can get GREAT closeups of flowers, insects, you name it.I'm just beginning to play with the cinema mode to see how closely it emulates a film-look. For the price and the features I am well pleased with this camera.

The forums say stay with one brand and quality of tape, for the health of the camcorder heads.  So I continue to buy these, here and elsewhere when I see a tempting sale price.  I do not find these tapes great, as something clearly lacking in the quality - I have seen better.  Yet these Panasonic DVC tapes provide reasonably good color for the price.  I question their stability as when I reuse them on the second go round, I do notice dropouts and catches and drags.  I have high def camera and monitor, so maybe I notice more than someone else.  Price on Amazon, good when on sale - yet watch expiry date, source (yes low quality versions or fakes exist), and avoid buying from extreme temperature locations (tape sensitivity).  Would not recommend using these for once in a lifetime memories (go better) such as world cruise, graduation, birthing, rare orchids, diva performances, etc.  For daily and unimportant shooting of the swimming hole, job, traffic, your house and yard, new car, etc., these do fine.

The switch is simple enough.  Easy to install, and worked without fuss for two years.  Then one day, it died.  Just a green power indicator light, and no Ethernet activity.  I give this unit very poor marks for reliability.  Get a Trendnet switch.  Metal enclosure, 5 year warranty.  This is the last D-Link product I'll buy.

I bought this at the recommendation of Amazon to go with my new "Canon PowerShot SX230HS".Construction: Well built, appears to be nylon fabric.  Has a thin piece of foam for padding inside the top and bottom sides of the case.  I would not intentionally drop my camera in this, but it looks like it would provide some protection.This case is a very snug fitting.  The Powershot SX230HS does not really fit.  I can jam it in there, but it's hard to get the zipper closed.  To be fair, the Powershot is a thicker camera because the lens adds 0.25" to the body.  This case would probably work fine with most compacts.

I had a 550 which I used for a good 4 years or so.  It worked fine but over time the buttons weren't as functional and it was very very slow (as if the IR wasn't reaching or something).The 650 is easy to setup, since I already had a harmony account with the 550, it was as easy as a few clicks and bam - all the custom settings got moved over.  I find the remote very easy to use and setup.  The ONLY negative and this is a personal thing is the aesthetics.  I find it to look more cheap and less "fancy" than the 550.  Maybe it's the shape, the color, the cheesy touchscreen...but it just doesn't look so nice.  Otherwise it functions great and I would purchase it again.

I thought this was a pretty good product until it got caught up in my dishwasher and burned onto the heating element.  I was lucky there wasn't a fire.Whilst I wouldn't give this product a 3 star for something that is my fault, I did want to warn others to be careful.  Because the product is so light weight, it's difficult to place it in the dishwasher in a secure place.Reasons I gave it three stars:It doesn't seem to clean very well in the dishwasher.  I had to put this through two or three times to clear up the lightest of food stains.  Often, food gets sort of glued into the material.  Would be better to hand wash.Also, the little "weights" aren't really very heavy and I've had several instances where this has slipped off of a dish whilst being microwaved.
The button used to make/end calls is located on the back of the main body, so whenever you hit it, you almost knock the thing out of your ear.

I purchased this direct from HP with a $30 coupon, so the total cost was $419 + tax.Nice little netbook, faster than the Atom.  Battery life was about 51/2 hours, so not too bad.It was fine with Netflix, which is what I wanted for travel purposes as well as internet.Recommend.

I love the LCD screen, so cool looking.The keyboard works great. There are lots of macro keys and overall no real complaints for this keyboard.The hand rest is a nice fixture.A great buy for any guy who wants to show off his keyboard.
This case is a total waste of money it's cheaply made and flimsy and on top of that it doesnt even fit my iphone 5 this case is clearly  not made for a iphone 5

Ive only used it once (just got it last week) but so far so good. It looked like it was supposed to (quality wise) and worked like it was supposed to. I also bought the 11 inch steaming ring and the parchment paper circles for steamers and they made it foolproof.

For those of us who need network connecting cables, this CAT-5e cable does the job admirably for far less cost than most retail stores charge.  The plastic tab on each end of the cable is protected by a heavy vinyl sleeve, so it won't be easily snapped off.  The connectors are gold-plated.  You can spend considerably more for a five-foot-long CAT-5e cable and not get any better performance than what this cable offers.  Recommended.

***After using this cover for a couple of months, I've noticed that the 'spine' has relaxed a bit- and fits the type cover just fine!  No more problems with closing the case, and interfering with 'sleep' mode.  I have upgraded the rating on this item to 4 stars now, and I am no longer shopping for another case***This is a decently made case, and fits my surface RT okay.  The 'touch cover' that came with my surface fits a little better in this, but when I got the 'type cover' (has actual keys), this case is a little too tight, which makes the 'sleep' mode a little tricky.  If I am careful to put the type cover next to the surface screen (without trying to close the whole case), I can make sure it's 'asleep' before I close the whole case up, but it's a bit of a pain.  The part of the case that holds the screen has little cut-outs, so you can swipe inward from the edges (LOVE that), and you can use the 'tilt' at many different angles (love that, too).  I don't use the 'kickstand' on the back of my surface RT any more.  It's not a bad case, but I will be replacing it at some point- with one that has a bit more generous spine, to accommodate the 'type cover'.

I've had a Cuisinart mini-prep for many years, and it was still working just fine except that the blade housing cracked.  I was looking for a replacement unit when I discovered that I could simply purchase a new blade!  It fits perfectly and I expect the small processor to work for years to come:  It's just right for chopping nuts, herbs, and garlic.

I use this Sodastream almost every day!! I absolutely love it! Super easy to use. I squirted one in too fast and it foamed all over the place =)
I purchased several of these HDMI Cables to connect my TV's when I moved.  Easy to connect and love them.

This is a great charger for my wife and me.  She has Samsung and I have the iPhone.  We never have to worry about who plugs there phone in where; the attached cable fits hers and the USB slot is for my iPhone.Quick charger, great price, quick shipping!

The chain was beautiful and exactly what I was looking for when making necklaces. For the amount you get for the price though, you might be better served to go shopping at Etsy in the craft supplies section.

We make 20 oz. lattes in the morning and have been using a 2 cup glass measuring cup.  I wanted to get a stainless frothing pitcher and this does just what I need.  Our lattes are two double shots plus milk.  There are no measuring marks but filling this to just above the bottom "V" of the pour spout is just the right amount of milk for one 20 oz. latte.
I needed a camera mount for my monopod that would allow me to tilt the camera and this ball head is perfect!
I bought this for my  daughter for Christmas she is enjoying the gift  a lot  and now her phone is protected

I love this case so much! It got shipped quickly, looks exactly like in the picture, it's super cute, and it fit perfectly. It gave my phone really good protection and it hasn't failed me. I also think it's a good luck charm because I got my case signed by Cody Simpson. :) Absolutely love it!

This is a greatly advanced product over the Thrive.Pros:  All my apps work well, speed and function wise: (Office Suite Pro, Weatherbug, Browser, FB Reader, etc}Browser speed is good to very good.Boot up in under one minute.Display color, brightness, resolution is very good.Excellent open inter-connectability (USB, SD Card, HDMI) (This is a deal breaker for me for competition)Thanks Toshiba.Gorilla Glass. (No screen protector required)Weight and thinness is excellent.Battery life is acceptable, but not quite excellent. Fast enough recharge with supplied cable and charger.Earphone jack for excellent sound. (Internal Speakers are of no use, but I don't expect Bose sound insmall, thin  pkg. I use Bose IE 2 earbuds and get powerful sound performance.)Cons:  Automatic brightness performance is poor. Needs lots of work. This is the only con that should not be there.Not all videos on internet will play.Bloatware that cannot be removed.All other cons are quibbles or cantankerous complaints.The infamous edge light bleed is only discernible on start up, for my unit and my usage. It is of no concern. However, I don't use the device for watching movies.

for a light weight of a kitchen scissors, it's not that bad at cutting chicken bones. it doesn't cut through clean, but it still does the job without you having to strain too much. it does rust easily in the dishwasher, all i do is handwash it. for the price it's not that bad of a buy.

I really like how Jabra didn't just go with traditional plastic shell and opted for a gel like ear buds, they don't feel like they are going to fall out every two seconds when I go running unlike so many others.  The magnet's also a pretty nice feature, I always wrap my earphones into loops before I throw them into my pocket. The magnet keeps the earphones seeming like one cable instead of having your earphones wrap around each other into impossible knots.I always use dictation on my iPhone and it picks up everything I say with ease. Nothing is more frustrating than having to repeat yourself five times for it to type something for you. I've also tried the microphone on my MacBook using Skype and everyone said the quality was on par with my Turtle Beach headset that I generally use for all my VOIP.All in all it's a great headset. If I had any quarrels with headset it would have to be with the curved headphone jack. Even though it being curved is nice for using it with laptops, this is meant for phones, I set my iPhone in my pocket with the headphone jack up. With the 90 curve it's just adding extra stress to the cable. However, even with the extra stress the earphones feel that they are well made and that they're not just going to fall apart in a month.

I have this card in my Tungsten E.  It works well.  I play a lot of MP3's from this this card without any problems and I store seven large reference books on the card that open up very fast.  If your pda opens these files slowly, it may be your pda or the operating system.  Works fine for me.
GREAT PRODUCT! Purchased as gift for someone who ALWAYS drops her phone and breaks it. VERY sturdy. This will help her not break her new expensive phone.

I like the grip texture on the back of the phone but this case suffers from what most all iPhone cases do when carried by men. I dropped the old one on its little mouse head a few times, so had to replace it after only one year.  I really like this model, so got the same thing again.  Would recommend.

I have this set up as my back up on time machine. This was easy to set up and use, it's pretty much a plug it in and forget about it type of product. Never a single problem with accessing it or getting it to power up. Super fast connection and good size make this a great product at a reasonable price.

I blame cheap travel mugs on my scalded tongue today because usually by the time I get to work my coffee from the morning brew is just lukewarm and barely drinkable.  I used this mug for the first time and immediately felt the difference.  I find I now have to pour my PIPING HOT morning coffee out of the travel mug into a ceramic mug to cool it down to the point where it's drinkable - but that's not a complaint.  I couldn't be happier that I can pour out cups of hot steamy coffee all the way through early afternoon from my morning travel mug.Oh yeah, and I can click the top on this and toss this into my favorite handbag without a bit of worry.  This thing DOESN'T LEAK.  Not at all.Only concern is cleaning the top as there are lots of parts.  But you can't have everything.Highly recommend.
Depending on your case this case may give you some issues due to the sheath on the connector. The quality is good and its long enough to be conftorable.

Of all my Rachael Ray, this is my favorite item! OMG, it holds so much, but doesn't hog up the stove! I can cook with this, and still use my other burners! Rachael Ray, you genius you! It's heavy duty, cooks even, non stick inside and out! Love the pour lip in the middle, makes draining/ pouring so easy! I've made soup, pasta, steamed veggies, and it holds enough for my huge family! I have gone all Rachael Ray in my kitchen, I love her stuff so much!

I really like the way this case feels and looks. I have large hands and carry my phone in my pocket. Large enough for a good hold and still goes in my pocket easy. I have not dropped my phone yet , but with the rubber all the way around it should take a hard hit. This is my first Otterbox.
This is really great for doing the smaller jobs but seems like it can do more. Easy on the hands
Fast usb3.0, the ports are plenty. Personally I have no use for the 2 charger ports if they are data usb2.0 that would've been great.

This is a really neat case, but on my phone, the blue plastic part would need to be trimmed off where the charger goes into. The case is too thick for the charger to fit into it, so you have to remove the case to charge at all. Otherwise, it's a nice case, sturdy and nice looking, great price!

My husband shattered his GS 3 screen about two weeks ago. He started watching YouTube videos about how to replace the screen on your own so began our search on Amazon for a quality screen to replace his broken one. Since this product was prime we decided to use it for the shipping benefits and that the kit came with the appropriate tools. I think only because of the condition of the screen we were replacing was pretty bad (shattered all across the top where you would start to remove the screen) my husband ended up his own razor blades and didn't use some of the included tools, but it was nice to have them just in case. He did say that the picks included were helpful. So before you begin this venture I would recommend to replace your screen as soon as you can to avoid more glass damage and have some patience if your screen has more than a few cracks because this little project took 3.5 hours and some very gentle handling. Now that it's all done the glass and adhesive are doing well!
light is gimicky. but the design is nice. plenty of hookups and extras in the box. out of the box only supports 4 sata devices, no biggie for me but maybe helpful to someone else.
There were no defect, a dead computer came back alive, it's been a few months and my computer is still going.
Paid 65 USD for this sandwich maker. Have had it for 4 weeks. Works great. The only thing is that the removal parts are slightly loose.

Have expensive iPod adapter that worked with my Nano 4g.  For whatever reason (thanks, Apple) the 5G Nano displays message it won't work with the adapter.This inexpensive Mini USB adapter works fine.  I presume it will work with other USB gadgets, but haven't tried yet.
this item arrived on time and exactly as ordered - have ordered before & is a good value - used to set up 2nd monitor on my computer

This is amazingly easy to hook up. It took me less than a minute to get it working.I have a setup using a Cisco-Linksys WRT160N Wireless-N. The router is located on the second floor of a 3-floor townhouse. Townhouse is built in 2000 - for those wondering how old is the electrical system I have. Gladly, I can say that there is no dead spot and I can even get my signals on the deck or around the house. I have a Dell Zinio, which is a small profile PC that I use to watch content to my HDTV, mostly for use with Netflix, Amazon Instant Video , Hulu and YouTube videos. With the wireless setup, I run a Speedtest and came up with 8.41 Mbps download and a horrible 0.17 Mbps upload. I can watch HD videos but will stop a couple of times if there are a lot of PCs working at the same time. When I plugged this adapter, I got the signal jacked up to a range of 18.64 to 27.66 Mbps download and 2.23 to 3.69 Mbps upload on multiple Speedtest runs. That's more than double what I was getting before. I don't know exactly how much I am getting from my provider, but is definitely not close to 500 Mbps, not even 100 Mbps so I know there is no way I can test the gadgets real capability. But otherwise, I am pretty happy with the results.What you get in the package is a pair of adapters and a pair of Ethernet cables.I am not really sure why you would need the Push-and-Secure button as you are not broadcasting the signal into the air, instead you are get them through the power line system in your house. Nevertheless, it is good to know that you have that added security if you really feel that you need it.
Seems very sturdy and is Apple registered so I can actually charge my phone with it without issue. (Apple's latest update made it difficult to charge using any non-Apple certified products).
This is by far the best headset I have ever had, and for a good price too! The sound quality is just superb. I haven't had them for very long but I'm already completely satisfied.
This case is very nice, my grand children love and so do I. I wish there were more colors to choose from.Thanks Amazon

This is a very good electric knife sharpener. The operation is incredibly easy. One literally just needs to pass the knife a few times through the machine, and the edge is nicely sharpened. The result is not surgical-knife sharp, but for regular kitchen chores, it is perfect. It even accept the hugely thick chopping knife. The only minor problem I ever had was with a cheap serrated blade, which was just too soft to hold a nice edge, but I don't consider it a fault of this machine.

I purchased this as a Gold Box special to augment the speakers on my laptop.  The build quality is quite nice, the speaker screen seems fairly rugged compared to a lot of travel speakers.  The bluetooth connects with no problem to my laptop and phone but not to my year old MP3 player (old version of BT.)  I decide to take it on a recent weekend outing into the woods with some friends.  I streamed music from my Tilt2 phone by bluetooth and from a Creative Zen player via the cord.  Both sounded great considering the size.  Speaking of the size, it fit easily in my shoulder bag and even in my sister's purse!  We were all laughing that she came with her own personal sound track because you couldn't tell where the sound was coming from.  I only ran if for about 4 hours on some rechargeable batteries so I can't say how long it will last, but it didn't give any sighs of pooping out.  The sound didn't fill the forest, but it was still loud enough for our picnic and a group hike.  It was more than sufficient back in the hotel room.  I will definitely be bringing it on most trips!

Holy crap I just had my Canon 430 EX2 die on me at a wedding and this has been my go to ever sense. I have no idea what happened to my old flash but I hate it and will never spend 450 bucks again. For the price of a canon flash tube you can just buy another one of these. I have had it for two years now with no complaints. :)
So far so good.  Not much to say they are straws, pretty easy to clean.  Feel good when using. USA!

first off, this is not for gaming. the only audio that comes from this (atleast for ps3) is the chat volume. the game volume will NOT come through the device. the earmuff things are extremely small as if it was designed for a baby. within about 1 week, the headphones (whenever i plug them into my computer everything goes through the headset) started to break. whenever i would watch a youtube video the sound would be kind of distorted. although i could easily understand what they were saying, it was very weird. i will be returning these soon...very soon.

This little mount is on it's 3rd phone now (and second vehicle).  It's firmly held an iPhone and 2 different styles of Androids.  The mount secures to the windshield with ease and releases easily as well.  I have a textured dash however and the suction mount won't work on that kind of surface, so be warned if you want to use this for a dash mount.  With a larger windshield however, I have not had an issue putting this little guy in the corner and dropping in my smart phone while using the GPS.  This is a great little holder.

I had this for about 2 years, it was great having the time for my favorite teas I even used it as a kitchen timer, then all of a sudden the timer stopped working, the buttons don't do anything and I even tried replacing the batteries.

This is an ingenious product to monitor many things in your home and business when you are no there. It is important to first open a SKYPE account in order to view the images being recorded on your computer. This is a free service but again you need an account. Many people get the impression that the camera can only be used to monitor a baby alone in their crib or in the care of a babysitter. These two scenarios are not main reason to purchase the Lorex. What do you do when you are on vacation or on a business trip? Many of us worry whether someone may break in and rob us. What about in the event of damage to the home. There could be a flood due to a broken pipe or go forbid a fire. If you had a person who is disabled or elderly this is also perfect to use to monitor and make sure they are not hurt or fallen ill. If you have a pet, a puppy or older dog or even a mischievous dog who loves to just destroy everything in the house..now you can keep on eye on them too.For the business owner, there are days you may take off or need to run out for a job or errand. There are instances you would like to have peace of mind that everything is running smoothly.What is also an added plus here is the extremely easy set up. Just place the camera where you want them, connect to your computer, sign in to your SKYPE account and you are ready to go. For the iPhone users you have the added convenience of viewing on your phone. This product is so versatile. Well worth the price when compared to just purchasing a hidden camera and rewinding and forwarding the days activity everyday. With the LOREX you can check what is going on all day, night at any moment.

OtterBox Defender Series Protective Case for Kindle Fire HD 8.9", Black (with built-in screen protection)The OontZ with Blue Grille by Cambridge SoundWorks - Top Rated Bluetooth SpeakerExcellent sound easy to pair with Kindle Fire, Toshiba Qosmio, Toshiba Satellite, and the Chromebook pixel.  Surprising sound.  Sounds better that the iLuv mo beats.  You will only miss the ability to set your tablet on the iLuv tablet stand.  Sound versus convenience, I would buy the Oontz and the otter box for your tablet.  Enjoy.

Awesome little pinch cups, especially given the low price (paid $6.91 for mine).  I measured mine: one bowl holds 3 tablespoons or 50 milliliters by volume.  Some other stats per Norpro's packaging:* Flexible silicon is heat resistant to 500F/260C.* Ideal for staging ingredients or serving condiments.* Pinch to funnel ingredients.* Does not retain odor or taste.* Dishwasher, microwave and freezer safe.* FDA approved.* Holds 1.75 oz each.Regardless of what Norpro says, these bowls do hold some odors.  I minced some garlic and held it in one of these bowls for about 30 minutes.  I hand-washed the bowl with hot soapy water and then held it to my nose.  I could still smell the garlic.  I washed the bowl again and let it dry.  The next day a faint odor of garlic was still detectable.  I've had the same experience with other pungent ingredients such as smoked paprika.Hope that helps :)

I would give these gorgeous goblets 10 stars if I could.  The cobalt blue is spectacular, and they are very large!  These are very well made, and heavy!  I love these and will be using these every day!  I ordered them two days ago - and received them today.  Unbelievably well packed - arrived in perfect condition!

I actually have two others of these Belkin chargers, and they've all held up great.  Belkin offers a few different kits so you can get one with the correct cable for your devices.  This one comes with a micro USB cable, which will work with most non-Apple devices.  You can plug in your own USB cable to charge devices that don't use micro USB.Pros:+ 2.1A 10W means that your newer devices will charge faster, and still works fine with older gadgets.+ Excellent build quality on the charger and the cables.  I haven't had any issues with them falling apart.Cons:- It's a little expensive compared to the AmazonBasics 2.1A charger.  However, I have had an AmazonBasics charger fail on me, which Amazon very quickly replaced at no cost.- The prongs don't fold in, so it's slightly less easy to pack for traveling.

Not much to say except 5 stars! Love the way it works with both SATA and PATA drives. Shoved a SATA 750GB Seagate drive in, and it runs like a champ. It's not going to be as fast as eSATA, but at this price, it's a winner for things like backing up your data, or as a huge "thumb drive" you can carry around (or grab-and-go in case of earthquake, fire, or flood).

Great case. Need a true black version. Snug fit, very precisely made, very minimal parting seams in the corners, and unlike a lot of TPU cases, this has "crossbars" surrounding the Lightning plug, which stabilizes the part of the "bezel" at the bottom of the screen below the home button. Other cases often have a long thin wimpy strip of material running across the bottom edge of the screen. Minor complaint: The cut-out spaces formed by the crossbars aren't perfectly aligned with the speaker and microphone holes.  I'm sure this will be tweaked in a future release.
The towels were NOT black in color. Had a brown tint and were a gift for my sister, which was rather embarrassing.
The product build quality is not some thing I was personally satisfied with...however may be for the price that is what one gets.

I'm reading the reviews for the newest Magicjack and wondering if I possibly received a couple of the few goods ones produced? I have been a Magicjack customer for a few years and have been satisfied with the services provided, particularly considering the cost of usage. The original Magicjack isn't anything special, but it has performed for me well over the years. My only concern was problems with faxing. Occasionally there will be a brief quality of sound drop. And now and then an incoming call will catch the darn thing sleeping ( All my friends know to call back if they get the auto attendant.I recently had to replace a couple of my devices, do to a power surge that wiped out my router, and the devices. I received one 2014 Magicjack Plus, and one of the older model Pluses (Magicjack was selling them for $29.95). Both are functioning beautifully. No problems with them at all.I really cannot understand the concerns of the people writing the negative reviews.I had Vonage for a few years. I keep it until I was satisfied that Magicjack was a reliable replacement. I now have three Magicjacks (2 Pluses, and old reliable).My mother just moved, so I purchased her a Magicjack Plus and a few years of service for under $200.00. She was able to keep the phone number she has had for twenty-five years, even though she was moving to another state. Nice!The bottom line is that my various Magicjack products have given me more bang for the buck then any other item in any other category that I have ever purchased in my life.I didn't give a 5 star rating because I am not fond of the chat only tech support. That said each time I have had to use tech support I have been helped with my problems. It may however take a bit of patience before the solution finds me.

Jabra aimed to please the active users with an all-in-one package.  This is a decent headset with many functionalities and sturdy construction.The headset looks sporty and works well with my iPhone via bluetooth connectivity.Unfortunately, the fit is suboptimal, especially when I concurrently wear a pair of sunglasses.  In fact, either one of the earpieces would fall off when I wear them along with sunglasses and go for a quick sprint.Audio quality is decent but not impressive.  I enjoyed the built-in FM radio very much, however.Jabra only offers a free 3-month trial of the Endomondo app.  The app itself isn't great and you would need to pay extra $ after the trial period.  For $99, this app should have been included.In summary, this headset isn't worth $99.

So far, this mouse has worked very well.  I have a small Windows tablet with a touch screen.  My fingers are too big to offer precise control, and the tablet stylus is also too big for some menu choices.  I wanted to try a Bluetooth mouse because the tablet has only a one USB port and I didn't want to have to hook an adapter to it.  No problems with battery life so far, as others warned about.

I used this pillow recently following hip...then knee replacement surgery. This pillow helped me sleep - with either both legs up or just the replacement leg up and the other flat down.  I recommend you get several sizes of wedge pillows.  It is hard to stay in one position for very long and you have to mix it up.  I just wish this pillow has something other than a white cover.
The unit arrived and I charged it.  No instructions.  I have yet to use this item.  I am not sure if I am doing something wrong.  You get what you pay for.

We ordered these about a year ago. They have held up decently in the washer and dryer.
