So, you know when you read a great book and go out and get 10 copies to give to your friends and family?  Well, this is like that.The "Tag" is like a necklace that has a clip so you could also clip to your collar or a front pocket.  It has earbuds.  You sync it up with any bluetooth enabled device and are free to go about your business.  Sync it with your phone and you can take calls with it.  Sync it to your computer and you can listen to your music library while you do dishes.  Sync it to your TV so you can watch your shows without waking up your spouse/baby/dog/cat/whatever.So far, the phone thing is awesome because it means no more hunting in my purse or computer bag to get my phone and no accidental dialing or hanging up like was happening with my jawbone.  I like that it can hang around your neck and weighs next to nothing and can clip to your shirt so is great for jogging/exercising or biking (PSA: do not wear headphones while jogging or bike riding and get run over or run into, be smart, duh.)It charges up using the same connector almost everything electronic I own uses except iPods (same charger as my Galaxy Nexus and can be charged off the USB port on my iBook).It is pretty unobtrusive(I'm not a big fan of people shouting into their headsets on their cell phones like they are the only person on earth) and it is has really good sound just like most of my other Jabra products.Comes with different sized earbud covers that are very soft.  These don't hurt my ears like the ones that come with my iPods.Finally, an added plus is you can sync to two items- listen to music on your computer and switch over if a call comes through on your phone.Last but not least, the loop that acts as the necklace/dogtag portion is adjustable.  You can clip it on your collar and shorten it or pop it in a pocket and lengthen it.I have not been a big fan of bluetooth accessories.  I'm one of those weirdos who doesn't take calls on my phone in the car- but I do like this so I don't have to leap across the room, dive over the bed and try to dg through my purse to get my phone before it stops ringing.One caveat: the wiring looks a little on the wimpy side but I'm hoping it holds up as well as my other Jabra products have(and Jabra has been awesome about customer service- I got a cord sent to me free when one I had got kinked and stopped working correctly.  Would be nice if it came with a case but I could also see myself just wearing this alot of the time.THIS IS NOT AN MP3 PLAYER- it does not store data- it is a bluetooth device with a great design for multiple purposes.  I love.

I chose this soap dish based on the reviews and am completely satisfied with it.  In fact, I plan on ordering a second one as soon as I have enough other items to order to qualify for the free shipping.

This cable works fine.In my setup, it connects my upconvert dvd player to my tv.  Works fine.  Signal is good, picture and sound are fine.Only flaw, as a previous reviewer noted, is that the connection is not tight.  Plenty of wiggle, would fall out if nudged.  But at this price, it's acceptable.

I upgraded from theSleep Innovations 12-inch Sure Temp Memory Foam Mattress - Queen Sizeto this. Now, I was already in love with my Sure Temp, so I had no idea where Sleep Innovations could make improvements.Well, they did!First, the biggest thing I noticed was the softness of the bed. I'd been sleeping with a memory foam and pillow top mattress topper on the Sure Temp since day 1 because, well, it was kind of hard. The Supreme Elegance, however, is a complete upgrade in comfort. It has just that right amount of withstanding to hold me up, while being soft enough to conform to my body. I can lay on my side and feel that my spine is straight, not slightly curved like it was with the Sure Temp.The next thing I noticed was temperature. The Sure Temp was supposed to stay cool, but it didn't. Many mornings I would wake up sweating for no apparent reason, but, in the two weeks I've been using the Supreme Elegance, I haven't broke a sweat, yet, even with the vaporizer going in the room.The layer of extra soft memory foam is what has me liking this bed a whole lot more. It may have 1" less base foam than the Sure Temp, but, I don't sink that low, so I never notice. The top layer, I notice!It also has a little bit of a quilted pillow top to it, not too much to make it too foofy (you can do that yourself with a mattress topper, of course), but enough to let you know it's there.There *is* a noticeable difference, although subtle. If you don't wake up with back pain in your current mattress you'll be amazed at how much back pain you really don't have when you wake up in one of these. They're amazing! I'm a Sleep Innovations customer for life, now.

The apple batteries come all compact with what we know as Apples usual simple packaging. The package includes six batteries all covered in silver wrapping, matching the current line of products they offer. Comes with instructions on how the light works on top of the wall adapter, green meaning full, orange charging and blinking when not place correctly. Simple concept, other things include usual manuals with notes on specs. The batteries for me charged about in 4 hours right out of the box, after the full charge i placed them inside my wireless keyboard. The batteries according to my computer were charged only at 76%, odd for saying that it was fully charged. So placed them back for another 2 hours until it was green again. The batteries were still at low charger around 80%, I don't know why exactly the batteries just don't fully charge. Currently it has been about 4 days without using my keyboard and they are staying strong at around 60% battery life. Overall the value is pretty good, just don't understand why these batteries just don't go all the way to 100.Personally I suggest if you are a Apple fan they buy them it's not a high price where you will regret to purchase them. If you are looking for batteries with high quality made them purchase these, other batteries of this kind don't tend to last while on standby. Something I didn't mention is that my keyboard is still on its just the fact that I'm not using it. Regular batteries would be close to dying, the amount of batteries they give you shouldn't be worrying about that anyways. It's a great buy, I would be lying if I said it wasn't. Apple is trying something else and it worked, still suggest to search for some that might be more suitable to you. But with full honesty these are the best you are going to find compared to other leading brands.
Came dead on arrival, battery would not take a charge. untested peace of junk! This is one of those you get (less) than what you pay for!

My husband, the cook in our household, is a stickler for the right tools for the job. These are traditional in shape and materials and work perfectly with our new hand-hammered wok. Their contours of the spatula are made for the curved sides of the wok. The very long handles help the cook keep his distance from oil splatters.You can't hang up the utensils because there are no loops or holes in the wooden handles to suspend them by. But you can't have everything. The main thing is, these utensils are very substantial, nothing flimsy about them.

I use this handy, large, glass pitcher to store water from myWater Wise Distiller, juice, milk, and iced tea.I soak it immediately on emptying it with a combination ofbaking sodaandwhite vinegar. Periodically mold can build up in any container in which you store food, so I use abottle brushto scrub it.This pitcher is easy to pick up and very attractive.Highly recommended.

I debated a four or a five star rating. I have made rice (excellent!), oatmeal (superb!), and steamed veggies (not bad, but you have to time them yourself, and turn the unit off manually). I don't know why someone else had trouble with oatmeal...I made it this morning, and it was perfect. The rice was perfectly cooked. This rice cooker far outshines the unit it was replacing (don't buy Panasonic!).  Also, this rice cooker does take longer to cook than the unit it was replacing...however the rice is SO much better. The old one made the rice so that it was always slightly hard in the center, no matter what kind of rice it was. I'm looking forward to baking a cake!Small details to keep it from a five...maybe 4.5? I had a little trouble initially, as the directions are not as clear as they could be (which is only a minor complaint). Also, before buying this, I read the review about "no audible signal"...I thought that was silly, but the reviewer was right! There is absolutely no sound when the rice is done cooking (not even a click). It's a small thing, but you do have to keep checking it visually to see if it is done. I find it helpful to set a timer to the suggested time for the kind of rice.

Several years ago, we bought 2 of these Lucid memory foam mattresses for our split king adjustable beds. We still love them! They feel great on our backs and yet still provide good support. They have lasted well so far and have not noticeably sunk or left major impressions. Then we bought another one for my youngest son who slept so well the first night he had it, that he was late for work the next day! Then we bought one for my father for his adjustable bed and finally one for another son, and they all equally love this mattress!! The only thing I would possibly caution is the smell initially. When we first bought ours a few years ago, we unpacked them and laid them out on blankets in the garage as the smell was very strong. I am quite sensitive to odors and we had to air them out over a week. Since then, the last 3 we have purchased within the last year, have had barely any odor to them and were put on the beds within a day or in one case, the same day as unpacking. I think that perhaps the company was aware of that strong odor and in the following years, might have done something to help alleviate it.

Needs every device to have their adapter DNS changed to 4.2.2.2 and 4.2.2.3 to work- so only works well with windows devices if you modify all of them!Called tech support several times to resolve issues. First time I was on the phone for hours, but they finally got it to work (would not work with default settings like every other router I've had on cox internet :o). I moved a few miles away, and have the same problems.Called tech support several times since then- and they finally got it working by setting both DNS servers to 4.2.2.2 and 4.2.2.3. I had to modify everyone in the house's wireless and lan adapter settings (6 computers), but can't do it on the wii, phone and in linux the dns settings reset to default on reboot!Tired of calling tech support(overseas), and spending hours on the phone getting nowhere(rebooting the router repeatedly, then finally getting transferred to higher tier). I submitted a ticket,so hopefully will get it replaced/returned,otherwise I have a 80 dollar paperweight!Pros:-nice specs-24 hr tech supportCons:-does not work as plug and play-does not work well with linux(have to constantly set static DNS hack on reboot)-does not work with non-windows devices (phone, wii, anything that can't input the DNS hack)-have to modify settings for every network adapter in every computerI would hope that I was just sent a faulty router that has these problems, but technical support had said it's a common thing, and that all their routers might have the same issues, so it would be a huge gamble to get another trendnet.Now all I can hope for is Amazon taking it back after the return policy, or having an expensive paper weight, because it is unusable for all my devices.-tech support in India, and a lot of wasted time rebooting the router repeatedly before being transferred

The characteristic I like the least is how easily this material scratches.  I've had this on for less than 3 weeks, and it already looks worse than my previous protector looked after 8 months.  The scratches are very visible when the screen is off.  When the screen is on, the scratches make it look dirty.  I deducted 1.5 stars for how easily this scratches.I was able to reposition this protector several times in the application process without losing adhesion.  I advise against doing this "dry", as you will likely need to reposition it during application, and this allows lint and dust to get in between the protector and the screen.The cut fits the Exhibit 2 very well.  I took off a half star because it's too perfect.  This makes placement significantly more difficult.  The screen is ever so slightly recessed relative to the plastic sides.  So if you place this off-center, you have to remove it and try again.  If they made this slightly smaller than the screen, it would still protect properly, but be easier to install.

If you are looking to make the healthy switch from plastic storage containers to glass, then I would recommend this set.  I had bought a few rubbermaid glass bowls at the store to try them out.  The ones I bought at the store have a removable gasket.  I thought this set from Amazon did too, but they don't.  It's all one piece, and easier to clean than the ones with the gasket.  I actually think the one piece might have a tighter seal.  I love the way these things stack in the refrigerator.  I hand wash my lids so I can't' tell you how they do in the dishwasher.  I've tried other glass storage container sets, and to me Rubbermaid makes the tightest seal.

I totally needed USB3.0 on my MacBookPro, mid2011. After some research I bought two of these for my and my partners job.They we're a little hard getting to work, but I believe It was some trouble with old drivers.A very good thing is Oyen has excellent customer support, so if you have any question or trouble, they are very helpful and quick.I had questions to them regarding bus powering it, with the supplied cable, and help came instantly with advice and a link to the new Mountain Lion drivers.It is expensive, but it works, it does what it promises, unlike other competitors that I haven't had experience with, but colleagues I've spoken to have.There might be cheaper solutions, I found this one and recommend it totally.I've also bought eSata + USB3.0 enclosures from them, and they work like a charm (compared them to Ankers, and both are excellent, similar write/read speeds, both work on Mountain Lion for me).Bye!

I use these for my surrounds with 16 gauge wire.  The description says that it'll accept 12 gauge wire, but I would highly doubt that you could fit the plastic insulation from 12 gauge wire.  I'd say that 14 gauge wire would be the max since there is very little clearance left with my 16 gauge wire.Dimensions - for those concerned about clearance behind your receiver, the base of these plugs are 1 1/16 inches.

I had used this before and it took forever to charge my ipad. I believe it is 500mAh and not 1000mAh (other reviews will tell you) so you may want to look for another charger like others have said for the ipad. I bought this for overnight charging for ipods and cell phones.

I am a Kindle-Phile.  I have Original Recipe, Keyboard, Fire, and Paperwhite.  The Paperwhite is by far my favorite, and part of my love is the great case it lives in (Amazon Kindle Paperwhite Leather Cover, Royal Purple (does not fit Kindle or Kindle Touch).  While the Golla cover is pretty, and does the job, the leather cover, which is the same price, is a MUCH better option.THE GOOD:+ Pretty+ Has a strap to keep it closed+ Thick and therefore protectiveTHE BAD:- Strap to keep it closed works; magnet closure of the leather cover is easier- Thick and unwieldy in the hand; hard to hold open with one hand. Front cover does fold backwards but very stiffly.- Bizarrely placed '4 corner' elastic straps that hold it in place- they are centered on the inside (as opposed to being on the edges) and it's hard to cram my teeny thin paperwhite inside.- Not a sleep/wake cover- With paperwhite inside, it's double (!) the thickness of the leather cover, plus 1/2 inch higher and wider.  Why so much bulk for such a sleek kindle??WHY THE LEATHER COVER IS BETTER:* Thinner, and smaller in both length and width, also very very light* Magnetic closure makes sure the case stays closed, even if you forget* Front cover easily flips all the way back for easy one-handed reading* Sleep / wake case so you never have to turn your kindle off or on again... the cover does it for you (with a sensor)* Kindle very secure in recess in case - not held bu straps* Textured surface nice and grippy* Comes in many colors* As sleek and easy as your paperwhiteBOTTOM LINE:This does the job, and is pretty, but the amazon kindle leather cover is SOOOO much better

The My Passport series of hard drives are awesome. I've used them for years and years and still have my oldest one that is about 5 years old. It still works great. I got the Oppo DV-980H expecting truly stellar DVD upscaling performance and while the image quality was better than the sub-$100 DVD players it wasn't as good as another DVD player in its price range - the Pioneer Elite DV-49AV.  The Pioneer has a noticeably sharper picture (not by a lot, but noticeable none the less).  Plus the Pio has better audio quality.  I don't have a home theater set up, and on standard TV speakers or my headphones the DV-49AV just sounds richer (the Oppo strikes me as a little too much on the Treble side - thin and irritating).  Plus the Oppo hung three times in quick succession for up to 10 seconds on a disc that the Pioneer played without issue.  Being able to track scratched Netflix DVDs  is pretty important to me.  Another odd thing, while physically smaller, the Pioneer had a good bit more "heft" to it.  The Oppo felt a bit more cheaply constructed for its size.Its a good player - definitely better than the lower tier models, but the Pioneer beats it for the same price (and the Oppo is going back).Pioneer Elite DV-49AV

My morning doesn't start without Morning Edition, but hotel radios never seem to work. Was looking for a portable bluetooth speaker to use with radio apps. Had planned to buy a Solemate or a Jambox, so I went to Best Buy. On the salesperson's recommendation, I had a listen to the Flip. It sounded better than the others, seemed a bit more solid, and cost a lot less. Would have bought it on the spot, but they were so popular they couldn't keep them in stock or even place an online order. . Now I had to find one.For both voice and music, it sounds great to my middle aged, non-audiophile ears. Pairing it up to the iPhone was a snap; it only lost connection for apps that go quiet when the phone goes into sleep mode on battery (i.e., WUNC's app fades, but Pandora doesn't). When the phone was plugged in, I had no issues. For an hour of play every morning, there was enough battery to last the week unplugged. The cord is long enough to keep it plugged in for longer use.It lays flat or can stand if space is limited, and I didn't notice it shaking around. Power and volume buttons are easy to reach in either position. I like that it includes a carry sleeve, but wish that sleeve had a pocket or strap for the cord. Really wish JBL had gone with a standard miniUSB so travelers wouldn't have to carry another cord & plug. The white really looks nice - makes me wish I had gone with white for the iPhone and the iPad.WorldWide Stereo delivered on time and securely packaged. Would purchase from them again.

After some little cockroach got into my SUV and stole my boxed 6x9's around the Holidays, I was dreading ever going through that again and decided it was time to rock some door speakers.After reading so many positive reviews of these speakers by Pioneer, I decided that I would go with 2 sets, one for front and one pair for the rear doors. I have them amped running full range using their crossovers, with bass boost on for now, but will probably turn that off once I get my subs back in there.They sound overall pretty fulfilling, not like I am missing any range across the 100Hz to 16KHz spectrum, since the highs are definitely there with the two tweeters on board, with a lot of midrange. They have nice rubber surrounds and some kind of mica cone.The Bass response is not bad considering the size of the woofer cone. I did use some 1/2" foam weather stripping to make a sealing\reducing gasket where the speaker frame meets the door sheet metal.I ran dedicated 16 gauge high strand Cu wire to each from the amp,- as opposed to using the 20 gauge existing factory wiring. It was a pain, but worth it. Soldering was the way to go here with heat shrink tape at each terminal. They come with mounting Philips head screws. Did not use baffles, but I decided to spray some rustproofing spray on the insides of the doors regardless to keep some of the acoustic resonance of the sheet metal down. They are worth the price for sure, no regrets so far, except for installing these in the dead of Winter in the upper 20's was a bummer.

Great camera.  I prefer to use the image stabilizer and High ISO mode and no flash.  I've been able to take some FANTASTIC indoor shots that capture the mood of the environment far better than the flash-heavy, sterile-looking images that all point and shoot cameras seem to take when in automatic mode with the flash turned on.These shots are better than the ones I'd be able to take by hand holding my Digital Rebel XTI -- no I have not yet taken the plunge and purchased an image stabilizing lens for it.I also love the wide angle lens, even the small bit of spherical aberration that shows along the edges of the images when fully zoomed out.Pros:- Image Stabilization will change the kind of shots you attempt with such a small camera -- it's like having a tripod on hand at all times.- Minimal shutter lag- Good looking images- Much easier to use (compared with recent predecessor models I've played with)- Great battery life even with image stabilization on.Cons:- High ISO mode is a bit grainy- Camera should charge when plugged into USB -- it does not.- I wish it had a fill flash mode -- if it does I can't find it.- I wish it had RAW mode -- I have come to appreciate the superiority of this mode thanks to the Digital Rebel XTi -- wow.Bottom line: I bring this camera everywhere.  You will too.

The Honeycomb update that was just released provides a much needed User Interface revamp and I couldn't be happier with it. This thing has excellent noise cancelling.  I drive a work truck with a Cummins diesel engine and with it sitting at idle and the window down my wife can't tell the engine is running and I sound clear.  That's pretty amazing.  If you are hard of hearing you'll like this headset because the audio can be turned way up in comparison with the usual BT headsets out there.  The battery life is also amazing.  I think it has like 100+ hours on standby with the light blinking.  I never ran the thing down and just went ahead and recharged it for the heck of it.Now for the bad stuff.  When I first got it I may have gotten 30 ft. away from my phone at max and that range decreased to less than 20 ft. before the audio got choppy and I tried it with my HTC EVO and my wife's LG Optimus.  I contacted VXI support and nothing they recommended fixed the problem so I sent it back.  The other poor thing was the ear loop.  It is not comfortable at all and that's not just because the headset it a little more heavy and bulky than almost anything out there.  The ear loop could have been designed better but, that is not how VXI intended for it to be worn.I'm thinking about ordering another one since I apparently received a bad one since the range appeared to diminish over time and I hope the next one has close to the 66 ft. range that is touted in the specifications.  I wish I knew somewhere to ask other owners if they are actually getting anywhere near 60 ft. before the audio gets choppy.

Pro: Looks great, able to put 32" led-lcd tv on top of it.Important: MUST have one RIGHT ANGLE SATA CABLE for Optical drive installation; Case will not fit with most modular power supplies; ONLY work with Low profile graphic card and low profile expansion cards.Note: Make sure to follow steps in Silverstones instruction booklet. It will make installation and planning much easier.Tricks: The metal bar on the upper right of power supply can be lower be removing the screw on the back side, for standard or larger optical drive, it may not fit if you slide it in from the front. Position the optical drive just right on top of case and slide in from inside. Also the power cable connector for sata optical drive, make sure to use the one in the middle, not the first one due to limited space between the optical drive and power supply.Will work with Micro ATX / M-ITX motherboards only.Optical drive: Liteon iHES212-08, Power supply Antec EA-380D, read the tricks.Have fun and enjoy HTPC.

June 11, 2010  No trouble & still working wonderful.  Food is great, but at $10 a gallon for oil, I can't brag about savings.  Best fryer I ever had & sorry to hear this model has been discontinued.HilditchIf you have a professional kitchen, this is for you.  Sub-Zero, Thermador, Bosch and a Waring Pro.  Go figure!  If not, but you are a good cook, this is the piece to get you started.  The amateur won't like it, but the good cook will love it.  By the way, it is 17"x12"(with handles)x12" high and the full mark is 2 cups short of a gallon.  You will find the temperatures are as indicated on the dial if you let it heat up for about 20 minutes.  I used a calibrated thermometer and found the oil temperature 10 degrees shy when the light first came on, but on the mark after the whole unit came up to operating temperature.  High temp was 376 degrees when fully warmed.Cooking room temperature 1 lb. batches didn't affect the oil temperature and normal recycle time is just a few seconds.  Larger or cold batches take longer as to be expected.  The next best thing out there is a 220-volt commercial fryer with 2500 watts, twice the cost and fewer features.  Remember after you rewire your kitchen, commercial equipment works best when it is on 10 hours a day under a $4,000.00 hood.This is the best professional fryer I could find at any cost.  Just use your cooking sense by adjusting time, temperature & batch size to the unit and it will treat you better than any other.If you don't like this fryer, it is surely because of the archer (cook), not the arrow (tool).  Best to go out to eat.Hilditch

It's going to be difficult to beat this camera in this price range. Everything from the metal build quality, retro form and modern function to the resulting image quality on the FujiFilm X20 seems excellent. Certainly, the image quality does not best a DSLR, but that's not what a small camera like this is about.If you are looking for a fairly small camera that has a zoom lens and want image quality that can at times rival that of a DSLR, look no further.The zoom lens retains an f2.8  speed throughout the zoom range, even when zoomed all the way to 112mm. Many other cameras have to go to something like f4.5 when zoomed in. The f2.8 when zoomed in is great for getting a nice bokeh for portaits for example. Note, this camera has f2 only when not zoomed in at all, at the 28mm focal length.The optical viewfinder has a nice crisp display with a 'head up' type of electronic overlay in it which is very nicely implemented. I really like the way that you turn on and off this camera is unique and feels very natural. You just turn the zoom lens ring. No having to find and fiddle with a switch on the camera body or press a button.While the image sensor on this camera is smaller than that on the higher end FujiFilm x100s model, the images are still of quite high quality since the lens and sensor are very well matched and engineered. This camera also has the zoom lens whereas the X100S does not and the X100S also costs double what this camera does.This camera also shoots full 1080p video.All in all, I'm very impressed with this camera as a 'take everywhere' camera, especially for the price. It also has a great classic design and sturdy feel to it which evokes a real sense of nostalgia and style that I really appreciate. When I don't want to carry around my big DSLR camera and heavy lenses, I can have faith in instead bringing the Fujfilm X20 with me and have a small lightweight and stylish camera that gives me professional results.

Pros+Recognized as a standard USB keyboard (works in supported BIOS, Windows Setup, etc)+Has shortcut/combo keys+Very long battery life so far+Key options to rotate to "vertical" orientation, allowing for one-handed operation and clickingCons-No dedicated Windows key (accessed via combo with the Function button)-F-Keys row ends at F8, which limits BIOS access options on older/some boards which wan F10, F11 or F12 for some functions-Range is pretty limited and a little inconsistent-Keys are in a grid pattern as opposed to QWERTY offset, so there are occasional 'by feel' miskeys-Enter and other long keys don't activate on all portions of the key only on one side. Not sure if this is a design flaw or a defect in my unit.Neutral/ Has a laser pointer, so if all else fails you can entertain your pets with it. I'm sure it was intended for presentations, but that's not how I roll.SummaryAll in all this is a pretty solid little device and my only major complaints would be the range, which it can be a bit tough to feel out where your limitations are. However, for a small, inconspicuous device this is tough to top. The track pad is reasonably sensitive and not too tough to get used to and in spite of the drawbacks it's indispensable. I would love it if the range were longer and I could easily forgive all the other faults as mere irritants, but the range limitation kind of makes you feel tethered - I'm debating routing a powered (and boosted, at this range) USB cable over to near my seating area just to overcome the range limitations. Either that or boosting the internal antenna, we'll see.

I bought these because I like Victorinox in general.  I have one of their bread knives and it is brilliant.  I was in the process of updating my kitchen-- no more cruddy utensils!  Got some nice heavy, quality flatware but needed steak knives.  These are sharp, don't get me wrong, and that is a big part of the purpose of a knife of course...but these feel cheap.  Really cheap.  So cheap I had to take a picture of the packaging and send it to the company to ask if they really made it, or if it was a horrible mistake/counterfeit.  They said it was indeed, theirs.  The flimsy dollar store paperboard package these come in make them absolutely unsuitable for say, a wedding gift or the like.  You'd be embarrassed.  It's the thinnest possible red paperboard (think like an index card) around a plastic tray, with generic white printing on it which does not even carry the traditional Victorinox logo nor any model numbers or company information.  No lie.The weight of the knives is almost nonexistant.  The handles are lightweight plastic.  I tried to find out if they were some sort of 'special' heat resistant wonder-space plastic to justify the price...but I could not.  Looks like it's just plastic.  Looking back, I would not have dropped this kind of money for these knives.  On the other hand, for a backyard BBQ or something they'd be perfect-- and they are sharp.  So much so, I've lost a few bits of fingertip slicing tomatoes etc.  I do find myself using them for quickly slicing sandwich toppings and then the sandwich-- for which these knives are great.  To each their own, but I expected more from this company.

We have had the older model of this coffee maker for years.  It started leaking and I tried to fix it but the seals were cracked.  Read all of the reviews on coffee makers and decided that this one is best for us.  We like the thermal carafe because it doesn't burn our coffee and we can just bring the pot wherever we are hanging out that day.  It keeps the coffee hot enough for us.  We're not coffee snobs but we really like the flavor this coffee maker produces.  I even drink the last of the pot the next morning.  With the older model, it still had a little warmth to it.  This one had cold coffee this morning.  The new model fixes the dripping carafe issues that the old one had.  Bunn redesigned the lid and pour spout.  Now, the lid is smaller and surrounded by a well that flows into a well designed pour spout. 4 pots of coffee in this model so far and not a drip out of place.The Bunn models make coffee real quick so they can't have a pause and  serve function.  I just put my cup in place of the carafe when I am pouring my wife's cup.  She just waits a minute - this thing is fast.  Another note, you have to use the Bunn filters.  They are about a quarter inch taller than the  others.  The Bunn coffee maker brews so quickly that it overflows the cheap filters and you will get grounds in your coffee.  Bunn filters are $1.50 for a box of 100 in our local grocery store or you can get them here on Amazon.Hope this helps.  I'm going to enjoy my coffee now.

Excellent buy. It's easily my favorite electronic device. Some thoughts on the various features:I tend to travel a lot. I usually lug around my digital Rebel and take a lot of pictures. A 6.3 megapixel camera tends to fill up memory cards quickly. On my last trip I brought along my Zen Vision and used it to back up my digital pics right from the CF card. I didn't need to carry around my laptop to download the pictures, and I only had to bring two memory cards with me. My back appreciated this change.The built-in screen far surpasses my expectations. It will not replace a laptop screen for some applications, but the resolution is FANTASTIC.I already used the Creative MediaSource Organizer to manage my music collection, because I had a Zen mp3 player. This software is superior to the offerings of any other vendor I am aware of. It's neat, fast and powerful. It's multiple panes and intuitive interface make it easy to organize and transfer content.The Media Explorer is used to transfer pictures and video. The transfer is amazingly quick and painless. When viewed on the Vision's screen, the video is bright and clear. The pictures have excellent contrast. I wish there was a rotate feature so that portrait-oriented photos could take up the whole screen. Then again, I haven't read the manual yet so maybe that feature is there somewhere.The interface used to navigate between the photo, video, music features is ridiculously easy to figure out (like I said, I haven't read the manual yet and I have used all the features successfully).One previous reviewer implied that you cannot charge the Vision with a USB plug only. This is not the case. The unit comes with a charger that can be plugged in to a standard wall outlet. However, the Vision will also charge while connected to your computer with the included USB cable, even if if is not plugged in to the charger. If you travel with a laptop, you don't need to carry your charger. I find that plugging in the Vision to a USB port will fully charge the device in a few hours - a bit slower than if you plug it in to the charger.When at home I plug the Vision in to a Bose Wave Music System. The combo is absolutely amazing. It fills my apartment with fantastic sound, and there is no need to mess around with CD's. It's nice not having to have my computer connected to the speaker system to listen to MP3's.One reqret - I purchased a white unit. In retrospect I would have chosen the darker color. The white unit tends to stand out while watching movies in dark environments - like an overnight flight.

I bought this to help me clean up after 20 animals; and because I have tile and wood floors.  The scrubbing on my hands and knees and then sopping every thing up with towels was getting old.  I also was not cleaning the floors it as often as they needed.  For heavy duty cleaning forget the solution tank, you will get a bad case of trigger finger.  I fill a gallon bottle with cleaning solution, slosh it on the floor, scrub with scrub brush (while standing), then pull out the machine to scrub a bit more and then suck up the filthy solution.  For touch up cleaning I leave the machine set up, slosh some solution on the floor and then scrub and suck up with the machine.  My floors are a lot cleaner, the clean-up is easy, and I am not crippled afterwards.  But, it is not faster.  I bought a used machine and got lucky that all the parts were there and working.

This looks like the exact same item asthis mug; I guess buy whichever one is cheaper at the time.The New York City Greek diner coffee cup is an icon. If you've ever seen an episode of "Generic Cop Drama: New York," then you've probably caught a glimpse of the paper version of one of these being purchased from a sidewalk food cart by your favorite detective, or perhaps littering the dashboard of a stakeout vehicle. Even with the Starbucks incursion, you can still get a cup of regular (that's with cream and sugar, for the uninitiated) served in one of these just about everywhere in the City.And that's what makes it such a great gift for the past, present, or future New Yorker -- its ubiquity. It's an immediately recognizable image of one of the greatest cities in the world. Give this to anyone with even a tangential relationship to NYC, and they'll have a smile on their face when see it. And while this does make a great gift, it's actually a pretty terrible coffee mug. Since there is no handle, and the ceramic is a good conductor of heat, this isn't really practical as hot fluid drinking vessel. It's better suited for something like holding pens and pencils on your desk, or maybe even as a little herb pot.This is a fun gift, and worth every penny. Heck, pick up one for yourself while you're at it!

I ordered two of these cabinets. I would have given it a 5 except for one little problem.. the leg snapped off of one  of them after assembling it and setting it in the bathroom. I had to use liquid nails to fix it, but now that it's fixed, it seems to be fine.. I'll be a little afraid of moving it though, for fear of the leg, or another leg popping off. You need to screw the legs onto the unit, however, that wasn't the problem. The leg is nailed to the piece of wood you actually screw onto the unit.. The leg popped off that piece of wood.However, having said that, it's a nice looking cabinet.. I like the darker finish and the knobs that came with it are nice looking. The directions were pretty easy to follow. It took me about 1 1/2 hours to put one together (and took my husband longer to put the 2nd one together. lol)... The parts went together accurately with no issues. Each piece has a little sticker on it with it's part number (all stickers are on the back side of piece so doesn't show when put together).. Also, there's a picture of each screw or connector in the directions, and the different sizes are all in their own little baggies, which have the part letter on the bag.. Really, I don't think they could have made it any easier to put this together... I'm impressed! If you follow the directions step by step, it'll go together pretty easily.I just redid my bathroom with old fashioned wainscoting (in cream) and wallpaper, with an old fashioned bathroom vanity sink done in dark wood, and these cabinets match perfectly. My bathroom is small, so these add storage without having to put in a big cabinet. You'll need to fold your towels a certain way to get them to fit in good, but it is very doable. Holds a lot of misc. items like soap, shampoo, etc.. Each unit has 3 shelves, two are adjustble, so you can adjust to fit whatever you want to store in them, plus there's a drawer that works fine on my units.. No issues at all other than the one leg that popped off (but is fixed).another plus..I looked at these on other sites (like Home depot and Overstock).. they cost twice what I paid for each unit everywhere I saw them, so I basically got 2 of them for what I would have paid for one if I'd purchased elsewhere.. As usual, Amazon offered the best deal!

This is a good, useful, precise timer. Easy to set, easy to use, and it has a loud enough alarm. The build is hard plastic. The buttons are soft rubber.  The timer stands upright by itself thanks to the curvature at the base. The timer is 2" tall, 3 1/2" wide, and has a depth of 1" (with clip).  There is a strong magnet on the back of the timer, so it will stay vertically stuck to any metallic surfaces, even BBQ grills. The magnet is on a clip, so you can also clip it to your clothing.Two AAA batteries are included. The buttons are comfortable, soft rubber, and easy to press.  This timer allows full precision by giving three separate buttons to change the hour, minute, and seconds. The screen is 1 and 5/8" wide and 3/4" tall. There is no backlight to the screen, so in dimly lit rooms, you need to get the light to hit it at the right angle to see it clearly. Outdoors and in good daylight, it's easy to see and read the numbers.Four modes: Clock, Stopwatch, Countdown A, and Countdown B. Set the precise time you want for the countdowns, set the alarm to beep, or vibrate, or both. You can set both countdown timers to two different times each and run them both, but the alarm you set will be the same for both. The vibrate alarm is kind of weak, and you won't hear it in a different room, but you'll notice it if it's attached to your person. The beep alarm is loud and can be heard a few rooms away. The beep alarm is not the best sound, just the basic double beep, but it works. This timer could use a backlight and a few more alarm choices. Rating 4.5 out of 5.

I received this at no cost in exchange for writing a review. Here are my comments.1. This is a basic "lipstick" charger that comes with a micro USB cable for charging.2. The back of the box says a full charge time is 6-8 hours, which is pretty long for a unit with such a small output.3. I fully charged this, then used it to charge my iPhone 4S from 70% to 96%, which took an hour. Acceptable, but not impressive.4. My main issue with this charger is that there is no way to know how much of a charge is left. There is no button to press to make the two indicator lights go on. We had so many USB devices and far, far too many charging bricks so I looked into this one.  Reviews were good, product description seemed to fit...I bought too.  Several months later, I bought this one for my MIL.These little units are the perfect way to replace all those power bricks.  They output 5 amps; 2 ports at 2 Amos, 2 at 1 amp.  That said, note the FIVE amp limitation; I regularly use all four ports, but I typically only have one full iPad plugged into it and I use it overnight, so even if one of the ports was dragging a bit, it still works fine.The only negatives - the USB ports are pretty snug; you're not going to be easily plugging/unplugging cords, so get a few extras to just leave in it.  Second negative, the blue power indicator is REALLY bright and a nuisance in the bedroom at night.Other than that, and they barely deserve mentioning, this is the best multi charger I've found so far, especially in the twenty-something price range.  Added bonus (at least in my mind)...this unit comes with a regular power cord, so you're not fighting to get it in an outlet or getting it to fit around other equipment.  I think I bought the first two in early 2013, maybe...? Either way, these things run great and you can't beat the price so I recommend them.

For the price, this is a great induction burner. It's amazingly fast and efficient to boil water, the low end is consistent and low, and features like the countdown timer work well. I use it all of the time. But there are some negatives to this specific unit. First of all, the available power settings range from 1 to 10, but the only useful ones are 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, and 10 (for boiling water). I wish they had given more options at the low end for precise control when sauteeing and sweating. Even 5 is a lot of heat, and works well for searing steak in a cast-iron pan. I imagine other, more expensive burners have better control. Secondly, the "maintain temperature" feature is completely useless. An iron pan set to 300 F will get to 600 F (measured by IR thermometer), overheat, and cause the unit to shut itself off. Do not purchase this unit thinking you will use that feature.Other than that, I highly recommend everyone with an electric stove purchase one of these immediately, as the first step towards moving your kitchen to a technology that's actually good for cooking!

I decided to get this after reading so many good reviews on this griddler and it seems to be a perfect product.  I got this a week ago and tried grilling twice.  Here are the problems:  1) It doesn't heat fast enough especially the bottom grill.  I was expecting sizzling sounds when I put the meat on but it just sat there until I closed the lid. There are a lot of temperature settings  and sears  is the only useful setting and there is no instruction on the different settings.  It is not heating  up as quickly as I've expected.2) Now here is the real bad part - the upper grill came apart when I open the lid during grilling and I had to push it back locked with a wooden spoon to close the lid and finish grilling.  I tried the grill for 2 days  and both time this happened.  Believe me, the grills were in the right place and locked before I started grilling.  I am not happy at all with cuisinart this time and will return it back to the store.  They have to make this griddler  heat faster and lock the removable grill securely during cooking.  This is too dangerous falling apart during cooking.  Cuisinart did you test this out ??

For the money, it works and does what it says.But that's about where the good news ends.The mounting system is the worst I've seen of any DVD players. Flimsy thin Velcro straps that just don't work very well, units constantly slip and move around on the back of the headrest. When they slip down your kids will be able to kick the units to death. A pain to put up & a pain to take down when you go into a restaurant or something.The cords are very poorly designed because they go in on the sides of the unit and hang to the floor. That means when any child walks around in the back getting in/out of car, they will step on the cords and place stress on the little jacks. This problem could have been remedied if Sylvania had simply placed the jacks on the bottom of the unit as other manufacturers have--that way the cords just pop out instead of pressure to the jack being applied at a right-angle. Because of this, we immediately started having problems getting the second screen to work.After six months of pretty light & relatively gentle use, the 2nd screen would not work ever. Very frustrating to kids when one screen works & one won't.Fortunately had a good return policy where I bought.SUMMARY:Good:works well when it works, decent picture and excellent audio, very good value for priceBad:terrible mount, cord attachment, and product life.Recommendation:spend a little more for a different brand, or better yet check out the DVD car mount that fits between the headrests by Digital Innovations on Amazon. Its under $30 and will enable you to do the same thing with only one screen that mounts between the seats so both backseat passengers can see, out of kicking distance and with less cord/2nd screen hassle. With the money you save on two screens you can buy one quality screen probably getting a bigger screen and better operating product.

Well the title says it all... this little tiny black box has the brains to convert your existing Alarm Panel into a PC compatible (not overly useful in todays world), or a bridge with added USB to a ZWave controller, such as Micasaverde (MCV).  When joining this product to the MCV, you have a virtual keypad of your security system anywhere you have internet access.  Of course, you must be sure your system is compatible, which is well documented on MCV web site, as well as Nutechs.Like all these low volume products, expect a few quirks.  But unlike most of these small electronic products, made in China with an 800# tech support going to the Phillipines, or some country where its nearly impossible to decipher their accents....this product is USA made, and USA supported.  So the few little quirks I had were immediately resolved by the company with very knowledgeable and courteous tech.  Kudos to Nutech.  Although I have only been up and running for a week, the system has exceeded my expectations.  Obviously others feel the same as so far, all 5 star reviews.

I got this so that I could control my desktop from anywhere in my basement (I have a ton of stereos that get their signals from the computer) and I must say that I am quite pleased. The controller is very easy to understand and there are no extra drivers needed to load. It has a number and letter pad, volume control, mouse with left/right click, a click-and-drag button (it's between the left and right click buttons) that allows you to drag files/windows with the remote, a sleep button (will only wake your computer up if your USB ports stay on all the time), and a lot more. The only problem is that the instructions are TERRIBLE. There are typos like crazy and overall the instructions do nothing to actually help you learn how to use the controller. The fact that it is very easy to learn is why I didn't take a star off for that.I have learned how to use the shortcut buttons on the top of the controller. Just place a shortcut on the desktop of the file/program that you want to run with the remote. Right-click the shortcut and select "Properties". Click the "Shortcut Key" box and press Ctrl+Alt and either 1/A/F1, 2/B/F2, 3/C/F3, or 4/D/F4. I tied 3 letters/numbers together in each group because the controller sends all 3 of those signals when you press the button. So, if you press the movie button, the computer receives Crtl+Alt+2 AND B AND F2. 1 is for the music button, 2 is for the movie button, 3 is for the picture button, and 4 is for the TV button. Apply the changes. Then, when you press the button on the remote, it will open/run all the linked shortcuts. You can set a bunch of different shortcuts with the same keys, so you can open a ton of programs/files all at one time. Nifty!

If you only get the product with one insert, that insert chops very small/fine.

The first rule of technology and business travel is to make sure you do not kill your back carrying around a lot of useless technology. This battery has earned a spot in my Bag of Holding. It can simultaneously charge multiple devices, such as your phone and iPad, which can be very useful after a long day on the road. I have found myself taking advantage of the extra battery life in a number of ways:1 - Reading on my iPad. I love the iPad but it's battery life does not rival that of other eReaders. Oftentimes I find that I am out of battery life towards the end of the day, just as I'm ready to do some reading. Standard iPad charging cords are not long enough for me to plug in and still read comfortably, and an extension cord isn't a great option either. I have found myself plugging in the iPad to this charger and reading much more comfortably that I have with other solutions.2 - We have had a couple of storm-related blackouts over the past few weeks. This device kept my iPhone and iPad charged so that I could keep up with developing news. It even has a built-in flashlight that came in handy during the blackouts.3 - I recently used this battery to power external speakers and a PICO projector for an ad hoc presentation. It had more than enough juice for a 30 minute presentation.I have read the comments about batteries over heating. I can believe it that some people have had bad experiences. Then again, all large modern batteries have the same potential for over heating. The problem is sometimes related to manufacturing problems, but is most often the result of damage done to the battery by the owner. The larger the battery's storage capacity, the larger the potential impact of over heating. That means you can either deprive yourself of a powerful battery out of fear, or you can take reasonable care of your batteries so that they don't bet banged around unnecessarily. It's a classic trade-off. In my case, I would rather have a single battery that can power all of my tech gadgets.

I have tried several manual sharpening systems including Spyderco and Edgemaker Pro but never used flat stones due to the learning curve and time involved to get that razor edge. Stones are the professional's choice but take a while to use and master.Until recently, I was most pleased with the electric Chef's Choice Trizor XV which takes any knife down to a 15 angle. But it is time consuming to say the least. It, however, makes knives extremely sharp despite "shaving" metal from the cutting edge. It is the best of all the Chef Choice sharpeners I have used for the past several decades.My new electric sharpener, the Smith's Edgeware with ceramic interlocking stones is the best I have ever used, period. My knives are sharper than ever before. And it is an effortless system in that it sharpens both sides of the cutting edge at the same time so you don't have to feel for the burr before continuing each stage of sharpening. It takes less than 30 seconds to pull the knife through the slot 10 times and, being ceramic, virtually no metal is removed.It is simple to use, produces extremely sharp edges and takes much less time than any of the other Chef Choice or manual sharpeners I've used, although all of these other sharpeners can do a great job with patience on your part.[...].It sharpens to a 17.5 angle which lays between the European (think Wustof) 20 and the Japanese 15. Since my knives were already at 15, it probably made it easier for this sharpener to reshape up to the 17.5 angle per side.But one of my half width cleavers was at 20 so it gets pulled through a manual carbide stone slot 10 times to reshape it's edge and then goes through the rotating wheels.Even my wife asked what I had done to the knives attesting to their sharpness.This sharpener has a manual ceramic slot for serrated knives and a pull out ceramic slot to sharpen right or left handed scissors.Both work extremely well and my serrated edge knives are sharper than when new.Sounds like a commercial but I ran my 30+ knives that I keep in rotation (I replace some now and then with others kept in storage) through the ceramic wheels and they came out razor sharp.I realize that many frown upon electric sharpeners but this is an entirely new concept using ceramic interlocking wheels so little metal is removed.Last year, I was singing the praises of the Chef's Choice Trizor XV sharpener and it is a great one. But it takes much longer and much more exact effort pulling the blades through all of the slots and stopping to feel for the burr.The Edgeware ceramic interlocking wheel sharpener is my sharpener of choice right now, surpassing what I had considered the best before.The Edgeware is the best of the best for me.

I use this table for casual listening, rather than DJing, and its perfectly suited for the task.  The pitch is immacuate, and the stock tonearm is adjustable in any way you'll ever need.  Because it's a direct drive table designed to put up with scratching, the motor will last forever with very little servicing under normal use (my table is over 30 years old, and has never been serviced so far as I know.) Even if you do need spare parts, the table is so common that they should be easy to come by.The solid cast aluminum body makes the table feel heavy and professional.  The weight keeps the table from vibrating, and the feet are adjustable to accommodate not-so-even surfaces.  Looks-wise, the table is solidly average, if not a bit on the chunky side.  It looks right at home with my vintage, silver face audio gear.Casual listeners should note that this table is not automatic in any way.  It is designed for direct manipulation, which means that it gives the user maximum control.  If you fall asleep to a record, this unit will ride the inner groove until you wake up and shut it off.  It does give you a handy start/stop button, and a little lever which gracefully drops the needle on the record for you, but you'll have to stand up to shut this unit off.I'd solidly recommend this table for people who want a quality piece of gear that they won't have to constantly perform maintenance on.  This table has stood the test of time, and will continue to do so.  Add to that infinite modding capability, perfect pitch, solid build, and a high resale value, and you can't go wrong.

I saw some reviews about sticking. IF YOU ARE HAVING A PROBLEM WITH THINGS STICKING TO THE PAN USE REAL BUTTER. ( I used unsalted sweet cream butter.) I used olive oil and palm oil first when I was doing eggs and they stuck like crazy. I let the pan cool and cleaned it out really good. As it was warming up it took a butter stick and rubbed it around on the insides. No muss no fuss. I always use low heat now and everything comes out perfect. I use it for bacon... Preseason it with butter, after you are done leave the pan to cool, add warm soappy water and let it sit a bit. The pan cleans right out.You just can't beat the efficiency of how these pans work. I got them because I was tired of my family eating teflon from non-stick pans. I would rather eat a little butter than poison my family with chemicals. It is also nice to know that if I take care of it I just bought something that will last the rest of my life.

I use this for my Samsung Galaxy S4. I test the current using the app "Galaxy Charging Current".When this cable is plugged into the OEM charger, the current rating is 1200, which is the same as the OEM cable. This is the rapid charge rate allowed by my phone.When plugged into a computer USB socket, the current rating is less at 420-460. I am able to transfer data via this cable. When combined with the portapow fast charge only USB adapter, I get  rating of 520-560 (not much better).I was actually looking for a charge only cable (this is not), but will keep the cable anyway as a spare.The end that connects into the phone is bulky as other reviews have said. It barely fits my phone with the Otterbox Commuter case on.

It's so big and the base is so tall that it blocks vision, the display is hard to read in daylight, it tells me to go the wrong way at times, the street  the names appear with dots rather on the streets themselves, and it shows me driving through fields rather than on roads at times. The OmniMount OC80FM Full Motion TV Mount is one of the best mounts available.  It's low profile lets your TV sit close to the wall when needed and easily adjust out any other time.  It's sturdy and well built.Installation is fairly simple.  If you have ever installed a mount before, you'll easily get this going.  Two studs or other support is not necessary as it can mount on one stud.  Even if you haven't installed a mount before, you'll have a pretty easy job at the OmniMount.  Thirty minutes max for those being more careful/detailed.I mounted a 46" TV and it performs very well.  It's very sturdy and adjustments/swiveling is solid, but easy to adjust.  No sagging over time.  It's a simple mount that gets the job done.

I will say this up front - for the price it does a decent job and seeing as it is less than $30 compared to a few hundred for Pocket Wizards I can't complain much. With that said, however, I am a bit disappointed with the reliability of firings. With brand new batteries in everything (flash, camera, trigger and receiver I can only count on it firing about 1 in 5 times. This can get very frustrating. I think it is due to the fact that it has a very loose connection with the flash (I have tried multiple, they all fit loosely on the receiver and jiggle a bit). The signal is great - I did some tests when I first got it and can be through walls and across the house (not that I would ever be shooting 3 rooms away from the flash) but it still fired (with the above stated reliability though that was the same whether I was a foot from it or 3 rooms away) ... It's not on par with golf or extreme mountain climbing, but housecleaning has become something of a hobbyist sport for me. Because there are only about three hours to spend on it each week, I am picky, picky, picky about the tools and products in my cleaning cupboard. And the cupboard isn't big, so each piece has to earn its space.The Enviro Steamer is one of the most effective and, frankly, fun tools I use each week. At first, though, it was horrible to see how much gunk the thing picked up from our supposedly clean kitchen floor. It's not great for scuff marks from the Crocs I should have taken off at the door, but those aren't my biggest problem. Rather, the issue is dirt practically ground in by the last (and maybe final!) housekeeper's cleaning products. It's going to take a while and I'm not sure this vinyl floor can be saved, but the kitchen looks and smells cleaner since I've been using the steamer and nothing else on the floor.This might be true only for cleaning enthusiasts, but I think it's a real charge to run the thing around. First, the area it has passed over or rested on briefly is immediately, visibly cleaner. Second, the machine itself is light enough that it is pretty easy to maneuver. Third -- and I think this is killer -- it is QUIET. Sorry to shout that out, but this might be more quiet than the little slap and squish of using a conventional mop. Except for an occasional soft hissing that lets me indulge a fantasy of conducting a little locomotive, it is inaudible.And then there's the matter of the effort and expense one doesn't have to make in obtaining the cleaning solution, which happens to be water. Yup, plain old straight-from-the-kitchen-faucet water. Not filtered, not doctored up with expensive tinctures or other smelly additives: plain water.At its price point, Eureka has probably taken the design as far as it can go. But I, for one, would be happy to pay more for a retractable cord and a sturdier pan for the steamer to rest on as it's heating up and cooling down. It would also be nice to have the steamer engineered in a way that one would not risk getting burned when refilling it. And a place to store the measuring cup and funnel (maybe a funnel could even be integral to the design) would be welcome. Mine are probably about to disappear, victims of my absent-mindedness. Finally, it would be nice to have a construction that were less floppy. It really is unwieldy.Still, there is a limit to what a company can be expecting to engineer and manufacture at this price. Many of the things I suggest would certainly have an impact on production costs. For many of us, this method of cleaning is arcane enough that we wouldn't chance even $100 on a machine. Now, though, there might be enough committed users to pay more.In the meantime, take it for what it is: an effective, relatively user friendly cleaning tool that runs only on water and is quieter than any other appliance in my house, except for the stove. I would not want to be without my Enviro Steamer.

Like many people we used stove top kettles for years.I was interested in electrics, but never wanted to pay the price to try one.I have to say that this appliance rates right up there as one of the best purchases we've ever made. These are the best of the NiMh on the market in my opinion.  I've used others, but the quality and reliability of these beats them all.  A few things I like and why I just bought 8 more of these third generation units.1.  Repeatable quality and made in Japan.  These all charge within about 50 mAH of each other.  I routinely see the capacity at 2000 mAh as measured on a MAHA C-9000 smart charger.2.  Low self discharge - that's the big advantage of these.  I use them in bluetooth keyboards, mice, trackpads; in remote controls; in weather sensors (yes they work outside at 10 below zero in Nebraska); in cameras, in headlamps, in flashlights.  In short, I use them in everything that takes a AA battery.I have bought all kinds of rechargeable over the years (and I've used rechargeable for as long as they have been on the market, starting with NiCad, etc.).  I have used Powerex,  Energizer, Radio Shack, LaCrosse, Rayovac, just about all the brands.  Now I've stopped buying the others and standardized on the Sanyo's.  I have a dozen or so of the 1st Gen, another 20 or so of the second ten, and now 8 of the third gen.  I'm getting rid of all the other brands I have that don't work as well.Don't let the 2000 mAH rating fool you and don't buy just for huge capacity.  The larger capacities self discharge much more rapidly and can't take the same number of cycles.In short, you can't beat these for reliability and quality.  The others are not as good.

I really hate having to split a line.  It lowers the signal strength a lot, exposes the signal to interference, and creates a failure point.  There isn't much you can do if you need a split, though, other than doing it as well as you possibly can.This is a very heavy duty splitter.  It has high gauge lines, and a well-sealed outer grounded shell to keep out interference.  While it is true that standard cable signals do not go over (or even approach) one gigahertz, I like that this one is designed to handle the much more difficult to protect two gigahertz signals that are used in DSS satellite dishes.  Frequencies that high are difficult to keep from leaking out, so if it can handle those I know I don't have to worry about the lower cable frequencies leaking out either.Overkill for most, but cheap insurance to help protect a common source of noise and interference.

This is a really good and affordable snap-on case for the Nexus 7.  It allows for convenient viewing angles in the "standing" mode and provides reasonable protection for the device.To put it into standing mode, you must un-clip two of the corners to get it to stand.  It's not obvious to me which ones you un-clip but the case helps by having two four clips on the side you're not supposed to un-clip.  I think a future revision of this design might omit the corner clips since it is held very tightly by the other four.One thing I and others have noticed is that it may not provide adequate protection from drops on the corners because it fits so nicely snug that, perhaps, it should be a little less snug allowing for some buffer space between the edge of the Nexus 7 and the edge of the case.You will enjoy this case since it's one of the few, affordable, good quality cases that are specifically designed for the Nexus 7.

The Duracell GoPower Longhaul backup battery is a really handy way to take extra power with you on the road.  This battery pack is about the size and weight of a deck of cards and has an LED display to tell you how much charge it currently has.  Having this in your carry-on luggage is a lot easier than hunting for charging stations in the airport and reduces my stress level when traveling (I know I won't end up with a dead phone battery).Even though the GoPower is fairly small, it packs a plenty of charging power, especially if charging smaller devices like cell phones.  Rated at 8800mAh, this can completely charge a drained iPhone 5  multiple times from 0 to 100%.  It has two USB ports so you can charge two devices at the same time.  I tested charging an iPad 3 and an iPhone 5 at the same time and the Duracell GoPower worked great.  I also tested charging two iPhone 5s at the same time, and again, it worked perfectly.The battery in an iPad 3 has higher capacity than the GoPower (iPad 3 is 11666 mAh versus 8800 mAh for the Duracell) so this battery pack cannot fully charge an iPad 3.  In my testing, the Duracell GoPower was able to take an iPad 3 from 10% to 70% charge.  That took just under four hours.  At that point, the Duracell was completely drained.  Keep this in mind if your primary use is going to be to charge one of the big-battery iPads: you only get a single partial charge.  Still, that is probably enough to get you through a brutal day of travel.One minor complaint is that Duracell did not include an actual wall charger for charging this battery.  Instead, they probably hope you'll spring for the wireless Duracell Powermat charger.  I don't have a powermat so I found the lack of a wall charger somewhat inconvenient.  To get around this, I simply used the charger for my iPad to charge the Duracell.  It worked great and can charge this from empty to full in about 7-8 hours.After more than a month of ownership, I can confirm that this battery pack retains its charge, even if it is not used for extended time periods (1 month+).I like the GoPower a lot and think it is a great product for road warriors.

***UPDATEOtterbox bought Lifeproof 5/22/2013. This confirms that even Otterbox knows they didn't make a winner. If you can't beat them... Buy them!********The case plastics are finished well but the pieces do not interlock with any precision. Once "sealed", a 1/16" and greater gap all around remained where the back plate meets the case. The case felt like a prototype and is in need of refinement.  The amount water trapped in the gap between the case and backplate (even after shaking and wiping dry) left me questioning if  a water-tight seal had been created.  Several drops water found their way inside on the water test prior to installing the phone. I could not confidently discern whether it happened during the test or from separating the case.Once the iPhone was enclosed calls seemed clear and the touchscreen remained very usable without any significant decrease in sensitivity. Buttons like the volume, home, power and mute all seemed functional and responsive.The Lifeproof Fre case also didn't impress me, but I do like the Fre over this plastic brick. I would find it difficult to recommend this case due to size, weight and questionable functionality of being truly water-tight.

UPDATE: February 2014After about five years of use, the black rubbery grip on the handle of my OXO fat separator fell off.  It's possible to slide it back into place and use the cup as usual, but the grip no longer stays on by itself.  I now store the grip inside the cup when not in use.  Although I could contact OXO and might receive a replacement cup, I don't really mind replacing the grip each time I use the cup.  I can't see throwing away something that still serves its purpose.  "Use it up; wear it out; make it do or do without" has been my motto for years.  Anyway - I changed my rating to four stars because of the inconvenience of the faulty grip.Here is my original 2008 review:I positively love my OXO fat separator!  It holds a good amount of liquid, but not so much as to be difficult to pour.  The lid helps strain out the solids, but if you want your broth to be absolutely clear, put a piece of cheesecloth over the lid.IMPORTANT - the little black stopper MUST be inserted in the pouring spout before you add liquids to the cup or the fat will simply rise up in the spout.  The stopper creates a vacuum, forcing the fat into the main body of the cup.  When you're ready to pour, remove the stopper and the fat will remain floating in the body of the cup.This cup is made out of high-quality plastic and washes up well in the dishwasher. It doesn't stain when you wash it with the tomato sauces! I've used mine for a couple of years and it's as good as new.  I wouldn't make gravy or broth without it.

durable and well made, much  better than the cheaper products available, which were intermittant.  you must pay $8 to get any durability and quality.

Researching humidifiers on Amazon was a somewhat tiresome exercise. Almost all products I looked at are subject to very serious complaints, most importantly noise, leakage, and lack of reliability. I decided to go with the AOS 7147 because most reviewers agree that it is really quiet, and because I liked the clean look and the features.Here is what I can report after about a week of usage (will update if anything changes over time):Noise level: Both my wife and I are extremely noise sensitive, but this unit can operate in our bedroom without affecting our sleep. Yes, there is a bit of a humming noise, but it is barely audible. Also there is a bubbling noise once in a while when water flows out of the tank into the tray. All-in-all, it seems about as quiet as can reasonably be expected.Effectiveness: We use the auto setting that lets the device decide the target level of humidity and the output level. This works very well and hassle free, keeping the room comfortably moist.Warm mist setting: I would recommend having it. Without it the room feels cool and damp. Even with using it there is a bit of a cooling effect, but it is acceptable.Filtering/water treatment: We did not see any white dust around the unit, which was a concern.Reliability/ease of use: No problems so far, straight-forward to operate, everything works as intended. I will post updates, if something comes up.Look: Matters more than you would think, because it is supposed to be three feet away from the ground and away from both heating and return vents. Inevitably it will end up in a rather visible place. This unit has a bit of a techy, but overall relatively neutral look, better than most.In summary, I am very happy with this unit.Update: After a winter, We are still happy with it. So we bought a 2nd one to be used in the office.Some tips on reducing the humming noise. Put a tray underneath the unit, then a thick sponge pad or similar cushion underneath the tray. The cushion dramatically reduces the noise level. The tray is very important. We first put the sponge directly underneath the unit, and that totally screwed up its moisture reading. We could watch the moisture level reduce from 50 to 49 to 48... to 37 in a few minutes and the unit cranked out steam like crazy. It seems that the unit needs to be placed on a hard surface to read the moisture level properly. Once we put a tray underneath it, the reading works normal again.

Three screen protectors, one cleaning cloth and a hard card come in an envelope. Wasn't too sure about what I was doing as I have never done this before. Read the instructions and then gave it a try. The hard part was ensuring I had aligned the protector EXACTLY. After that it was just a matter of ensuring there were no air bubbles. The surface feels different but works as before. A regular rubber stylus will not work, at least MINE didn't. I use a stylus with a tip that looks like a cloth ball on the end. Best is a finger and there are no streaks. Glad I bought these.

I want to like this camera, but the one I got has a serious flaw:  the battery drains when sitting still.  I noticed after a week that every time I tried to use it the battery was dead.  After observing it for awhile, I noticed that it would turn itself on without being touched or moved in any way. First the viewer turned in, then the lenses popped out and the whole thing fired up. I tried to figure it out but finally decided it was just broken.  Unfortunately I waited one day too long to return it to Amazon, so had to send it back to Sony.  That was three weeks ago.  If I get it back soon and it works alright I'll figure I just got a dud and will upgrade this review.  If it still has a problem I'll downgrade it to one star.  But from what I read from other reviewers my problem is not unique.Update:  after 3 weeks, I got the camera back from Sony.  The tech did not read the letter explaining the problem, tested the battery and pronounced it good, then returned the camera without actually fixing it.  Useless.Update 2:  After giving up on the useless Sony technical support, I took self-help and foisted the turkey back on Sony at their store.  The replacement so far has not exhibited the same problems.  I took it on a 10 day trip to Rome and did not have to charge it once.  The pictures I took came out well.  What I particularly like about the camera is the size.  I LOVED being able to carry it in my pocket knowing that it can take as good a picture as most of the massive DSLRs I saw other people lugging around.  I LOVED being able to take pictures in any light without a flash.  The controls are easy to operate.  Frankly, though, I care much more about the artistic aspect of photography than the technical.  I found it laughable to see yet another person using their expensive camera to take yet another picture of the Colosseum, as though something novel might be revealed.  A good picture is unexpected, and depends more on the eye of the photographer than the equipment s/he is using.  The size of this camera makes it discrete, and that helps capture the unique moment.  The sophistication of the RX100 makes it possible to do that with technical quality as well.Overall, a big thumbs up to the camera, and a big thumbs down to Sony service (hence the 4 star rating not 5).

I was looking for a USB car charger that offered a higher mA output to charge my new Note 3. I read several reviews about similar chargers and settled on this one. The one thing I noted was that a CHARGE-ONLY USB cable was needed to utilize the full 2.1A output advertised. So I also ordered one of those which was harder to find than I thought.To be clear, you CAN use a regular Micro USB cable, but from what I have read you will only get a fraction of the 2.1A charge. Also know that amperage is "pulled" by your device, so your phone will only use as much of the 2.1 amps that it is built for.I ended up also buying a 39" Red Band Charge-Only Angled Micro USB cable which arrived on time and is a quality product as well. It seems to work well with the Scosche charge and my phone does charge very quickly. I don't know how to tell if I am getting all 2.1A, but it does keep my Note 3 charged very easily and seems to charge it more quickly than my basic Motorola Micro USB car charger charged my Galaxy nexus in the past.Here is a direct link to that cable. Be aware that there are different versions of this cable. The version you need is dependent on:1) the orientation of the micro USB port on your device and2) the orientation of your phone when/if it is docked.I have also written a review for this cable if you look around. The link below is for the cable itself.http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B00GTXLTHK/ref=oh_details_o03_s00_i00?ie=UTF8&psc;=1
